title: e2e on by default: registry default subagent:general-purpose + explore-propose-seed recipe authoring
description:
Thesis (grilled 2026-07-03, FT-1330 origin): silent-skip-by-default hides the pipeline's highest-leverage correctness gate. The e2e stage is the ONE stage that observes the change actually behaving; it significantly improves end-to-end accuracy and correctness. It must default ON; skipping stays possible but always deliberate: per-ticket 'skip: <reason>' at plan time, or an explicit workspace 'e2e = "none"'. No silent omission anywhere.

Changes:
1. stage-registry.toml: e2e default_handler none -> subagent:general-purpose. Reword description: drop 'Opt-in.', add importance framing.
2. verb-spec.md step 4 (recipe settling): when .flow/e2e-recipes.md is absent, EXPLORE the repo (CI config, mise/make/npm tasks, docker-compose, test layout), PROPOSE 1-3 concrete candidate recipes for the user to confirm/edit, then SEED .flow/e2e-recipes.md with the settled pattern. Cookbook = engine convention adopted from brinta's reference implementation (decision table ticket-touches->runner, fixtures, env-prep incl auth quirks, sentinels, unambiguous pass signal E2E_OK/E2E_FAIL). Machine-local in user repos (.flow/ gitignored) and self-regenerating via the same explore path on a fresh machine. Add a references/ authoring template doc loaded at that step.
3. --auto path (verb-spec ~line 220): cookbook present -> author a real recipe from its decision table (deliberate-by-proxy; the cookbook was human-confirmed); cookbook absent -> keep the test-ci-only floor. Never silent-skip, never block autonomy.
4. Importance framing at EVERY touchpoint the diff crosses (registry description, stage-e2e.md preamble which currently opens 'This stage is opt-in', verb-spec step 4, cookbook template header, skip-sentinel wording): e2e as first-class correctness gate; skip is the exceptional justified path, never the convenient one. Half the audience is agents reading these docs verbatim; motivated instructions change how hard an agent tries before reaching the escape hatch.

Blast radius: stage-e2e.md Purpose + Skip conditions wording; background-pipeline.md:77 ('when e2e is opted in'); SKILL.md:81 mention (verify, keep SKILL.md thin); inventory.md tables; init tests asserting bare-bundle defaults; seam_check green. Existing workspaces unaffected (init writes all handlers explicitly, so their workspace.toml pins e2e). Interplay with flow-nnft (reconfigure preserve-by-default keyed on differs-from-registry-default): after this flip an old workspace's explicit none differs from the default and must be preserved as a customization - that is correct behavior; mind the semantics if both land close together.

Dogfood: flow's own workspace.toml (TRACKED here, self-target) flips e2e to subagent:general-purpose + seeds .flow/e2e-recipes.md in a separate small PR (2026-07-03), so the maintainer eats the friction before users do; nightly --auto evolve runs then exercise the test-ci-only floor.
close_reason: Shipped: engine change merged in PR #428 (registry default flip + explore-propose-seed cookbook + --auto test-ci-only floor + importance framing); dogfood half in PR #427.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Dogfood half shipped separately: PR #427 flips this repo's own workspace (e2e = subagent:general-purpose) and seeds the tracked .flow/e2e-recipes.md cookbook. This bead remains the engine change: registry default flip + explore-propose-seed + --auto cookbook-else-test-ci-only + importance framing.

title: init --reconfigure silently resets customized [pipeline.handlers] to registry defaults
description:
Incident: in brinta-data-platform, /flow init --reconfigure (2026-06-19) silently reset e2e = "subagent:general-purpose" back to the registry default "none". Nobody noticed until 2026-07-03; every ticket specced in between skipped e2e entirely (17 worktrees carry none; the 06-01/06-09 worktrees still carry the customized handler, which is how it was dated).

Root cause: init.py _compose_handlers (line ~476) rebuilds handlers from stage-registry defaults + that invocation's --handler flags only. It never reads the existing workspace.toml, so any prior customization is dropped unless the user re-passes every --handler on every reconfigure. _backup_for_reconfigure already snapshots the old workspace.toml but nothing compares against it.

Decided direction (grilled 2026-07-03): preserve by default. On --reconfigure, read the existing workspace.toml [pipeline.handlers]; any handler whose value differs from the registry default is kept unless the new invocation explicitly overrides it via --handler. Explicit override still wins; silent clobber becomes impossible. Optionally print what was preserved.

Scope notes: applies to the reconfigure path only (fresh init has nothing to preserve). Watch the bundle=recommended path too: discovered-manifest handlers overwrite via the same _compose_handlers merge, so preserved user values need a defined precedence vs manifest-provided ones (suggest: existing customization > manifest > registry default, with --handler above all).

title: Semantic recall PR1: bge/fastembed embedder + top-K cosine selection + enable
description:
Fix the silent no-op + harden against recurrence.

- NEW embedder_fastembed.py (sibling of embedder_model2vec.py): stdin texts -> JSON vectors via fastembed.TextEmbedding('BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5'). Same wire protocol (newline-joined stdin, JSON array out). Only this file imports fastembed; runs under uvx. Verify exact fastembed API via Context7 at impl.
- memory_embed.py: _DEFAULT_MODEL -> BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5; _default_command (78-87) -> uvx --with fastembed python embedder_fastembed.py --model ... ; dim stays auto-inferred (236, bge-small=384); model-id mismatch auto-triggers --full reindex (219-220).
- recall.py _semantic_rank (300-390): replace sim>=threshold with TOP-K by cosine (K~=2*top_n) + low relative floor (drop sim<=0). Demote DEFAULT_THRESHOLD (56) to floor / retire as primary gate.
- recall.py rrf_fuse (281-298): add optional weight param defaulting to equal (DORMANT, not config-exposed).
- Enable: [memory.semantic] enabled=true in this repo .flow/workspace.toml AND brinta FT workspace; update init.py config template (453-459) + calibration note. One recall.py --reindex --full per enabled workspace.
Verify: live embedder via uvx returns 384-float vec; reindex header model/dim correct; cosine_candidates>0 for every query (no starvation); semantic reorders vs BM25 on a paraphrase query; mise run test green + new top-K + dormant-weight tests; seam_check green.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Draft PR #353 — https://github.com/vdsmon/flow/pull/353. Embedder swap (bge/fastembed) + top-K cosine selection + batch-scaled timeout + enable. Verified live: cosine_candidates=20 where potion+τ=0.30 gave 0. 2236 tests green.

title: Semantic recall: repair the silent no-op, swap to bge, make recall measured
description:
WHY: flow's semantic-recall overlay (shipped by flow-vuff/PR#348) is a SILENT NO-OP. Embedder loads (semantic-active) but absolute cosine threshold tau=0.30 (recall.py:56) is miscalibrated for potion-retrieval-32M: on the live corpus relevant cosines sit 0.15-0.5, so most queries yield cosine_candidates=0 and recall falls back to byte-identical BM25. Flagship self-referential query returned ZERO semantic candidates. Root cause: using an absolute threshold AT ALL for an embedder-scale-dependent quantity. And nothing measures recall quality, so it rotted undetected.

WHAT: (1) swap embedder potion->bge-small-en-v1.5 via fastembed (ONNX, no torch, rides the existing uvx subprocess seam, runtime stays pure-stdlib); (2) retire absolute tau, select top-K cosine candidates (embedder-agnostic, cannot starve); (3) keep equal-weight RRF, wire a DORMANT optional weight param; (4) OBSERVABILITY keystone: reflect emits used_recall_ids -> recall-usage.jsonl -> recall-hit-rate metric (precision) PLUS bge near-dup miss-detection at knowledge-write (re-learned-what-we-knew); (5) intent-framed query construction (augment ticket text with a problem/risk preamble naming the form/domain) so form-grouping falls out by consequence.

SUBSTRATE LOCKED: L1 jsonl files (truth) + L2 pure-stdlib BM25+cosine+RRF stay. Turso/sqlite-vec/vector-DBs rejected (no embedding-gen, ANN unshipped/overkill at ~340 vectors, break bare-python3/zero-infra/git-portable invariants; sqlite-vec empirically dead -- runtime python has enable_load_extension compiled out).

DEFERRED (metric-gated): fusion-weight tuning, multi-query, tags-facet (= existing flow-a0qu, references the same form:iva_2083 case). Plan: ~/.claude/plans/agile-percolating-sunrise.md

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MERGE BLOCKED by GitHub Actions minutes/spending cap (private repo). Sharp cutoff: all CI runs <=21:22Z green (incl PR1 #353 merged, PR3 #355 green), all runs >=21:26Z fail in 2s with no runner assigned + 0 steps (classic minutes-exhaustion signature). Not a code issue: PR2 content passes locally (2248+20 tests, ruff+ty+seam) and e2e with real bge (hit_rate=1.0, RECALL_MISS @0.9561). State: PR1 MERGED to main; PR2 re-cut as #357 (nylh2-obs->main, CI red=billing); PR3 #355 ready (green) but needs retarget to main once PR2 lands. Resolution needs maintainer: raise Actions spending limit / wait for monthly reset / make repo public — then re-run CI + merge. Note: version-stamp.yml ALSO needs Actions, so a bypass-merge now would not auto-stamp.

title: review_loop polls review-threads once at CI-green with no review-bot-completion gate — late CR review (Major+) silently missed, false 'review-clean'
description:
WITNESSED on a real forge-seam work run (FT-1019 / Bitbucket PR brinta-uy/brinta-data-platform#2849, 2026-06-19; the ship-it-retirement validation run).

GAP: stage-review_loop.md couples review-thread polling to CI-green, but CR review COMPLETION is a separate async event. §3 ('Poll review threads') runs forge_cli review-threads exactly ONCE, immediately after the §1 CI Monitor breaks on green. §5 terminal = 'CI green AND zero unresolved Major+ threads'. There is NO gate that the review bot has actually POSTED its review before trusting an empty thread list. CI-green != CR-has-reviewed.

WITNESS TIMELINE: PR #2849 opened ~14:02Z; CI went green ~2min later; review_loop fetched threads -> [] -> terminal 'completed' at 14:06:04Z, report 'Review threads: [] (CodeRabbit posted no actionable findings). Fix cycles run: 0.' CodeRabbit then posted a real (Minor) thread (id=815071892, 'Add the positive boundary-date test') AFTER the loop closed. forge review-threads itself is fine — it correctly returned [] then and returns the thread now; the loop just never waited for CR.

SEVERITY: benign this time (Minor never triggers a fix cycle per §4, so outcome unchanged) — BUT a late CR review carrying a MAJOR+ finding would be silently missed: the loop terminates 'review-clean' past an unaddressed blocker, and fires the PR-ready notification. Also the stage report falsely asserts 'CR posted no findings' when CR simply hadn't run.

FIX DIRECTION: add a bounded review-bot-completion gate before §3's empty-threads conclusion. Options: (a) after CI green, re-poll review-threads on a bounded wait (e.g. up to N min, interval re-poll) and only conclude clean if still empty at the cap; (b) gate on a CR review-complete marker (CodeRabbit posts a walkthrough/summary comment) as proof-of-review before trusting threads==[]; (c) require at least one bot artifact present before terminal. MUST stay bounded (the flow-lv34 sibling: CR may never review a draft on some repos / no-bot hosts -> the existing {supported:false} skip + a timeout must hold so it doesn't hang). Tune the wait to the observed CR latency (CR posted within a few min of CI-green here).

PARITY NOTE (relevant to flow-2je / ship-it retirement): verify whether ship-it's feedback skill waited for CR review completion. If it did and the inline forge review_loop does not, this is a REGRESSION introduced by the seam migration, and should land before ship-it is deleted.

BLAST RADIUS: stage-review_loop.md §3/§5 prose (+ possibly a forge_cli review-bot-presence probe). review_loop handler is inline; stage-*.md prose governs next run. Likely non-hot (not a guard file), but it changes a stage terminal condition — review carefully. Related: flow-lv34 (CR-wait-window class, closed).
close_reason: merged PR#352 (squash 2a7a2f9), stamped main 0.61.1, marketplace vdsmon-flow updated; review_loop now gates on CodeRabbit check-state completion

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED → draft PR https://github.com/vdsmon/flow/pull/352 (branch worktree-flow-arva-cr-gate). forge.bot_review_present(pr) completion gate: bitbucket keys on the CodeRabbit commit-status CHECK terminal-state (NOT a comment marker — verified zero-finding PRs post no 'Actionable comments posted' count, only a Walkthrough which lands at review START; a marker gate would burn the full wait on every clean PR). github→NotSupported→floor. stage-review_loop §3 bounded-wait + §5 terminal tie-in. Full suite 2233 green, lint+seam clean, 6 new tests + live-API smoke on #2849/#2846. Confirmed REGRESSION from the forge-seam migration (ship-it feedback.md Step 3.2 waited for the reviewer check; the inline loop dropped it) — so flow-2je (retire ship-it) stays gated on this merging.

title: L2: engine detect-and-recover for dirty-main edits made before bootstrap
description: Python can't intercept a non-CC harness's raw edit tool, so it can't PREVENT coding-on-main-before-bootstrap; it can DETECT-and-RECOVER at the first engine call. flow_worktree create / bootstrap detects a dirty main checkout with ticket-relevant uncommitted edits -> relocate into the seeded worktree, or refuse with instructions. GATE ON THE SYMPTOM (dirty main), not harness identity: on CC plan-mode keeps main clean -> guard finds nothing -> silent no-op -> zero CC ergonomic cost. This is the portable substitute for CC's plan-mode write-block.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #347, merged to main d7f1496, stamped v0.58.0, marketplace updated.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Built + PR'd in #347. Engine spill recovery via flow_worktree create --recover-spill; flag-gated so CC path unchanged. Advisor caught + fixed: stash→copy (loss-proof), and the flag (not symptom) is the CC/non-CC discriminator.

title: L1: ship AGENTS.md loader so non-CC harnesses load SKILL.md+harness.md
description: Off-CC there's no plugin manifest, so nothing loads the flow skill (root cause of Cursor's skipped stages). Ship a canonical AGENTS.md stanza that (a) imports/inlines SKILL.md + references/harness.md, (b) states the approval-NOT-coding contract + soft-gate discipline, (c) carries the CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR export from harness.md. AGENTS.md is the cross-harness standard (Cursor/Codex/opencode/Windsurf). CC-NEUTRAL: CC ignores AGENTS.md (uses plugin manifest), so the file is invisible to CC. /flow init writes/appends it. Document in harness.md Entry-point section.

comments.text: Built + PR'd in #347 (epic flow-mob7). AGENTS.md loader, opt-in --agents-md, CC-neutral.

title: Harness-portable nudge: CC-first, additive fallbacks for non-CC agents
description: flow runs degrade on non-CC harnesses (Cursor skipped stages: coded on main before bootstrap + freelanced the do-loop). CC-FIRST invariant: zero change to the CC hot path; every layer is an additive fallback branch gated on the SYMPTOM (primitive absent / dirty main / no plan-approval signal), never on harness identity. See references/harness.md. Children: L1 AGENTS.md loader, L2 engine detect-and-recover dirty-main.

title: Knowledge consolidation pass: cluster + merge redundant entries to raise corpus signal density
description:
WHY: recall caps results (top_n=5 today) partly because the corpus carries REDUNDANCY -- many non-contradictory entries restating ~one fact -- so each retrieved entry is low-signal. supersede (memory_append) kills CONTRADICTIONS and flow-ufvu adds rank-decay, but neither merges redundant-but-consistent entries. Consolidation attacks the root cause: raise signal density so a HIGHER recall cap / semantic threshold (flow-vuff) returns a cleaner set instead of duplicative noise.

WHAT: a PERIODIC BATCH pass (NOT inline at append -- inline merge fights the append-only + flock + crash-safety invariant). The pass READS knowledge.jsonl, clusters near-duplicate live entries (semantic similarity -> depends on flow-vuff embeddings), synthesizes ONE canonical entry per cluster, and writes it via the normal path PLUS supersede tombstones for the merged dups. Append-only preserved (canonical is a new append; dups die by supersede, same mechanism recall.filter_superseded already resolves).

GUARDRAILS:
- auto-merge can destroy nuance / hallucinate a synthesis. Apply reflect's discipline: merge only on ground-truth equivalence, or PROPOSE-and-review; likely maintainer-gated (like hot auto-merge). Conservative default: merge only provably-equivalent entries.
- belongs as an evolve-janitor-style periodic job, not a per-run step.

DEPS: depends-on flow-vuff (needs embeddings for clustering). relates flow-ufvu (both manage corpus health / the live set).
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#403: sweep_knowledge cluster lane (deterministic complete-linkage over memory_embed sidecar, threshold 0.90, within-type) + apply-cluster manifest path writing ONE canonical via list-valued memory_append supersedes; recall filter handles scalar+list; corpus-health metric added. Review fix d76ee80: empty supersede targets now error at the apply layer instead of silently appending junk. CI note: GitHub dropped all pull_request events for this branch's later pushes (synchronize AND reopened) — merged on full local gate run (ruff+format+ty clean, 2456+20 tests, seam 224/45 clean, identical commands to CI); post-merge main push CI is the backstop. Corpus cleanup itself deliberately NOT run in-PR: follow-up bead flow-0ruw (maintainer propose-and-review one-shot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: ALTITUDE PASS 2026-07-02 (deep review, adversarially verified): promoted to P1, second in drain order after flow-oe9. The loop's own instrumentation validates this bead: recall-usage.jsonl shows 22 used of 264 surfaced (8.3% precision — ~92% of recall injection is noise), knowledge.jsonl carries 356 entries with only 4 supersede tombstones ever (all from reflect 3b; the sweep_knowledge propose/apply lane wired in verb-evolve.md has never produced one). Interim actions worth folding into scope: actually run the existing sweep_knowledge lane on the DECISION/FACT backlog, and have stage-reflect surface metric.py recall-hit-rate propose-side so the number reaches the loop, not just the maintainer.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED. Ship the consolidation MECHANISM (not the corpus cleanup). Extend sweep_knowledge.py with a deterministic `cluster` propose (cosine over the memory_embed sidecar, within-type, complete-linkage above a conservative threshold) + an `apply-cluster` write that collapses a cluster to ONE canonical live entry via list-valued `supersedes=[m1..mN]`. This requires memory_append.py (accept str|list, validate all targets in one flock scan), recall.superseded_ids (union list targets), and metric.py corpus-health (the :581 set-comprehension throws unhashable-list on a list tombstone) — all NON-guard files, so non-hot (confirmed is_hot=false with the full planned-files set). Also surfaces metric.py recall-hit-rate propose-side in stage-reflect (interim action 2), and extends the verb-evolve.md `## curate` recipe with the cluster->synthesize->apply lane (NOT a new SKILL.md verb — a routed verb edits the guard-file routing row and would force hot; flow-ufvu.3).

Safe to auto-ship: shipped code is propose-only + append-only; the hallucination-prone synthesis is human-authored between the two deterministic steps and never runs unattended. Forward-compat ordering clinches it — no list-valued tombstone can exist until this recall.py is deployed (apply-cluster is maintainer-run post-merge), so every reader (incl. the SessionStart hook, which routes through recall.py) resolves lists by the time one is written. Reversible, CI-gated (ruff+ty+pytest+seam_check), single-consumer maintainer repo.

MANDATORY implementer commitment (the one closeable hole from the sub-90% self-score): do NOT ship a bare untuned threshold. During implement, run sweep_knowledge.py cluster --threshold X against the live 353-vector sidecar (the worktree memory redirect points at main .flow) and calibrate tau so proposed clusters are genuinely near-duplicate, before finalizing the default.

DEFERRED to a follow-up: interim-action-1 (actually running the sweep on the live 356-entry corpus) — corpus-mutating + needs a human on each synthesis; this PR ships the mechanism, value realized when a maintainer runs it.

title: Semantic retrieval for knowledge.jsonl: hybrid embedding index over the BM25 recall layer
description:
WHY: recall.py is pure lexical BM25 (hand-rolled, recall.py:1). A query worded differently from the stored body misses relevant knowledge entirely -- 'local lake table writes' will not surface FT-1061's duckdb-iceberg FACT because no tokens overlap. This caps the compounding value of the memory layer: knowledge is there but un-retrievable unless the query happens to share vocabulary. Witnessed against brinta-data-platform (92 entries, 25 tickets, single FT namespace).

WHAT: add an OPTIONAL semantic recall path as a hybrid over the existing store. knowledge.jsonl stays source-of-truth (keep append-only + flock + fsync + quarantine -- do not touch the storage layer). Build an embedding sidecar index (e.g. .flow/<ns>/knowledge.embed) refreshed at append-time or lazily on recall when stale. recall fuses BM25 + cosine (reciprocal-rank fusion or weighted) and falls back to PURE BM25 when no embedding runtime is present -- the stdlib-only + offline invariant MUST hold (gate the embedding dep behind a flag/optional import; byte-identical BM25 behavior when absent).

RETRIEVAL CONTRACT (from design discussion -- threshold only becomes meaningful WITH embeddings, so it lives here):
- Replace single-knob top_n with THRESHOLD-gated retrieval: return all entries with cosine >= tau, hard-capped at K (K >> 5, e.g. 30-50). Threshold filters relevance; cap bounds context blowup. (BM25 scores are unnormalized/query-length-dependent => NOT thresholdable; this is why the current cap is a count of 5. Cosine in [-1,1] is. Do NOT add a BM25 threshold.)
- PER-CONTEXT budgets, not one global top_n: (1) SessionStart auto-recall fires every session into context -> keep tight (threshold + modest cap, protects the context budget; matters more as the corpus grows to thousands). (2) explicit mid-run /flow recall + spec/plan deep recall -> looser, higher cap.
- LABEL queries (flow-a0qu) BYPASS the relevance cap: form:iva_2083 IS the relevance filter, return the whole cluster up to a high safety ceiling.

NOTES:
- Coordinate with flow-ufvu (temporal supersession / rank decay) -- both rewrite recall ranking; sequence so they compose, not collide.
- memory_append.py / recall.py are NOT guard files -> normal (non-hot) path.
- Open question for spec: local embedding model choice (must run offline, no network) vs accept a soft network dep behind the flag. Decide in plan mode.

title: capture-implement-diff misclassifies a staged-deletion planned file as untracked, gitignore guard aborts commit
description: MACHINERY (surfaced by flow-kwai run 99e27e442f7b66ce, friction fb23b4f033ea42508930c02311cd3517). capture_implement_diff (diff_extract.py:332) classifies planned files via _untracked_files (git ls-files). A 'cached-removal' staged deletion (git rm --cached, used to untrack a file while keeping the working copy) is absent from git ls-files, so the path reads as untracked-new. _gitignored (diff_extract.py:337-342) then matches it against a .gitignore rule added in the SAME change and raises _IgnoredPlannedFile: 'planned file(s) gitignored, cannot be committed: <path>' (exit 1), aborting the commit stage. But the diff IS committable: git diff HEAD emits the staged deletion + the .gitignore hunk cleanly; no intent-to-add is needed for a deletion. The guard's premise (untracked + gitignored = uncommittable newly-created file) is false for an intentional untrack-and-ignore. FIX: exempt staged deletions (git diff --cached --diff-filter=D --name-only, or git ls-files --deleted) from the untracked/intent-to-add candidate set before the _gitignored check. HOT: diff_extract.py is in triage._GUARD_FILES (content-ownership commit gate), so the fix needs the merge-time guard-property review. Repro: any /flow ticket whose plan untracks-and-gitignores a planned file (exactly flow-kwai: untrack .claude/settings.json). BLOCKS flow-kwai.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed. The fix is correct and safe to auto-ship despite being a hot/guard-file change. Which option: exempt staged deletions (git diff --cached --diff-filter=D --name-only) from the untracked candidate set in capture_implement_diff before the _gitignored check and intent-to-add; ls-files --deleted is the wrong query (opposite case). Why safe: blast radius is one function (_untracked_files has a single caller), the change can only un-break a previously-false abort (additive subtraction from a list), revert is trivial, and CI coverage is strong (existing capture tests + 3 new TDD regression tests, incl. preservation of the genuine gitignore-rejection guard). Independent reviewer scored 93% and ruled proceed on both axes. Hot proceed permitted under [evolve] adjudicate_hot; merge-time guard-property review remains the gate.

title: tmp paths use bare /tmp, blocked by sandbox write allowlist
description:
Flow reference files write scratch files to bare /tmp, which the Claude Code sandbox write allowlist denies (only $TMPDIR, /tmp/claude, /private/tmp/claude permitted). Commit stage dies before push with: 'operation not permitted: /tmp/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt exit=1'. Surfaced in transcript 4121956e (Jun 10 12:07); misdiagnosed at run time as a bitbucket network whitelist issue.

Fix: swap /tmp/ -> $TMPDIR/ in:
- plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-init.md:17  (mktemp /tmp/flow-init-XXXXXX.json)
- plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-spec.md:51  (PLAN=/tmp/flow-plan-$KEY.md)
- plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-commit.md  (/tmp/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt, 5 refs)

Leave test mock fixtures (/tmp/x.png in test_beads_adapter.py, test_tracker.py) untouched - no real write. CLAUDE.md already mandates $TMPDIR.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Already resolved at base by PR #227 (18cd9b7, 'fix: survive harness bash sandbox in bg runs'), merged before this bead was filed. Every line this ticket cites already uses ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} on origin/main: verb-init.md:17, verb-spec.md:51, stage-commit.md:69/71/92. Verified no bare-/tmp write site remains anywhere in flow scripts or docs at origin/main (only $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp in verb-evolve.md prose, which is legit). flow --auto branches off @default (origin/main), so the planned fix would land as a no-op empty PR (flow-749 drift-vs-base). Closing as duplicate / already-fixed rather than shipping an empty PR. The launcher checkout (HEAD) lags origin/main, which is why the bug was still visible at filing time.

title: version_remerge recover: exits 0 leaving merge uncommitted when stamp-replace fails ('no version line to replace')
description: Witnessed 2026-06-09 draining PR #190 (flow-hw1.5): branch stamped 0.27.61 after main moved to 0.27.60 (PR #191) -> mergeStateStatus DIRTY -> version_remerge.py recover --branch ... printed 'no version line to replace in .../plugin.json' and exited 0, leaving the worktree MID-MERGE: both version files UU (unresolved index stage) though their working-tree content was already the resolved 0.27.61, merge commit not created, nothing pushed. Protocol (stage-merge step 3 Option B) requires recover to push a new SHA on RC=0; an RC=0 that pushes nothing breaks the drain's 'RC 0 -> re-wait CI -> merge' contract silently. Likely cause: the auto-resolve replace regex does not match a conflicted (or already-stamped same-version) file, and the failure path returns success without git add/commit/push or aborting the merge. Fix: make that path either complete the resolution (add+commit+push) or abort the merge and exit 3 (non-version conflict path); never exit 0 mid-merge. Hand recovery used: git add both files, git commit --no-edit, git push. Repro fixture: branch with stamp commit X+1 while origin/main sits at X.

title: create_pr base branch: respect [create_pr] base config instead of hardcoded --base main
description: stage-create_pr.md step 1 hardcodes '--base main' and create_pr.py's --base default is 'main' with no config fallback. A workspace whose integration branch is not main (e.g. brinta-data-platform, default branch dev — first real forge-seam Bitbucket workspace, migrated 2026-06-09 for flow-2je step (a)) gets its PR opened against the wrong branch (or fails if main does not exist). Fix: create_pr.py reads optional [create_pr] base from workspace.toml (same pattern as _draft_config; CLI --base wins only when explicitly passed, else config, else 'main'), and stage-create_pr.md drops the literal '--base main' in favor of the config-resolved default. Add tests beside the existing create_pr tests. Seam: keep the --base flag (prose may still name it); run seam_check.py. Not hot (create_pr.py not in guard set). Until this merges + the vdsmon-flow marketplace updates, /flow runs on brinta-data-platform must not reach create_pr.

title: Falsifiable self-edit contracts: grade whether a machinery fix killed the friction it claimed
description:
## The gap (canon principle, web-cited + internally witnessed)

The loop-engineering canon's defining sentence (loop-engineering.md): "a separate verifier (not the maker self-grading) decides the stop condition." For the SELF-EVOLUTION loop, the maker is reflect (Producer A): it diagnoses friction, applies/slings a fix, writes a `MACHINERY:` entry asserting the fix. The stop condition for that unit of work is "CI green + the diff-review reviewer approves" — which verifies the change is correct IN ISOLATION, never that the friction it targeted STOPPED RECURRING. The producer self-grades the outcome ("I fixed it") and nothing independent ever checks it. The verify step of friction->fix->verify->learn is absent.

Witnessed (reproduced from on-disk state):
- `.flow/flow/knowledge.jsonl` holds ~39 `MACHINERY:` entries (recorded self-heal/sling claims).
- `.flow/flow/friction.jsonl` shows the SAME classes recurring: `(implement, RECONCILE)` x13, `(commit, RECONCILE)` x7 — planned-file reconcile fired 20 times across runs.
- `reflect_inputs.py:122` filters friction to `fe.get("run_id") == ts.run_id` — every reflect agent sees ONLY its own run's friction, never the historical series. No code path reads prior friction.
- The bead `--dedup-key` only prevents refiling an open/closed FINDING; it is silent on whether the closed fix WORKED. A closed bead and a still-recurring friction class coexist with no link.

So the loop can record "fixed planned-file reconcile (sha abc)" and then hit RECONCILE 13 more times with nothing noticing. That is folklore-with-commits extended one step: the record exists, but its claim is never falsified.

## The field moved here too (the web grounding)

A self-edit is "done" not when it commits but when a later run confirms it did what it claimed:
- AHE / arXiv 2604.25850 "Decision Observability": every harness edit ships a recorded PREDICTION; the next iteration scores prediction-vs-actual, making each edit "a falsifiable contract rather than a rationale-driven justification."
- neosigmaai/auto-harness: each iteration writes `learnings.md` (what was tried, what worked, what it needs); no edit kept unless it clears a regression gate.
- Hexo Labs SIA: names the open Goodhart risk (no external validation anchor) that an internal friction-recurrence check partially answers.

The sparse-signal transfer: benchmark systems grade against hundreds of k-rollout task deltas; flow has none. So this is the QUALITATIVE version — an edit predicts a friction-class it will eliminate; a later run checks whether that class recurred. No statistical base required; it rides signal flow already captures (`flow_friction.py` already tags class via stage/type).

## VISION anchor

VISION.md: "a self-edit must leave a durable record of the friction it answers ... or it is folklore with commits"; today's `MACHINERY:` entry is a BACKWARD changelog, never a FORWARD claim that gets graded. And "better must be measurable, or it is vibes ... friction outranks audit" — a fix whose class recurs is higher-evidence than a cold-audit finding. Squarely on the propose side of the auto-vs-propose line (instrumentation that informs the maintainer/producer, never an auto-gate); erodes no robustness floor.

## TIMING / SEQUENCING (read before triage)

flow's measurement layer is ~1 day old (flow-ml7 in_progress; attribution self-stamp PR#175 and revert-rate PR#181 just landed; near-zero accumulated data yet). This epic is the NEXT LAYER ON TOP of that substrate: it needs (a) flow-ml7's measurement seam to settle and (b) weeks of friction-vs-fix data to accumulate before a recurrence grader produces meaningful signal. Building it now would grade against an empty history. Recommended SEQUENCE: shelve until flow-ml7 matures and friction data accumulates, then reassess.

## Distinct from flow-ml7 (the dedup, and the honest overlap)

flow-ml7 = AGGREGATE window metrics (attribution, time-to-pr, revert-rate, tokens, trend-rollup); substrate = ship-events joined to run-state; answers "is flow's output improving." This epic = grading INDIVIDUAL self-edits (did a `MACHINERY` fix's friction-class stop recurring); substrate = friction.jsonl joined to MACHINERY entries; answers "was this specific harness change honest." Mechanically disjoint joins. HONEST CALL FOR THE MAINTAINER: a reasonable read is to fold this in as a flow-ml7 CHILD rather than a standalone sibling epic, since both close the measurement loop. Filed standalone because the join/substrate is disjoint; the maintainer decides at triage whether to reparent under flow-ml7.

Also distinct from revert-rate (flow-148): that is a shipped bead reopened (code-level regression, something broke); this is a friction class recurring despite a claimed fix (harness-level non-improvement, nothing breaks but the papercut persists).

## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via §E expand)

1. KEYSTONE (NET-NEW) Recurrence-detection read: given the friction log, compute per-(stage,type[,file-anchor]) recurrence counts and surface classes that recurred AFTER a `MACHINERY:` fix for that class was recorded (forward join: MACHINERY timestamp/sha -> later friction of the same class). dedup `epic:falsifiable-machinery-edits::child-1-recurrence-detector`.
2. (NET-NEW) Widen the reflect bundle: feed (1) into `reflect_inputs.py` so lens-B sees "this class fired N times, last fix sha X, M runs ago" — turning a per-run-blind reflect into one with memory of its own prior fixes. The maker gets the verifier's signal. dedup `epic:falsifiable-machinery-edits::child-2-reflect-bundle-widen`.
3. (NET-NEW) A `recall --metric fix-efficacy` (or roll-up) printing, per closed `MACHINERY` bead, whether its friction class recurred since — the separate-verifier readout the maintainer triages. Sequence after flow-ml7's trend-rollup. dedup `epic:falsifiable-machinery-edits::child-3-efficacy-rollup`.
4. (NET-NEW, optional, propose-only) Reopen-or-escalate policy: a fix whose class recurs >=K times after the claimed fix auto-files an `evolve` bead labelled `recurrent`. Judgment-gated. dedup `epic:falsifiable-machinery-edits::child-4-recurrence-escalation`.

## Blast radius

`flow_friction.py` (no schema change needed — stage/type/ticket/run_id already present; additive read-side, stays non-hot if schema-stable, settle at spec), `reflect_inputs.py`, `stage-reflect.md` lens-B protocol, a new small grader script joining friction-classes to closed `MACHINERY` beads, `verb-recall.md`. No safety-guard file. Read-mostly.

## Open risk (why moderate confidence)

RECONCILE recurrences may be LEGITIMATELY DISTINCT events (each a different planned-file edge case), not a failed fix — so the keystone detector must distinguish "same fix didn't take" from "new instance of an inherently long-tail class." That nuance is judgment, not a clean ground-truth join, and is exactly why this is an epic to triage, not an auto-drain defect.

## Grounding cited

Web cites (AHE decision-observability, auto-harness, SIA Goodhart) + reproduced witness (39 MACHINERY entries vs 20+ recurring RECONCILE; reflect_inputs.py:122 per-run blindness). Convergently surfaced by two independent lenses (field-scan + meta-loop).

## Recommended default: needs-discussion (standalone vs flow-ml7 child) + shelve-until-flow-ml7-matures (metrics are ~1 day old). Confidence 0.7.
close_reason: Epic complete — the friction->fix->verify->learn loop is closed end to end: PR#402 keystone recurrence detector (dual clustering incl. root-cause signature, DF-ceiling exemption for fix-claimed anchors), PR#405 reflect bundle recurrence block (per-run-blind reflect now sees its own prior fixes; lens-B re-diagnose protocol), PR#407 recall --metric fix-efficacy separate-verifier rollup (live baseline: 72 fix-beads, 45 recurred, rate 0.625 — folklore-with-commits now measurable), PR#410 propose-only recurrence escalation (recurrent-labelled bead at K>=3, structurally drain-ineligible). VISION anchor satisfied: a MACHINERY entry is now a forward claim that gets graded, not a backward changelog.

Revisit: when recall --metric trend has real windows and friction.jsonl has post-ml7 volume. Secondary fork still open at revisit: standalone epic vs reparent under flow-ml7.

(1) Volume: friction.jsonl now has 87 entries; top classes (implement,RECONCILE) x22, (merge,RECONCILE) x16, (commit,RECONCILE) x9 — the exact recurring-despite-MACHINERY-fixes pattern the epic targets, still accumulating. (2) Standalone-vs-reparent fork: flow-ml7 is closed, so reparenting under it is moot — stays standalone. Epic is ripe: next step is /flow evolve epic flow-oe9 to materialize the 4 preview children (recurrence detector keystone first, incl. the 06-15 root-cause-signature sharpening).

This is the single highest-leverage structural fix: it closes friction→fix→verify→learn. Next step: /flow evolve epic flow-oe9 to materialize children (keystone recurrence detector must cluster by root-cause signature per the 06-15 sharpening, not only (stage,type)).

title: flow --auto: advisor adjudicates judgment forks instead of deferring; fold confidence into ship/block/defer verdict
description: Replace the --auto defer-and-exit on judgment forks with an advisor RULING (3-way proceed/block/defer verdict assessed independently of is_hot_change), and fold the flat 90% confidence cliff into that verdict. Flag-gated maintainer-only. HOT (approval/bootstrap seam). Unblocks the deferred queue (flow-doo/nr4/yga/594). Full design in design notes.
design:
## Problem

In `--auto`, the spec/plan gate (`references/verb-spec.md` step 5) defers a bead whenever the headless Plan subagent raises a clarifying question, scores < 90% confidence, or BAILs — and there is no maintainer-recorded decision. The launcher walked away, so there is nobody to ask; the bead drops to `deferred`/`blocked` and waits for a human triage→reopen→re-run-without-`--auto`. This parks every judgment fork even when the right call is obvious to a strong reviewer. Current live victims: flow-doo, flow-nr4, flow-yga, flow-594 (the deferred queue is mostly these).

Two coupled flaws:
1. **No autonomous adjudication.** A judgment fork (a design knob, an opt-in-vs-unconditional choice) is parked rather than resolved, even though the `advisor` second-mind is already wired in this same path (it scores confidence today).
2. **The 90% confidence gate is a flat cliff.** It is a proxy for "how bad if wrong," but ignores the two axes that actually decide that: blast-radius/reversibility and CI-verifiability. flow-nr4 deferred at 88% — two points under a round number, on a clean contained change.

## Mechanism (reuse, don't rebuild)

At the one defer point (verb-spec.md step 5, the "NOT decided → defer-and-exit" branch), instead of parking, escalate the fork to the `advisor` for a RULING, not just a score. The advisor writes the `DECISION:` comment a human triager would have written; everything downstream is unchanged — the existing decided-probe (`scripts/triage.py` `_DECISION_STEMS`) sees it, the run proceeds, and a relaunch is idempotent (no re-ask).

The advisor returns a **3-way ship verdict, assessed independently of `is_hot_change`**:

| verdict | action |
|---------|--------|
| `proceed` | write `DECISION: (advisor) <ruling>`, self-approve, ship (CI-gated) |
| `block`   | rulable but unsafe to auto-ship (broad blast radius / irreversible) → comment + `bd status blocked` for human merge |
| `defer`   | needs maintainer-only information, or advisor low-confidence → defer-and-exit (today's behavior, unchanged) |

### Why the 3-way matters — flow-doo is the trap

`is_hot_change` is purely file-path based (guard/lease/safety files). flow-doo is NOT a guard file → "not hot" → a naive "non-hot judgment fork → proceed" design would auto-ship it. But the bead says verbatim: "BLAST RADIUS: get() is called broadly... propose, not auto-apply." File-path hotness is not blast-radius risk. So the advisor must answer "safe to auto-ship?" SEPARATELY from "which option is right?". `is_hot_change` stays a hard floor (hot → never `proceed`), but is no longer the only gate.

Mapped to the live queue: flow-nr4, flow-yga → `proceed` (clean, contained); flow-doo → `block` (rule the opt-in param, but broad blast → human merges); flow-594 → `block` (hot + irreversible).

## Fold the confidence gate into the verdict (kill the cliff)

With advisor-adjudication added, a separate 90% hard cliff is redundant — the advisor IS the second mind. Scope this to the `--auto` path only (the interactive path's 90% gate is correct: it means "exhaust read-only artefacts, then present to the human"; the human is present).

- Below a low floor (~70%): defer immediately, WITHOUT burning an adjudication call (plan too shaky to even ask).
- At/above the floor: hand the advisor `{plan, confidence, blast-radius, reversibility, CI-coverage}` and let it return `proceed` / `block` / `defer`.
- 90% stops being magic: an 85% clean-reversible change can `proceed`; a 95% irreversible one can `block`.

## Guardrails (non-negotiable)

- **Advisor absent → defer, never self-judge.** The existing "general-purpose Agent fallback" is the same model tier grading its own plan — fine for scoring, NOT for a binding ship decision. No stronger mind present → fall back to defer.
- **Distinct stem `DECISION: (advisor)`** so `/flow triage` can surface advisor-minted rulings for optional maintainer review; otherwise they are indistinguishable from human decisions on every relaunch and the audit trail is purely passive. (`triage.py _DECISION_STEMS` / `verb-triage.md` need the new stem recognized.)
- **`is_hot_change` hard floor preserved** — hot files never `proceed`, regardless of advisor verdict.
- **Flag-gated, maintainer-only** (`[evolve] advisor_adjudicates`, default OFF in `workspace.toml`), mirroring `[evolve] auto_merge_hot`. User projects keep the human-decision keystone; only the maintainer self-target repo opts in.

## Files (HOT — touches the approval/bootstrap seam)

- `references/verb-spec.md` — rewrite the step-5 "NOT decided → defer-and-exit" branch into the floor-check → advisor-adjudicate → proceed/block/defer flow.
- `scripts/triage.py` — recognize the `DECISION: (advisor)` stem (extend `_DECISION_STEMS`); possibly a helper to mint the advisor decision comment.
- `references/verb-triage.md` — document the advisor-minted decision stem + how triage surfaces it.
- `workspace.toml` + the config reader — `[evolve] advisor_adjudicates` flag (default off).
- Version bump (`plugin.json` + `marketplace.json`, in sync) — auto-included by bootstrap for maintainer plugin-code tickets.

## Tests

- advisor `proceed` on a non-hot clean fork → self-approves, ships.
- advisor `block` on a non-hot broad-blast fork (flow-doo shape) → status `blocked`, no PR.
- `is_hot_change` true → never `proceed` even on advisor `proceed` (hard floor).
- advisor absent → defer (not self-judge).
- confidence < floor → immediate defer, no adjudication call.
- flag off → today's behavior exactly (defer-and-exit).
- relaunch reads `DECISION: (advisor)` as decided → no re-ask.

## Note

This is the dogfood case for the very autonomy it adds — run it through `/flow`, not a hand-edit. HOT, so it routes through human merge regardless of `auto_merge_hot`.

title: Post-merge #93: switch nightly to evolve audit + drain (atomic with marketplace update)
description: After PR #93 (L3 evolve sub-verb namespace) merges, do these in ONE sitting so the 03:17 nightly never no-ops: (1) advance local main to origin/main; (2) claude plugin marketplace update vdsmon-flow; (3) edit ~/.flow-evolve/nightly-evolve.sh line ~70 'claude --bg /flow evolve' -> 'evolve audit' and line ~81 'claude --bg /flow evolve --ship' -> 'evolve drain'. Then live-smoke the dispatch (the only L3 verification gates cannot do): /flow evolve -> sub-verb listing; /flow evolve launch --dry-run -> plan no action; /flow evolve drain --dry-run -> both plans no action. Hazard if non-atomic: nightly fires bare evolve (now lists, files nothing) + evolve --ship (now unknown sub-verb) = silent no-op night.

Step 1: tracker_cli.py create subcommand (flow_beads_create wrapper; BEADS_DIR targeting; refuse non-Flow targets). Step 2: reflect sling-bead on the propose+record path, gated on maintainer mode + recall filter for MACHINERY entries. title: Jira adapter write/mutation methods have zero test coverage (create, link, all setters, board_rank, attachments)
description:
EVIDENCE:
tracker_jira.py defines create (554), set_summary (580), set_description (588), set_priority (591), set_labels (594), set_assignee (597), link (649), board_rank (819), get/upload_attachments, set_components, set_custom_field. Verified: grep for `.create(`/`.link(`/`.set_summary(` in test_jira_adapter.py = 0; the 42 tests cover only reads/transitions/sprint/watcher/fix-versions/epic-link. The beads equivalents ARE tested (test_beads_adapter.py:333,353,365 call adapter.create). These methods build branching REST payloads (create: plain-vs-ADF summary, optional parent/labels/assignee at 563-577), so a payload-shape regression ships silently to live Jira.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Jira write paths are how /flow mutates real tickets; an untested PUT/POST payload that Jira rejects or mis-applies corrupts tracker state for paying users, and the asymmetry means the cheaper backend (beads) is the verified one. High value, strong evidence — but many methods, so not 'small/isolated'; ranks below the one-liners. Touches only the test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_jira_adapter.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: jira-write-methods-untested

title: pending-mutations queue is built + tested but no adapter or stage ever enqueues; transient tracker mutations are silently lost
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: append_mutation has zero non-test, non-self callers (grep of append_mutation across *.py returns only its definition at pending_mutations.py:152, a docstring, and the __all__ export). Only the drain side is wired: sync.py:87 list_mutations, :101 compact. tracker_beads.py and tracker_jira.py contain zero 'pending' references. The commit-stage transient-failure branch (stage-commit.md:94-97) logs a warning and continues — it drops the mutation, never enqueues. sync.py:3 and verb-sync-baseline.md assert the file is 'written by adapter failure paths' — false for both adapters. This is the unaddressed core of the stale TODO at tracker_beads.py:19-23 ('TODO (phase 8): transient-failure paths should append to pending-mutations.jsonl ... until phase 8 ships'), which contradicts dev-history.md (phase 8d shipped /flow sync) — fold the stale-TODO finding in here, it is the breadcrumb for the same unwired enqueue.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Highest value of the set: in unattended runs a network/auth blip on a tracker transition (or comment/link/edit) is dropped permanently and /flow sync can never reconcile it because nothing wrote it. The whole pending-mutations subsystem (module + CLI + drain + verb prose) is dead weight until a write path is wired in. BUT: blast-radius-safety pulls this down — wiring append_mutation into the commit/ticket transient-failure branches touches wired adapters (tracker_beads/jira) and stage-commit.md, so it is NOT a safe small ship. Rank high on value, flag the tradeoff. Touches wired handlers = hot.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_beads.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_jira.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-commit.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-ticket.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/pending_mutations.py
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: friction-history mining
AUDIT ID: pending-mutations-never-enqueued

title: Flow self-evolution: Rung 1 audit backlog

Backlog from the Rung 1 cold self-audit of the flow repo (see plan: Flow self-evolution proactive nightly loop).

The premise holds: this cold self-audit produced a genuine, non-noise yield. The shippable-as-small-tickets head is real — ty-skips-hooks (one-token fix, every-session blast), inventory.md --labels/--set-labels (one-line, ironclad), git quotePath (concrete correctness bug, confirmed 0 quotePath calls), the missing-stage-docs fix+guard pair, and the pending-mutations dead-enqueue (highest value, though it touches wired adapters so not a safe-small ship). Below that sits a legitimate but low-stakes housekeeping tail (duplicated _parse_iso/_atomicio/Runner helpers, complexity hotspots, stale comments, perms-narrowing). All top claims were re-verified empirically (lint task omits hooks, MODULE.md has 0 heartbeat refs, both registry stage docs MISSING, append_mutation has no enqueue caller, set_labels uses --set-labels, 0 quotePath in diff_extract, jira write methods have 0 test refs). Net: self-evolution mining works; ship the high-evidence head, batch the tail.

Every child carries file:line / reproduced-command evidence. Label `evolve`. The small leaf head ships as draft PRs first (Rung 3).

title: ticket stage: claim tracker backend (open->in_progress) to prevent fleet double-pick
description: The ticket stage (references/stage-ticket.md, handler=inline) stamps .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md frontmatter status=in_progress but never transitions the tracker BACKEND. On beads the bd issue stays 'open' for the whole run, so 'bd ready' still lists the in-flight ticket as available and a parallel fleet agent could double-pick it. Fix: add a best-effort backend transition (open->in_progress) to the ticket stage, mirroring the stage-commit transition step (MCP-first, REST/tracker_cli fallback), tolerant of exit 3 (no transition available / already in_progress) and transient faults so it never fails the run. Surfaced on flow-7qm with the machinery lens ON (PROPOSE+RECORD).

Wire enforcement so the fixed classes stay fixed. Report any rules worth ignoring as annoyances.

title: [refuted tombstone] workflow-primitive spine/fleet port: verified unfit (session-scoped, non-reboot-surviving)
description:
REFUTED at the evolve-epic conviction gate 2026-07-06 (skeptic 0.90 / 0.80). Filed CLOSED as a dedup tombstone -- the Workflow primitive will keep being advertised; this records why flow declined, so re-runs converge.

(ii) drain-fleet -> Workflow orchestrator: VERIFIED UNFIT -- a Dynamic Workflow (official docs) is session-scoped, harness-required, does NOT survive process exit or reboot, and is explicitly not a replacement for a launchd/cron plain script. The drain loop IS that detached launchd-fired claude --bg script. fleet-race-matrix.md section-5's three binding constraints (execution boundary: the liveness read runs in a detached stdlib subprocess that cannot call harness tools; headless TTY; cross-reboot fleet) are unchanged by the new primitive. Porting = dropping load-bearing safety properties (reboot-clearability, TOCTOU serialization) = robustness-floor violation; would reverse flow-8by2.5.

(i) do-spine -> Workflow script: PREMISE FALSE -- the deterministic core already lives in dispatch_stage.py (tested, flow-owned); the residual SKILL.md do-loop prose is agent-spawning + exit-code judgment, exactly what a Workflow keeps INSIDE agent() calls. Fights the SKILL.md-stays-thin invariant and the flow-nu1w/flow-10px precedent (absorb mechanics into flow-owned scripts; stop at the cognitive glue). Corrected crux: a Workflow holds the loop OUTSIDE the model's context, so reflect lens-B loses its in-context file:line DIAGNOSIS surface -- the edit mechanism survives (machinery_edit edits files) but the self-noticing that feeds it starves.

The drift cited as grounding is incidental and already fixed: LEASE_LOSS x2 root-caused to empty boot_id, fixed PR#223; stray scripts/.flow lockfiles neutralized by the unanchored **/.flow/runs/ gitignore (verified at origin/main).

ADVISORY (non-binding): if Dynamic Workflows are ever explored, the only plausible fit is in-session subagent fan-outs (attended stage dispatch; evolve-audit miner fan-out) -- never the detached fleet or the self-editable spine.
close_reason: refuted at evolve-epic conviction gate: Workflow primitive verified session-scoped/non-reboot-surviving, unfit for detached fleet + self-editable spine; tombstone for dedup convergence

title: [refuted tombstone] memory-consolidation dream pass = closed epic flow-ufvu re-pitched
description:
REFUTED at the evolve-epic conviction gate 2026-07-06 (skeptic 0.88). Filed CLOSED as a dedup tombstone so re-runs converge -- this candidate already evaded dedup once (semantically = closed epic flow-ufvu "Temporal memory", lexically disjoint slug).

The pitch (scheduled between-run consolidation of knowledge.jsonl, "dream pass") duplicates shipped work: sweep_knowledge.py cluster/apply-cluster (complete-linkage cosine tau=0.90, list-valued --supersedes collapse) EXISTS and is tested; the corpus-health metric SHIPPED (flow-ufvu.5/PR#349, metric.py compute_corpus_health); recall already filters superseded entries pre-rank. No witnessed recall degradation (886 recall-usage records, no precision-drop signal; the single recall friction entry is a flag-drift note). The one un-built delta (nightly auto-confirm scheduling) violates the skeptic-hardened PROPOSE-ONLY line (sweep_knowledge docstring; verb-evolve.md curate; flow-ufvu WHY) and the recorded decision that the standing producer for future rot is reflect-stage supersession (flow-ufvu.2).

REOPEN ONLY IF: a witnessed recall-precision drop (recall-hit-rate trend) or corpus-health regression -- and then as a thin propose-only digest child, never auto-confirm.
