title: Executed-acceptance gate (recorded->enforced DoD): explicit maintainer decision, shelve pending first witnessed green-but-wrong PR
description:
PROPOSAL -- DECISION RECORD (evolve epic 2026-07-06; field-scan lens 0.72; skeptic collapsed to decision-first, 0.88 against auto-building). DECISION-OWNER: maintainer. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: SHELVE pending evidence.

THE ASK: upgrade the acceptance-invariant from recorded to EXECUTED -- a verifier that synthesizes and runs a check from the DoD with bounded kick-back. Field precedent: Claude Code "Outcomes" blind rubric grader (+10.1% reported); Agent-as-a-Judge (arxiv 2601.05111, 2604.18240); execution-based QC (arxiv 2510.24358).

WHY SHELVED (skeptic-verified): (1) ZERO WITNESS -- flow's own revert-rate metric reads n_reverts=0 across ALL history; the green-but-wrong class this would catch has never occurred here. Friction outranks audit; this is a cold field-scan candidate with no friction witness. (2) The central child SELF-CONFIRMS -- same-session synthesis from the DoD is not blind; the only additive form is a BLIND executing grader, which then overlaps e2e (executes; human-settled recipe = STRONGER blindness than model-synthesized), the P2d plan-blind reader (blind), mandatory-TDD, and CI. (3) VISION names recorded-not-enforced as a deliberate maintainer decision and "does not pre-authorize widening any gate" -- this bead IS the explicit decision route, filed per the surface-it duty, not a build order.

UNSHELVE WHEN: the first witnessed green-but-wrong PR (a revert, or a recorded acceptance-invariant a check would have caught). OPTIONAL minimal slice if wanted now: a first-witness watcher that trips on that first event -- nothing more.

CONSTRAINT if ever built: the verifier must be BLIND (independent of the authoring session), lane-gated, guard-clamped; never a stage on every run absent a witnessed miss-rate.

CONFIDENCE: 0.72 the field trend is real; 0.88 that auto-building now is wrong. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: shelve.

title: fix-efficacy recurrence join is noise-dominated (0.62 headline): redesign the join, supersede the oe9.3 anchor-parity decision
description:
PROPOSAL (evolve epic 2026-07-06; meta-loop lens; skeptic ENGAGE-WITH-MODS 0.82 -- re-scoped to investigation-first). De-noise the fix-efficacy recurrence join.

EVIDENCE (reproduced): recall --metric fix-efficacy --namespace flow -> fix_beads=74 recurred=46 clean=28 rate=0.62. The join (metric.py compute_fix_efficacy) flags a recurrence when ANY later friction entry shares ONE claimed anchor; claimed anchors are ubiquitous filename tokens, so the loop's core "did my self-fixes hold" measure is noise-dominated (VISION: measurable-or-vibes).

FALSIFIED naive fixes (do NOT ship these): (i) excluding planned_files alone is cosmetic -- 14/46 recurred beads carry it, flips 4 verdicts, 0.62->0.57; it is 1 of ~12 equally-ubiquitous catch-all anchors (spec.md 10, machinery_edit 8, evolve.md 7, dispatch_stage.py 7, seam_check.py 6, implement.md 6, commit.md 5, skill.md 5, ...); excluding the full cohort flips 13 verdicts -> 0.45, which is where the signal actually is. (ii) requiring non-empty fix_sha annihilates the sample (74->8, n meaningless): empty fix_sha means untraceable-not-unfixed -- friction_recurrence.fix_sha resolves an inline sha else the ship-event evidence.commit_sha fallback, and ship-events have been dark (see flow-09bg); the gate would survivorship-bias against older fixes.

REAL LEVERS to characterize: exempt the MEASURED catch-all cohort (data-driven, not the single knob default) OR tighten the join to a (stage,type,anchor) tuple match (structural_classes already computes this). NOTE: recurrence_exempt_anchors today is a friction_escalate.py filing-threshold knob only -- neither friction_recurrence.analyze() nor compute_fix_efficacy consumes it; the candidate that spawned this bead conflated the two.

BINDING PRIOR ART: DECISION 8be0001dc55357f2 (flow-oe9.3 design): fix-efficacy must reuse analyze()'s EXACT distinctive-anchor selection so the two agree -- any join change must explicitly supersede it (memory_append --supersedes). oe9.3 shipped with this noise triaged as a known low; this bead is the follow-through now that it dominates the headline rate.

BLAST RADIUS: metric.py fix-efficacy path + friction_recurrence.py anchor semantics; observability-only, non-hot. DELIVERABLE: the de-noised ladder characterization + ONE chosen join redesign, single PR.

CONFIDENCE: 0.6. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (investigation-first).

title: Merge-authority mechanism: CC classifier denies --auto self-merge; pick hold / permission rule / server-side Action / native auto-merge
description:
PROPOSAL (evolve epic 2026-07-06; 2 lenses; skeptic ENGAGE-WITH-MODS 0.78 -- collapsed from epic to proposal+spike). DECISION-OWNER: maintainer.

WITNESS: two merge-stage BLOCKER frictions -- 2026-07-01 (run flow-uc8n, PR#387: "Merge Without Review") and 2026-07-03 (run flow-nnft: "Self-Approval"): evolve_self_merge returns action=merge (green, non-hot, eligible, CLEAN) but the Claude Code auto-mode permission classifier denies gh pr merge in-session. The --auto drain's last stage dies; green PRs park for the human.

ALREADY DECIDED (do not re-ask): self-merge here is authorized -- .flow/workspace.toml [evolve] auto_merge_hot=true + self_target=true; self-evolution.md records it. What is OPEN is only the MECHANISM around the external classifier. Two maintainer memories are in genuine tension and this bead hands the fork back unresolved: "a green ready PR is a complete deliverable" (accept the park) vs "auto-merge by default when the gate is met" (close the gap).

OPTIONS: (a) STANDING DEFAULT -- hold: green PR parked for human is the deliverable; zero code. IF closing the gap: (b) scoped Claude Code permission rule authorizing flow's self-merge -- settings-only, MAINTAINER-APPLIED ONLY (the loop must never self-apply a permission widen); (c) label-triggered server-side merge Action (version-stamp.yml is the precedent) -- viable for hot ONLY if the Action fires strictly AFTER the in-session guard-property review applied the trigger; (d) GitHub native auto-merge for non-hot leaves -- structurally non-hot-only (cannot gate on an in-session review).

SPIKE (opportunistic, before choosing c/d): is an agent-applied merge trigger (label add / gh pr merge --auto enable) itself classifier-gated? Piggyback on the next green self-target PR; not a standalone drain item.

BLAST RADIUS: mechanism-dependent; <=1 PR for any single option ((b) is settings-only). Binding on all options: the in-session guard-property diff review for hot is never bypassed (VISION robustness floor).

CONFIDENCE: 0.78. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion -- hold at (a) until the maintainer picks.

title: Loop self-observability: restore the ship-event sense + a deadman on the loop's own measurement layer
description:
HIGH-ALTITUDE EPIC (evolve epic 2026-07-06; THREE lenses converged independently; skeptic-verified, ENGAGE-WITH-MODS 0.85).

WITNESS: .flow/flow/ship-events/ newest event 2026-06-11 (flow-yyzi) while PRs #395-#434 merged since; recall --metric tickets-per-week reads shipped=0 for the trailing window; friction.jsonl/knowledge.jsonl current to 2026-07-03 (runs kept happening). The measurement layer went dark ~25 days, unnoticed. tickets-per-week, fix-efficacy, revert-rate, tier visibility, and acceptance-invariant records all starve on this store.

ROOT CAUSE (command-verified): observe_ship_event.py's only pipeline caller is references/stage-reflect.md (grep-confirmed at origin/main; control-arm-recipe.md is an experiment recipe; background-pipeline.md:58 is a doc gloss). stage-registry.toml orders reflect BEFORE merge (merge requires reflect as predecessor), and PR#277 (merged 2026-06-12, the day after the last event) correctly made tracker_beads.is_shipped require the commit reachable from origin/<default> -- structurally always too early at reflect time. The observer only ever fired when a close raced ahead of reflect; PR#277 removed that accident. Of 20 events, 17 are real pre-mid-June merges; 2 are closed-unmerged experiment cells PR#277 rightly killed. HARD CONSTRAINT: no child loosens PR#277's is_shipped gate (named measurement-integrity property) -- the fix is a post-close observation path, never a gate rollback.

GAP: ops-deadman exists for RUNS; NO deadman exists for the loop's SENSES (grep deadman in scripts/ at origin/main: empty).

RATIONALE (VISION): "'Better' must be measurable, or it is vibes" + "move-fast works only when the breakage is seen, not when it is silent". The loop currently cannot distinguish progress from motion.

BLAST RADIUS: observe_ship_event.py, worktree_janitor.py, evolve_reap.py, metric.py, ops/ nightly; one prose touch to stage-reflect.md. Non-hot (no guard files, no SKILL.md).

DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (contract for the expand step; children NOT filed until the epic is accepted):
1. (NET-NEW, dedup epic:loop-self-observability::child-1-observe-at-close) Observe-at-close: wire ship-event observation into the post-merge reap seam -- worktree_janitor.py sweep + evolve_reap.py orphan path -- capturing run_id/attribution from the worktree's .flow/runs/<key>/state.json BEFORE teardown (the sequencing IS the work: the janitor deletes the worktree that holds state.json). Restores the sense going forward; is_shipped correctly reads not_yet_observed->shipped there. ~1 PR. [BUILD-NOW]
2. (NET-NEW, dedup epic:loop-self-observability::child-2-senses-deadman) Senses-deadman (KEYSTONE): nightly check that files ONE deduped P0 bead when ship-event count diverges from merged-PR/bead-close count over a window; folds the health-digest content (telemetry freshness, metric-trend deltas, loop liveness) into its nightly evidence output. Mirrors ops-deadman-for-runs. Would have turned this 25-day blindspot into a 1-day alarm. ~1 PR. [BUILD-NOW]
3. (NET-NEW, dedup epic:loop-self-observability::child-3-backlog-liveness) Backlog-liveness metric: a recall --metric measure for the drained/empty-backlog signal (backlog is currently fully drained; the loop is producer-limited and cannot see it). ~1 PR. [OPTIONAL]

OPEN QUESTION (needs-discussion at expand, NOT a default child): backfill the ~30 stranded events? Needs a synthetic run_id mode that muddies windowed metrics for historical events. Skeptic default: going-forward-only.
EXCLUDED by the skeptic (do not re-add silently): /flow health verb (HOT SKILL.md surface, no witnessed need -- its value folds into child-2's output); scheduler ping (redundant: a deduped P0 in a drained backlog IS the ping); producer-yield metric (blocked on a producer-attribution field on ship events -- a separate data-model decision).
SIDE NOTE for child-1: once events flow again, verify tier:light downshift events actually stamp acceptance_invariant (unverified 5-vs-0 gap flagged by the acceptance skeptic).

GROUNDING: witnessed signal (dead store; day-exact PR#277 timing) + per-claim command verification by an independent skeptic. CONFIDENCE: 0.85. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build-now (children 1+2).

title: review_loop: detect CodeRabbit-disabled and warn, do not assert review-clean
description: MACHINERY (blocker for review trust): references/stage-review_loop.md treats an empty review-threads list as review-clean once the bot-completion gate is 'satisfied'. Two problems surfaced on CO-226/PR#2939 (Bitbucket): (1) the flow-uc8n draft short-circuit rests on the premise that CodeRabbit skips draft PRs, which the maintainer says is false for this org (CR reviews drafts too); (2) when CR is momentarily DISABLED org-wide, review-status stays reviewed:false and review-threads returns [] indefinitely, and the current logic silently terminates review-clean on the empty list. Fix: when the bot exposes a completion signal but never finishes AND threads stay empty past the cap (or review-status is structurally unavailable while CR is expected), do NOT assert review-clean -- record and SURFACE a 'CR did not review this PR (likely disabled); proceeding on CI-green only, automated review did not happen' warning in the stage report and to the user, and proceed on CI-green. Resilience, not a block. Evidence: stage-review_loop.md sec.3 (draft short-circuit) + sec.5 (terminal on empty list). See project memory reference_coderabbit_review_absence.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Draft PR #433 (fix/flow-enr8-cr-not-reviewed): retires the flow-uc8n draft short-circuit (re-poll runs on drafts too), cap-expiry + empty threads now surfaces 'proceeding on CI-green only — automated review did not happen' to report + user, §5 terminal completes as not-reviewed never review-clean. seam_check + test_seam_check green.

title: PR-ready PushNotification fires on --auto runs; suppress for drain-launched runs
description: User request 2026-07-03 (mid-drain): flow-e9mp's --auto run pushed a PR-ready notification to the maintainer's phone; completion pings from auto-drained runs are unwanted noise — the drain orchestrator report + bead close is already the surface for those. Evidence: references/verb-do.md lines 7-9 make the ping unconditional ('This is unconditional — fire it on every run'), rationale being stall-detection for unattended runs; that rationale holds for user-backgrounded (/bg) spec runs but NOT for drain-launched --auto runs, whose lifecycle the orchestrator watches via lease/fleet. Fix direction (decided by maintainer): mode-conditioned suppression, no new config key — an --auto run SKIPS the PR-ready PushNotification entirely (its PR link still lands in create_pr.out, the drain report, and the bead close reason); attended + /bg non-auto runs keep the ping unchanged. Touchpoints: references/verb-do.md PR-ready notification block (lines 7-10) + SKILL.md do-loop step 4e pointer line (hot: SKILL.md is a guard file). Blast radius: notification prose only; no script/seam change.

title: post-merge worktree janitor: reap orphaned local worktrees/branches whose PR is merged
description:
Audit 2026-07-03 found 43 orphaned local worktrees + 60 dead branches in the maintainer checkout, some dating to June 8. Root cause is a teardown gap: stage_merge.py execute delete-branch is remote-only (local worktree teardown deferred to the drain reap), and evolve_reap/queue_drain.classify_reap only run inside drain loops keyed off in-flight beads and open PRs. Once the merge stage closes the bead, the run drops out of every drain's field of view and its worktree is orphaned permanently. Attended runs merged by hand on GitHub never had a local teardown path at all. Ancestry-based cleanup (git branch --merged) is structurally blind here because the repo squash-merges.

Proposal: an orphan sweep (e.g. flow_worktree.py reap --orphans or a reap-step extension) that walks registered worktrees under .flow/worktrees, resolves each branch's PR state via gh pr list --head <branch> --state merged, and reaps worktree + local branch when the PR is merged and the tip matches the merged PR head (never ancestry). Must respect the lease/fleet is-live gate before any destructive act, and tolerate untracked run droppings (.flow/tickets/<key>.md[.lock], err.log) as clean.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed. Build a NEW standalone maintainer sweep scripts/worktree_janitor.py (pure classify_orphans + 'sweep' CLI); do NOT extend the guard/hot flow_worktree.py. Reap only when a worktree's local branch tip == the merged PR's headRefOid (tip-sha equality, NEVER ancestry — repo squash-merges); tip-ahead => skipped_ahead (protects unpushed reflect/machinery commits from branch -D loss). Inherit all destructive safety by calling flow_worktree.reap_worktree (lease.classify_then flock + checkpoint-orphan recovery + :(exclude).flow droppings-tolerance); fleet.is_live as a cheap lease-only pre-skip. Per-branch 'gh pr list --head <branch> --state merged --json number,headRefOid' (resolves post remote-branch-deletion, verified). Safe to auto-ship: NON-HOT (leaf script, no guard file), additive (no existing behavior changes), reversible, CI+seam covered. MANDATORY implementer commitments: (a) terminal-bead READ gate — skip reap when raw bd status is in ACTIVE_STATUSES (open,in_progress,blocked), reap otherwise; import ACTIVE_STATUSES from _evolve_common, check the RAW status (beads emits 'closed', not 'done', so a {done,cancelled} check would no-op on the primary target); fail-safe SKIP on any bd read error; this prevents stranding queue_drain's active-bead close path; surface active-bead-merged worktrees in a skipped_active_bead bucket. (b) NEVER bd close (read-and-skip only; closing is queue_drain's job). (c) surface skipped_ahead + skipped_active_bead + no_merged_pr buckets in the JSON receipt. Scope: worktree-anchored orphans only; dead branches with no worktree are owned by the git-cleanup skill. e2e: test-ci-only.

title: lavish plan-review gate: JOB_DIR-unset is a wrong attendance proxy; drop it and attempt-with-degrade
description:
Evidence (2026-07-03, flow-l014 spec session): a backgrounded session (CLAUDE_JOB_DIR set) had a live attending human; verb-spec step 4's lavish gate condition (b) silently skipped the surface, the user asked 'why didn't you present this plan through /lavish?', and when run explicitly lavish worked END TO END from the bg session: heredoc HTML to TMPDIR under plan mode, browser opened, poll long-ran, verdict + free-text note round-tripped. Phase 1 (flow-1xv1/PR#411) chose attended-only via the JOB_DIR proxy; the proxy is wrong for attended-bg sessions, which for this maintainer is the DEFAULT way of running specs.

Fix in references/verb-spec.md step 4 lavish block: drop condition (b) (JOB_DIR unset) and rely on attempt-with-degrade — the degradation contract already covers every failure (npx absent, offline, non-zero exit), and an truly unattended session simply never answers the poll (bounded: poll deliberately, never leave one armed unattended; a no-response poll timeout degrades to plain prose + ExitPlanMode as today). Also make the plan-mode footing explicit in prose: the lavish HTML lives in TMPDIR exactly like the markdown plan file, so NO exit from plan mode is needed for the lavish route (user-observed confusion; the block already implies it via the heredoc rule, say it plainly).

Blast radius: verb-spec.md step 4 lavish block only (conditions list + footing sentence); the boundary text ('lavish is an optional ADD-ON, never a dependency') stays binding; seam_check unaffected (no script flags involved).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #426: JOB_DIR-unset dropped from the lavish gate (explicitly excluded, attempt-with-degrade holds) + plan-mode footing paragraph added to verb-spec step 4.

title: flow_beads_create fuzzy same-file dedup over-collapses formulaic machine dedup_keys
description: flow_beads_create.create_bead runs a fuzzy same-file dedup pass (Jaccard>=0.45 over bead titles sharing an evidfile: anchor) whenever --dedup-key contains '::'. For MACHINE producers with formulaic keys/titles this wrongly collapses distinct findings: trace_mine cluster keys '<primary>::<kind>-<stage>' + summaries '<kind> in <stage> (<primary>)' score anchorless 'stall_gap in implement' vs 'stall_gap in commit' = 0.5, same-file 'tool_error...(dispatch_stage.py)' vs 'drift...(dispatch_stage.py)' = 0.571, so the later bead is dropped as a false dup. Evidence: flow_beads_create.py:218-224 (partition on '::' -> evidfile fuzzy pass), :147-173 (_find_fuzzy_duplicate). child-3 (flow-017z) and friction_escalate both work around it caller-side by stripping/omitting '::' so only the exact evid: net fires. Proposal: make the fuzzy pass opt-in, or auto-skip it for keys whose left part is not a real file path, so the next cluster-style producer does not have to know the footgun. Judgment/proposal: changing dedup semantics affects all existing audit callers, so review before shipping (not auto-drain). Recommended default: needs-discussion. Confidence 0.6.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#423 per the recorded DECISION: flow_beads_create's fuzzy same-file dedup pass auto-skips machine-formulaic keys — separator-carrying left parts keep the fuzzy pass (audit callers byte-unchanged, pinned by test), bare non-tracked left parts and epic:<track>::child keys ride the exact evid: net only (the epic-child case removes a latent cross-child over-collapse). git-probe failure fails safe toward keeping fuzzy. Review MERGE-OK, both-ways pins comprehensive (91 tests); the optional prose low folded pre-merge (verb-evolve.md convergence claim scoped to file-path keys). Caller-side workarounds left untouched per DECISION scope constraint (now defense-in-depth).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION (delegated maintainer, 2026-07-03): ship the auto-skip variant. The fuzzy same-file pass (Jaccard>=0.45 over titles sharing an evidfile anchor) exists for HUMAN-ish audit findings whose dedup keys are `<relfile>::<symptom>`; a dedup key whose left-of-`::` part does not resolve to a real file path in the repo (git ls-files probe, or simply "contains no path separator and does not exist") is a MACHINE-formulaic key, and for those the fuzzy pass must auto-skip so only the exact evid: net fires. Constraints: (1) existing audit callers' behavior byte-unchanged — their keys are real relfile paths and keep the fuzzy pass; (2) the two current caller-side workarounds (flow-017z filing + friction_escalate stripping/omitting '::') may be simplified afterward but that is OPTIONAL follow-up polish inside the same PR only if trivially safe — do not widen scope; (3) the skip decision must be pinned by tests both ways (file-path key keeps fuzzy; formulaic key skips it); (4) non-hot (flow_beads_create.py is not a guard file) but dedup semantics are load-bearing for the audit lane — the PR body must state the byte-unchanged claim for audit callers explicitly.

title: P4 fleet layer: review queue, Jira drainer, CI sentinel, cross-repo runs [YET TO BE REFINED]
description: PLACEHOLDER — deliberately unrefined; gated on a pain signal, not a decision. Concept (gates roadmap 2026-07-02, phase 4), four sub-items in priority order: (1) REVIEW QUEUE DASHBOARD — one surface listing PRs-awaiting-you across repos with risk tier + ask-user counts; gate 2 at fleet scale is a queue, not a page. (2) THIN JIRA DRAINER — extend flow's drain/launch-ledger machinery from beads to the Jira tracker seam: cron/loop session reads ready tickets, launches bg /flow runs, reports actionable events only (~90% of firstmate's value, zero new tools). (3) CI SENTINEL (daemon) — session-independent watcher for open flow PRs; see assessment comment: NOT recommended as a daemon yet; cheaper cadence-loop increments first. (4) CROSS-REPO LINKED RUNS — parent ticket -> per-repo flow runs -> stitched PRs (data-platform + notebooks case); nobody solves this first-class (gastown rigs are repo-scoped, firstmate tasks target one project). UN-DEFER WHEN: sustained ~10+ concurrent runs, OR manual dispatch/notice latency becomes a felt pain, OR review-queue hopping costs real time weekly. Rationale trail: this session's gastown/firstmate/ultracode synthesis — at 2-6 parallel runs an orchestrator's management overhead exceeds session-hopping cost; invest in review throughput (P2/P3) first.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CI-sentinel (item 3) assessment, on request (2026-07-02): DIFFICULTY moderate-not-trivial — the poll loop itself is easy (stdlib script around forge_cli ci-rollup/review-threads + revise's PR->ticket seam, all primitives exist), but it adds a permanent OPS surface: lifecycle/staleness/auth (headless gh 401s, GH_TOKEN), leak risk (see the 295-daemon fsmonitor scar — one machine-wide sentinel, never per-run), and an architecture fork that must be decided first: (a) post-delivery-only watcher (run contract unchanged: run still delivers green PR; sentinel covers the finished-PR->merge gap: new CR threads, CI re-runs, conflicts) vs (b) no-mistakes-style ownership (run ends at PR-open, daemon babysits to merge — changes flow's delivery contract, big). BENEFIT honest read: substantial ONLY for (i) killing review_loop's in-session polling scar tissue (600s ceiling fights, Monitor deaths, token burn per poll cycle) and (ii) the work-repo post-delivery notice gap. At current N (2-6 runs, user actively hopping) notice latency is small — xqt verdict applies: machinery pays at the complexity/concurrency ceiling. RECOMMENDATION: do NOT build the daemon yet. Cheaper increments first: extend queue_reviews-style parked-PR checks to work repos + a cadence loop (/loop or cron attended session) = ~70% of benefit, zero new ops surface. Build the stdlib sentinel only if reaction latency still hurts at higher N, and then variant (a) only.

title: P3 reviewer's brief: per-PR review surface fed by P2 data [YET TO BE REFINED]
description: PLACEHOLDER — deliberately unrefined; spec it properly when its inputs exist. Concept (gates roadmap 2026-07-02, phase 3): a per-PR 'reviewer's brief' generated by flow at create_pr and served through an annotate-and-respond surface (lavish's code playbook is the leading candidate after the P0/P1 dogfood, but the build-approach decision — build-on-lavish vs standalone renderer — was EXPLICITLY left open until P2 lands and flow-1xv1 proves lavish in the plan gate). Contents sketch: ask-user findings agenda front and center; each diff region annotated with plan step + risk tier + evidence; guard-property analysis for hot changes; CodeRabbit thread status inline; annotations flow back as /flow revise cycles (symmetric with P1: plan annotations edit spec, review annotations drive revise). Only a rendering problem BECAUSE P2 (flow-argo) makes the data exist. UN-DEFER WHEN: flow-argo children landed (at minimum .2 + .3) AND flow-1xv1 shipped with positive experience. First refinement act: answer the build-approach question, then re-spec against whatever P2 actually shipped.

title: P2d plan-blind diff reader: diff-only subagent at code_review on full lane
description: At code_review completion on full-lane runs: spawn one cheap (sonnet, same downshift philosophy as model_resolve.py) subagent given ONLY the diff -- no plan, no ticket, no transcript -- prompted 'what does this change do; what looks wrong or surprising'. Closes the residual planner-bias window (inline reviewer shares the planner's mind; a flawed plan faithfully implemented passes it) with one spawn. Findings are ADVISORY-ONLY inputs to the orchestrator's triage: the stronger-model session classifies each through the P2b taxonomy (hallucinated/irrelevant -> dismissed or no-op with plan citation; real catches -> auto-fix or ask-user); the reader has NO blocking power -- a Critical still requires orchestrator agreement. Worst case of a hallucinating reader = one wasted cheap spawn + a few triage tokens (user raised this risk at spec review; this is the answer). Depends on P2b (vocabulary). OPEN: Q3 gating decision -- every-full-lane (recommended) vs no-bot-workspaces-only vs workspace.toml opt-in; default to every-full-lane unless answered otherwise before spec. Touchpoint: stage-code_review.md new step, full-lane-gated.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#424: plan-blind diff reader (P2d) at code_review on the full lane — one fresh subagent whose prompt embeds the post-auto-fix diff verbatim (embedding is the blindness mechanism: no plan/ticket names, instructed no file reads/commands; soft-but-mitigated, honestly documented) answering only 'what does this do / what looks wrong'. Observations are advisory candidates classified through the P2b taxonomy plus a reader-only dismissed disposition. Review NEEDS-FIX (light) fixed pre-merge: dismissal to no-op now requires the orchestrator to INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRM the deliberate choice is correct (deliberate != correct — the bias-laundering route on the flawed-plan-faithfully-implemented catch is closed; unconfirmed contradictions fail safe to ask-user or the Critical gate) and the dismissed disposition absorbs inline-finding duplicates so a decision never renders twice in ## Your call. Lane-gated full-only, sonnet-tier by default, fail-open on spawn failure. Prose-only, seam green.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Q3 RESOLVED at spec review (2026-07-02): gating = EVERY full-lane run (uniform; one cheap spawn; simplest rule — no bot-presence detection, no config surface). Supersedes the 'OPEN: Q3' note in the description.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — auto-ship approved at ~90%. Design: (1) Placement A — the plan-blind reader is a NEW step AFTER code_review's auto-fix pass and BEFORE the Critical gate, reviewing the post-auto-fix (shipping) diff; grounded in the ticket's 'at code_review completion' + 'real catches -> auto-fix or ask-user' wording. Frame it as a DISTINCT, differently-sourced single pass (fresh plan-blind mind), NOT a re-entry of step 4's loop — one inline pass + one reader pass = two single passes, no loop; reader-triggered auto-fixes apply once, review-bot+CI backstop. (2) Gate = LANE (ticket_frontmatter lane; skip on express/light, run on full), NOT model_resolve non-empty — Q3's 'every full-lane run, no config surface' is dispositive; a work_model=off opt-out must still run the reader. (3) Model = verb-do implement-stage idiom verbatim: M=$(model_resolve.py …); pass model=$M when non-empty, omit otherwise (inherit session). No literal sonnet fallback. (4) 'dismissed' is a fourth DISPOSITION (drop / no-op with plan.out cite), not a new .out section; reader has NO blocking power (a Critical needs independent orchestrator agreement). Safe to auto-ship: blast radius = one prose reference doc (stage-code_review.md) governing one stage; trivially reversible; non-hot (not a guard file); seam_check + test_seam_check gate the two new CLI citations; no create_pr/reflect change (create_pr's ## Your call lift is source-agnostic).

title: P2c plan-rationale carryover: author ## Changes from the plan's per-file notes
description: create_pr's ## Changes bullets START from the plan's 'Files to change' per-file one-line notes (carried, then updated where implementation diverged -- never verbatim-only), and any file added by the post-implement reconcile is marked '(added during implementation: <why>)'. Reviewer sees premeditated vs discovered diff parts. File-level only; hunk-level mapping explicitly out of scope (needs new metadata; file-level is the 90% win at ~zero cost -- the data already flows: plan doc in run dir, planned_files in frontmatter, reconcile history in state). Touchpoint: stage-create_pr.md step 1 only. Smallest P2 item, safe starter.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#421: create_pr's ## Changes is authored by carrying the plan's per-file notes onto the shipped diff (walk the diff set as ground truth; planned file -> carry-then-update its one-line note; unplanned file -> '(added during implementation: <why>)' best-effort from RECONCILE friction or inferred; planned-but-absent file -> no bullet; plan absent -> pre-P2 diff-only compose, same degrade as ## Your call). Scrub-safe format (backtick-led bullets dodge _BOLD_BULLET_RE). Producer side pre-existed (stage-plan.md Files-to-change). Review MERGE-OK; the one LOW folded pre-merge (format-agnostic reference to the plan's Files-to-change list — the H2 assertion would have silently degraded a literal consumer to diff-only). Prose-only, seam 223/45 green.

title: P2b finding taxonomy: ask-user/auto-fix/no-op axis + code_review out-file + Your-call PR section
description: Every code_review finding carries BOTH axes: severity (Critical/Major/Minor unchanged, Critical still blocks) and decision owner: auto-fix (agent fixes before commit) / no-op (deliberate, must cite the plan line making it deliberate) / ask-user (human's call; parks on the PR, never silently dropped). Findings written to stages/code_review.out as a structured list via orchestrator heredoc (same pattern as pr_body.md -- no new script, no seam_check surface). create_pr renders ask-user items as a 'Your call' PR-body section: the reviewer's agenda. SCOPE LOCKED at spec review: code_review stage ONLY -- review_loop/CodeRabbit keeps its existing structural actionability gate untouched. Invariant: ask-user does NOT lower the --auto defer bar; autonomous runs still exhaust self-confidence-raising; ask-user findings ride to the PR as flagged decisions, never divert to deferred. Touchpoints: stage-code_review.md step 3 (taxonomy + out-file contract), stage-create_pr.md (Your-call render), verb-do.md capture note. Blocks P2d (vocabulary dependency).
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#419: code_review finding taxonomy (P2b) — every finding gets a decision owner on the ask-user/auto-fix/no-op axis (exhaustive, ask-user is the fallback; no-op requires a verbatim plan.out citation; auto-fix confined to planned_files, single-pass, confident-only, grants no self-merge power; a Critical's only non-failing owner is auto-fix), persisted to $TICKET_DIR/stages/code_review.out under the flow:code_review-taxonomy v1 sentinel; create_pr renders ## Your call from ask-user findings only (defensive read: absent/malformed/empty -> PR body byte-identical to today). Reviewer traced the load-bearing path: auto-fix mutations ride into the commit via capture_implement_diff's unconditional fresh re-capture, no drift risk. Review MERGE-OK; 3 prose pins folded pre-merge (exhaustive-fallback sentence, label->header mapping P2d keys on, annotation-not-PR-visibility framing). Prose-only, seam 223/45 green. Branch CI events dropped (GitHub flake); local gates + main-push backstop.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed / auto-approve (clean plan, no clarifying questions, ~90%). P2b is a prose-only change to 3 non-guard reference docs (stage-code_review.md, stage-create_pr.md, verb-do.md): low blast radius, prose-reversible, CI-covered (seam_check + test_live_docs_are_green), non-hot so the human-merge keystone still gates the draft PR. Mandatory implementer commitments folded into the plan: (1) preserve the Critical floor as code_review becomes a writing stage (unresolved-Critical->status=failed + rerun-vs-override surfacing; no Critical shunted to ask-user; single-pass auto-fix); (2) the code_review.out ## auto-fixed section is non-optional (reviewer must see every silent pre-commit mutation); (3) auto-fix edit-path discipline (worktree-absolute/heredoc per flow-cjgy) lands in the stage prose; (4) run seam_check + test_live_docs_are_green first, watch the 3+ stage-doc re-enumeration guard.

title: P2a evidence on PR: preserve verification transcripts + render ## Evidence section
description: Preserve verification outputs (implement verify run, e2e recipe run when enabled) and render an ## Evidence section in the PR body at create_pr (optional-when-empty, collapsed details, trim rule sized to the stricter forge cap; PRIMARY forge is Bitbucket, GitHub second; render stays forge-neutral markdown via the existing forge seam). Evidence ladder (cheapest first): 1 transcript (command+result tail, already in stages/*.out); 2 delta report (baseline vs branch output) -- NOTE brinta's parity/snapshot framework is TO-BE-DELIVERED, not live: parity is one producer of rung 2, NOT a prerequisite for this bead (rungs 1/3/4 work today; rung 2 activates per-repo when its tooling lands); 3 fingerprint + targeted excerpt (sha256+size+section counts+key totals, before/after lines the ticket targets); 4 external blob link (S3 URL + sha for the full artifact, never committed). Which rungs a ticket keeps is declared at plan time inside the e2e/verify recipe (an evidence note, no new frontmatter field). Touchpoints: stage-e2e.md step 4, stage-create_pr.md template, pr_body.py trim/format helper (lib only). DEFERRED at filing: Q1 evidence-storage decision unanswered (text-first recommended) AND user explicitly unsure P2a is being delivered at all ('we need to understand if we are delivering p2a'). Un-defer only after both resolve.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Deferred at filing (2026-07-02 spec review): (1) Q1 evidence-storage decision unanswered (text-first recommended); (2) user explicitly unsure P2a is being delivered at all; (3) brinta parity framework (the rung-2 delta-report producer) is to-be-delivered, not live — not a hard prereq, but weakens the ladder there. Un-defer after Q1 + delivery confirmation.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Concrete render examples requested at spec review. GENERIC repo (test-suite evidence): '## Evidence' + collapsed details per verification run — summary line 'command — N passed, M failed (duration)', body = transcript tail (~40 lines, trim rule). DATA-PLATFORM repo (brinta form-generation): Run line (command, exit, duration) / Output fingerprint (filename, size, sha256, line count) / Baseline delta (N lines differ, which block, tiny diff excerpt, expected-vs-unexpected note) / Full output S3 URL pinned by the sha / Section record counts vs baseline. Same section, same rung vocabulary; rungs populated per the plan's evidence note.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Config layering settled at spec review (answers 'who tells the engine the rungs'): v1 = per-ticket plan recipe ONLY, mirroring e2e_recipe exactly — the plan-authored recipe gains an evidence note (rungs to keep, fingerprint spec, S3 destination, expected-delta scope), stamped at the plan gate; zero new frontmatter fields, zero workspace.toml schema change; engine default with no config = rung-1 transcript (stages/*.out already exists). Repo conventions reach the plan via plan-time exploration + memory recall of prior recipes. v2 (separate later work, only if per-recipe repetition annoys): optional [evidence] workspace.toml block (blob_prefix / fingerprint / delta command when parity lands) — costs a validate_workspace.py schema touch, deliberately excluded from v1.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: S3/rung-4 concern resolved at spec review (user: 'not sure I want to be uploading artifacts to our s3 right now; definitely configurable and opt-in'): v1 scope NARROWED to rungs 1-3 only — everything local and textual, ZERO external writes. Rung 4 (blob upload) is strictly opt-in: fires only when a human writes an explicit destination into the plan recipe they approve at the gate; the engine has no default bucket and no implicit upload path; --auto planners may NOT introduce upload destinations — autonomous rung 4 requires the future v2 [evidence].blob_prefix workspace.toml opt-in to exist first. For repos without big artifacts (flow itself): rung 1 transcript is the whole story; the ladder is a menu the plan picks from, not a checklist.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Q1 RESOLVED (2026-07-02): evidence storage = TEXT-FIRST — transcripts, delta/parity reports, fingerprints+excerpts, and external blob links (URL+sha) inline in the PR body under collapsed details with a trim rule; no committed binaries. Explicitly carries the rung-4 caveat from the prior comment: v1 ships rungs 1-3 only, blob links appear only when a human wrote the destination into the gate-approved recipe. DEFER now rests on ONE remaining unblocker: the delivery yes/no ('are we delivering P2a at all'). Everything else is spec-complete.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: go — deliver P2a. The parked question (confirm P2a's worth after living with the P2 review data) is answered by the maintainer 2026-07-06: build it. Scope as settled at spec review: v1 rungs 1-3 only (text-first, zero external writes), per-ticket plan-recipe evidence note (no new frontmatter/schema), rung-4 S3 strictly opt-in via human-authored recipe destination. Interactive spec session follows.
