title: spec planned_files: cross-check test paths against repo layout + recalled entries
description: MACHINERY (from FT-1334, knowledge id abf1fa9a00358a77): /flow spec planned_files derivation (references/verb-spec.md step 6) stamped a nonexistent test path (specifics/arg/iva_2083/test_writers_working_papers.py) by analogy to the source tree, when the repo's tests live in a tests/spark/ mirror. Needed a post-implement reconcile + re-baseline. The correct path was in the recalled set (6e55d43139f05fb1) but unused. Fix: step 6 should cross-check each stamped test path against git ls-files existence AND recalled entries naming a test path, flagging one that neither exists nor sits beside a sibling. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only references/*.md edit. Severity minor.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#417: verb-spec step 6 now cross-checks every stamped test path (git ls-files existence + tracked-sibling probe, crossed with step-3/6 recall) with three dispositions — sibling present: stamp as-is; no sibling but recall names the module's test path: re-root onto it (the FT-1334 fix: analogy-stamped source-tree path vs the real tests/spark mirror); neither: keep with an uncertainty note in the plan text, never drop (a genuinely new test dir is legitimate; post-implement reconcile is the backstop). Complements the _typo_planned parent-dir guard which cannot see the test-less-sibling case. Review MERGE-OK; note-sink minor folded pre-merge (4de8ca5); pattern stays Python-convention-scoped by design (witnessed case + engine are Python, non-matching paths behave exactly as today).

title: brinta-data-platform: adopt [memory] label_facets = ["form"] + facet-inference-tips convention
description: Follow-up from flow-a0qu (faceted knowledge memory: labels[] on entries + label-scoped recall, engine side). flow-a0qu ships the generic engine machinery (memory_append.py --labels, recall.py --label hard-filter cluster retrieval, [memory] label_facets config seam read by validate_workspace.py + reflect_inputs.py) but the config ships empty (label_facets = []) in this repo since it has no natural facet. brinta-data-platform DOES have a natural facet: forms (e.g. iva_2083, desif, dimp). This bead adopts the convention there: set [memory] label_facets = ["form"] in that workspace's workspace.toml, and establish/document the facet-inference-tips convention (how the reflect stage or a human decides which form: value applies when tagging a new knowledge entry with --labels form:<value>). References flow-a0qu's plan.out Risks section ('label_facets ships empty here... real value belongs in brinta-data-platform').
close_reason: Adopted 2026-07-02: brinta-data-platform's .flow/workspace.toml (local config — .flow is untracked at origin/dev, so no PR applies) now sets [memory] label_facets = ["form"] with the facet-inference-tips convention documented in the adjacent comment block (form id = the regulatory artifact the work targets, lowercase snake_case, inferred from ticket title / mapping path / register name, never incidental mentions; only form-specific claims get tagged). validate_workspace.py from the merged flow-a0qu engine (PR#412, checkout e89365d) exits 0 on the new config. Engine-side --labels/--label/label_facets seam is PR#412; the digest consumer is flow-14zj (running).

title: Form-card digest: consolidated form-specific knowledge view over recall.py --label clusters
description: Follow-up from flow-a0qu (faceted knowledge memory: labels[] on entries + label-scoped recall). The plan's author flagged a 'form-card digest' as out of scope, deferred to spec: a consolidated, human-readable digest view over a form's full knowledge cluster (built on the new recall.py --label facet:value exhaustive retrieval landed in flow-a0qu), rather than the raw JSON array recall.py currently emits. Needs its own spec pass to pin the exact shape/consumer (a reflect-stage aid? a standalone script? a recall.py output mode?) before implementation. References flow-a0qu's plan.out Goal section and Risks (label_facets ships empty in this repo; real facet values belong in a downstream repo).
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#414: recall.py --digest output mode per the recorded DECISION — requires --label (argparse-level, exit 2), renders the label cluster as a markdown card (sections by type, ts DESC within, one line per entry: ts / ticket / first sentence), superseded excluded, plain-JSON path byte-unchanged, forwarder + stdin guards intact. Review MERGE-OK; both minors fixed pre-merge in 6b67cd7 (first-sentence whitespace collapse keeps bullets one-line; --digest --label '' now rejected instead of re-opening the stdin path). 83 recall tests green.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION (delegated maintainer, 2026-07-02): the form-card digest ships as a recall.py OUTPUT MODE, not a new script and not a reflect-stage aid. Shape: `recall.py --label <facet:value> --digest` renders the label-scoped exhaustive cluster (the flow-a0qu retrieval path) as a human-readable markdown card — sections by entry type (DECISION / FACT / LEARNED), newest-first within section, one line per entry (ts, ticket, first sentence), superseded entries excluded as usual; plain JSON behavior without --digest is byte-unchanged. --digest without --label is an argparse error (digest is defined over a label cluster, not over BM25 results). Consumer: a human (or an agent mid-plan) reading one form's accumulated knowledge before working that form; no engine consumer, no new stage wiring. Non-hot (recall.py is not a guard file); verb-recall.md documents the mode; seam_check + the recall-metric generic-forwarder convention must stay intact. Constraint from flow-a0qu: label_facets ships empty in this repo, so tests use synthetic facets; the real consumer lives in brinta-data-platform (flow-lw35).

title: mise python@3.14.6 pin: broken freethreaded install breaks the dev gate (mise run lint/test)
description: The mise.toml-pinned python@3.14.6 fails to install in a worktree ('missing lib directory', a freethreaded-build issue); 'mise install --force' fails identically. This makes the documented dev gate 'mise run lint'/'mise run test' unusable. Workaround in use: uvx at the exact pinned versions (ruff@0.15.15, ty@0.0.42, pytest==8.3.3). Consider re-pinning to a stable (non-freethreaded) python build or documenting the uvx fallback in CLAUDE.md. Observed on flow-oe9.4.
close_reason: Environment healed, verified 2026-07-02 ~18:50 -03: mise install python@3.14.5 succeeded and afterwards BOTH documented gates run green via the pinned toolchain from the scripts dir — mise run lint (ruff check + format --check + ty all pass) and mise run test (engine + hooks suites pass). python@3.14.6 itself now executes (import tomllib fine). The 'missing lib directory' freethreaded-install failure that motivated this bead is no longer reproducible; the uvx fallback pins (ruff@0.15.15 / ty@0.0.42 / --python 3.14 pytest@8.3.3) remain documented in session memory as belt-and-braces. No re-pin needed; closing as healed-with-evidence rather than code-fixed.

title: checkpoint-then-reap: commit-before-push crash window orphans the WIP on next reap (vpg1 follow-up)
description:
Follow-up to flow-vpg1/PR#409 (reviewer Finding 1, LOW). Window: flow_worktree checkpoint-then-reap crashes AFTER `git commit` (WIP checkpoint) but BEFORE the rescue push lands. The next reap then reads the tree as CLEAN (the WIP is committed), removes the worktree, and `git branch -D` orphans the local-only commit — exactly the "local commit dies with branch -D" loss the vpg1 DECISION forbids. Window is a network round-trip wide and the reap orchestrator can die by the same spend-limit path that created the orphan.

FIX SHAPE (constraint from the review): naive "HEAD has commits not on any remote ref -> not clean" BREAKS the merged-orphan reap path (squash-merge rewrites shas, so already-merged work always looks unpushed). The checkpoint commit must be self-identifying: give it a fixed message marker at creation; at reap, a HEAD whose message carries the marker AND whose sha is absent from the rescue ref namespace re-attempts the rescue push (same no-force, flow-rescue/<key>-<sha> target) BEFORE any remove/branch -D; push failure keeps the worktree intact (same exit-5 contract as PR#409).

HOT: same teardown/lease machinery as PR#409; guard-property review + property tests required (crash-window fixture: committed-not-pushed -> re-push -> remove; merged-orphan regression: squash-merged worktree still reaps clean).

ACCEPTANCE-INVARIANT: A worktree holding a committed-but-unpushed checkpoint commit is never removed before its rescue push lands (re-push then remove; push failure preserves worktree, exit 5); squash-merged orphan reap behavior unchanged (regression test).
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#420: the checkpoint commit is self-identifying (_checkpoint_marker(ticket) single-sources the commit subject and the reap compare, exact-subject match); a reap finding a marker-bearing HEAD whose sha is absent from origin re-attempts the no-force flow-rescue/<key>-<sha> push BEFORE any remove/branch -D (push failure = worktree intact, exit 5, same PR#409 contract; already-pushed = idempotent skip via ls-remote). Merged-orphan regression pinned (squash-merged unpushed-sha HEAD still reaps clean — the naive not-on-remote heuristic the bead forbade). All under the existing classify_then flock; guard review: 7/7 HELD, 126 tests. The flow-vpg1 crash-window work-loss class is closed end to end.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed (~92%, both axes). Fix: marker-exact-match recovery in flow_worktree._checkpoint_dirty_worktree. On a clean tree, if HEAD's subject == the checkpoint marker for this ticket AND its flow-rescue/<ticket>-<sha> ref is absent on origin, re-push (no-force, same target) BEFORE any worktree-remove/branch -D; push failure -> status=failed -> worktree preserved, CLI exit 5 (PR#409 contract). Right option: exact %s marker-match (not naive unpushed->dirty) is what distinguishes an orphaned checkpoint from a squash-merged HEAD, so the merged-orphan reap path stays clean. Safe to auto-ship: narrow blast radius (single call chain _checkpoint_then_remove<-reap_worktree<-_run_reap; insert fires only when HEAD-subject==marker, common clean path byte-unchanged), trivially reversible (one function + tests), strong CI coverage (merged-orphan regression + crash-window recovery are the mandated property tests). HOT (guard file flow_worktree.py + hot label); adjudicate_hot on so proceed not downgraded; merge-time guard-property review + CI + one-hot isolation remain the keystone. MANDATORY implementer commitments: (1) merged-orphan regression test (clean tree, non-marker HEAD -> reaps clean, no flow-rescue ref); (2) single-source _checkpoint_marker(ticket) helper used at both the commit site and the recovery compare, with the byte-exact subject assertion preserved. ls-remote is an optimization only; the idempotent no-force same-sha push is the source of truth (push-anyway on a transient ls-remote failure, never strand a worktree).

title: plan-gate lavish surface: render spec review via lavish-axi (attended lanes)
description: Phase 1 of gates roadmap (2026-07-02 synthesis session). Wire lavish-axi into the spec verb's plan-review moment, attended lanes only: render the plan doc as a lavish HTML surface (plan+comparison+input playbooks), decision forks as input controls, user annotations flow back as spec revisions, converge, then ExitPlanMode. Constraints: (1) plan doc (markdown) stays ground truth, HTML is a disposable view; (2) optional dependency, graceful when lavish absent; bg/--auto lanes never invoke it; (3) pin lavish-axi version (npx -y supply chain risk) before wiring into prose. Touchpoint: verb-spec / plan-phase references (prose edit, no new verb). Phase 0 validated by user on live artifact: 'It is way better to read this way.' Not green-lit yet (same roadmap): P2a evidence artifacts, P2b ask-user taxonomy, P2c hunk-plan mapping, P2d plan-blind diff reader.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#411: lavish-axi plan-review surface wired into interactive spec step 4 (verb-spec.md, prose-only, +14 lines). Constraints verified by review: attended-only structurally (auto-approve path never reaches interactive step 4; CLAUDE_JOB_DIR gate catches backgrounded-interactive), pinned npx -y lavish-axi@0.1.35 (npm-verified), markdown stays ground truth with disposable TMPDIR HTML (never .lavish/, never committed, ended before ExitPlanMode), full degradation contract (any failure -> plain-prose fallback). Review MERGE-OK; the one LOW (fallback phrasing leapfrogged de-fork/e2e/lane sub-blocks) fixed pre-merge in f073f16. Final-head CI event dropped by GitHub (same flake as PR#403); delta was 2 prose phrases, seam_check green locally, main-push CI is the backstop.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Wiring caveat discovered dogfooding lavish for the P2 spec (2026-07-02): the entire lavish loop ran OUTSIDE Claude plan mode (auto mode) and worked — but flow's spec verb runs its plan phase read-only with ExitPlanMode as the one gate, and lavish needs Write (artifact HTML) + Bash (npx serve/poll). Implementation must either (a) author the artifact via Bash heredoc to a NON-REPO temp path and document serve/poll as read-only-by-discipline (same carve-out class as verb-spec's git fetch — loopback server, no repo mutation), or (b) run the lavish review loop immediately before entering plan-mode-proper. Either way the gate is unchanged: lavish nests inside the plan phase as the review surface; ExitPlanMode fires on the converged plan. Also from dogfooding: mirror every resolved decision into the plan doc/tracker as you go — the artifact mutates in place with no history; and do not leave polls armed unattended (each return burns a turn on a large DOM snapshot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: AMENDMENT SHIPPED (PR#418, merged): the 22:40 dogfood caveat is addressed — the HTML is authored via Bash heredoc to the throwaway TMPDIR path (the Write tool is plan-mode-blocked regardless of target, which would have tripped the degradation contract and silently killed the surface), under the same read-only-by-discipline footing as step 3's git fetch. Both dogfood lessons folded in (mirror resolved decisions into the markdown immediately; never leave polls armed unattended). Maintainer decision hardened into binding prose: lavish is an optional ADD-ON — no flow verb/stage/script may require it or reference it outside the step-4 block; lavish-absent IS the original plan-mode implementation, verbatim.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CORRECTION (2026-07-03): the 'attended-only structurally' constraint recorded at close is deliberately reversed by commit 92e12cb (branch fix/lavish-backgrounded-gate). The CLAUDE_JOB_DIR-unset gate condition is removed from the lavish plan-review surface in verb-spec.md step 4: backgrounded != unattended — a backgrounded interactive session still has a human who can attach via the harness cockpit (e.g. claude agents), so the optional review surface must not be disabled there. Structural exclusion of --auto (never reaches step 4) remains the guarantee that a human is on the other end. Do NOT re-add the CLAUDE_JOB_DIR condition as a 'regression fix'.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: ^ pointer update: the reversal shipped as PR#426 (amended commit b79c82c; 92e12cb no longer exists post-amend). Join by PR number.

title: metric.py recall-hit-rate requires --namespace but stage-reflect.md 3f says to omit it
description: stage-reflect.md step 3f (recall-hit-rate echo) says: "--namespace is omitted deliberately; the command auto-resolves it from workspace.toml." But metric.py recall-hit-rate REQUIRES --namespace (metric.py exit-code doc line ~40: "1 = bad args (namespace required when not --checkpoint...)"), erroring "metric: --namespace is required" when omitted. Confirmed live in flow-oe9.2's reflect. Fix (pick one): (a) make metric.py auto-resolve namespace from workspace.toml for recall-hit-rate (match the doc's stated intent, mirrors how other subcommands resolve), or (b) update stage-reflect.md step 3f to pass --namespace <ns>. Option (a) is preferable (keeps the reflect prose call-site clean). Files: scripts/metric.py, references/stage-reflect.md. Not hot (neither is a guard file). seam_check may already or should cover this prose->flag drift.
close_reason: Already fixed on origin/main by PR#403 (flow-ro3w): metric._run_recall_hit_rate auto-resolves namespace via _memory_paths.resolve_namespace when --namespace is omitted (the bead's preferred option a), verified on origin/main. The witnessing run (flow-oe9.2) hit the error because it launched from a plugin version predating the #403 merge; stage-reflect.md 3f prose is now accurate as written. Bead was stale at filing time (pre-launch staleness class).

title: jira adapter in_progress transition picks arbitrary native status (landed 'Testing')
description: tracker_cli transition --to-state in_progress on FT-1328 (native 'To Do') landed native status 'Testing' (diagnostic: category=indeterminate + native='Testing' -> in_progress (default)). The adapter's default pick among In-Progress-category transitions chose 'Testing' over 'In Progress', so the board shows Testing while work is only starting; the commit-stage in_review hop then correctly matched 'Review' via the review hint. Fix: before the indeterminate-default fallback, prefer a native status matching an in-progress hint list ('in progress', 'doing', 'in development'), mirroring the existing review-hint match. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#408: tracker_cli._select_transition_id gains an in-progress hint pre-pass (substring, case-insensitive, hints 'in progress'/'doing'/'in development') gated on target==in_progress, breaking the FT-1328 tie where the indeterminate default picked native 'Testing' over 'In Progress'. Purely additive (fallback loop untouched), no beads/other-state behavior change. Review: MERGE-OK, one non-blocking low (hint text folds in button name; safe under the normalized-state gate).

title: bg work-stage subagent stalls on run_in_background/Monitor waits
description: implement subagent in a bg /flow --auto run twice ended its turn 'waiting for the Monitor notification' on a long pytest launched via run_in_background; spawned stage subagents never receive background-task completions after their turn ends, so the stage stalls until the orchestrator SendMessage-resumes it (FT-1328, 2x). Fix: stage-implement.md + verb-do subagent dispatch prose should mandate FOREGROUND test runs with explicit Bash timeout (<=600000ms, chunked if longer) inside spawned stage subagents. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only edit.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#413 (salvaged from the spend-limit-killed run, prose diff complete + line-reviewed): stage-implement.md step 5 + verb-do.md now mandate foreground chunked test runs with explicit timeout <=600000ms in spawned stage subagents, unconditionally — a spawned subagent never receives background-task completions (they route to the orchestrator), so run_in_background/Monitor stalls the stage (FT-1328 witness, twice). seam_check 222/44 green.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — prose-only mandate that spawned stage subagents run tests FOREGROUND with explicit Bash timeout <=600000ms, chunked if longer, never run_in_background/Monitor. Both axes clean: non-hot (no guard-file basename), no drift vs origin/main, low blast (2 on-demand reference docs), reversible, seam_check/test_live_docs_are_green guard the checkable surface. Mandatory implementer commitments folded into plan: (1) lead stage-implement.md Step-5 callout with chunk-to-stay-short citing mise run test's two invocations (scripts/tests + hooks/tests) as the concrete chunk unit; (2) name the ~360s idle-watchdog kill (flow-rbr) as a distinct mechanism below the 600000ms ceiling. Files: references/stage-implement.md + references/verb-do.md.

title: Run knowledge-consolidation sweep on the live corpus (flow-ro3w mechanism follow-up)
description: PR#403 (flow-ro3w) shipped the consolidation MECHANISM only: sweep_knowledge.py cluster (deterministic near-dup grouping over the memory_embed sidecar) + apply-cluster (list-valued supersede merge). The corpus cleanup itself was deferred because it is corpus-mutating and each synthesized canonical body needs human review. To realize the value: run 'sweep_knowledge.py cluster --threshold 0.90 --workspace-root <flow root>' over the live corpus (namespace flow), synthesize ONE canonical body per confirmed cluster into an apply-cluster manifest, review each, then apply-cluster. See the ## curate consolidation recipe in verb-evolve.md. Maintainer one-shot, propose-and-review; do NOT auto-run.
close_reason: Executed the maintainer one-shot consolidation sweep (propose-and-review, per bead + verb-evolve curate recipe) using the PR#403 lane: memory_embed reindex -> sweep_knowledge cluster --threshold 0.90 over the live corpus (namespace flow) -> 4 clusters proposed (2 members each) -> each pair reviewed against CURRENT code before merging (verified: state.finish_stage in_progress guard shipped by flow-nzx3; snapshot._engine_component still anchors main checkout post-flow-2pp with branch gate; dispatch_stage._gate_drift generalized per flow-56s/PR#113) -> apply-cluster manifest with 4 synthesized canonicals: 5ff47ea9b5764ccc (takeover_clear force-reclaim seam, merges flow-5lg3+flow-1k75), 9a70f6afcc915d4a (finish_stage guard story, merges flow-6hn2+flow-nzx3 and corrects the stale no-guard claim), ef906a26c18b1263 (engine component main-checkout anchoring, merges flow-72d9+flow-dpz0), 51c1370776fd43fd (owned-drift auto-reconcile generalization, merges flow-u3s+flow-56s). 8 dups tombstoned via list-valued supersede, any_error=false, sidecar reindexed, recall now surfaces the canonicals first. Corpus at tau 0.90 had only these 4 conservative near-dup pairs; lower thresholds left for a future pass if recall precision metrics justify it.

title: nu1w child-2 (HOT): absorb stage-merge mechanical recipe into dispatch advance — de-risking experiment per flow-10px verdict
description:
Child-2 of epic flow-nu1w, materialized per the flow-10px spike verdict (PR#401, docs/research/cognitive-yield-burn-spike-2026-07.md): absorb the stage-merge deterministic recipe into the engine as the de-risking experiment. NARROWED SCOPE per verdict: stage-merge ONLY — create_pr absorption is dropped (low-drift, feature-churned), stage-commit absorption is adjudicated out (core token share ~10% < 15%, not a drift hotspot).

WHAT: move stage-merge.md's mechanical plumbing (eligibility probe, CI rollup wait, squash merge, bead close + covers transition, worktree reap sequencing) from prose recipe into dispatch_stage advance (or a dedicated engine path it drives), leaving in prose ONLY the judgment steps: the hot guard-property review delegation and any maintainer adjudication branches. The prose seam-bug class the drift arm measured (shell conditional-expansion quoting, push refspec, exit-code swallowing — 22 commits of fixes on stage-merge.md) must become structurally impossible: tested code, not re-typed shell.

CONSTRAINTS:
- HOT: engine-core + merge-path change; merge-time guard-property review + CI gate apply.
- Property-equivalence bar (same bar as flow-8by2.5): the absorbed path must provably preserve current merge-stage behavior — eligibility refusals, CI-green gate, hot isolation, auto_merge_hot gating, bead close/covers semantics, reap lease gate. Corpus-green alone insufficient.
- seam_check: prose invocations shrink; update the corpus honestly (no gaming the checker).
- Do NOT absorb the guard-property review, the adjudication branches, or anything judgment-shaped — cognition stays with the LLM.

ACCEPTANCE-INVARIANT: Merge-stage behavior property-equivalent pre/post absorption (eligibility, CI gate, hot isolation, bead close, reap ordering) with tests proving each; stage-merge.md reduced to judgment-only prose; seam_check green without weakening; drift bug-classes (quoting/refspec/exit-code) now impossible-by-construction in the absorbed path.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#415: stage-merge mechanical plumbing absorbed into stage_merge.py (probe/execute; 476 lines, 66 tests) with stage-merge.md reduced to judgment-only prose (guard-property review delegation + verdict branches). Guard review: complete old-step->carrier mapping table with no orphans; all 8 guard properties HELD; property-equivalence bar met via identity decision code (the four gate scripts shelled unchanged) + per-invariant pinning suite. Headline strengthening: merge-failure now closes NOTHING (the old no-set-e bash could close a bead after a failed merge — the exact exit-code-swallowing class the flow-10px drift arm measured, now impossible). Review pins folded pre-merge: probe side-effect-freedom across PR states + every shelled-sibling argv parsed against the real argparse surface (closing the one layer seam_check/mocked tests cannot see). Self-application inert this run; takes effect on future self-target runs.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED to auto-ship. Absorb stage-merge.md's mechanical plumbing into a new tested engine script scripts/stage_merge.py with two entry points (probe=eligibility §1, execute=merge/close §3+cover-close); the §2 hot guard-property review stays LLM prose BETWEEN the two calls. Ruling: the two-phase shape is forced by the LLM-in-the-middle constraint (not a design fork), and safe to auto-ship because the change is inert for its own landing (this run merges via the frozen cache engine; the new path governs only future self-target runs post-merge, never user projects where merge=none), reversible (draft PR, squash-revertible, maintainer repo), and CI-covered by mock-based per-invariant pytest (harness_eval replays the decider corpus, which this does not touch, so pytest is the real net). Blast radius contained; every named weakness is closeable-in-run. MANDATORY IMPLEMENTER COMMITMENTS: (1) SHELL the existing gates unchanged (evolve_self_merge.py, main_ci_health.py, forge_cli.py) via subprocess argument-lists — do NOT reimplement their decision logic; this byte-identical reuse IS the property-equivalence guarantee. (2) TDD red-first: each invariant test fails before the code exists — the reviewed test-mapping IS the equivalence proof (no automated old-vs-new differential is possible against prose). (3) Exact-argv passthrough tests asserting the precise flags threaded to evolve_self_merge.py (--ci-status/--eval-status/--main-ci-status/--changed-files present/absent) — seam_check cannot see internally-built argv. (4) Do NOT edit stage-registry.toml's merge entry (keep merge=inline -> references/stage-merge.md) — this is what keeps the change inert for its own landing. (5) delete-branch is REMOTE-only; NEVER tear down the local worktree/branch (reap-defer invariant; teardown stays with the drain janitor). (6) seam_check.py green + test_live_* after the prose rewrite + the MODULE.md stage_merge.py row; update MODULE.md/inventory.md corpus HONESTLY, no gaming. PR body must name the residual: property-equivalence is proven by shell-unchanged-gates (airtight) PLUS reviewed test-mapping + §2 guard review (strong, not a prose differential) — that is as well as the bar can be met; the maintainer signs that at PR review.

title: oe9 child-4 (propose-only): recurrence escalation — class recurs >=K after claimed fix auto-files evolve bead labelled recurrent
description: Child of flow-oe9, depends on child-1 and child-3. Propose-only escalation policy: a fix whose friction class recurs >=K times after the claimed fix auto-FILES an evolve bead labelled `recurrent` (via flow_beads_create.py dedup) — it informs the maintainer, it never auto-gates or reopens anything. Squarely on the propose side of the auto-vs-propose line. Judgment-gated: K and the long-tail-class exemption need a human call at spec time.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#410: friction_escalate.py propose-only recurrence escalation — a class recurring >=K (default 3, [evolve] recurrence_escalation_k) after its latest claimed fix files ONE bead labelled recurrent (never evolve/proposal: structurally drain-ineligible), bd verb surface = list+create only, dedup via bare-anchor evid key (re-failure after a closed escalation routes to deduped: under-notification, the safe direction), long-tail exemption via recurrence_exempt_anchors knob (planned_files default). Wired best-effort into stage-reflect prose; maintainer-gated dormant no-op elsewhere; bd-absent degrades to exit 3 + one reflect log line. Review MERGE-OK; the one MEDIUM (propose-only pin asserted presence not absence) fixed pre-merge: label set + verb surface now locked in test.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) recurrence escalation (propose-only). Threshold on friction-since-LATEST-claimed-fix (not post_fix_count, which over-counts multi-fix classes). K=3 (calibrated on live corpus: honest signal has natural gap at 11/3/3/then <=1). exempt=[planned_files] (14 fix attempts, by-design reconcile-widening, intrinsic not a failed fix). labels=recurrent ONLY (NOT evolve, NOT proposal): a bead lacking 'evolve' is never a drain candidate -- 'bd ready -l evolve' -- so propose-only holds independent of any include_proposals flag. Both K + exempt configurable via [evolve] recurrence_escalation_k / recurrence_exempt_anchors. Home=reflect stage (mandatory deterministic step, machinery-lens-gated; dedup makes per-run firing idempotent). Advisor verdict: proceed (propose-only, maintainer-gated, non-hot, reversible, CI-covered).

title: oe9 child-3: recall --metric fix-efficacy — per closed MACHINERY bead, did its friction class recur since
description:
Child of flow-oe9, depends on child-1 (recurrence detector). Separate-verifier readout: a `recall --metric fix-efficacy` rollup printing, per closed MACHINERY-fix bead, whether its friction class recurred since the fix — the maintainer-triaged falsification report. Rides child-1's join; flow-ml7 trend-rollup shipped so the original sequencing precondition is satisfied. Blast radius: metric.py (or grader script) + recall.py forwarding + verb-recall.md + tests; seam_check must stay green (recall --metric is a generic forwarder — getattr-gate new args).

ACCEPTANCE-INVARIANT: recall --metric fix-efficacy prints a per-closed-MACHINERY-bead table with recurred/clean verdict + evidence counts; seam_check green; no new args break the generic forwarder.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#407: recall --metric fix-efficacy (metric.py subcommand, generic forwarder untouched) prints per-closed-MACHINERY-bead recurred/clean verdicts with evidence counts + --json; verdict = post-fix friction sharing the bead's distinctive fix anchors (same df + machinery-exempt ceiling as friction_recurrence); namespace auto-resolves; no bd dependency (degrades on missing files). Review: MERGE-OK, 6 lows non-blocking (table lacks per-row measurable marker; min-fix-ts smear on multi-entry beads documented). Live corpus readout: fix_beads=72 recurred=45 clean=27 unmeasurable=4 recurrence_rate=0.625 — the separate-verifier falsification readout the epic demanded, now measured.

title: oe9 child-2: widen reflect bundle — recurrence detector output into reflect_inputs.py so lens-B sees historical fix efficacy
description:
Child of flow-oe9, depends on child-1 (recurrence detector). Widen the reflect bundle: reflect_inputs.py is per-run blind (filters friction to fe.run_id == ts.run_id, see ~line 218) — every reflect agent sees only its own run's friction, never the historical series. Feed the child-1 detector output into reflect_inputs.py so lens-B sees "this class fired N times, last fix sha X, M runs ago" — turning a per-run-blind reflect into one with memory of its own prior fixes. The maker gets the verifier's signal. Update stage-reflect.md lens-B protocol to consume the new section. Blast radius: reflect_inputs.py, stage-reflect.md, tests.

ACCEPTANCE-INVARIANT: Reflect bundle for a run in a repo with a recurring class contains the recurrence block (class, N, last fix sha, runs-ago); block absent when no recurrence; existing per-run friction section unchanged.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#405: reflect bundle gains an always-present friction_recurrence block (distilled {cluster_key, anchor, fired_count, last_fix_sha, runs_ago} from friction_recurrence.analyze; per-run friction section byte-unchanged) and stage-reflect.md lens-B treats a non-empty block as a high-priority machinery finding (prior fix did not hold -> re-diagnose, not re-apply). Review hardening in ec0c3fe: detector guard broadened to any exception (closing-stage enrichment degrades to [] instead of killing the bundle), worst-15 cap by fired_count, lens-B prose clarifies fired_count (class-scoped, earliest fix) vs runs_ago (global, latest fix). The per-run-blind reflect (reflect_inputs filtering friction to its own run) now has memory of its own prior fixes.

title: oe9 child-1 KEYSTONE: friction-recurrence detector — surface classes recurring after a claimed MACHINERY fix (dual clustering: stage/type + root-cause signature)
description:
KEYSTONE child of flow-oe9 (falsifiable self-edit contracts). Build the recurrence-detection read: a new small read-only script (friction_recurrence.py) that joins .flow/flow/friction.jsonl to MACHINERY entries in .flow/flow/knowledge.jsonl and surfaces friction classes that recurred AFTER a MACHINERY fix for that class was recorded (forward join: MACHINERY timestamp/sha -> later friction of the same class).

Clustering MUST key by BOTH: (a) (stage, type[, file-anchor]) and (b) root-cause SIGNATURE (shared API / call-pattern cluster) per the epic's 2026-06-15 sharpening. Witness for (b): the no-upstream-tracking class (sandboxed worktree records no upstream) was patched site-by-site (create_pr.py PR#225 -> version_remerge.py -> stage-merge.md s3) and kept resurfacing at the next unpatched site sharing the call-pattern; it spans implement+merge+commit stages and RECONCILE/DRIFT/BLOCKER types, so a (stage,type) cluster structurally cannot see it.

Output must carry evidence (entry ids, run ids, counts, fix sha) so a downstream judge can distinguish "same fix didn't take" from "new instance of an inherently long-tail class" — the detector reports, it does not adjudicate. Read-mostly, propose-side; no safety-guard file. Current live witness: (implement,RECONCILE) x22, (merge,RECONCILE) x16, (commit,RECONCILE) x9 in friction.jsonl vs ~97 machinery-referencing knowledge entries with zero falsification.

Blast radius: new script + tests + MODULE.md row; no schema change to flow_friction.py (stage/type/ticket/run_id already present).

ACCEPTANCE-INVARIANT: Detector emits >=1 correctly-joined recurrence class from live friction.jsonl with post-fix evidence (entry ids, run ids, fix sha); join unit-tested on synthetic fixtures covering both clustering keys; a class with no post-fix recurrence is NOT reported.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#402: friction_recurrence.py keystone detector (dual clustering: structural (stage,type,anchor) + cross-cutting signature anchors; forward join MACHINERY fix ts/sha -> later same-class friction; evidence-carrying, report-not-adjudicate). Review hardening in 04c2c5f: fix-claimed anchors exempt from DF ceiling, null-body/ts-less tolerance. Live output: 37 signature classes, top planned_files x13 post-fix, @{u} x11.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED. New read-only propose-side script friction_recurrence.py (library + thin CLI, stdlib-only) forward-joins friction.jsonl to MACHINERY-prefixed knowledge entries; anchor-as-class model (distinctive-anchor token, df-band [2,15], no union-find, no CLI-flag/kebab noise) fires correctly on live data (top class @{u} = the upstream-tracking witness). Safe to auto-ship: narrow blast (new script + tests + one MODULE.md row + gitignored-fixture), fully reversible, no guard/safety file (is_hot=false), CI-covered by the new pytest suite. MANDATORY COMMITMENTS folded into the plan: (1) live friction.jsonl/knowledge.jsonl are GITIGNORED (.gitignore:42,46) so a live-tree read would error in CI and stall the merge -> the invariant test asserts against a COMMITTED fixture tests/friction_recurrence_corpus.json (harness_corpus.json pattern); synthetic fixtures cover the key-logic; 'emits >=1 from live' is demonstrated as a CLI run in the PR body. (2) MODULE.md must add friction_recurrence to the _memory_paths.py 'imported by' row or test_seam_check.test_live_docs_are_green goes red. (3) module docstring states signature classes are single-anchor facets and cross-anchor unification is the downstream judge's job; add a related_anchors field (co-occurring anchors) so oe9.2/oe9.3/oe9.4 inherit a documented, unifiable shape.

title: --auto self-teardown fails on macOS: nohup child killed before its sleep, panels leak
description: verb-do.md #Self-teardown (lines ~44-49) detaches the teardown as `nohup sh -c "sleep 30; claude stop $JOB_ID; <dir-removal>" &` when setsid is absent (macOS has no setsid binary). A plain `nohup ... &` child stays in the session's process group, so when the run's session tears down the group is killed BEFORE the `sleep 30` elapses, so the stop + dir-removal never run and the --auto panel leaks anyway. Witnessed 2026-07-01: flow-uc8n (job b74770b0) + flow-wxli (job d000d92c) both finished (beads closed, leases reaped) but neither self-tore-down; both cleaned by hand. flow-opnl's drain post-done sweep covers the DRAIN case, but a STANDALONE `claude --bg /flow <key> --auto` run has NO other cleaner and leaks forever. Fix candidates (judgment): (1) survive process-group teardown on macOS (double-fork + setpgid via a tiny python helper, or schedule the teardown out-of-process via `at`/launchd/a one-shot cron); (2) verify whether setsid is truly the only gap and whether `disown` after backgrounding helps. Evidence: references/verb-do.md #Self-teardown (pure prose recipe, no script). Non-hot (verb-do.md not a guard file). Blast radius: standalone --auto panel cleanup only.
close_reason: Fixed in PR#390 (merged): verb-do.md #Self-teardown setsid-absent fallback replaced with a python3 double-fork daemonizer (fork → setsid → fork → execvp), TEARDOWN passed as argv never interpolated. Live-verified: marker survives process-group SIGTERM with the new recipe, dies with the old bare-nohup one (negative control reproduced the leak). Standalone --auto panels no longer leak on macOS.

title: drain terminates before A2 can reap its last launched batch; sessions leak as panels
description:
The drain loop exits at step D on lease-free (`done` fires the instant the last run's LEASE frees), but a bg run's session goes idle AFTER its lease frees (it still runs reflect, then rests). So the final A2 pass (evolve_session_cleanup.py) runs while the last batch is still non-idle and skips it; no later drain turn exists to re-check, so the last batch's session panels ALWAYS leak. This is the exact toil A2 exists to remove (maintainer must `claude stop` + Ctrl+X by hand).

Compounding cause: evolve_session_cleanup.py applies the longer `--stale-idle-threshold-secs` (default 600) whenever `state` is not a clean terminal. A finished bg run rests at state=working OR state=blocked forever (state never flips), so the 600s bar is ALWAYS the gate for a just-finished run — and a run that finished <600s before the loop exited can never clear it in time.

Witnessed 2026-07-01 drain: flow-uc8n (job b74770b0) + flow-wxli (job d000d92c) both finished with beads closed + leases reaped + NO live claude process, but state=working/blocked + transcript ~2min idle -> A2 skipped every pass 'transcript not idle past stale threshold (600s)'; loop had already exited `done` on uc8n lease-free. Cleaned by hand.

Evidence: references/verb-evolve.md #drain step D (done on lease-free) + step A2 (600s stale bar, applied because state != clean terminal); scripts/evolve_session_cleanup.py (--stale-idle-threshold-secs default 600, the three-signal doneness = lease AND transcript AND bead).

Value: every drain leaks its last launched batch's panels; per-batch toil, recurs every run. Blast radius: 1-2 files (evolve_session_cleanup.py classify and/or the #drain loop-exit prose); involves the destructive stop+rm teardown path so review-adjacent, but no guard FILE.

Fix candidates (judgment, pick at plan): (1) after the loop reads `done`, run ONE final A2 sweep that stops sessions whose doneness is proven by the three independent signals (bead terminal + lease non-live + tempo in {idle,blocked}) regardless of the 600s state-override bar; OR (2) in evolve_session_cleanup, drop the stale-bar requirement when bead-terminal AND lease non-live AND tempo in {idle,blocked} -- those three already prove doneness, so the 600s override (a guard against stopping a still-writing session) is redundant there and only bites the just-finished last batch. Keep the 600s bar for the state-non-terminal-but-bead-still-open case.

title: seam_check role-citation over-captures backticked tokens after 'roles includes'
description: seam_check.prose_role_citations (scripts/seam_check.py:712-725) runs _ROLE_LITERAL_RE.finditer over the whole logical line after the roles-membership anchor, so any backticked/quoted lowercase token later on that line reads as a cited role absent from the registry (false ERROR). Bit flow-c8jp / PR#384 at SKILL.md:144 (worked around by `model`->`model=`). Candidate fixes (pick with judgment): (1) restrict _ROLE_LITERAL_RE at seam_check.py:95 to double/single quotes only, dropping the backtick delimiter, since the idiom always double-quotes the role literal; (2) capture only the token(s) immediately adjacent to the anchor. Add a regression test (a doc line: roles includes "x" then a later backticked lowercase word must NOT flag). corpus delta: not scored — seam_check is not a corpus decider. severity minor.

title: flow: opus plans, sonnet writes — work-subagent model downshift
description:
# /flow model split: opus plans, sonnet writes code

Opt-in per workspace. Downshift the code-writing subagents to sonnet while the session (planning + all judgment/inline stages) stays on the opus-class session model. Sonnet 5 is a strong instruction-follower — with an opus plan in hand it's the better executor; opus stays on every judgment surface.

This edits `SKILL.md` + `stage-registry.toml` (guard files) → it is a HOT change: full lane + guard-property review apply.

## Design (settled via a grilling pass + independent review)

The key realization from review: subagents inherit the session model by default, and in every run-class the session model already equals what a "plan_model" would be. So a `plan`-phase pin is a provable no-op. The ONLY non-redundant effect is downshifting the two code-writing subagents to sonnet on non-tier runs. Build that, nothing more.

### Run-class table

| run_class | session / `--auto` launch | work subagents (implement, review_loop fix) |
|---|---|---|
| normal (untiered) | opus (`[evolve] worker_model`) | sonnet (`work_model`) |
| hot | opus | sonnet |
| `tier:trivial` / `tier:light` | sonnet | sonnet (inherit; no downshift) |

Planning stays on the session model (opus for normal/hot; sonnet for trivial). Only the work subagents drop below it, and only on non-tier runs.

### Config (opt-in)

Add ONE key: `[models] work_model = "sonnet"`.
- `[evolve] worker_model` is unchanged — it stays the session/launch model (opus in this repo).
- `work_model` ABSENT → inherit session everywhere = today's behavior exactly. This is the opt-in: only a workspace that sets `work_model` gets the split. Ships OFF for every other project.
- Set `work_model = "sonnet"` in this repo's `.flow/workspace.toml` to dogfood it.

### Code-writing sites to pin (the only two)

1. `implement` stage — dispatcher-spawned subagent.
2. `review_loop` fix subagent — prose-spawned inside the inline review_loop stage (`references/stage-review_loop.md` §2, "delegate the fix to a subagent the same way implement uses subagent:general-purpose").

`code_review`, `commit`, `create_pr`, `reflect` are inline and do NOT write code (review/mechanical/memory) — leave them on the session model. Confirmed no opus code-writing leak.

## Build (6 edits)

1. `stage-registry.toml`: tag `implement` with `phase = "work"` (or a `code_writing` role — pick one; a dedicated `phase` field is clearer).
2. `dispatch_stage.py`: when building a stage descriptor, set `model = work_model` iff `stage.phase == "work"` AND run is non-tier (not `tier:trivial|light`) AND `[models].work_model` is set; else omit (inherit session). `run_class` comes from the existing tier/lane labels (triage.py lane / tier_policy). Also expose a small `work-model` subcommand (prints the resolved work model or empty) so prose call-sites use the SAME resolver — no duplicated policy.
3. `SKILL.md` do-loop: the subagent-dispatch `Agent()` call passes `model = descriptor.model` when present. One line. SKILL.md stays thin — no model policy baked into prose.
4. `references/stage-review_loop.md` §2: the fix subagent queries `dispatch_stage.py work-model` and pins the spawned Agent to it (same non-tier gate).
5. `evolve_select.py` / `queue_select.py` (+ `references/verb-evolve.md` §C, `references/verb-queue.md`): make the `--auto` launch pass `--model` EXPLICITLY — opus (`worker_model`) for normal/hot, sonnet for trivial/light — instead of omitting it for the hot/default case. This closes a latent risk: an omitted `--model` may inherit the parent orchestrator (which can be Fable per workspace.toml), which would make hot beads' opus safety layer (inline code_review, §6A reviewer, the --auto ship gate — all session-model) fictional. worker_model's VALUE is unchanged.
6. `references/verb-do.md`: ONE-SHOT opus retry. On a work-stage failure in a NON-tier run (where the stage was sonnet-pinned), re-run that stage exactly once with no model pin (→ opus session model) before recording `failed`. Friction-log it. This restores the recovery net that the old whole-run sonnet→opus ladder gave hard beads — needed because under per-subagent pinning a sonnet work-DNF returns control to the live opus session and marks the bead `blocked`/`in_progress` (NOT `open`), so the reappearance-triggered ladder never fires. The existing whole-run ladder stays for trivial (sonnet-LAUNCHED) whole-session DNFs, unchanged.

## Guards

- Revision sub-runs: `cmd_revise_open` (`dispatch_stage.py`) seeds implement + review_loop but stamps no tier/lane → resolve has no `run_class`. Stamp a `run_class` on revision seed (inherit the original bead's class; default normal → sonnet work). Without this, revision work subagents leak to the session model or the resolver crashes.
- `validate_workspace.py`: warn (or refuse) when `implement = "inline"` AND `[models].work_model` is set — an inline stage can't be model-pinned, so its code-writing would silently ride the opus session, violating work=sonnet. Dogfood is safe (`implement = "subagent:general-purpose"`), but the opt-in must not foot-gun other projects.

## Explicitly NOT building (killed as no-ops / net-negative by review)

- `phase = "plan"` tags, a `plan_model` config key, pinning the §6A merge reviewer, a `resolve_model(plan)` path — all no-ops (session already == plan model in every row; subagents inherit session).
- Retiring `[evolve] worker_model` / the config "consolidation" — reintroduces migration scope across verb-evolve/verb-queue/tier_policy for no gain once `plan_model` is dropped.

## Known accepted gap

Interactive planning = the user's session model (plan mode lives in the main thread, can't be a subagent). Launch opus for opus planning; a non-opus attended session plans on that model. The work-subagent downshift still applies interactively (implement → sonnet) when `work_model` is set.

## Acceptance

- `seam_check.py` green; `mise run lint` + `mise run test` green.
- With `work_model="sonnet"` set: a normal/hot `--auto` run launches opus session and its implement + review_loop-fix subagents run sonnet; a `tier:trivial` run stays all-sonnet.
- With `work_model` absent: zero behavior change (all inherit session).
- A forced work-stage failure on a non-tier run triggers exactly one opus retry before `failed`.
- Revision sub-run resolves a `run_class` (no crash, no leak).

title: review_loop stage has no draft-PR awareness; spins to cap on [create_pr] draft=true workspaces
description: review_loop (references/stage-review_loop.md sec3) polls forge.bot_review_present() waiting for CodeRabbit, but CodeRabbit never registers a check on a draft PR (bkt pr checks shows no CodeRabbit line), so bot_review_present() (forge_bitbucket.py ~line 254-274) returns False forever and the wait spins to the documented 10x45s cap every time. Confirmed on FT-1316/PR#2905 (16 polls, then diagnosed via bkt api draft:true). Proposed fix: before the sec3 wait loop, call forge_cli.py pr-info --pr PR_ID and check .draft; if true, skip the wait (CodeRabbit will not run until marked ready), log why, and proceed straight to the (legitimately empty) thread poll. Prose-only fix to stage-review_loop.md sec3, no scripts/*.py change (pr-info + .draft already exposed). Not applied inline because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR (the flow skill checkout) is on protected branch main.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Duplicate of flow-tbnj and flow-rnjb (same finding, same evidfile:8fa49b74a3cd -- recurring: FT-1309/PR#2900, FT-1311/PR#2902, now FT-1316/PR#2905). Dedup-key fuzzy same-file matching didn't converge these into one bead across three separate flow reflect runs -- worth a look separately. This occurrence adds the most concrete fix detail of the three: exact bot_review_present() location (forge_bitbucket.py ~254-274) and the exact pr-info+.draft short-circuit to add in stage-review_loop.md sec3. Leaving open rather than self-closing since consolidating triage is an evolve/maintainer call, not reflect's.

title: review_loop bot-wait wastes full window on draft PR when bot skips drafts

FT-1311/PR#2902 (bitbucket+CodeRabbit): draft PR got 0 comments, 'bkt pr checks' showed only Pipeline (no CodeRabbit check) after ~10min, review-status stayed reviewed:false, run burned the full wait before the §3 CI-green timeout fallback fired. Fix idea: references/stage-review_loop.md §3 detect 'host posts no bot check on a draft' and short-circuit the wait. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

title: Orchestrator inline-implement edits can silently land on the main checkout
description: When the orchestrator runs the implement stage INLINE (not via the implement subagent), Edit/Write with repo-root-absolute paths escape the active worktree and land on the main checkout's working tree; the CC bg-isolation guard only blocks the shared checkout BEFORE EnterWorktree, not absolute writes that escape an active worktree. Silent until a later step (pytest) can't find the file. flow-side mitigation: stage-implement.md + verb-do.md only address subagent edits; add explicit guidance (or a pre-commit guard) that inline orchestrator edits must use worktree-relative Bash paths or worktree-root-absolute paths, never repo-root-absolute. Evidence: references/stage-implement.md:62-64 (subagent-only), references/verb-do.md do-loop step 4d. Confidence: medium; corpus delta: not scored - no concrete script edit yet (guidance/guard, location ambiguous). FT-1214 run 135ef93094ce1ac6, knowledge entry 3b4a8421be8c8b97.

title: implement subagent can't create binary planned_files (xlsx); orchestrator must copy post-implement
description: MACHINERY: binary planned_files entries (e.g. .xlsx templates) can't be produced by the implement subagent (Write/Edit emit text only). Orchestrator must copy them into the worktree post-implement before the diff baseline reconcile. references/stage-implement.md does not document this. Fix: note binary planned_files are orchestrator-copied + have the plan flag binary deliverables. Severity minor. Evidence: FT-1272 copied AR-IVA-2051.xlsx by hand. corpus delta: not scored — references/*.md doc-only edit.

title: review_loop bkt review-threads returns [] for CodeRabbit inline comments, misses Major findings
description: On PR #2867 CodeRabbit posted 2 actionable inline comments (1 Major) in the format '_🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_'; forge_cli bkt review-threads returned [] so review_loop terminated review-clean and missed the Major. resolve-thread/post-reply work against the same comment ids -> the bug is in review-threads parsing of CodeRabbit's current emoji/pipe inline format. Also note: BB comments list endpoint serves stale resolution for minutes; per-comment GET is authoritative.

title: implement subagent spend-limit death tears down worktree, loses uncommitted work
description: When the implement subagent hits a monthly spend limit, the session unwinds and the worktree+branch+uncommitted implement work are destroyed (implement never commits). flow_worktree.py create does not auto-reap the stale fleet lease. Proposals: checkpoint implement work (stash/WIP commit) for recoverability; auto-reap a dead-run_id fleet lease in create. Witnessed on FT-1266.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#409: checkpoint-then-reap per the recorded DECISION (direction c) — dirty orphan teardown captures uncommitted work as a WIP commit pushed no-force to flow-rescue/<key>-<sha> BEFORE any destroy (capture failure = worktree intact, exit 5); flow_worktree.py create auto-reaps a dead sibling gated on under-flock lease re-classification (classify_then; live/corrupt refuses, TOCTOU test pins it); recovery stays human-only fresh-off-origin/main, reap output names the rescue ref. Guard review: (a)-(g) all HELD, 121 tests, seam 0 errors. Deviation (rescue namespace instead of run branch) ACCEPTED with rationale in the DECISION ADDENDUM comment; reviewer Finding 1 (commit-before-push crash window) filed as flow-81xn (hot follow-up).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: this is a design fork needing a maintainer intent decision, not a closeable hole.

Mechanism (inferred from code, NOT confirmed against witnessed evidence — evid file ungreppable, no bead comments; treat as a risk): a pre-PR spend-limit death during implement leaves a dirty orphan worktree (uncommitted implement edits, lease non-live, bead in_progress, no PR). The drain stranded-recovery path (queue_drain/evolve_drain §Recover) then tears the dirty worktree down via `flow_worktree.py reap` and relaunches FRESH off origin/main — it deliberately does NOT do-resume (flow-mmh3: do-resume re-dies at implement entry). The uncommitted work is destroyed in that teardown. A MANUAL relaunch (`/flow KEY --auto` by hand, no drain) instead errors at `git worktree add -b` on the pre-existing branch+worktree, because _assert_no_live_sibling proceeds past an expired lease but the worktree-add then collides.

Premise correction: the bead says create does not auto-reap a stale "fleet lease". But create does NOT consult fleet at all — it classifies the per-ticket RUN lease (run.lock) in sibling worktrees. The real blocker is (a) the ~900s live-lease window, then (b) the worktree/branch collision at git worktree add. No fleet check exists in create.

The fork (why it cannot self-approve): the two proposals conflict and both fight the flow-mmh3 design.
1. Proposal 2 (auto-reap dead sibling in create) DESTROYS the uncommitted work that Proposal 1 (checkpoint) wants to save. Shipping 2 alone makes the stated problem strictly worse (auto-destroyed on relaunch instead of erroring + giving a human a rescue window).
2. Checkpoint (proposal 1) only buys recoverability if the WIP is PUSHED to remote — a local stash/commit dies with the reap branch -D — and even pushed, the work is human-recovery-only because the automated path relaunches fresh and will not reuse it.

Decision needed from the maintainer: which direction?
(a) unblock-only — make relaunch not error/not get blocked (auto-reap dead sibling), accept work loss;
(b) preserve the work — and if so, push-WIP-to-remote vs local-only;
(c) both with a defined ordering (checkpoint-then-reap so capture precedes destroy).

This touches the load-bearing teardown/lease machinery (CLAUDE.md: never simplify the safety machinery), so it is a hot change as well. To unstick: pick a direction here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively. [defer-reason: open-question]

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: direction (c) — checkpoint-then-reap, capture strictly precedes destroy. Binding constraints for the run:
1. CHECKPOINT FIRST: before any destructive teardown of a dirty orphan worktree (drain stranded-recovery reap AND any new auto-reap path), capture uncommitted implement work as a WIP commit on the run branch and PUSH it to remote explicitly (git push origin refs/heads/<branch> — sandboxed worktrees silently fail to record upstream, so never rely on -u/@{u}). A local-only stash/commit dies with branch -D and does not count as capture.
2. THEN UNBLOCK: after capture succeeds (or there is provably nothing to capture: clean tree), auto-reap the dead sibling in flow_worktree.py create so a manual relaunch no longer errors at git worktree add. Gate strictly on non-live run-lease classification via the existing lease.classify_then seam; live/corrupt still refuses. Capture failure = leave worktree intact and error out (fail toward preserving work).
3. RECOVERY MODEL: human-only rescue. The automated relaunch path stays fresh-off-origin/main (do NOT fight flow-mmh3 no-do-resume); the pushed WIP branch is a rescue artifact — reap output must name it.
4. HOT: touches load-bearing teardown/lease machinery — hot label added; merge-time guard-property review + CI gate apply; never weaken the classify/quarantine seams.
Reopened for an --auto run under adjudicate_hot (decision recorded per triage decision-ingest).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION ADDENDUM (hot-merge gate sign-off, 2026-07-02): the reviewer-flagged deviation is ACCEPTED — the rescue ref is flow-rescue/<key>-<sha>, not the run branch feat/<key> named in the original decision text. Rationale: the decision's binding property is durable REMOTE capture before destroy; pushing WIP onto feat/<key> itself would non-ff-reject a fresh relaunch's create_pr push, so the dedicated rescue namespace preserves the property AND removes a relaunch failure mode. FLOW_KEY_RE / is_inflight verified blind to flow-rescue/* (a rescue ref cannot mark the bead in-flight). Finding 1 (crash window between checkpoint commit and rescue push orphans the WIP on the next reap) filed as a follow-up bead rather than folded in: the clean fix must distinguish checkpoint commits from squash-merged orphan commits and deserves its own reviewed run, not an unreviewed rider on this hot merge.

title: new: offer to kick off pipeline after creating a ticket
description: After `/flow new` creates a ticket, flow should ask the author whether to kick off the pipeline (`/flow <KEY>`) right away. Most of the time that is the intended next step, so an explicit prompt removes a manual step. Current behavior (verb-new Step 6) is deliberately no-prompt; this ticket requests changing that to an opt-in offer.

title: Plan phase: investigate to dissolve forks before surfacing questions to user
description:
During the spec/plan phase, run investigation (e.g. /investigate) to narrow down assumptions and de-fork as much as possible BEFORE surfacing questions to the user.

Problem: questions are frequently raised which, after careful investigation, dissolve entirely or become much simpler/clearer. This burdens the user with decisions the machine could resolve itself.

Goal: spoon-feed the user as much as possible. Surface ONLY real tensions that survive careful read-only investigation. Everything resolvable by digging should be resolved before asking.
close_reason: Shipped in PR#374 (merged to main 2026-06-23+): de-fork-before-ask prose live in SKILL.md gate section, references/verb-spec.md step 4, and references/stage-plan.md — plan phase now dissolves forks via read-only investigation before surfacing questions. Verified against origin/main fab9f45.

title: Consolidate jira-create into /flow new (rich ticket creation via REST adapter)
description:
Fold the loose ~/.claude/skills/jira-create orphan into /flow's existing `new` verb. Enrich `new` to full ticket authoring, route through flow's REST adapter (tracker_jira), retire the orphan.

## Direction (decided via grilling)
- Write path = flow's tracker_jira REST adapter, NOT the Atlassian MCP. Reasons: headless/bg/--auto-safe (claude.ai MCP absent headless), keeps the one-Jira-client seam invariant, sidesteps jira-create's sprint-edit assignee-clobber bug (adapter's agile POST /sprint/{id}/issue doesn't touch other fields), tracker-agnostic (beads degrades via capability table).
- `new` becomes FULL authoring (not thin-with-optional): all org fields populated, friction cut by infer/default not omission. Jira = fields matter; beads degrades.

## Per-field sourcing
- summary: ASK (branch/context suggestions)
- description: ASK (conditional humanize pass)
- type: INFER (default Task, bump Bug/Story on signal, preview-correctable)
- epic/parent: ASK (single-select active epics; auto if only one; None available)
- sprint: ASK Y/N, default YES
- assignee: DEFAULT self (config assignee_account_id), override in preview/--assignee
- status: DEFAULT To Do
- labels: NONE (exempt from must-fill; avoid label sprawl)
Interactive surface = summary/description/epic/sprint(Y/N). Rest silent + Step-4 preview gate (Create/Edit/Cancel) as the single correction point.

## Builds
- tracker_jira.py: list_issue_types() (with hierarchyLevel -> type vocab + epic-type), list_epics() (JQL active hierarchy-1)
- tracker_beads.py: list_epics->[], list_issue_types->bead types (sprint already gated by capability table)
- tracker_cli.py: expose list-types / list-epics subcommands (+ seam_check)
- verb-new.md: rewrite 17-line stub -> full flow (infer type, ask summary/desc/epic/sprint, preview, create, post-create transition To Do + set_sprint, conditional humanize, handoff Next: /flow <KEY>)

## NOT touched
- create() signature already sufficient (summary/description/type/parent/label/assignee; sprint+status are existing post-create ops set_sprint/transition)
- SKILL.md router: `new` already wired -> NOT a guard-file/hot change -> normal lane
- assignee /myself fallback: deferred (config carries account id)

## Deferred
- multi-ticket batch creation
- /myself self-resolution fallback

## Tests
- adapter list_issue_types/list_epics units (inject fake http)
- tracker_cli subcommand tests
- seam_check green after verb-new.md edits

## Retire
- delete ~/.claude/skills/jira-create AFTER `new` ships + verified (nothing upstream invokes it; jira-workflow is downstream)

title: verb-spec step 7: warn against MAIN-checkout-absolute paths in bg --auto edits
description: MACHINERY (minor): in a bg --auto run with cwd inside the worktree, the orchestrator's Edit against an absolute MAIN-checkout path silently writes to the main checkout (the bg-isolation guard blocks worktree Edits but NOT main-checkout writes). flow-cjgy stage docs were Read from main-checkout absolute paths, then an Edit reused one and polluted main (recovered via git restore + Bash re-apply). references/verb-spec.md:110 documents the Bash/heredoc fallback for bg runs but does not explicitly warn against main-checkout-absolute paths. Fix: add a one-line caution to step 7 'edit only worktree-relative or worktree-absolute paths; never a main-checkout absolute path (it bypasses the isolation guard).' corpus delta: not scored — prose-only references/*.md edit (cannot move the deciders).

title: Transient false-positive engine drift in dispatch_stage advance
description: MACHINERY: dispatch_stage.py cmd_advance returned 'config/version drift mid-run' (detail: 'engine') after code_review advance, but recover.py detect immediately showed snapshot.ok:true and a standalone dispatch_stage.py next call succeeded with no drift. Witnessed on flow-quuz (PR#363, doc-only run, no engine files touched). Friction id e33ea77b. Likely cause: race between the test suite (mise run test invoked by the implement subagent) reading skill files and the snapshot hash comparison in cmd_next running concurrently, producing a transient mismatch. The advance call finishes the prior stage (cmd_finish) then calls cmd_next which calls _gate_drift which calls classify_drift; if the test runner reads a skill file during that hash walk, the OS page-cache inconsistency could produce a transient bad hash. Fix candidate: snapshot.py's file hashing should use O_NOATIME or a short retry on unexpected mismatch; or the test suite run should complete (and all child processes exit) before advance is called.
close_reason: duplicate of flow-p9sc (same 'drift: engine' dispatch_stage false-positive, evidfile ea5809c4cef7); transient-race evidence merged into flow-p9sc

title: engine drift 'drift: engine' trips spuriously on lagging-main between bootstrap and advance
description: dispatch_stage next/advance exits 1 with 'drift: engine' when origin/main gains a commit between the bootstrap snapshot (taken at worktree create) and a later next/advance call — a lagging-main trip, not a real engine modification conflict. Recurred twice in flow-79mi (after create_pr and review_loop). Fix: recover.py reload-snapshot. Root cause: snapshot.py engine-component hash compared live; a sibling merge shifts the hash. A tolerance window, a time-bounded skip on the engine component, or auto-reload-on-drift in dispatch_stage.py would remove the manual reload step. Known from MEMORY.md 'engine-drift-on-planned-engine-file-edits' note (cites 'residual lagging-main trip = flow-mozu, unfiled'; flow-mozu is a closed unrelated bead).\n\nEvidence: flow-79mi run, 2026-06-22 (2 occurrences same run). Knowledge entry id: e471516ed31d8b8b.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-22: fix via recover.py reload-snapshot / re-anchor only. Do NOT add a tolerance window or time-bounded skip to the snapshot engine-component hash — that drops drift-detection and re-opens the flow-2pp TOCTOU (the reason flow-s9w was deferred STALE). Snapshot drift-detection must be preserved as a guard property; replace it only with one that provably still holds, never drop it.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DUP-MERGE from flow-rpme (same 'drift: engine' false-positive, evidfile ea5809c4cef7): second mechanism witnessed on flow-quuz (PR#363, doc-only run, NO engine files touched), friction id e33ea77b — recover.py detect immediately showed snapshot.ok:true and a standalone next succeeded, i.e. a TRANSIENT mismatch (likely the implement subagent's 'mise run test' reading skill files concurrently with cmd_next's snapshot hash compare), not just lagging-main. The decided recover.py reload-snapshot / re-anchor fix must heal BOTH the lagging-main shift AND this transient concurrent-read race (re-verify on drift, don't hard exit-1), while preserving drift-detection.

title: recall_usage cli_main exit codes + _run_id_started_at no-state raise are untested
description:
TEST GAP (both probes clean-empty against the live 2249-test suite, verified absent at origin/main). recall_usage.py cli_main (recall_usage.py:331) maps FileNotFoundError→3, LockContention→2, _MemoryConfigError→4, OSError→4 and is never invoked in tests; _run_id_started_at (recall_usage.py:143, raises FileNotFoundError('no usable state.json') at :148) is never triggered. recall_usage is wired into the do-loop prose seam (record-usage|detect-misses) and the 4 exit codes are a documented contract — argparse + try/except dispatch are exactly the seam unit tests miss.
Probes (from scripts/):
- probe1: grep -rn 'recall_usage.cli_main|from recall_usage' tests/ → empty; grep -rn '_run_id_started_at|no usable state.json' tests/ → empty
- probe2: pytest tests/ --collect-only -q → 2249 collected (non-zero, valid); grep for fastembed/cli_main/started_at node ids → empty
Fix: one test calling recall_usage.cli_main(['record-usage', ...]) against a ticket_dir with no state.json asserts rc==3, covering both the cli error-mapping and the _run_id_started_at raise. Value: documented exit-code contract callers branch on is unexercised. Blast radius: test-only, behavior-preserving.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SONNET-LADDER: sonnet-attempt-1

title: check_ownership drops the unowned source of a staged rename (gate fails to refuse)
description: ROBUSTNESS TIGHTEN (verified at origin/main). diff_extract.py check_ownership parses `git status --porcelain` and for a rename token `R old -> new` keeps ONLY the destination: `token = token.split(' -> ', 1)[1]` (diff_extract.py:425-426). So if the implement agent runs `git mv unowned_src owned_planned_dst`, the porcelain line is `R unowned_src -> owned_planned_dst`; the unowned SOURCE deletion is dropped from `changed` and the gate returns ok:true. A plain staged deletion `D unowned_src` emits its own porcelain line and IS caught; only the combined rename form leaks. Fix = also collect the source side of an `R old -> new` token into `changed` (it is an unowned deletion). Value: the ownership tripwire fails to refuse an out-of-scope rename. Blast radius: LOW / worktree-confined: the committed patch comes from implement.diff scoped to planned_files, so stray source-deletion never reaches the commit/PR; the gap is that the tripwire does not refuse, not that unowned content ships. TIGHTEN never erode. diff_extract.py is a guard file → hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — auto-ship the rename-source ownership tighten. Both axes cleared: (1) correctness — fix collects the unowned SOURCE side of a porcelain 'R old -> new' token, routing BOTH endpoints through the existing _unquote_porcelain_path + .flow/.claude exclusion (NOT a naive raw append, which would regress quoting + the exclusions); (2) safe to auto-ship — reversible, maintainer self-target, CI + merge-time guard-property review downstream, no downstream consumer of 'changed' expects destinations-only, and the new false-positive surface (an unplanned rename-source now returns ok:False) is a RECOVERABLE reconcile cycle: exit 3 is a clean refusal, the post-implement reconcile widens planned_files to include a legit rename source and implement agents name such files in their report so reconcile widens before the gate runs. Mandatory implementer commitment: amend test_check_ownership_renamed_planned_file_with_space to plan BOTH endpoints (stays ok:True) AND add a new test proving an unowned rename-source now refuses (ok:False), plus a .flow/-internal rename-exclusion guard test. planned_files = diff_extract.py + tests/test_diff_extract.py.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Fix is correct and the only coherent one: check_ownership is a filename-level gate on planned_files membership, so a rename whose source is not planned MUST flag (cannot distinguish leak from incomplete-plan rename; ticket contract refuses both). Collect BOTH sides of an 'R old -> new' porcelain token into changed (source = unowned deletion). Safe to auto-ship: blast LOW/worktree-confined (committed patch is scoped to planned_files via implement.diff; stray source-deletion never reaches commit/PR), reversible, CI-gated, hot guard-review at merge. comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) file trace-mine failure-signature findings as beads labelled ["evolve","proposal","trace-mined"] via flow_beads_create.create_bead, dedup_key = the child-2 signature key with '::' replaced by ':'. Rationale: this is the literal ticket reading — the evolve label pulls them into 'bd ready -l evolve' (the 'existing evolve drain consumer picks them up'), while the proposal label makes evolve_select.active() exclude them from the launchable set, so they NEVER auto-implement, with ZERO new consumer code (the exclusion filter already exists). Verified safe against evolve_select.py. evolve_select's 'mislabeled' comment targets generative feature-proposals (§propose), not this deliberate failure-mode-evidence use; considered proposal-only (convention) but it would silently drop the ticket's explicit evolve/drain-pickup requirement. trace-mined is additive provenance (distinguishes these from feature proposals; child-4 scheduling handle). The '::'->':' strip is load-bearing: the raw '::' key would trigger create_bead's fuzzy same-file dedup (Jaccard>=0.45) and wrongly collapse distinct signatures (anchorless stall_gap in implement vs commit -> 0.5; same-file tool_error vs drift on dispatch_stage.py -> 0.571); the strip keeps an identical exact evid: fingerprint while skipping the fuzzy pass. Reversible: dropping 'evolve' to file proposal-only is a one-label edit if the maintainer meant a cleaner proposal-backlog placement.

title: drift: engine fires on mid-run main-checkout engine-tree mutation (plugin auto-update / reflect-trailing) — pin snapshot or auto-reconcile?
description:
SUCCESSOR to flow-hsvw + flow-umvf (both closed not-reproducible-as-framed; staleness pass 2026-06-19).

REAL TRIGGER (verified, code-confirmed against snapshot.py this pass): snapshot._engine_component hashes ONLY the MAIN checkout tree under plugins/flow/skills/flow with suffixes .py/.sh/.md/.toml (engine_root = main_root / rel; resolved via 'git worktree list' first stanza). So:
- flow-hsvw FALSE PREMISE: a version-only bump (plugin.json + marketplace.json) cannot trip drift:engine — both files are excluded twice (wrong dir AND .json not hashed).
- flow-umvf FALSE PREMISE: static lag of the local main checkout alone trips nothing — the component hashes whatever main HEAD is, consistently, snapshot vs re-hash.

What DID fire drift:engine on FT-1226 and FT-1233 is the same single event under two wrong labels: the main-checkout engine TREE mutating mid-run. A flow plugin auto-update (e.g. 0.56->0.57) git-pulls real engine .py/.md changes (not just version json) into the local main checkout DURING a long implement/reflect stage; the next advance re-hashes a different main tree -> master-hash mismatch -> drift:engine -> forces recover --reload-snapshot. This is fail-closed-on-engine-swap behaving as designed.

DESIGN QUESTION (maintainer call): should a run be insulated from a mid-run main-checkout engine mutation it did not author? Options:
(1) pin the engine snapshot to the run's start-of-run main SHA (read engine tree at a fixed ref, not live worktree), so a benign mid-run plugin update auto-reconciles instead of aborting;
(2) auto-reconcile reflect-trailing-drift specifically (reflect mutates engine files by design; the trailing drift on the NEXT advance is self-inflicted and could reload-snapshot silently);
(3) leave fail-closed (status quo) and just document the recover --reload-snapshot recovery.

BLAST RADIUS: snapshot.py (_engine_component ref-pinning) and/or recover + reflect wiring. Guard-file territory -> is_hot. Do NOT self-edit mid-run.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Option 3 — leave engine-component drift fail-closed; document the recover reload-snapshot recovery + lock the decision with a test. Option 1 (pin engine hash to start-of-run SHA) REJECTED: snapshot.py:135-140 documents _skill_root_from_script() is bistable mid-run (do-loop invokes engine via the main-checkout absolute path OR the worktree copy). When invoked via the main checkout (SKILL.md's ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} absolute path), a mid-run git pull advancing main changes the EXECUTING engine code; pinning the hash to the start-of-run SHA would hash the old tree while new code runs, blinding the guard to exactly the swap flow-2pp exists to catch — erodes the canonical-snapshot TOCTOU guard (CLAUDE.md invariant). Option 2 (auto-reconcile reflect-trailing-drift) REJECTED: near-false premise — machinery_edit refuses self-edits on protected branches (exit 2) and reflect edits the worktree not the main checkout, while the engine component only hashes the main checkout on a protected branch, so engine reflect-trailing drift mostly cannot occur. Option 3: fail-closed IS correct for a mid-run engine swap; recovery is one recover reload-snapshot. Real gaps closed: (a) engine recovery documented only in self-evolution.md:59, missing from the drift-recovery enumerations in verb-recover.md + verb-do.md; (b) no test-of-record asserting engine drift -> cmd_next exit 1, never owned-reconciled. Scope: 1 test in test_dispatch_stage.py + engine row in verb-recover.md + verb-do.md. No snapshot.py/dispatch_stage.py change. Safe to auto-ship: zero runtime-behavior change, reversible, CI (pytest + seam_check) covers it.

title: Faceted knowledge memory: optional labels[] on entries + label-scoped recall (e.g. form:iva_2083)
description:
WHY: recall ranks the whole namespace by relevance and truncates at recall_top_n (default 5). There is no way to say 'load EVERYTHING about subject X'. Concrete need (brinta-data-platform): work clusters by tax FORM (iva_2083, F.2051, etc). When starting work on the 2083 form the agent should recall the full 2083 knowledge cluster and feel already-familiar -- a relevance-ranked top-5 cannot guarantee that. Semantic retrieval (sibling bead) helps fuzzy reach but is still probabilistic + top-N capped; a label gives DETERMINISTIC exhaustive cluster retrieval.

WHAT (generic engine machinery):
- add optional 'labels': ["facet:value", ...] array to knowledge entries (memory_append.py). ADDITIVE, like supersedes/ts: NOT part of the dedup id formula (id = sha256(namespace+ticket+type+body)), metadata only.
- reflect stage writes labels when a run's knowledge belongs to a facet.
- recall.py: add a --label exact-filter (mirror the existing branch_filter / ticket_filters at recall.py:163) AND a labels field weight in BM25; '--label form:iva_2083' returns the whole cluster (consider bypassing/raising top_n for an explicit label query so the cluster is not truncated).
- recall_by gains a 'labels'/facet dimension so SessionStart can auto-recall the active facet.

WHAT (project-specific guidance, the second half of the ask):
- a per-project knowledge-organization convention telling reflect WHICH facet to tag. For data-platform: facet=form, infer the form from the branch slug / changed paths (e.g. iva_2083). Land this as workspace.toml [memory] config (e.g. label_facets=["form"]) and/or a project knowledge-org tips doc reflect reads, NOT hardcoded in the engine.

NOTE: 'feel familiar with the WHOLE form' may want a summarized form-card (digest of the cluster), not just N raw entries -- flag for spec.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#412 (salvaged from the spend-limit-killed run: its implement commit d92d22c + my review-fix c88a368): faceted knowledge memory — memory_append --labels (CSV facet:value, key absent when empty, mirrors bd-json convention), recall --label hard pre-filter with exhaustive retrieval (top-n lifted, query optional), [memory] label_facets config seam (ships empty here).
