Review NEEDS-FIX findings fixed pre-merge: label-only recall no longer reads stdin (live Bash pipe wedged; must-not-read pin test), sweep apply/apply-cluster carry labels forward (canonical = union of member labels), verb-recall.md --label stanza. Full suite 2557 green, seam 223/45. Follow-ups filed by the run: flow-14zj (digest mode, decided), flow-lw35 (brinta adoption).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Ship the generic engine machinery + label_facets config seam; defer the two soft parts as follow-up beads. WHICH: (1) additive optional labels[] on knowledge entries (memory_append.py, mirrors supersedes: NOT in dedup id); (2) recall.py --label facet:value as a HARD exhaustive cluster filter threaded through BOTH rank() and _semantic_rank(), plus labels in FIELD_WEIGHTS + labels passthrough; (3) [memory] label_facets config seam (validate_workspace optional list[str] + reflect_inputs bundle key + init template default []) + additive stage-reflect.md tagging prose. NOT extending recall_by (vestigial: SessionStart no longer recalls; only validate+init touch it) -- that sub-ask is moot. label_facets ships EMPTY in this repo (no natural facet), so behavior is inert here; plumbing is unit-tested. WHY SAFE TO AUTO-SHIP: non-hot (is_hot=false on real planned_files), reversible, CI-covered by direct unit tests. The only change touching the existing default recall path is adding 'labels' to FIELD_WEIGHTS, which is provably byte-identical for label-free corpora (advisor-verified 3 sub-claims: avgdl=0 zero-contribution; the no-query guard change is a no-op when label_filter is None; the _doc_field_text list-join fires only on list values) -- and no entry carries a label until label_facets is non-empty, which it is not here. MANDATORY IMPLEMENTER COMMITMENTS (survive relaunch): (a) file 2 follow-up beads before/at implement -- form-card digest (flagged for-spec) and brinta-data-platform label_facets=['form']+facet-inference-tips convention; (b) stage-reflect.md 3b must carry a superseded entry's labels FORWARD onto the superseding entry (else the exhaustive cluster silently shrinks); (c) add a positive test_init.py assertion for label_facets=[] in the [memory] template. Full lane, direct-unit-test coverage (reflect prose does not govern this run).

title: test_cli_skip_when_not_self_target is env-coupled: leaks ~/.flow/config.toml maintainer pointer
description: tests/test_evolve_self_merge.py:270 test_cli_skip_when_not_self_target sets self_target=False (the tmp workspace.toml carries no [maintainer] self_target marker) and expects is_maintainer(tmp)->False->action 'skip'. But resolve_maintainer_repo (maintainer.py:48-65) has a SECOND resolution path: when the workspace lacks the committed marker it falls back to the global ~/.flow/config.toml [maintainer] repo_root pointer. On a maintainer's machine that pointer resolves to the real flow repo (which carries self_target=true), so is_maintainer(tmp) returns True even for a marker-less tmp workspace -> cli_main emits action 'merge' -> test fails 'merge' == 'skip'. Passes in CI (no global config / no self_target pointer) but persistently RED in any local 'mise run test' on the maintainer machine, masking real regressions in that test. Same class as flow-cfj8 (machine-level probe leaking into a unit test). Fix: isolate the global-config probe in the affected cli_main test(s) -- monkeypatch maintainer._global_config_path to a non-existent tmp path (only test_cli_skip_when_not_self_target reaches the fallback; the self_target=True tests short-circuit on the workspace marker). Production code is correct; this is test isolation only. Discovered during flow-0xex (lease TTL) where mise run test showed 2153 passed / 1 pre-existing failure verified red on the clean base. corpus delta: not scored -- test-file edit, not a decider script.

title: flow_worktree bootstrap leaks worktree+branch on NON-deliberate exception in the flock block
description: flow-n2a6/PR#341 fixed the gitignored-planned-file REFUSAL site to delete the orphan branch. But the broader gap remains: a non-deliberate exception later in the 'with _locking.flock_blocking(...)' block in flow_worktree.bootstrap (e.g. _seed_state, ticket_frontmatter.update, _copy_config, mise trust raising) leaves the 'git worktree add -b'-created worktree AND branch uncleaned, since cleanup is only wired into the explicit refusal sites. Fix: wrap the flock body in a try/except that on any exception removes the worktree and deletes the branch before re-raising. Out of scope for the P2 one-line flow-n2a6; filed per advisor adjudication. flow_worktree.py is a guard/hot file. corpus delta: not scored - no concrete edit yet.
close_reason: merged via PR #346 (hot auto-merge, guard-property review passed)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — wrap the flow_worktree.bootstrap flock body (after 'git worktree add') in try/except Exception that runs 'git worktree remove --force' then 'git branch -D <branch>' (best-effort run, not _git) before re-raising, and drop the now-redundant inline cleanup at the gitignored-refusal site (keep its raise _ConfigError). Narrow blast radius (one function, error-paths only, happy path unchanged), reversible single-PR revert, CI-covered; hot guard file but adjudicate_hot on + merge-time guard-property review backstops. Cleanup order load-bearing: worktree remove BEFORE branch -D. Bare 'raise' to preserve type/exit-code mapping. Add a mid-body-raise test (rev-parse rc=1 -> _GitError, and a direct raise on mise trust) asserting both cleanup calls fire + propagation.

title: review_loop/ship-it can wait on CodeRabbit that does not review a DRAFT PR
description: On FT-1123, waiting on CodeRabbit cost ~33min on a DRAFT PR that CR never reviewed (an explicit @coderabbitai nudge was ignored); the guidance (references/feedback.md) only had a generic 'reviewer never appears' note. Fix direction: detect draft-PR + no-review and either mark ready or time-box the CR wait. NOTE: possibly stale/resolved -- on FT-1233 CodeRabbit DID review draft PR #2820 (SUCCESSFUL); maintainer should verify whether CR-on-drafts is now enabled for this repo before acting. Recorded user-mode on FT-1123 (knowledge 3a18cb3cd0a6e34e).
close_reason: misrouted (fix lives in claude-skills ship-it, not flow) + likely stale (CR now reviews drafts); re-file in claude-skills if it recurs

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: this bead is likely misrouted AND possibly stale.

1. Misrouted (the bigger issue). The fix target is the ship-it skill's references/feedback.md (the CodeRabbit-wait guidance) and its draft-PR detection. ship-it lives in the claude-skills repo (~/repos/personal/claude-skills, vdsmon-skills marketplace), NOT this flow repo — confirmed via git ls-files + find (empty here). A flow --auto run PRs only into flow, so it cannot deliver this fix. flow's own review_loop (stage-review_loop.md) does NOT have this bug: §3 polls review-threads once and proceeds on CI-green if no threads appear; it never hangs waiting for CodeRabbit, and flow deliberately delivers DRAFT PRs (draft-PR-as-deliverable), so the 'mark ready' fix direction contradicts flow's model. Net: there is no flow-side change to make. Decide close-as-out-of-scope vs re-file in claude-skills against ship-it.

2. Stale. Per the bead NOTE, on FT-1233 CodeRabbit DID review draft PR #2820 successfully — CR-on-drafts may already be enabled, making the premise moot. Verifying this needs maintainer-only access to the brinta Bitbucket repo (unreachable from the flow repo). 

To unstick: confirm whether CR-on-drafts is enabled, decide the routing (close here / re-file in claude-skills), reopen (status->open), and act in the correct repo. [defer-reason: open-question]

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: STALENESS PASS 2026-06-19: closing wontfix-HERE (misrouted). Fix target is the ship-it skill's references/feedback.md (CodeRabbit-wait guidance + draft-PR detection), which lives in ~/repos/personal/claude-skills (vdsmon-skills marketplace), NOT this flow repo — a flow self-target run cannot deliver it. Also likely stale: CR DID review draft PR #2820 on FT-1233. Re-file in claude-skills if the draft-wait still bites after confirming CR-on-drafts is enabled for that repo.

title: recover takeover cannot clear a stale lease whose holder PID is provably dead before wall-clock expiry
description: lease.classify (scripts/lease.py) keys state purely on lease_expires_at vs now (returns 'live' before expiry); the holder pid is informational only and never used for liveness. So a recover takeover cannot reclaim a lease whose holder process is provably dead until the ~36min TTL elapses. Fix direction: let classify treat a dead holder pid (same host) as reclaimable before expiry. Recorded user-mode on FT-1178 (knowledge b7d095f896d9f45b). Related to the lease-TTL/heartbeat bead (lease.py::ttl-shorter-than-long-subagent-stage).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: the fix direction as written is unsafe on a load-bearing invariant, and the legitimate need maps to a UX choice only the maintainer can make.

Finding (primary-source, advisor-confirmed):
- lease.classify keys 'live' on lease_expires_at vs now. The recorded holder pid is the EPHEMERAL dispatch-subprocess pid (lease.py:621 pid=os.getpid(), rewritten on every init/next/finish/release), not a long-lived process. The docstring (lease.py:5-11) states this directly: dispatch runs as short subprocesses that exit immediately, so a perfectly LIVE run has a 'provably dead' holder pid between every dispatch call. That is exactly why pid is documented 'informational only; never used for liveness gating.'
- Auto-classifying a dead holder pid as reclaimable in classify would let a recover takeover steal a live run's lease in the gap between dispatch calls. Those gaps run 20-40+ min during CI re-waits (flow-72d9), and the TTL exists precisely to span them. This reopens the flow-72d9 / flow-d4s blind window. Hot, property-eroding.

Capability check:
- recover abort --force releases a live-looking lease before expiry, but it is release/abandon only (no stage reset). 
- recover takeover (reclaim + reset in_progress->pending + snapshot = resume) calls lease.takeover_clear WITHOUT force, so it refuses a not-yet-expired lease (recover.py:91-99). So operator-explicit 'reclaim-before-expiry-AND-resume' genuinely does not exist today — a real ergonomic gap.

Maintainer fork (pick one, then reopen + re-run WITHOUT --auto):
1. abort --force + re-run /flow do is enough -> close as wontfix.
2. Add operator-explicit --force on recover takeover (mirrors abort --force; small, SAFE — a human asserts deadness, no automatic classify change). Recommended.
3. You actually want AUTOMATIC pid-death detection -> requires re-architecting 'pid' from the ephemeral dispatch pid to a long-lived SESSION pid (substantial, fragile, harness-specific). Only this option auto-clears without operator action.

The ticket asked for #3 (automatic). #2 is the safe realization but is a different UX than requested, so an --auto run must not silently ship it. To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively. [defer-reason: open-question]

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-19: option 2 — add operator-explicit --force to 'recover takeover' (mirrors 'recover abort --force'). A human asserts holder deadness; NO automatic classify change (rejects option 3's ephemeral->session-pid re-architecture and the wontfix). Scope: takeover_clear gains a force path that bypasses the not-yet-expired refusal (recover.py:91-99) ONLY under the explicit flag, reclaim+reset in_progress->pending+snapshot=resume as today. lease.classify stays untouched (no auto pid-liveness). Hot (recover+lease guard-adjacent) -> ships via merge-time guard review, not auto. Reopening to open.

title: tracker_cli comment defaults to markdown; Jira adapter rejects it and there is no --fmt flag
description: tracker_cli comment defaults to markdown content; the Jira adapter rejects it ('markdown not supported by Jira adapter; use Content.fmt=adf or plain') and the comment subcommand exposes no --fmt/--format flag. The MCP fallback (addCommentToJiraIssue) is blocked by the write-safety classifier for a ticket not created in-session. Minor. Fix: add --fmt {plain,adf,markdown} to tracker_cli comment (or default the Jira adapter to plain). Recorded user-mode on FT-1226 (knowledge 35e66cb3e845bc67).

title: version-only plugin bump mid-run trips 'drift: engine' and forces recover --reload-snapshot
description: A flow plugin auto-update (0.56.0->0.57.0) landed DURING a long implement stage; the next advance tripped 'drift: engine' and forced recover --reload-snapshot. The engine-version component of the canonical snapshot treats a benign version-only bump identically to a real workspace.toml/stage-registry change. Fix direction: distinguish a version-only delta (no semantic config/registry change) from real drift so a mid-run bump auto-reconciles like owned drift. Recorded user-mode on FT-1226 (knowledge 43d0baf5232f1d20). NOTE: same class recurred on FT-1233 (trailing 'drift: engine' after reflect).
close_reason: false premise (code-confirmed): version-only bump excluded from engine tree_hash; real mid-run-engine-mutation trigger re-filed as flow-qt2s

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: the premise does not hold and the only behavior-changing fix is constrained out — needs a maintainer design call.

FALSE PREMISE (verified this run): a genuinely version-only bump CANNOT trip 'drift: engine'. version.py stamp touches only plugin.json (plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/) and marketplace.json (root .claude-plugin/) — both live OUTSIDE plugins/flow/skills/flow, so neither is in the engine tree_hash (snapshot._engine_component hashes git ls-files under rel=plugins/flow/skills/flow, .py/.sh/.md/.toml, stage-registry excluded). A main-HEAD advance that is purely a stamp commit changes no hashed file -> no drift. The 0.56.0->0.57.0 update tripped the guard only because that release carried real .py/.md/.toml code. That is a TRUE positive w.r.t. the engine code the running session loads, a false positive only w.r.t. the run's frozen content. There is nothing to 'distinguish' at the tree_hash level: by the time the engine tree_hash differs, real code differs.

SAME MECHANISM AS flow-umvf (shared evidfile:a3274359a7e4): the engine guard anchors on the LOCAL MAIN CHECKOUT (a moving target); 'drift: engine' fires when local main HEAD moves between snapshot-write and a later next (git pull / marketplace auto-update / mid-batch advance). flow-umvf's analysis reached the identical conclusion.

FIX DIRECTION IS CONSTRAINED OUT: 'auto-reconcile a version-only delta like owned drift' is exactly what flow-s9w forbids — making the engine component owned-reconcilable re-opens the flow-2pp TOCTOU hole (flow-2pp anchors on the main checkout precisely to catch mid-run marketplace swaps, since the running session loads engine code from the main checkout). CLAUDE.md marks the canonical-snapshot TOCTOU guard as load-bearing safety machinery, never to be simplified away. adjudicate_hot lets a hot change ship; it does not license reopening a deliberately-closed safety invariant.

REALISTIC RE-SCOPE (maintainer call): flow-umvf option (b) — snapshot records the engine basis SHA, init/next detect a benign forward fast-forward of the main checkout and emit an actionable 'flow plugin updated mid-run -> run /flow recover --reload-snapshot' message instead of a generic drift abort. Still messaging-only (does not prevent the halt), still edits hot guard files (snapshot.py + dispatch_stage.py), so it needs a deliberate decision that the clearer abort is worth a hot PR.

To unstick: decide the disposition (close as dup/invalid of flow-umvf, OR re-scope to the messaging-only option b), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively. [defer-reason: open-question]

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: STALENESS PASS 2026-06-19: closing not-reproducible-as-framed. Version-only bump CANNOT trip drift:engine (snapshot._engine_component hashes only plugins/flow/skills/flow **/*.py/.sh/.md/.toml on the MAIN checkout; plugin.json+marketplace.json excluded twice). Real trigger (mid-run main-checkout engine-tree mutation) captured in successor flow-qt2s.

title: per-ticket lease TTL (~36min) shorter than a long subagent stage; no heartbeat during a long handler
description: The per-ticket lease TTL (~36min) is shorter than a realistic single subagent stage. On FT-1226 the implement stage ran 38min as one Agent call with no intervening next/advance to refresh the lease, so it expired mid-stage (expired_foreign on our OWN run_id) and the post-stage advance needed a recover takeover. The do-loop does not heartbeat the lease during a long handler. Fix direction: heartbeat the lease around long subagent stages, or scale TTL to the stage timeout_min (implement=30min). Recorded user-mode on FT-1226 (knowledge 3e85c629252334b5).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: (advisor) Fix via direction (A) scale-TTL, NOT direction (B) heartbeat daemon. verb-do.md:117-119 confirms the prose orchestrator has no live poller and the implement Agent call is a single blocking invocation, so a true mid-call heartbeat is impossible without a background daemon touching the lease safety module (rejected: heavy, fragile, maximally hot). A is the only pragmatic fix and the ticket offers it.

Chosen formula: ttl = default_timeout_min*60*K with K=2.0 (replacing the additive +300s buffer). Rationale: observed overrun was 1.27x (38/30min); a false-positive self-eviction of a HEALTHY run is strictly worse than a slower dead-run reclaim, and /flow recover --force already covers the dead-run case. K=2.0 gives implement a 60min lease (clears the 38min overrun with wide margin, survives even 50min) and caps the worst-case crashed-run hold on the longest stage (review_loop, 60min budget) at 120min, which is acceptable.

Mandatory implementer commitments (advisor):
1. Confirm exactly ONE TTL-from-timeout site before extracting the pure helper (verified pre-bootstrap: only dispatch_stage.py:563 + the constant at :52; origin/main identical).
2. Make the test a real regression test (implement->3600 and >38*60 both fail against old 2100) AND pin the helper WIRING: a pure-helper-only test passes even if the helper is defined-but-unwired at 563. Add one non-flaky lower-bound on the on-disk expiry after a next (e.g. expires_at - now > 50*60 for implement) OR rely on diff review for wiring.
3. Grep test_dispatch_stage.py / test_lease.py for any hard-coded OLD value (2100/35) to update (verified pre-bootstrap: none).
4. Do NOT touch lease.py logic; the lines 9-11 docstring reads fine under the multiplier - keep it a no-op rather than manufacture an edit.
Safe to auto-ship: narrow blast radius (one constant + pure helper, unchanged lease.refresh signature), clean single-commit revert, CI-covered, hot but adjudicate_hot on and no lease LOGIC changes. Merge-time guard review remains the keystone.

title: create_pr push falls back to stale upstream tracking branch and gets rejected
description: create_pr git push fails when the feature branch has a stale upstream tracking a different branch (origin/dev); the push falls back to the tracking branch and is rejected. Workaround: explicit 'git push origin <branch>:<branch>'. Fix: always push full refspec (git push origin HEAD:refs/heads/$BRANCH) rather than relying on branch tracking. Recorded user-mode on FT-1191 (knowledge 6ebf7a7620806d27); same root as the FT-1184 push-default finding.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Co-delivered by flow-rlc8 (same PR).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: co-delivered by flow-rlc8 via PR #343

title: diff_extract record-baseline --files mishandles a JSON-array-literal argument
description: record-baseline --files with a bash var holding a JSON array literal ('["a","b"]') passes the whole string as one --files arg; diff_extract.py emits ownership entries with embedded quotes, then check-ownership fails exit 3 (paths not in baseline). Workaround used: hand-edit baseline.json. Fix: record-baseline --files should JSON-parse the arg when it starts with '[', else whitespace-split. Recorded user-mode on FT-1191 (knowledge 51cf0de2a5d16af7).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED — make record-baseline --files JSON-parse a '['-prefixed arg (the frontmatter planned_files=[...] form), else comma-split. DELIBERATE deviation from the bead's 'whitespace-split' wording: the established contract is comma-separated (--files <comma-sep>, verb-do.md:113) and every existing caller depends on it, so the else branch stays comma-split. Implementer commitments: (1) a '['-prefixed arg that fails json.loads (or isn't a list of strings) must FAIL LOUD (raise), not silently comma-split back into the garbage the bead is about — pin with a test; (2) regression test isolates the parse from _union_frontmatter_planned (frontmatter absent, assert planned_files is EXACTLY ["a.py","b.py"] with no quote/bracket entries). Safe to auto-ship: one parse site, strict superset, reversible, CI-covered; hot/guard-file allowed by adjudicate_hot + merge-time guard review + CI.

title: create_pr push uses 'push -u origin <branch>'; push.default=upstream rejects on protected-branch hook
description: scripts/create_pr.py:108 runs 'git push -u origin <branch>'. On a worktree whose branch tracks origin/<default> (flow_worktree --base @default sets tracking to origin/dev) with push.default=upstream, git maps the push to the upstream ref (dev) and the protected-branch hook rejects it ('feature/... -> dev'). Major; blocks PR creation on a fresh worktree. Fix: push an explicit refspec '<branch>:refs/heads/<branch>' (immune to push.default). Recorded user-mode on FT-1184 (knowledge cb7d8dd1880a72d3); also FT-1191 (6ebf7a76).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Fix create_pr.py:109 push to an explicit fully-qualified refspec 'git push -u origin <branch>:refs/heads/<branch>'. KEEP -u (do NOT drop it): the close-the-holes assessor found a downstream bare 'git push' in stage-review_loop.md:84 (CI fix-loop) that relies on the upstream tracking; -u with the refspec repairs stale origin/<default> tracking to origin/<branch> so that path stays correct, while the refspec makes THIS push immune to push.default=upstream. Strictly never-worse than the bare push. Mandatory implementer commitments: (1) keep -u + refspec form; (2) add regression test test_push_uses_explicit_refspec pinning exact argv (red on old form); (3) plan rationale 'no downstream bare push' is false - do not assert it. Non-hot, single-line, single-commit revert. Safe to auto-ship (low blast radius, high reversibility, new test covers it). Covers flow-pm3z (same root).

title: flow_worktree create leaves orphan branch when gitignored-planned-file refusal fires
description: scripts/flow_worktree.py:688 runs 'git worktree add -b <branch>' (creates the branch), then the gitignored-planned-file guard at :699-720 refuses via _ConfigError. On refusal :714 runs 'git worktree remove --force <worktree>' but NOT a branch delete, so the -b-created branch is orphaned. The comment at :696-697 claims 'refusing leaves no orphan' but only the worktree is cleaned. Effect: a retry of the same ticket hits 'fatal: a branch named <branch> already exists' (observed on FT-1233: first bootstrap refused on a gitignored planned .csv, the retry failed on the orphan branch, needed a manual 'git branch -D'). Under --auto this is a confusing hard failure on retry. One-line fix: after the worktree remove at :714, also run(['git','branch','-D',branch], main_root) before raising. Severity: major (manual intervention / broken retry). corpus delta: no regression (scored candidate vs baseline, 34/34 + 14/14, regressed=[]; flow_worktree.py is not a pure decider so this is absence-of-regression only). Surfaced from FT-1233 flow knowledge entry b633ddb7a02ae550.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Narrow one-line fix: in flow_worktree.bootstrap, after the gitignored-planned-file guard runs 'git worktree remove --force', also run(['git','branch','-D',branch], main_root) (tolerant runner, mirroring the reap idiom) BEFORE raising _ConfigError, so a refused bootstrap leaves no orphan branch. Plus update the stale 'leaves no orphan' comment and extend test_bootstrap_rejects_gitignored_planned_file to assert the branch-delete fires. Safe to auto-ship: blast radius = one line in a rare refusal path (happy path untouched), trivially revertable, CI covers it via the extended test. Hot (guard file) but adjudicate_hot=on so the hot proceed is not downgraded; merge-time guard-property review remains the gate. Out of scope (separate follow-up): a try/except around the whole flock block to also clean up on non-deliberate later exceptions.

title: evolve_reap gh pr list query rejected by GraphQL node-cost limit (commits field)
description: evolve_reap.py:333-348 runs `gh pr list --state open --json number,headRefName,isDraft,mergeStateStatus,statusCheckRollup,files,commits --limit 200`. The `commits` field carries a nested authors connection; gh's GraphQL cost estimator computes 200 PRs x commits x authors = up to 1,000,000 possible nodes and REJECTS the query before execution (>500,000 limit). Repro (2026-06-17, repo root): `gh pr list --state open --json number,headRefName,commits --limit 200` -> 'GraphQL: ... requesting up to 1,000,000 possible nodes which exceeds the maximum limit of 500,000'; dropping `commits` (same query without it) returns []. Because the cost is validated pre-execution, this fails even with ZERO open PRs, so it breaks evolve_reap (and thus the whole drain reap step A) on every turn for this repo. The `commits` field exists to parse `Closes <COVER>` trailers for the covers list (evolve_reap.py:167). Fix: drop `commits` (and likely `files`, also used only for hot-detection on candidates) from the bulk --limit 200 list query; fetch them per-PR via `gh pr view <n> --json commits,files` only for the small set of PRs that reach classification/merge, bounding node cost to single PRs. Value: unblocks orphan reaping on every drain. Blast radius: evolve_reap.py only; pure query-shape change, behavior-preserving (same data, fetched per-PR).

title: Mechanize VISION 'Visible' for behavior-changing tier:light downshifts (silent-wrong gap)
description: FT-1191 produced a silent-wrong cheap-model failure: a green, CodeRabbit-approved /flow PR (sonnet, #2809) that violates the ticket DoD ('all amounts positive'; it emits negative credit_note amounts) with a self-confirming snapshot test that passes against the buggy output. This is a failure class the xqt corpus lacked (silent-wrong-but-green, vs the visible DNF classes) and a counterexample to VISION's 'Visible' move-fast precondition: green CI + self-referential tests + AI review can mask a DoD violation, so 'Visible' is an assumption, not a mechanism. The SONNET-LADDER catches DNFs (they re-appear in bd ready) but a silent-wrong run closes green and is never re-examined. NOTE: FT-1191 was a manual Jira run, not a tier-gated bead, and an opus producer would not have stamped it cheap; it does not indict the gate, it tests the visibility premise the gate leans on. Scope the fix to behavior-changing tier:light only; tier:trivial (behavior-preserving) is low-risk and out of scope.
design: Predictor refinement: add 'behavior-change + verifiable-but-subtle invariant (sign/unit/rounding/ordering)' as a second cheap-model failure axis orthogonal to read-size. Evidence: PRs #2809 (silent-wrong) vs #2813 (correct); tell = sonnet expected fixture amount=-45189717.96 on credit_note rows vs opus +45189717.96. Cross-check xqt-counterfactual-verdict-2026-06.md (sonnet fine on trivial/small tiers), which is why the fix scopes to behavior-changing tier:light, not the trivial floor.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior-changing tier:light work no longer trusted on green alone. Either (A) exclude from tier:light any behavior-changing finding carrying a checkable spec invariant, routing it to launcher-default opus (no new machinery; recommended default); or (B) gate a downshifted behavior-changing PR on a cheap opus diff-review against the bead's acceptance criteria before merge, verifying invariants against actual output/fixtures not just green CI. Independent of A/B: a producer stamping tier:light on behavior-changing work records the acceptance invariant on the bead. tier:trivial unchanged.

title: evolve drain should run a group-fold pass before fan-out (like /flow group)
description:
Drain fans out each ready bead as its own /flow <key> --auto run. The selector's anchor partition (held_anchor) that would keep two beads sharing a primary file out of one batch is BLIND to freshly-filed beads: it keys on planned_files frontmatter, which is not stamped until spec, so a same-day batch of related beads passes the partition and launches as N colliding parallel runs.

Evidence (this drain, 2026-06-16): select returned flow-x1yq, flow-nr8c, flow-pms6 in one batch, held_anchor=[] -- all three rewrite the create_pr.py PR-open path (x1yq: build a real PR body template; nr8c: humanize/de-AI gate on the body; pms6: default reviewers on open). nr8c's own text says it should land 'once create_pr.py::raw-commit-body lands' (x1yq) -- a soft dependency the DAG never saw. Launching all three near-guarantees 2 DIRTY conflict PRs + wasted opus runs.

Proposed: before the launch fan-out (step C), drain runs a group-analysis pass over the launchable batch (the /flow group / spec --covers logic): cluster ready beads that are one coherent change (shared primary file / shared dep / evidence overlap), pick a lead, and launch ONE /flow <lead> --auto --covers <rest> run per cluster instead of N runs. Independent beads stay separate.

SUBTLETY found while doing this by hand this drain: the bootstrap (flow_worktree.py create --covers) only stamps the covers list in frontmatter; it does NOT mark the covered beads in-flight. So a covered bead stays OPEN in bd ready and the NEXT turn's select relaunches it (and the Recover stranded detector can misclassify it once a launch_ledger marker TTL-expires). Manual workaround used this drain: wired bd deps (lead blocks each cover) so covers drop from bd ready, stay off the stranded detector, and self-heal if the lead dies. A real implementation needs first-class cover suppression in select/stranded (cover beads = in-flight under the lead's lease), not a dep hack.

BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.py / evolve_drain.py + verb-evolve.md drain step B/C + the stranded/launched_pending suppression. Non-hot (no guard file, no SKILL.md). Judgment-side: grouping criteria + lead selection + cover-lifecycle are design calls, so this is a proposal for the maintainer to spec, not an auto-drainable audit fix.

title: create_pr has no humanize/de-AI gate on the PR body
description:
flow's create_pr writes the PR body with no AI-writing-tell scrub. jira-workflow enforces one before any POST/PUT (references/pr.md Step 7.2.5): a grep for em-dashes, title-case '# Headings', and inline-header '**Term:** body' bullets, plus a preferred handoff to a humanize-capable skill, hard-gating submit until clean. flow should run the same gate (or invoke a humanize skill if installed) on the PR body before opening/updating the PR.

Lower priority once 'create_pr.py::raw-commit-body' lands (a template body beats a commit dump), but a templated/generated body can still leak tells, so the gate still earns its place.

Evidence: PR#2811 (FT-1223) body went up unscrubbed; clean only because the commit body was hand-written.
close_reason: co-delivered by flow-x1yq, self-merged via PR #335

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Co-delivered by flow-x1yq (same PR).

title: create_pr uses raw commit body as PR description (commit trailer + hard wraps)
description:
scripts/create_pr.py:121 sets the PR description to git log -1 --format=%b verbatim. That dumps the conventional-commit body straight into the PR: the machine trailer block (ticket: KEY / files: ...) and the commit-style ~72-col hard wraps. Bitbucket re-wraps on its own, so the hard wraps render ragged, and the trailer is metadata noise in a PR body.

jira-workflow builds a real PR body instead (references/pr.md: a .pr-description-tmp.md template with markdown sections, no trailer, single-line paragraphs). create_pr should build a proper PR body (summary / what+why / test plan, drop the trailer, no manual line wrapping) rather than reusing the commit message verbatim.

Evidence: PR#2811 (FT-1223) description was the verbatim commit body incl. 'ticket: FT-1223 / files:' trailer and 72-col wraps; rewritten by hand.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: (advisor) proceed — grouped chore (covers flow-nr8c, flow-pms6) on the create_pr/forge PR-creation path. Non-hot, reversible, CI-gated; GitHub (flow's own backend) no-ops the reviewer path via NotSupported, so flow's own runs are mock-tested only. Design right + safe to auto-ship (~90%). Mandatory implementer commitments (closeable in-run):
1. build_body MUST NOT fabricate sections — strip trailer, preserve Closes footer, unwrap prose paragraphs, passthrough the prose as-is. Do NOT inject empty 'Summary'/'Test plan' headings there is no data for (also avoids colliding with the heading-scrub).
2. build_body + scrub must be TOTAL — never raise on adversarial input (empty %b, trailer-only, no trailer, stray unicode, unterminated code fence); degrade to passthrough/subject. Test these inputs explicitly.
3. Verify the '# body fill in below' marker is actually gone by PR time against a realistic %b fixture; if it can survive, build_body drops it and scrub must not mangle it. No hand-clean fixture that masks this.
4. Doc-drift sweep: grep references/*.md + MODULE.md for prose describing create_pr using the raw commit body/%b and update it (seam_check only catches script-call drift, not behavior-description drift).
Scope note: flow-nr8c is body-only — leave the PR title (commit subject) alone, state that explicitly. Verify no GitHub all-supported capability assertion breaks when default_reviewers:False is added.

title: create_pr forge adapter does not set default reviewers on PR open
description:
flow's create_pr stage opens a PR with zero reviewers. The bitbucket forge adapter (scripts/forge_bitbucket.py open_pr) and the create_pr driver (scripts/create_pr.py) never fetch the repo's default reviewers, and Bitbucket Cloud does not auto-apply them on API/CLI PR-create (only the web UI does). Every flow-driven PR opens with no reviewers and a human adds them by hand.

jira-workflow solves this in references/pr.md Step 7.5: GET /repositories/{ws}/{repo}/default-reviewers, drop the author by account_id, then PUT {reviewers:[{uuid}...]} onto the PR. Mirror that in the forge adapter so open_pr (or a post-open step) sets default reviewers minus the author. GitHub path can use requested_reviewers / CODEOWNERS equivalently.

Evidence: PR#2811 (FT-1223, brinta-data-platform) opened empty; 6 default reviewers added manually. forge_bitbucket.py open_pr has no reviewer logic; create_pr.py:121 builds only title+body.
close_reason: co-delivered by flow-x1yq, self-merged via PR #335

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Co-delivered by flow-x1yq (same PR).

title: Bound or drop the write-only recall-pending .stale eviction sidecar
description:
recall_pending.evict_stale (recall_pending.py:262-301) and promote_matching (recall_pending.py:213-256) append aged-out (>24h / unparseable hook_observed_at) recall-pending entries to a .stale sidecar (_stale_path, recall_pending.py:77-79) to compact the live queue on the main checkout (where the hook appends but nothing promotes). PROBLEM: the sidecar is WRITE-ONLY. _stale_path is only ever _append_line'd (lines 256, 298); zero readers in production code (verified rg across scripts/ + hooks/ + metric.py; only tests read it, and only to assert the writer wrote). It grew to 420 lines / 184k here, dated back 2026-06-03, unbounded. (Separately, it escaped .gitignore -> dirtied main; fixed in PR#332.)

The data is not pure noise: a stale entry = recall fired + surfaced memories but the session never resolved to a ticket within 24h, i.e. a recall-utilization/waste signal (count, not relevance -- relevance needs the ticket join that promotion alone provides). So a hard drop forecloses a future 'recall fired but never resolved' metric.

OPTIONS: (a) keep archiving but BOUND it -- rotate/cap at N lines or TTL the sidecar (removes unbounded growth, keeps a short forensic trail); (b) drop stale entries on the floor, no .stale write at all (simplest, removes the file entirely, loses the data). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: (a) bounded eviction -- the vision leans on compounding memory + metrics, so keep the option cheap rather than delete it for a few hundred KB. Pick (b) only if you're confident no recall-waste metric is wanted.

CONFIDENCE: 0.8 the sidecar is genuinely write-only dead weight; the bound-vs-drop choice is taste, hence proposal not auto-drain audit. BLAST RADIUS: recall_pending.py only (evict_stale + promote_matching + _stale_path), plus test_recall_pending.py assertions. Non-hot (not a guard file).

title: bound STRANDED-RECOVERY relaunches (attempt-N -> blocked) + extend stranded detection to queue_drain
description: Follow-up to flow-9ljv (pre-PR stranded-bead drain recovery). flow-9ljv adds reap+reopen recovery for a stranded in_progress bead, but the reap->reopen->relaunch cycle has NO attempt bound: a DETERMINISTICALLY-crashing non-sonnet bead (opus-default or hot) re-strands every drain turn forever, re-burning a full claude --bg run each cycle (the done-gate guarantees the loop never reads done while stranded, trading a false-done for a possible-never-done). Sonnet-tiered beads are ALREADY bounded by the existing SONNET-LADDER marker (verb-evolve.md:225-229) which reads its marker across relaunches; the gap is non-sonnet beads only. FIX: a STRANDED-RECOVERY: attempt-N marker comment mirroring the sonnet-ladder (attempt-1 -> relaunch, attempt-2 -> bd update --status blocked + triage stem) so a doomed bead gives up to the human instead of looping. SECOND: queue_drain imports evolve_drain.decide and has the identical drain shape (memory: 'queue_select same shape') so it shares the stranding blind spot -- extend the same detection there. NOT hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Non-hot (is_hot:false), low blast radius (one internal fn + its one caller, fully reversible, CI+seam_check cover it), auto-ship safe. BOUND = the 3-state SONNET-LADDER mirror (TWO recovery relaunches before block), NOT one: ticket 'attempt-1 -> relaunch, attempt-2 -> blocked' are observe-rules (what you DO on reading each marker), so no-marker->reap+reopen+stamp attempt-1; attempt-1->reap+reopen+stamp attempt-2; attempt-2->reap(cleanup, no reopen)+bd update --status blocked + 'flow --auto could not self-approve' triage stem + drop. Distinct STRANDED-RECOVERY: stem (no collision with SONNET-LADDER/DECISION/TRIAGE-DECISION). Mandatory implementer commitments: (1) verb-queue §Recover MUST keep the fleet is-live recheck FIRST before reap (queue_drain shares the worktree pool -> same classify->recover TOCTOU as flow-72d9/8by2.3; confirm fleet.py is-live is tracker-agnostic for non-evolve keys). (2) reap-before-reopen / reap-before-block order load-bearing in EVERY branch (a failed reap leaves bead in_progress -> re-qualifies -> idempotent self-heal; flip blocked-first then failed-reap strands a dirty worktree out of the detector forever). (3) queue_drain.py docstring decision shape launch|wait|done -> add recover; MODULE.md queue_drain row gets the stranded detection + the evolve_drain-row parenthetical 'positional decide(sel,live) -> never recovers' is now stale. (4) seam_check.py is a hard gate after editing references/*.md - run it in verify. (5) bd list --status in_progress --json field availability (labels/issue_type) is the one real unknown - carry the per-bead bd show fallback, do not assert. Residuals confirm-and-dismiss: hot-non-evolve beads never auto-launch so never strand (empty gap); stale-marker-on-later-reopen matches SONNET-LADDER's own behavior. Implement-time: grep self-evolution.md/inventory.md for stranded/decision-shape drift (origin/main shows 0 hits -> likely no edit, reconcile widens planned_files if needed).

title: Test the cross-name skill fallback in resolve_handler._resolve_skill
description: resolve_handler.py:67-90 (_resolve_skill): when bd.select_bundle(result, name) returns None, the code falls back to scanning result.valid for a bundle whose manifest PROVIDES handler skill:<name> under a DIFFERENT bundle name (_provides_skill, resolve_handler.py:60-64). This branch is untested. Probe 1: `git grep _provides_skill origin/main -- plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/*` -> empty (no test references _provides_skill or _resolve_skill). Probe 2: pytest tests/test_resolve_handler.py --collect-only -> 17 tests, all setups build a bundle named 'ship-it' via _full_manifest_text() and request skill:ship-it* (bundle-name == handler-name), so none exercises the X-provides-Y fallback. Value: the API supports a bundle providing a skill whose handler name differs from the bundle dir name; a regression in the fallback select would be invisible today. Add one test: a bundle 'tools' providing skill:my-action, resolved via skill:my-action. Blast radius: test-only, one test file, no production code change. Non-hot (resolve_handler.py is not a guard file).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SONNET-LADDER: sonnet-attempt-1

title: pre-PR mid-run death strands bead IN_PROGRESS with no drain auto-recovery (done reads false-positive)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Add STRANDED pre-PR detection to evolve_drain: in_progress + non-live lease + no PR (open OR merged) + not launched_pending -> recover (reap dirty worktree THEN reopen bead) so next drain turn relaunches FRESH; decide() gains a pure 'stranded' param gating done. Non-hot (is_hot:false), safe to auto-ship under CI + merge-time guard review; fleet is-live recheck before both destructive acts is mandatory. Accepted residual: deterministically-crashing non-sonnet bead can relaunch unbounded (sonnet-tiered beads already bounded by SONNET-LADDER marker across relaunches); follow-up filed for an attempt-N bound + queue_drain parity.

title: drain orchestrator re-defers maintainer-reopened+decided beads (overrides TRIAGE-DECISION)
description:
MACHINERY (drain orchestrator judgment gap, witnessed live this drain): the §drain pre-launch staleness/validity check re-deferred flow-cijn AFTER the maintainer had deliberately reopened it (status deferred->open) and posted a "TRIAGE-DECISION: ship-as-proof" comment. The orchestrator misread the deferred->open reappearance as a concurrent --auto bd-sync clobber and re-deferred (bd update --status deferred), silently overriding the maintainer decision; only caught because the maintainer asked why.

ROOT CAUSE: §drain (references/verb-evolve.md, step C pre-launch staleness check + the orchestrator re-defer reflex) has NO rule that a bead carrying a maintainer-decision marker (TRIAGE-DECISION: / DECISION: / freeform "MAINTAINER DECISION <date>:") must be HONORED, not re-deferred. The decided short-circuit (triage._recorded_decision, flow-k7or; adjudicate_hot honored) gates the --auto RUN spec stage, but the drain ORCHESTRATOR own hold/defer judgment never consults it. Also: a deferred->open transition between drain turns on a bead the orchestrator itself deferred is maintainer intent, not a sync clobber -- there is no guidance distinguishing the two.

FIX SKETCH (prose-only, references/verb-evolve.md §drain step C): before the orchestrator defers/holds ANY bead -- especially one it previously deferred -- check for a maintainer-decision marker (reuse the triage._recorded_decision case-insensitive detection of TRIAGE-DECISION:/DECISION:/MAINTAINER DECISION). If a decision exists AND the bead is open: NEVER re-defer; launch it (honor the decision, clear the merge-time property review for hot). State explicitly that a deferred->open reappearance of an orchestrator-deferred bead is maintainer intent (honor), not a concurrent bd-sync clobber (ignore). The bd status write persists in shared-local Dolt across concurrent --auto runs, so the "sync clobber" failure mode the re-defer guarded against does not actually occur -- remove/correct that mistaken rationale.

EVIDENCE: this drain (2026-06-14) -- flow-cijn comment thread shows DRAIN HOLD -> maintainer reopen + TRIAGE-DECISION: ship-as-proof -> orchestrator re-defer -> maintainer correction. references/verb-evolve.md §drain step C "Pre-launch staleness check (per key, judgment)". triage._recorded_decision (the existing marker detector, not reused by the orchestrator).

VALUE: stops the autonomous drain from silently reversing an explicit human triage decision -- a trust-breaking override on the one human gate that survives in evolve. BLAST RADIUS: prose only (references/verb-evolve.md). NOT hot (verb-evolve.md is a reference doc, not in _GUARD_FILES).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SONNET-LADDER: sonnet-attempt-1

title: commit-stage body-fill must warn against literal CI-skip tokens in the commit message
description: MACHINERY (major, autonomous path): a commit message BODY containing the literal [skip ci] token (flow-uikh: prose describing the stamp commit's CI-skip marker) makes GitHub suppress ALL CI for the push. Symptoms: zero checks register; review_loop's CI Monitor spins to its cap reading 'pending' (25 min wasted this run); and on squash-merge the token lands in main's commit body and silently skips main's test.yml AND the new version-stamp.yml. In an --auto run this is a silent stall (no human to force-push the amend). Root: stage-commit.md step 4 (body fill) and compose_commit.py give no guard; the agent's free-text body can carry the token. Evidence: references/stage-commit.md step 4 (body fill prose); GitHub honors [skip ci]/[ci skip]/[no ci]/[skip actions]/[actions skip] anywhere in the pushed commit message. Fix: add a body-fill caution in stage-commit.md step 4 (never embed literal CI-skip bracket tokens; spell them out), optional compose_commit.py lint/strip. corpus delta: not scored - proposed fix is prose-first (stage-commit.md), no concrete scripts/*.py edit yet. Witness: flow-uikh/PR#315.

title: Strip the self-merge version stamp from stage-merge.md §3 (rely on the Action)
description: Parent flow-3w71 (order 2/3). GATED on child-1 proven on a real merge. Remove from stage-merge.md §3 the version.py stamp + the duplicate-stamp guard + the mandatory post-stamp CI re-wait on the self-merge (action=merge) path; the self-merge now relies on the server-side Action like a human merge does. The push-state guard ('merge ONLY the CI-validated SHA') STAYS. HOT: rewrites the merge invariant — needs the property-review path (no safety property dropped: lease, snapshot, ownership, CI-validated-SHA all unchanged; only the version bump moves off-branch). Update inventory.md/MODULE.md prose that describes stage-merge §3 stamping.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DRAIN HOLD 2026-06-14 (pre-launch validity gate): NOT launched. flow-cijn strips the branch-side version stamp (stage-merge §3) to rely solely on version-stamp.yml, but its gate "child-1 proven on a real merge" is UNMET — and is a DEADLOCK, not merely pending.

Why: version-stamp.yml has a guard step "Skip if this push already changed the version files" (skip=true when HEAD~1..HEAD touches plugin.json/marketplace.json). While the branch-side stamp is active, EVERY flow merge touches those files, so the Action skip-guards on every merge and its "Stamp and push" path has NEVER executed. Evidence: PR#315 + PR#318 both ran version-stamp.yml = "completed success" but skip-guarded (#318 squash b335427 changes plugin.json + marketplace.json). The Action cannot self-prove through the normal loop.

Escape (maintainer call at triage): (a) PROVE-FIRST — push one non-version-touching commit to main, watch version-stamp.yml actually stamp green, then reopen+relaunch cijn; OR (b) SHIP-AS-PROOF — accept cijn itself as the first exercise of the Action stamp path, revert-ready. Prove-first vs ship-as-proof on the merge invariant is the maintainer's decision, not the drain's. Unstick via /flow triage flow-cijn "<decision>".

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: ship-as-proof. Accept cijn's own merge as the first exercise of version-stamp.yml's stamp path. Its squash removes the branch-side stamp from stage-merge §3, so it touches no version files -> the Action skip-guard passes -> the Action's Stamp-and-push path runs (proving child-1 flow-uikh) AND ships cijn in one. Revert-ready; failure mode is version-lag (recoverable via still-live version_remerge, flow-mmh3 open), not corruption. HOT merge-invariant rewrite: relaunch must clear the merge-time property review + CI (no safety property dropped: lease, snapshot, ownership, CI-validated-SHA all retained; only the version bump moves off-branch). Maintainer call at triage 2026-06-14.

title: Retire version_remerge.py + evolve_reap duplicate-stamp logic (the pre-merge race is gone)
description: Parent flow-3w71 (order 3/3). GATED on child-2. Once stamping is post-merge + serial, branches carry no version line, so they never go DIRTY on it and never collide on the same number. Delete version_remerge.py + its tests, the MODULE.md live-map row, the duplicate-stamp classify branch in evolve_reap.py (_duplicate_stamp, version_recoverable), and the DIRTY/version_remerge recovery block in stage-merge.md §3. VERIFY no other caller depends on version_remerge first (grep the import graph + prose). HOT: touches merge/reap safety machinery — property review. This is the payoff: the simplification the server-side move unlocks.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. DAG gate satisfied live (children flow-uikh + flow-cijn both closed; server-side version-stamp.yml now stamps main serially post-merge, so feature branches carry no version line). Retire version_remerge.py + its tests, the evolve_reap version_recoverable bucket (DIRTY + duplicate-stamp branches), _gather_versions/_duplicate_stamp + main_version/branch_versions params + the now-dead 'import version', the MODULE.md version_remerge row, and the stage-merge.md §3 + verb-evolve.md version-recovery prose. HOT (merge/reap safety machinery) but ships under adjudicate_hot=on, gated by CI + merge-time guard-property review. Two implementer commitments from review: (1) stage-merge.md §3 DIRTY must REPLACE the remerge block with a 'leave for human; STATUS=completed' skip, NOT collapse into the else merge path (gh pr merge refuses DIRTY → STATUS=failed regression); (2) prose consumers of the version_recoverable bucket are wider than the ticket's literal list — verb-evolve.md lines 106/108/115/145-200/296/300 + MODULE.md line 77 all reference it and must be reconciled. Out of scope (noted): fleet-race-matrix.md is a superseded-epic analysis snapshot, not a live caller; left untouched (seam_check won't trip). Reversible; lint+seam_check+pytest are the backstops.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DRAIN RECOVERY 2026-06-14: mmh3 DNF twice, NOT systematic. (1) attempt job 08d4ea06 ran ~25min, got DEEP into implement (staged version_remerge.py + test deletions), then zombied (state=working, transcript idle 40min, lease non-live) -> drain falsely read done on the non-live lease; nothing merged, main clean. (2) recovery attempt job 4246e841 was a do-resume into attempt-1 dirty worktree (staged D version_remerge.py in index + state.json implement:in_progress + half baseline); died ~1min at implement entry right after records_diff_baseline ran against already-deleted planned files -> self-inflicted dirty-resume death, no transcript/log error. Disposition: tearing down the dirty worktree, reopening bead, ONE clean fresh /flow flow-mmh3 --auto off origin/main. If THAT dies at implement too -> environmental (long session, many bg children) -> maintainer runs ATTACHED (/flow do flow-mmh3, no --auto, in the worktree) for a live transcript; do NOT launch a 4th headless attempt. Nothing merged; fully reversible.

title: Add GitHub Action: stamp plugin version on merge to main (protected-push token + skip-ci guard)
description: Parent flow-3w71 (order 1/3, BUILD FIRST). NET-NEW .github/workflows/version-stamp.yml. On push to main (post-merge), compute the next plugin version from main itself and write it into BOTH plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json AND .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (marketplace.json is OUTSIDE plugin_root), then commit back to main. Semantic bump: feat->minor else patch — derive the type from the merged squash commit's conventional-prefix subject (the per-PR commit_type frontmatter is gone at this point; the squash subject is built from commit_summary/commit_type so it carries the prefix). Reuse version.py's stamp math where possible (it already does the semantic next-version + dual-file write). GOTCHAS: (1) protected main blocks the default GITHUB_TOKEN — use a GH App token or deploy key with bypass, or open a stamp PR; pick one and document it. (2) the stamp commit must not loop CI/this Action — [skip ci] in the message + paths-ignore on the two version files, and/or skip when the head commit is the stamp commit. VERIFY on a real merge (stamp lands, no loop, both files synced) BEFORE children 2/3 delete anything. Run via /flow <key> at its own accept gate.

title: Server-side version stamping: move plugin bump off the self-merge path into a GitHub Action, retire the pre-merge stamp-race machinery
description:
WHY (witnessed flow-kx17.1/PR#310): a human `gh pr merge` lands a feat with NO version bump — main stayed 0.46.0. Root cause: `version.py stamp` is invoked ONLY in stage-merge.md §3, which runs ONLY on the self-merge gate (action=merge). The gate skipped ("not an evolve bead"), so §3 never ran; a human merge invokes none of flow's stamp logic and no CI Action does it (only test.yml). So 'stamp at merge time' silently holds ONLY for self-merged evolve beads — EVERY human-merged PR (the normal keystone for all non-evolve feature beads + any gate-skip) lands unbumped.

LAYERING: the plugin version is a property of THIS repo (a marketplace/plugin). flow the skill runs in many repos (brinta-*, user projects) with no plugin.json; the stamp logic is self-target-only dead weight guarded off everywhere else — a repo concern smuggled into the portable skill. A .github/workflows Action lives in the repo, fires on every merge regardless of who merged, and removes the repo-specific logic from the skill (maintainer instinct, 2026-06-14).

BONUS (retires machinery): the stamp today is PRE-merge, on the branch — the sole reason version_remerge.py + the evolve_reap duplicate-stamp guard + the mandatory post-stamp CI re-waits exist (concurrent drains race the version number → DIRTY-on-version-line). A POST-merge Action stamps on main SERIALLY after merge → the race vanishes → that machinery can retire. Net simplification, not just relocation.

GOTCHAS to design in: (1) the Action commits the stamp back to PROTECTED main — default GITHUB_TOKEN is blocked; needs a GH App token/deploy key with bypass, or stamp-via-PR. (2) the stamp commit must not re-trigger CI/the Action — [skip ci] + paths-ignore on the two version files, or guard against its own commit. (3) marketplace.json lives OUTSIDE plugin_root (snapshot omits it) — the Action stamps BOTH plugin.json + marketplace.json and keeps them in sync; the live-checkout marketplace tracking must keep working.

DECOMPOSITION (verify-then-retire; order 1->2->3): 1 build the Action FIRST and prove it stamps a real merge before deleting anything; 2 strip the self-merge stamp from stage-merge.md §3; 3 retire version_remerge.py + the duplicate-stamp guard. CONFIDENCE 0.8, build-now.
close_reason: epic complete: server-side version stamping shipped — child-1 flow-uikh (version-stamp.yml Action, PR#315), child-2 flow-cijn (strip branch-side stamp, PR#323, ship-as-proof verified e4dd60d+6074c19), child-3 flow-mmh3 (retire version_remerge.py + dup-stamp logic, PR#324). Pre-merge stamp-race machinery fully retired.

title: evolve_reap _gather_versions symbolic-ref-failure fallback untested (thin)
description:
EVIDENCE (verified at origin/main): evolve_reap.py:312-314 -- 'head=symbolic-ref...; default_ref = head.stdout.strip() if returncode==0 else ""; if not default_ref: default_ref="origin/main"'. The failure branch (symbolic-ref rc != 0 -> default_ref empty -> fallback origin/main) is unexercised: the test fixture _git_version_runner hard-codes git symbolic-ref -> rc 0 (test_evolve_reap.py:749-750).

PROBE1: grep -rn 'symbolic-ref|default_ref|symbolic.*fail' tests/test_evolve_reap.py -> only the rc-0 fixture, no failure case.
PROBE2: pytest --collect-only -q | grep -iE 'symbolic|default_ref' -> the two hits are in test_beads_adapter.py (a different module's fallback); NONE in reap.

MATERIALITY: LOW -- a fail-open default (origin/main) feeding the already-fail-open dup-stamp guard; a wrong default only degrades dup-stamp detection, never corrupts. Filed as a thin tier:trivial gap, NOT a peer of the open_pr_keys gap.

FIX: add a fixture branch where git symbolic-ref returns rc!=0 and assert default_ref falls back to origin/main. One test. BLAST RADIUS: tests only.

title: drain marker-removal open_pr_keys term untested (mutation survives) -- both drains
description:
EVIDENCE (verified at origin/main): the launch-marker removal in BOTH deciders keys on 'registered = live_runs | open_pr_keys' -- evolve_drain.py:146 and queue_drain.py:159. The 'open_pr_keys' half of the union is NEVER exercised: every marker-removal test registers the launched key via a LIVE LEASE (live_runs=['flow-k'], open_pr_keys absent) in test_evolve_drain.py:330 and test_queue_drain.py:268.

PROBE1 (content-grep): grep -rn 'launched_pending|open_pr_keys|live_runs' tests/test_evolve_drain.py -> the marker-removal test sets live_runs+launched_pending, never open_pr_keys overlapping launched_pending. Empty for the open_pr_keys term. (NOTE the flow-aod/flow-cam trap: a naive grep 'test_cli_removes_launch_marker' FINDS a same-named test in both files -- read its body, it uses live_runs, not open_pr_keys.)
PROBE2 (collect): pytest tests/ --collect-only -q reports 1971 collected (non-void); | grep -iE 'open_pr.*remov|open_pr.*regist|regist.*open_pr|marker.*open_pr' -> empty.
MUTATION: delete '| open_pr_keys' from registered and the whole suite still passes.

MISSED REAL SCENARIO: a run that opened its PR but whose lease then expired (session ended, PR still open) -- live_runs no longer lists it, so without the open_pr_keys term its launch marker is never unlinked -> sticks in launched_pending -> decide() returns 'wait' forever and the drain loop cannot terminate. This is the exact line the 'drain live_runs must stay lease-only' memory (flow-8by2.3/PR#309) calls load-bearing; the corpus tests the pure deciders but not this registration term.

FIX: add a regression test in BOTH test_evolve_drain.py and test_queue_drain.py: a key in launched_pending registered ONLY via open_pr_keys (live_runs empty) must remove its launch marker. <=2 files, test-only, no guard. VALUE: protects drain termination (a missing test, VISION ground truth). BLAST RADIUS: tests only.

title: main-checkout recall-pending.jsonl never compacted (unbounded growth)
description:
EVIDENCE (verified at origin/main): the main maintainer checkout .flow/recall-pending.jsonl is 425 lines, ~415 older than 24h, never evicted. recall_pending.promote_matching is the SOLE compactor (it applies the 24h-stale partition and rewrites the file), and its ONLY production caller is dispatch_stage.py:105, invoked with workspace_root = the run's WORKTREE. Stage dispatch runs in worktrees, so the main-checkout file is never the one compacted. The SessionStart hook (hooks/session-start.py:162-168) APPENDS a recall-pending entry on every session started in the repo -- and since PR#293 the deadman renders machine-wide, so non-flow sessions in this repo append too. session-start.py has no stale-evict/compact pass for recall-pending (its 'stale' refs are the unrelated deadman loop). Net: append-only, no janitor reaches it, grows unbounded.

REPRO: wc -l .flow/recall-pending.jsonl (425); grep -rn promote_matching across scripts/+hooks/ shows the single worktree-scoped caller.

FIX SKETCH (one of): add an evict/compact subcommand to recall_pending.py and call it from the SessionStart append path (evict-stale before/after append) OR from an evolve-drain hygiene step. <=2 files, no guard file.

VALUE: leaner/reliability hygiene (VISION). BLAST RADIUS: low -- gitignored workspace-local JSONL; 425 lines is tiny today but grows faster under the machine-wide deadman. LOW SEVERITY, filed honestly as a janitor gap not an active bug.

title: flow_worktree.py docstring omits exit 7 (epic refusal)
description:
EVIDENCE (verified at origin/main): flow_worktree.py module docstring 'Exit codes:' block lists 0,1,2,3,4,6 and omits 7. The code returns 7 (raise _EpicBead at :551 -> except _EpicBead -> return 7 at :805; class at :88), and verb-spec.md (step on epic refusal) documents exit 7 externally. Pure internal doc-drift: the script's own help text contradicts its code + the reference doc.

FIX: add '7 = bead is an epic (a container, not a single-PR unit) -- refuse to bootstrap' to the docstring exit-codes block. One line.

VALUE: low but real -- VISION names doc-drift-from-code as ground truth. BLAST RADIUS: one docstring line. HOT NOTE: flow_worktree.py is in triage guard-files (flow-bgh), so this one-line docstring fix rides the full hot path -- HIGH OVERHEAD for the value; deprioritize accordingly. seam_check does not catch internal docstring exit-code drift.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Add the missing '7 = bead is an epic ... refuse to bootstrap' line to the flow_worktree.py module docstring exit-codes block. All four facts verified at origin/main: docstring lists 0,1,2,3,4,6 and omits 7; code raises _EpicBead (:562), returns 7 (:825); verb-spec.md documents exit 7 externally. Scope confirmed complete: grepped all return statements at base (0,1,2,3,4,6,7) — exit 5 is genuinely unused, the 4->6 docstring jump is correct, no adjacent drift. Safe to auto-ship: blast radius one comment line, zero behavior change, fully reversible, CI-gated. is_hot (guard file) but adjudicate_hot=true so the hot floor does not downgrade proceed; merge-time guard-property review + CI remain the gate.

title: merge-stage lease heartbeat swallows lease.py refresh exit 7/3 (re-opens double-merge)
description:
EVIDENCE (verified at origin/main): references/stage-merge.md heartbeats the run lease during the bounded CI re-wait at TWO loci -- lines 121-122 (first re-wait) and lines 148-150 (the restamp re-wait). BOTH run 'python3 .../lease.py refresh --ticket-dir ... --run-id ... --ttl-seconds 1800' WITHOUT binding RC=$? or branching on the exit. lease.py refresh returns EXIT_LEASE_LOST=7 on a LeaseLost (lost/taken-over/nonce-rotated lease; lease.py:714-716) and exit 3 on LeaseError (lease.py:717-719). The prose ignores both and proceeds unconditionally to the duplicate-stamp guard, the MERGE_STATE read (stage-merge.md:152), and the self-merge + bd close + delete-branch (:181-184).

CONTRAST proving intent: the version_remerge.py recover call 9 lines below (:158-160) DOES bind RC=$? and branches on every code (3/0/else). The merge heartbeat is the lone unchecked safety-CLI call in the block.

REAL TRIGGER (not the liveness-gated reap, which the heartbeat already prevents -- 60s poll << 1800s TTL): a human 'recover takeover --force' escape hatch (flow-5lg3) OR a nonce rotation mid-re-wait flips lease ownership; the next heartbeat refresh returns 7 (lease no longer ours), the run ignores it and STILL self-merges the PR, closes the bead, and deletes the branch -- the exact flow-ztfv double-merge/double-reap class the heartbeat was added to prevent, re-opened by a swallowed exit code on the auto-merge keystone.

FIX SKETCH (prose-only): bind RC=$? after each merge-stage lease.py refresh; treat exit 7 (and 3) as 'lease lost -> STOP, leave PR for drain reap/human, STATUS=completed', mirroring the version_remerge block immediately below. Both loci in one change.

VALUE: closes a lease-exclusivity hole on the hot auto-merge keystone (maintainer self-target only). BLAST RADIUS: references/stage-merge.md prose only; in-run self-merge path. HOT: changes auto-merge-keystone behavior touching the lease-exclusivity safety property -- must ride the hot path for the guard-property review. seam_check is structurally blind to prose exit-code handling, so the live-docs gate stays green with this hole present.

title: flow_worktree.py create: warn on planned_files paths in a non-existent dir (typo guard)
description: MACHINERY (minor, flow-kx17.1/PR#310): flow_worktree.py create accepts --planned-files without checking the paths resolve. kx17.1's spec stamped plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/references/stage-review_loop.md but references/ is a SIBLING of scripts/ under skills/flow/ (the real path has no scripts/ segment). implement edited the real file, leaving it outside the recorded baseline set until the do-loop reconciled baseline.json + frontmatter planned_files. Fix: a bare existence check is WRONG (it would false-refuse legit NEW-file plans, e.g. TDD test files implement creates). The right heuristic is warn-only (not refuse) when a planned file's path does not exist AND its parent dir also does not exist -- a new file in an existing dir is fine; a file in a non-existent dir is a likely typo. flow_worktree.py is in triage._GUARD_FILES (hot). corpus delta: not scored -- no concrete edit yet. Evidence: flow_worktree.py create / planned_files frontmatter stamp.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — add a warn-only typo guard to flow_worktree.py create: a private _typo_planned helper warns (never refuses) when a planned-files path does not exist AND its parent dir also does not exist, checked against the worktree base checkout (mirrors _gitignored). New file in an existing dir stays silent. Safe to auto-ship: pure-additive stderr warn, zero behavior change, reversible, CI-covered; hot label is guard-file-borne not blast-borne. MANDATORY implementer commitment: the no-warn tests must mkdir the existing dir / touch the existing file under the RESOLVED worktree path (the helper uses Path.exists(), and the test harness's fake 'git worktree add' may not populate the tree) so the no-warn cases reflect a real tree, not an all-missing one. Keep the single-parent-level check; do not walk up multiple levels.

title: retirement
description:
Child-5 (HOT) of epic flow-8by2. RE-SCOPED by the flow-8by2.1 spike (PR #306).

Retire the launch_ledger.py TTL blind-window logic FULLY. But DEMOTE the ~/.claude/jobs dir scan to a reconcile/orphan-detection fallback rather than deleting it: a run that crashes BEFORE its first ledger register is invisible to a pure ledger, yet visible today as a ~/.claude/jobs/<id>/ dir, so deleting the scan is a regression. Gate retirement on a property-equivalence proof, NOT corpus-green alone (CLAUDE.md robustness floor). GATED on children 2-4 landing.
close_reason: Shipped via merged PR#404 (head 2fc2b56): launch_ledger.py retired fully, launched_pending derived from the fleet ledger (heartbeat-staleness filter carries the old TTL semantics, STALE_AFTER_S == old LAUNCH_TTL_SECONDS); ~/.claude/jobs scan DEMOTED to orphan-detection fallback (mechanism unchanged, still wired at verb-evolve A2 + dry-run). Property-equivalence proof present and genuine: tests/test_launch_window_equivalence.py executable oracle replicating retired markers-minus-registered logic across blind-window/lease-live/PR-open/clean-finish/merged-not-open + exact STALE_AFTER_S boundary; sole divergence (crash-post-lease re-enters launched_pending, recovery delayed to <=1800s, never premature done) isolated + pinned. Guard-property review (agent): all 6 properties HELD, incl. drain live_runs stays lease-only and hot isolation via inflight_pre. Review follow-up folded in pre-merge (2fc2b56): fleet.py prune wired into the reap turn where launch_ledger prune was removed.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: [defer-reason: hold-property-erosion] HOT retirement of launch_ledger blind-window logic, but bead is labeled proposal not hot (launch_ledger.py is not a guard file), so an --auto self-merge would ship on corpus-green and bypass the property-equivalence proof the bead itself demands (CLAUDE.md robustness floor). Human-gate: run WITHOUT --auto, or add the hot label so the merge-time guard-property review fires before any auto-merge.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER-DELEGATED TRIAGE 2026-07-02: un-deferred + hot label added per this bead's own 06-19 adjudication clause (the label makes the merge-time guard-property review fire, closing the defer-reason). Altitude pass evidence for urgency: four overlapping liveness channels (refs, lease, launch_ledger, fleet) now coexist and this retirement child is the fleet epic's whole payoff — the accretion it exists to collapse keeps growing while it sits frozen. Binding constraints unchanged: retire launch_ledger TTL blind-window logic fully, DEMOTE (not delete) the ~/.claude/jobs scan to orphan-detection fallback, property-equivalence proof required before merge (CLAUDE.md robustness floor), corpus-green alone insufficient.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED with the launch_ledger retirement, self-approved via the --auto advisor-adjudication branch (adjudicate_hot=true, so the hot proceed is not downgraded; confidence ~88% > 70% floor; matches the maintainer's 2026-07-02 route-through-auto decision). Option ruled correct: retire launch_ledger.py fully and re-derive the drain selector's launched_pending from the fleet ledger as (fleet.live_keys - live_run_keys - open_pr_keys), keeping live_runs LEASE-ONLY (flow-d4s untouched) and the derivation on the non-guard evolve/queue_select surface (never dispatch_stage). Safe to auto-ship: revert-cheap maintainer self-target, correctness gated by the property proof + CI + the merge-time guard-property review (auto_merge_hot=true). The ~/.claude/jobs scan is DEMOTE-not-delete (doc-only; it was never a drain liveness channel, only session-teardown/orphan fallback for the pre-first-register crash window).

MANDATORY IMPLEMENTER COMMITMENTS (advisor, closeable-in-run -> required on this proceed):
1. The property-equivalence proof (test_launch_window_equivalence.py) must be an EXECUTABLE invariant/oracle proof, NOT corpus-green-plus. Include a ~5-line pure oracle of the retired 'markers - registered' logic INSIDE the test file (survives the module deletion); assert new == oracle on every must-match lifecycle state; pin the divergence states explicitly and assert any divergence is bounded by STALE_AFTER_S and only ever yields 'wait' (never a premature 'done'/'recover'); test the TTL boundary (just-under vs just-over 1800s). State the invariants explicitly: pre-lease launched in launched_pending; registered-then-cleanly-finished not in it; drain never 'done' while a launched-but-unfinished run exists.
2. Characterize the ONE real non-equivalence precisely, do NOT over-claim strict equivalence: launch_ledger LATCHED 'registered' via physical marker removal; the fleet set does NOT latch, so a crashed-post-registration run re-enters launched_pending and its stranded-recovery is delayed from ~lease-expiry to ~fleet-staleness (both <= STALE_AFTER_S=1800, strictly the safe/'wait' direction). Do NOT pursue the run_id=='' discriminator (partial fix, added complexity). Word the PR body as 'correctness-equivalent, recovery latency bounded by TTL' and surface the proof + this delta PROMINENTLY (auto_merge_hot review reads it).

title: merge-token
description: merge-serialization token in the fleet ledger; version_remerge demoted to fallback
close_reason: superseded by flow-3w71 (closed): server-side stamping retires the merge-token/version_remerge race entirely

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Spike sizing (PR #306): STAYS, scope CLARIFIED to token-only. The ledger token does NOT subsume version_remerge's git-resolution (version-only-conflict detection, next-from-main) — that intrinsic content arbitration stays; version_remerge is DEMOTED to fallback. Token must wrap the fetch+merge+push span, not just gh-merge.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
Scope notes from the 3c (PR #311) advisor review — both land in the flock-atomic merge-token:

1. **Invocation-failure is fail-DANGEROUS.** 3c's fail-safe lives *inside* `fleet.is_live()` (catches Exception → True → exit 0 → skip). But a script that fails to *run* (ImportError, bad `CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR`, exit 127, argparse exit 2) returns non-zero, and the bare `if fleet.py is-live; then skip; else act; fi` reads any non-zero as "not live" → the destructive act proceeds. It is a strict improvement over pre-3c (which always acted), so not a regression — but it is not TRUE fail-safe. Note: Python conflates uncaught-exception with exit 1, so a naive `rc==1` gate cannot fully separate "provably not live" from "module-load crash". The merge-token should gate the act on an explicit AFFIRMATIVE not-live signal (distinct exit code reserving 1 for errors, or a stdout token), so live(0) + any-error both skip and only a clean not-live proceeds.

2. **Gap-relaunch residual** (do NOT reopen lease-only — it is the right default). 3c's `is_live` is lease-only by design: an OR-with-fleet would skip every legit dead-orphan reap for ≤1800s (the common case). The one case lease-only misses is a relaunch that lands a key in its pre-lease (launch→init) window *during* the classify→act gap — there fleet has an entry but no lease exists yet. This is where the flock-atomic merge-token actually closes the race: if launch takes the fleet flock and the act re-reads under that same flock, the pre-lease window is covered without the dead-orphan false-positive. Bind this into the merge-token's flock scope (launch-register must take the flock for the act-side re-read to be authoritative over the window).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
SUPERSEDED by flow-3w71 (server-side version stamping) — deferred, not done.

child-4's merge-token serializes the branch-side stamp->merge span so two concurrent drains never stamp the same next version (demoting version_remerge to a fallback). flow-3w71 dissolves that race at the SOURCE: it moves the plugin-version stamp OFF the branch into a post-merge GitHub Action that stamps main SERIALLY after each merge. With no branch-side stamp there is no duplicate-stamp collision, so version_remerge + the dup-stamp guard + the merge-token all become unnecessary (flow-3w71 explicitly retires that machinery). flow-3w71 is also strictly better — it fixes human-merged PRs landing unbumped (flow-kx17.1/PR#310), which the merge-token never addressed.

Built then reverted (uncommitted): fleet.py merge-token primitives (merge_acquire/merge_release + CLI) + 11 tests + MODULE.md + the stage-merge §3 wiring. Not a stopgap: the dup-stamp race is already backstopped by version_remerge today, so a temporary token would be double-churn on the keystone merge path for low value.

If flow-3w71 is ever abandoned, re-open this; otherwise close once 3w71 lands. Children 1-3 of epic 8by2 (fleet ledger + readers cutover + the is-live re-check, PR#308/#309/#311) are UNAFFECTED — they address run-liveness (destroying a live run at reap/cleanup), a different race 3w71 does not touch.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: STALENESS PASS 2026-06-19: closing as superseded. flow-3w71 (server-side post-merge version stamping) is now CLOSED/shipped — it dissolves the dup-stamp race at the source (no branch-side stamp → no merge-token needed, version_remerge retired). Confirmed via comment thread + memory flow-8by2-4-superseded-by-3w71. Built-then-reverted; do not rebuild.

title: readers-cutover
description: readers cutover: selectors/reap/cleanup read the fleet ledger; reap requires positive dereg + staleness fallback
close_reason: stale in_progress bookkeeping: work shipped 2026-06-13 via merged PR#309 + PR#311; no run dir / non-live lease; sibling dep flow-8by2.2 already closed. Closing to unblock flow-8by2.5 (retirement). Reversible (reopen if cutover incomplete).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Spike sizing (PR #306): STAYS, CONDITIONED. reap requires positive dereg WITH a heartbeat-staleness fallback. Cutover MUST move each destructive act (reap gh-merge + bd-close; cleanup claude-stop + teardown) to re-check the ledger UNDER the fleet flock immediately before the irreversible step, else the worst race survives the rebuild.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DESIGN CONSTRAINT (from flow-8by2.2 build + advisor review): fleet.live_keys's staleness fallback uses a FLAT STALE_AFTER_S=1800, and the heartbeat (dispatch_stage.cmd_next -> fleet.register_run) fires ONLY at stage TRANSITIONS. A long intra-stage gap with no transition exceeds the flat window and makes a LIVE run read DEAD. The concrete case is the merge stage's section-3 CI re-wait, which holds a session 20-40+ min between dispatch calls (flow-72d9, the live-run-reaped-as-orphan bug this epic exists to kill).

So when child-3 makes live_keys AUTHORITATIVE, it MUST NOT trust live_keys alone: reconcile it against the lease (lease.classify) before any irreversible act, OR fix the staleness to be expiry-based (lease's stage_timeout_min*60 + buffer, already computed at dispatch_stage.py:427) OR add an intra-stage heartbeat. Trusting flat-1800 live_keys alone reintroduces flow-72d9 via the very mechanism meant to fix it. The caveat is documented in fleet.py's STALE_AFTER_S comment + the MODULE.md fleet row.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
REMAINING SCOPE — flow-8by2.3 is NOT complete until this lands (the spike's binding condition; blocks closure).

3a (reconciled selector liveness read) + 3b (clean-exit deregister) shipped in PR feat(fleet) 9743a75/this PR. They cut the SELECTOR/drain liveness read over to the reconciled lease|fleet authority and make fleet accurate via positive dereg. CORRECTION (advisor review): 3a+3b do NOT touch the worst TOCTOU window — that is the REAP merge+close (site 5, evolve_reap) and the session-cleanup stop+rm (site 6, evolve_session_cleanup), which are untouched. So the reap race is unchanged, not improved.

3c (remaining): move the destructive-act re-check UNDER the fleet flock right before the two irreversible acts (spike fleet-race-matrix.md §3 / line 193):
