  - reap: `gh pr merge` + `bd close` (verb-evolve.md drain reap step ~line 124; also stage-merge.md §3 self-merge ~line 173/182).
  - session-cleanup: `claude stop` + `rm -rf <job_dir>` (verb-evolve.md step A2 ~line 202) — the one destructive path with NO under-flock re-check today (unlike reap's lease.classify_then worktree teardown).
Re-check that the key is NOT live (fleet fresh OR lease live) immediately before the irreversible step; if live, skip.

DESIGN NOTE: the merge+close + stop+rm live in PROSE (verb-evolve.md / stage-merge.md), so true under-flock atomicity (read+mutate in one flock span like lease.classify_then) is not achievable from prose alone — a synchronous re-check immediately before the act narrows the window from select-time (minutes) to act-time (ms) but is not atomic. Closing it fully may need moving the merge into a small CLI that re-checks + merges under the fleet flock. Needs its own design pass; deferred deliberately rather than rushed.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
3c (act-time is-live re-check) shipped to PR #311 (stamped v0.47.0, merging). Lands the spike's binding condition: a synchronous lease-only `fleet.py is-live` re-check immediately before the drain's two irreversible acts (reap merge+close, session-cleanup stop+rm), so a run that acquired a lease in the lock-free classify→act gap is not destroyed under itself. is-live is LEASE-ONLY by design (an OR-with-fleet would skip a reapable dead orphan whose fleet entry outlives its ~900s lease to 1800s). verb-evolve.md wires all 3 sites (act in else/elif, skip on live).

Bead stays OPEN: 3c NARROWS the worst TOCTOU from select-time to act-time but does not CLOSE it — the read is still lock-free; true atomic read+act under one flock is child-4's merge-token (flow-8by2.4, scope notes added there). Two residuals filed to flow-8by2.4: (1) invocation-failure fail-dangerous on the bare `if`, (2) the gap-relaunch pre-lease window lease-only misses.

title: flow_worktree.py create lacks an epic-type guard (spec/bootstrap chokepoint)
description:
WITNESS: `/flow flow-8by2 --auto` was launched on an EPIC bead. flow_worktree.py create refuses terminal (exit 6), hot (exit 2), dup-claim (exit 4) beads but has NO issue_type=='epic' refusal, so an epic CAN reach bootstrap and be cram-shipped as a single PR -- the 'auto-ship fragments of an unaccepted epic' ouroboros the design explicitly names (verb-evolve.md §epic). This run dispositioned correctly in prose (left the epic open + commented, no bootstrap), but the structural floor is missing.

GAP: evolve_select.py filters `issue_type != 'epic'` UNCONDITIONALLY (drain structurally never launches an epic), but the spec/bootstrap chokepoint (flow_worktree.py create) does not mirror that filter. A manual or misrouted `/flow <epic> --auto` is the unguarded path.

FIX (single PR): add an epic-type refusal to flow_worktree.py create at the bootstrap chokepoint (alongside _refuse_terminal_bead), new exit code, + matching SKILL.md/verb-spec.md prose for the --auto disposition (leave-open, no defer -- defer's loop-prevention is moot since drain already filters epics, and --status deferred would wrongly shelve a build-now epic and mutate maintainer accept/shelve state).

HOT: touches flow_worktree.py (guard file) + SKILL.md -> is_hot:true, adjudicate at spec gate.

REF: parent witness flow-8by2 (epic), evolve_select.py epic filter, verb-evolve.md §epic/§E expand.
close_reason: shipped via PR #307 (epic bootstrap guard exit 7 + tracker_beads issue_type fix) merged to main 0.44.19

title: evolve_select._hot_inflight bd list query has no --limit 0 (50-limit truncation, PR#299 class)
description: evolve_select.py _hot_inflight() runs 'bd list -l <label> --status <active> --json' WITHOUT --limit 0. bd's default 50-row limit + priority sort can truncate a launched hot key out of the hot-keys set under a large active backlog, so hot_inflight could read False and let a second hot launch (breaks >=1-hot-at-a-time). Same class as PR#299/flow-8zdy (bd-list-default-limit-50). Not load-bearing today (<=handful active evolve beads) so deferred from flow-xp5r to keep that hot auto-merging diff minimal. FIX: add '--limit','0' to the bd list call; strictly more conservative (only adds keys -> only over-serializes a hot, never under). BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select._hot_inflight + a test asserting the query carries --limit 0.

title: queue_select.partition budget ignores in-flight launched_pending/live (same shape as flow-xp5r)
description: queue_select.py:76 partition() has its own copy of the evolve_select budget bug fixed in flow-xp5r/PR#303: budget is computed without subtracting in-flight active sessions (launched_pending UNION live_runs) from concurrency, so the day-job queue drain over-launches in the launch->init window. queue_select.py:165-185 already builds inflight_keys = live|launched but passes it only for candidate-exclusion, not budget. FIX: mirror the evolve_select fix — add inflight_count=len(live_keys | launched_keys) to partition() and budget = min(cap - open_pr_count, max(0, concurrency - inflight_count)). BLAST RADIUS: queue_select.partition/select + a regression test. Concurrency safety -> hot.

title: stage-merge.md §1 gate call uses zsh-breaking ${VAR:+--flag} conditional expansion
description: references/stage-merge.md:62 invokes evolve_self_merge.py with ${EVAL_STATUS:+--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS"}. zsh keeps that whole construct as ONE token, so argparse rejects it ('unrecognized arguments: --eval-status pass') and the merge §1 gate errors out on any PR that touched scripts/*.py (i.e. every hot engine self-merge, where EVAL_STATUS is non-empty). Worked around in the flow-qwf3 run by re-invoking with an args-array form. This is the exact class flow-i256/PR#287 fixed (zsh-word-split-conditional-expansion); the fix is to build an args array (ARGS=(...); [ -n "$EVAL_STATUS" ] && ARGS+=(--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS"); python3 ... "${ARGS[@]}") instead of the :+ conditional. Hot: edits references/stage-merge.md (a /flow reference doc on the merge keystone hot path).
close_reason: Duplicate of PR#287 (1d1439c): EVAL_STATUS args-array fix already at origin/main; local checkout lagged 24 commits. No-op PR avoided. Real fix = advance marketplace checkout.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Already fixed at origin/main — duplicate of PR#287 (commit 1d1439c, "fix: fix EVAL_STATUS conditional expansion quoting in stage-merge.md §1"). At origin/main, stage-merge.md:62 is the prescribed args-array form (_merge_args=(...); [ -n "$EVAL_STATUS" ] && _merge_args+=(--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS")); the ${EVAL_STATUS:+...} construct is gone. Verified: `git log HEAD..origin/main -- references/stage-merge.md` => 1d1439c (#287); grep of ${VAR:+ across SKILL.md + all references/*.md at origin/main => zero hits. A worktree off @default would land an empty PR, so no bootstrap. ROOT CAUSE of the false sighting: the local main checkout (which the vdsmon-flow marketplace tracks) lags origin/main by 24 commits, so live /flow runs still load the stale prose with the broken :+ at the merge gate (this is what flow-qwf3 hit). The real remaining action is the operational maintainer step: advance local main -> origin/main, then `claude plugin marketplace update vdsmon-flow` — NOT a code PR. Closing as duplicate.

title: evolve_reap._file_main_red_p0 open-bead dedup scan misses --limit 0 (same footgun as flow-8zdy)
description:
evolve_reap._file_main_red_p0 (evolve_reap.py ~line 357) runs [
  {
    "id": "flow-xp5r",
    "title": "evolve_select budget ignores in-flight launched_pending/live runs: over-launches in the launch-\u003einit window",
    "description": "OBSERVED (drain run 2026-06-12): evolve_select.select() computes budget = min(cap - open_pr_count, concurrency) (evolve_select.py:101). inflight_keys only DROP in-flight beads from the candidate 'active' set (line 88, prevents re-launch); they are NOT subtracted from budget. In the launch-\u003einit window (runs launched but no PR open yet: live_runs registered but open_pr_count still low), the selector re-offers a full budget every turn, so action stays 'launch' continuously until open PRs reach cap. Concretely: 8 runs launched (launched_pending=8, live_runs=[], open_pr_count=0) and the very next turn the selector offered 7 MORE (skipped_in_flight correctly listed the 8, yet budget=min(10-0,8)=8). Following action=launch blindly would fan out ~15 concurrent backgrounded · \u001b[36m2326ae21\u001b[39m\u001b[2m (idle — send a prompt to start)\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude agents             list sessions\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude attach 2326ae21    open in this terminal\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude logs 2326ae21      show recent output\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude stop 2326ae21      stop this session\u001b[22m sessions (real sessions, not workflow subagents) before any backpressure exists. WORKAROUND used this drain: the orchestrator throttled manually, holding total in-flight at concurrency via effective = max(0, concurrency - |launched_pending UNION live_runs UNION open_pr_keys|), launching only that many per turn and D-waiting for a real completion to free a slot. VALUE: the only cross-turn throttle today is open-PR backpressure (cap), which builds minutes late; concurrency=8 is set below cap=10 precisely as the tighter simultaneity bound but is not actually enforced across the window. FIX (candidate): subtract in-flight (launched_pending UNION live_runs) from budget, or count live+pending against concurrency directly. BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.select() budget calc + a regression test (launched_pending non-empty, open_pr=0 -\u003e budget shrinks). Concurrency safety is load-bearing -\u003e treat as guard-adjacent / hot. SECONDARY (same window, minor): a run that completes faster than a drain poll cycle (flow-26zg: plan-\u003ePR-\u003eself-merge-\u003eclosed inside ~1 poll) leaves a stale launch_ledger marker that never cleared at registration (cli_main removes only while a live lease/open PR is observed; both were already gone), so it lingered in launched_pending until the 1800s TTL, falsely occupying a slot.",

    "id": "flow-nu1w",
    "title": "Cognitive-yield driver: absorb deterministic inline-stage recipes into the engine (spike-gated)",
    "description": "WHY (witnessed bug-class; skeptic verdict SURVIVE NARROWLY -- spike-only authorized, retargeted): the prose-CLI seam-drift bug class exists only because orchestration is prose (a 698-line seam_check.py + three drift-gate extensions: flow-nr4, flow-yyn, flow-yga); prose is not self-applying (flow-bzz: a stage-doc fix activates next run); the deterministic inline-stage recipes (stage-commit.md 154 lines, stage-merge.md 212 lines, create_pr) are where the drift class lives. SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding, they reshape the epic): (1) the original token-burn grounding was EXAGGERATED -- dispatch_stage already owns the plumbing and the skeleton sits in context once per session; the spike must MEASURE mechanical-orchestration token share over ~5 recent --auto transcripts and everything downstream is gated on it being material (\u003e15%) OR justified by the drift-surface rationale alone; (2) retarget from none-handler/advance plumbing (already code, near-zero win) to absorbing the deterministic inline-stage recipes; (3) frame as EXTENDING dispatch advance (a run-until-cognitive-yield co-routine over repeated CLI calls -- only the LLM session can spawn Agent/Skill tools, so the driver is necessarily cooperative, NOT a new flow_run.py inversion); (4) the structured handler-result contract must not delete the descriptor seam user-mode runs depend on; (5) every child edits SKILL.md/dispatch_stage.py -\u003e hot throughout, drains sequentially. Thesis note: prose-as-orchestrator is part of self-evolution, but machinery_edit covers scripts equally and code is corpus-evaluable where prose is not -- the tension resolves in favor of moving DETERMINISTIC recipes to code while cognitive-stage protocols stay markdown. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept; children 2-4 gated on child 1):\n1. (NET-NEW) spike: classify stage-iterations in 5 recent --auto transcripts mechanical-vs-cognitive, measure orchestration token share, publish the gate verdict -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-1-burn-spike\n2. (NET-NEW, hot) dispatch advance run-until-cognitive-yield mode + structured result contract (descriptor seam preserved) -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-2-yield-mode\n3. (NET-NEW, hot) absorb the stage-commit recipe -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-3-commit-absorb\n4. (NET-NEW, hot) absorb stage-merge/create_pr recipes + prose shrink + seam_check/corpus extension -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-4-merge-absorb\nCONFIDENCE: 0.6. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (run the spike, decide on its numbers).",

    "id": "flow-8by2",
    "title": "Fleet coordination authority: positive registration/heartbeat/deregistration ledger for the run fleet",
    "description": "WHY (witnessed, strongest friction backing of the pass; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.75): the coordination layer was built for one run per ticket launched by a human; reality is 5+ parallel bg runs from two drain loops. The architecture generates the bug class faster than point-patches retire it: live run reaped mid-CI-wait (flow-72d9/#231), takeover TOCTOU twice in different paths (flow-ocp/#211, flow-s85/#235), launch_ledger prune crashed on every call since introduction (flow-l48/#168), marker-TTL blind window (flow-d4s/#173), version-stamp collisions needing a whole recovery script (#220/#226/#196); 36 of 246 repo commits churn the six coordination files; SIX coordination fixes landed on 2026-06-10 alone. Liveness is currently INFERRED from a five-source eventually-consistent join (leases + launch markers + gh PRs + bd status + ~/.claude/jobs scans -- the last reverse-engineered, schema-less, and the flakiest). SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): scope is collapsing the three LIVENESS sources (lease + launch markers + jobs scan) into one flock-serialized .flow/fleet/ authority -- gh/bd remain as work-state reads (one inference site instead of five, not zero); positive deregistration keeps a heartbeat-staleness fallback or a crashed run blocks the drain forever; per the VISION robustness floor the lease/TOCTOU properties are replaced only by provably-equivalent ones and the retirement child needs that proof, not just corpus green; the Agent-Teams alternative is folded into the spike (expected to lose: cross-boot detached processes, stdlib-only headless reads, and the one platform-coupled source today is the flakiest -- evidence against deeper coupling); success metric = coordination-fix commit share + reap incidents post-cutover. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py (guard), launch_ledger.py, evolve_select/drain/reap/session_cleanup, queue_select/drain, verb-evolve.md/verb-queue.md, harness corpus. Hot throughout; children serialize under one-hot isolation. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept):\n1. (NET-NEW) spike: race-matrix inventory of every coordination read/write + Agent-Teams comparison memo -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-1-race-matrix-spike\n2. (NET-NEW, hot) fleet ledger: .flow/fleet/ flock-serialized registration+heartbeat+deregistration, subsuming launch markers -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-2-fleet-ledger\n3. (NET-NEW, hot) readers cutover: selectors/reap/cleanup read the ledger; reap requires positive dereg with staleness fallback -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-3-readers-cutover\n4. (NET-NEW) merge-serialization token in the ledger; version_remerge demoted to fallback -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-4-merge-token\n5. (NET-NEW, hot) retire superseded machinery (TTL blind-window logic, jobs-dir scan) after property-equivalence proof + corpus eval -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-5-retirement\nCONFIDENCE: 0.75.     "title": "/flow revise: post-delivery human-review-feedback ingestion (the missing second half of 'review the PR')",
    "description": "WHY (witnessed workflow; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.80, all cites verified): maintainer leaves change-request comments on a flow-delivered PR -\u003e every revision round is manual today. Pipeline terminal is 'CI green + no unresolved Major+ BOT threads' (stage-review_loop.md p3); human review happens strictly after; GitHub review-threads capability is hard-off (forge_github.py:55 supported:False, :192-199 NotSupported -- its stated reason 'nothing to test against' is dissolved by this use case); no resume path for a done run (dispatch next returns done:true at dispatch_stage.py:353; verb-recover.md:3 scopes to stuck/failed/drift only).     "id": "flow-ufvu",
    "title": "Temporal memory: supersession + selection pressure on knowledge.jsonl (stale lessons must die)",
    "description": "WHY (witnessed + web-grounded; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.8): knowledge.jsonl is append-only with NO supersede/retire mechanism (memory_append.py:18 'NEVER rewritten'); recall.py rank() is pure BM25 with ts only as tiebreak -- a stale term-rich DECISION outranks its successor. Live contradictions coexist NOW: flow-8we (06-04, 'version bumps PATCH-only never minor', line 44) vs flow-mse (06-10, 'MINOR on feat', line 187); flow-014 (bootstrap auto-includes version files, PR#117, line 91) vs flow-6gx.4 (removed entirely, PR#186, line 153). 244 entries in one month; the auto-memory MEMORY.md already hand-curates with OBSOLETE/RETIRED annotations -- the need is being paid manually. Web: Zep arXiv 2501.13956 / Graphiti validity-window facts; Mem0 reports large gains from consolidation+forgetting. VISION: memory is one of the two compounding loops; superseded DECISIONs fed into spec/recall poison it. SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): supersession must be ENTRY-ID-TARGETED, never topic-matched (flow-6gx.4 retained version_remerge as backstop -- a topic sweep would have hidden live knowledge); retro sweep is propose-only with human confirm; recency decay is cut or type-gated (FACTs are evergreen); the filter applies in recall.py AND the SessionStart recall hook path; tombstones-as-new-records preserve append-only and make wrong supersession recoverable. BLAST RADIUS: memory_append.py, recall.py, stage-reflect.md prose, hooks/session-start recall path, one new sweep producer. No guard files. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept):\n1. (NET-NEW) supersession schema (--supersedes \u003centry-id\u003e, tombstone records) + recall/hook filter -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-1-supersede-schema\n2. (NET-NEW) reflect-stage wiring: a run whose merged change disproves a recalled entry writes the id-targeted supersession -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-2-reflect-wiring\n3. (NET-NEW) retro-curation sweep over the existing 244-entry stock, propose-only -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-3-retro-sweep\n4. (NET-NEW, optional) type-gated recency handling in rank() -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-4-recency-decay\n5. (NET-NEW, optional) corpus-health metric (live vs superseded counts) -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-5-corpus-health\nCONFIDENCE: 0.8. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build-now (children 1-3 are the load-bearing core).",

    "title": "Post-hoc transcript trace-mining producer: surface failure modes the in-flight friction log never recorded",
    "description": "## The gap (web-cited + a structural blind spot in the producer set)\n\nflow's friction feedstock is flow_friction.py — an append-only log HAND-WRITTEN by the do-loop prose, IN-FLIGHT, only when a step notices it worked around a snag (SKILL.md \"Friction logging (in-flight)\"). Producer A (the reflect sling) and Producer B (evolve audit, a cold scan over SOURCE CODE) both consume that log or read code. NEITHER mines completed run TRANSCRIPTS. A failure mode the prose never thought to log — a silent retry, a stage that took three attempts, a recurring drift the agent ate without writing an entry, a stall gap — is invisible to the loop. The feedstock is self-REPORTED, never self-EVIDENT.\n\n## The field moved here (the grounding)\n\nMining raw execution traces post-hoc is now a recognized discovery surface:\n- Self-Harness (arXiv 2606.09498) \"Weakness Mining\": reads execution traces, clusters failed runs by verifier-grounded failure signatures (terminal cause + agent-behavior contribution + reusable mechanism), feeding the harness-proposal stage.\n- Addy Osmani, Agent Harness Engineering: names the frontier directly — \"agents that analyze their own traces to identify and fix harness-level failure modes.\"\n- LangChain, The Anatomy of an Agent Harness: agentic judges inspect production traces, identify emerging failure modes, mint reusable labels.\n\n## VISION anchor\n\n\"The worker at the station sees what I can't\" — the transcript IS the worker's record, and flow currently only sees the friction the worker remembered to write down. A trace miner makes lived friction self-evident rather than self-reported, deepening Producer A's feedstock. Maintainer-gated, self-target only (mines flow's OWN dogfood run transcripts under ~/.claude, never user telemetry — not a platform). Produces deduped PROPOSALS through the existing seam; never auto-implements. A net-new producer at a DISCOVERY altitude flow lacks — distinct from the measurement epics, which grade edits flow already knows it made.\n\n## Distinct from existing producers\n\n- reflect (Producer A) = in-flight, self-reported friction during a run.\n- evolve audit (Producer B) = cold static scan of source code.\n- evolve propose / epic = generative judgment + theme-scale.\n- THIS = post-hoc mining of completed-run TRANSCRIPTS for failure modes none of the above recorded. Net-new feedstock, not a re-run of an existing scan.\n\n## Blast radius\n\nNew read-only producer script + a reflect/evolve entry point. Reads transcript JSONL (filesystem-scan only — NEVER `claude agents --json`, which blocks on a TTY per the known gotcha) + the existing friction log, files deduped evolve beads through the existing flow_beads_create.py + evid:\u003cfingerprint\u003e seam, so the existing drain consumer picks them up with zero new consumer code. Additive; no existing-contract change; no safety guard touched.\n\n## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via §E expand)\n\n1. (NET-NEW) trace_mine.py extract: read a finished run's transcript (~/.claude job/session JSONL, filesystem-scan only), emit structured per-stage events (retries, tool-errors, eaten drifts, stall gaps). dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-1-extract.\n2. (NET-NEW) Verifier-grounded failure-signature clustering: group events into Self-Harness-style signatures (terminal cause + mechanism), dedup against already-logged flow_friction.py entries so the miner only surfaces MISSED friction. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-2-cluster.\n3. (NET-NEW) File mined signatures as deduped evolve beads through flow_beads_create.py + the evid: fingerprint, so the existing drain consumer picks them up. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-3-file.\n4. (NET-NEW) Wire as a scheduled producer: add a trace-mining pass alongside evolve audit on the nightly cadence (loop-engineering.md), so transcript mining runs unattended. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-4-schedule.\n\n## Grounding cited\n\nWeb cites (Self-Harness Weakness Mining arXiv 2606.09498; Addy Osmani trace-analysis frontier; LangChain agentic trace judges) + reproduced witness (flow_friction.py is hand-logged in-flight; no producer reads run transcripts; friction.jsonl shows recurring RECONCILE classes the in-flight logger captured only partially).\n\n## Recommended default: needs-discussion (a net-new producer adds a standing surface; weigh against keeping the producer set lean). title: evolve_select budget ignores in-flight launched_pending/live runs: over-launches in the launch->init window
description:
OBSERVED (drain run 2026-06-12): evolve_select.select() computes budget = min(cap - open_pr_count, concurrency) (evolve_select.py:101). inflight_keys only DROP in-flight beads from the candidate 'active' set (line 88, prevents re-launch); they are NOT subtracted from budget. In the launch->init window (runs launched but no PR open yet: live_runs registered but open_pr_count still low), the selector re-offers a full budget every turn, so action stays 'launch' continuously until open PRs reach cap. Concretely: 8 runs launched (launched_pending=8, live_runs=[], open_pr_count=0) and the very next turn the selector offered 7 MORE (skipped_in_flight correctly listed the 8, yet budget=min(10-0,8)=8). Following action=launch blindly would fan out ~15 concurrent backgrounded · [36m2326ae21[39m[2m (idle — send a prompt to start)[22m
[2m  claude agents             list sessions[22m
[2m  claude attach 2326ae21    open in this terminal[22m
[2m  claude logs 2326ae21      show recent output[22m
[2m  claude stop 2326ae21      stop this session[22m sessions (real sessions, not workflow subagents) before any backpressure exists. WORKAROUND used this drain: the orchestrator throttled manually, holding total in-flight at concurrency via effective = max(0, concurrency - |launched_pending UNION live_runs UNION open_pr_keys|), launching only that many per turn and D-waiting for a real completion to free a slot. VALUE: the only cross-turn throttle today is open-PR backpressure (cap), which builds minutes late; concurrency=8 is set below cap=10 precisely as the tighter simultaneity bound but is not actually enforced across the window. FIX (candidate): subtract in-flight (launched_pending UNION live_runs) from budget, or count live+pending against concurrency directly. BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.select() budget calc + a regression test (launched_pending non-empty, open_pr=0 -> budget shrinks). Concurrency safety is load-bearing -> treat as guard-adjacent / hot. SECONDARY (same window, minor): a run that completes faster than a drain poll cycle (flow-26zg: plan->PR->self-merge->closed inside ~1 poll) leaves a stale launch_ledger marker that never cleared at registration (cli_main removes only while a live lease/open PR is observed; both were already gone), so it lingered in launched_pending until the 1800s TTL, falsely occupying a slot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SIBLING BUG (same launch->init window, observed same drain): hot-isolation also leaks. evolve_select hot_inflight does NOT count a hot key sitting in launched_pending (pre-lease). Repro this drain: flow-ac7z (hot) in launched_pending, live_runs=[], and the selector offered flow-umvf (the next hot) into launch_offer while holding only flow-xp5r — i.e. it would put TWO hots in-flight, breaking the >=1-hot-at-a-time invariant. Same root as the budget bug: in-flight launched_pending keys are dropped from candidates but not reflected in hot_inflight (nor budget). Fix should make BOTH budget AND hot_inflight count launched_pending (UNION live_runs). Transient (self-corrects once the pending hot registers a live lease, which hot_inflight does count), but a fast second-hot launch in the window breaks isolation.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED on the budget half (hot, adjudicate_hot). Fix: subtract in-flight ACTIVE SESSIONS from the concurrency budget in evolve_select.partition/select — budget = min(cap - open_pr_count, max(0, concurrency - inflight_count)), inflight_count = |launched_pending UNION live_runs|. open_pr_keys is EXCLUDED (already bounded by cap-open_pr_count; cap>concurrency is deliberate, double-booking open PRs against concurrency makes the top cap slots unreachable). Safe to auto-ship: partition is internal-only (queue_select has its own), reversible, CI-covered incl. a discriminator test (open_pr>0, launched=live=0 -> budget=min(cap-open_pr, concurrency)). HOT_INFLIGHT sibling concern: verified EMPIRICALLY not reproducible on current base — _hot_inflight already unions launched_keys via extra_keys=inflight_pre (since PR#158), ACTIVE_STATUSES includes 'open', and a launched status=open hot key IS counted (second hot -> held_hot). Comment was filed 7 min AFTER main tip, so not a lagging checkout; reads as a code-reading inference that missed the separate extra_keys plumbing. No _hot_inflight behavioral change; added a select-level status=open regression test to lock it (prior test used in_progress). Follow-up beads: queue_select.partition same budget shape; --limit 0 hardening on _hot_inflight bd list (bd-50 class, PR#299); stale launch_ledger marker TTL note.

title: evolve_reap misses a green+conflicting orphan (dead session, bead in_progress): not classified into any bucket
description: OBSERVED (drain run 2026-06-12, flow-q9en): a run died before self-merging — its .flow/runs/<key>/ run dir + lease were gone and its session transcript was stale (genuine orphan) — but its worktree under .flow/worktrees/ still existed and PR #293 was left OPEN, green (CI SUCCESS), mergeable=CONFLICTING/DIRTY, with bead status=in_progress (never closed) and EMPTY PR labels (no 'evolve' label on the PR). evolve_reap.py --workspace-root . returned EVERY bucket empty (merge/not_green/skipped_hot/skipped_live/version_recoverable/blocked/held_main_red all []), so the orphan was invisible to the reap safety-net and would have stranded indefinitely. A2 evolve_session_cleanup also could not catch it (it gates on a TERMINAL bead; this bead was in_progress). Manual recovery worked: version_remerge.py recover --cwd <the existing worktree> remerged current origin/main, restamped 0.44.6->0.44.9, pushed; CI re-waited green on the new sha; merged. TWO candidate mechanisms fit the evidence, NOT yet disambiguated (read evolve_reap.py at plan time): (a) reap enumerates/indexes evolve PRs by the PR's 'evolve' LABEL, which #293 lacked; (b) reap maps PR->bead and skips beads not in a terminal/expected state (q9en was in_progress). VALUE: the orphan reap is the drain's only safety-net for runs that die before self-merging; a hole here silently strands shipped-ready green work. Whichever mechanism, the gap is: a green orphan whose bead is in_progress and/or whose PR is unlabeled is not reaped. BLAST RADIUS: evolve_reap.py classification + its PR-enumeration/bead-join; add a regression covering green+conflicting+in_progress+unlabeled orphan. Also relevant: this was the FIRST run all drain to die before self-merging, so the orphan path had not been exercised this run.

title: verb-spec.md bg Edit-block over-scopes to main agent (only subagents blocked)
description: MACHINERY (minor, knowledge id f37159c4d09ef114, flow-kykn/PR#296): in a bg `claude --bg /flow <key> --auto` run, after the spec step-7 `cd <worktree>` workaround the MAIN agent's Edit/Write tools worked on absolute worktree paths (this run edited verb-do.md + SKILL.md via the Edit tool, not Bash string-replace). references/verb-spec.md:80 (the 'Backgrounded --auto fallback' block) and verb-do.md's bg recipe read as if the orchestrator's Edit stays blocked, telling it to 'drive the entire do loop via Bash'; observed behavior is that only SPAWNED subagents (cwd pinned at repo root) stay blocked. Fix: scope the Edit-block prose to subagents only, noting the orchestrator's Edit is unblocked once the persistent Bash cwd is inside the worktree. Caveat: single observation, possibly harness-version-specific; Bash editing remains a safe superset, so keep it as the documented fallback. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only references/*.md edit. Touches verb-spec.md (referenced by SKILL.md routing but not a guard file itself); not hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed on STRICT two-block scope — fix references/verb-spec.md (Backgrounded --auto fallback block) and references/verb-do.md (bg recipe block at line ~111) only. Do NOT touch verb-do.md:115 (the .out heredoc-capture block): its operation is Write of a NEW untracked file, distinct from the witnessed Edit-on-tracked-files evidence; the single observation does not cover it, and softening it would promote an inference to fact and erode a safe fallback. Phrase the 83/111 rewrites with the ticket-mandated hedge (orchestrator Edit/Write OBSERVED to work once the cd workaround moves Bash cwd inside the worktree; single observation, possibly harness-version-specific; Bash/heredoc remains the documented safe-superset fallback) so 115 stays consistent without a third edit. Safe to auto-ship: prose-only references/*.md, low blast radius (not a guard file, not SKILL.md), trivially reversible, CI lint+seam_check covers it.

title: state.finish_stage lacks a current-status guard (marks a rolled-back pending stage completed without raising)
description: Discovered during flow-6hn2/PR#292. state.finish_stage (state.py:256-274) has no guard on the stage's current status, unlike begin_stage (state.py:233-234 rejects status not in {pending,in_progress}). finish_stage's mutate unconditionally replaces the record with the target status. Consequence: after state.read quarantines a corrupt state.json and rolls back to a .bak where a stage is pending, cmd_finish(stage, completed) marks that stage completed without raising — the rollback is silent on the finish path. PR#292 mitigated the SYMPTOM (the state_recovered_from_backup marker now tells the do-loop to re-verify before re-running), but the deeper hardening — finish_stage refusing to finish a stage that is not in_progress (or at least flagging it) — was left out of scope. Decide whether finish_stage should mirror begin_stage's guard, or whether the marker mitigation is sufficient. EVIDENCE: state.py:256-274 (no guard) vs state.py:233-234 (begin_stage guard). BLAST RADIUS: state.py (guard file, hot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Option A — finish_stage gains a current-status guard raising ValueError when record.status != 'in_progress', mirroring begin_stage (state.py:233-234). B (warn-only) leaves the bad write; C (marker-only) contradicts the ticket's deeper-hardening framing. Safe to auto-ship: production blast = 3 call sites (dispatch cmd_next:426, flow_worktree _seed_state:172-176, state.py CLI), all begin-before-finish; reversible 2-line guard; raised guard rides the existing ValueError->exit-1 path at dispatch_stage.py:475 so /flow recover -> force_stage_status composes; full CI suite turns any broken fixture red pre-merge. is_hot=true but adjudicate_hot=true so proceed is not downgraded; merge-time guard-property review remains the keystone. Implementer committed to the FULL suite (mise run test + lint), not just test_state.py. test_reflect_inputs.py confirmed begins-first (no fixture breaks).

title: advisor->Agent adjudication fallback should trigger on rate-limited/erroring advisor, not only absent
description: verb-spec.md adjudication step 2 + the step-4 confidence-rating fallback specify the advisor->strong-tier-Agent fallback trigger as 'a ToolSearch for advisor returns nothing' (advisor ABSENT from the harness). They do NOT cover advisor PRESENT-but-RATE-LIMITED (or otherwise erroring). On the flow-4hug bg --auto run (Opus 4.8, advisor-adjudication branch), advisor() returned 'The advisor is rate-limited. Do not try to use it again this turn.' and the run fell back to the general-purpose Agent assessor by analogy (rate-limit == unavailable). Fix: in references/verb-spec.md, broaden the fallback trigger wording in (a) the adjudication-branch step 2 ('If advisor is not in this harness (a ToolSearch ... returns nothing) ...') and (b) the step-4 confidence-rating fallback ('fall back only after a ToolSearch for advisor returns nothing') to ALSO name 'or advisor errors / is rate-limited' as a fallback trigger. Non-hot prose (verb-spec.md is not a guard file). Severity: minor (handled correctly by analogy, no run impact). Evidence: flow knowledge id 20a24fd40d11a89d (flow-4hug).

comments.author: Victor De Simone

title: stage-merge.md §1 EVAL_STATUS conditional expansion mangles quoting in some shells; argparse sees 'unrecognized arguments'
description: Witnessed by the flow-ml7.2 run (2026-06-12): stage-merge.md §1 recipe uses ${EVAL_STATUS:+--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS"} which mangled quoting in the run's shell so evolve_self_merge argparse errored 'unrecognized arguments'; passing --eval-status plainly worked. Fix the prose recipe to a shell-safe form (e.g. build args array, or document plain pass-through). Blast radius: references/stage-merge.md only. The run could not self-edit (reflect already closed; a stage-doc prose edit does not govern its own run, flow-bzz).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SONNET-LADDER: sonnet-attempt-1

title: spec/bootstrap should refuse a CLOSED bead; reconcile tracker_cli get staleness vs bd/Dolt truth
description: WITNESS (flow-fd88, run 4539d6c298c06c85, 2026-06-12): /flow <key> --auto bootstrapped + ran a CLOSED, already-judged experiment cell to the implement stage before the closed state surfaced. flow-fd88 ([EXP of]) and its 2x2 parent flow-zajc are both closed (parent close reason: 'merged via [EXP ov] PR #264, wave-4 winner, v0.37.4'); fd88's own judge comment is a wave-4 DNF. ROOT CAUSE: tracker_cli.py get returned status=open (stale .beads/issues.jsonl passive export) while bd show + the ticket-stage transition probe (exit 3, available=['to open']) both read CLOSED from the local Dolt DB. The spec verb has hot-floor + duplicate-claim gates but NO closed/terminal-status gate, and it trusts tracker_cli get's status. FIX OPTIONS: (a) spec/flow_worktree.py create refuses (defer/exit) when the bead's authoritative (Dolt) status is closed/terminal; (b) make tracker_cli get read Dolt truth (or cross-check) rather than the stale jsonl export so status is trustworthy at plan time. Relates to flow-dpz0 (pre-launch bead staleness check). BLAST: spec path + tracker_cli get. Non-hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed — two-layer terminal-bead refusal. Root cause corrected: not stale jsonl (tracker_cli get already reads Dolt via bd show; get==bd==jsonl all agree CLOSED today) but a mid-run close RACE — fd88 was open at spec-fetch, closed by its parent epic's merge before the run advanced. Witnessed leak locus = stage-ticket.md step 3b: a CLOSED bead's open->in_progress transition returns exit 3, which the prose treats as benign ('already in_progress, continue silently'), conflating in_progress with closed. Fix (1 PR, both layers): (a) flow_worktree.py bootstrap gate (_refuse_terminal_bead via make_tracker(config).state(), refuse if normalized in {done,cancelled}, fail-OPEN on a genuine read exception only) before git worktree add -> new exit 6, --auto disposition silent-clean like exit 4 (no comment/status-change/friction); (b) stage-ticket.md step 3b: on transition exit 3, read tracker_cli state and FAIL the stage when normalized in {done,cancelled}, else continue silently (covers the post-bootstrap race, the actual witnessed window). Tracker-agnostic, reuses existing CLI/adapter surface. Safe to auto-ship: narrow blast (one status read each on the spec path + the exit-3 branch), pure refusal gates revert cleanly, bootstrap layer unit-tested, ticket-stage is seam-green prose. HOT via flow_worktree.py (guard file) — ships under adjudicate_hot=true + merge-time guard-property review + CI. Implementer commitments: (i) inject the gate's tracker read in tests by monkeypatching make_tracker/adapter.state (mirror how _enforce_hot_floor tests stub triage.decided), (ii) confirm the ticket stage is NOT re-run on resume so flow-749 mid-run auto-merge (bead CLOSED, run legitimately continues) can't trip the new gate, (iii) test that a confirmed-terminal read always refuses AND an empty/None/unparseable read does NOT proceed (fail-open stays exception-only).

title: pre-launch DNF retry should verify fix not already shipped by sibling cell
description:
flow-6wyb DNF retry (sf cell of flow-h8s7 2x2) spun up a full worktree + pipeline only to discover sibling cell [EXP sv]/PR#261 had already shipped the identical fix. No PR, wasted full spec->bootstrap->implement cycle.

ROOT CAUSE: the pre-launch staleness check (see bead flow-dpz0 / memory pre-launch-bead-staleness-check) covers config-fixes and closed siblings, but not the case where a sibling EXPERIMENT CELL has already landed the fix in @default. A retried DNF experiment bead should probe @default for the targeted code change before bootstrapping.

FIX: in the spec pre-launch staleness check (or as a new gate in verb-spec.md auto-approve path), when the bead has a parent experiment (parent=flow-h8s7-style), grep @default for the key invariant the fix would introduce (e.g. _check_flow_dir, the guard function name from the plan). If present -> close as superseded immediately, no worktree created.

BLAST RADIUS: verb-spec.md pre-launch staleness prose + optional seam_check gate. Non-hot.
close_reason: wontfix: anti-waste gate too narrow (experiment-cell-only path, infrequent) and grep-for-invariant hook is fuzzy; re-file if it recurs

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: STALENESS PASS 2026-06-19 (maintainer): valid, not stale, but LEAVING DEFERRED. Real anti-waste (prevents a full DNF-retry pipeline when a sibling experiment cell already landed the fix), non-hot, cheaply scoped to verb-spec.md. But payoff is narrow — keys on parent=experiment (a maintainer-only 2x2 evolve construct), and the 'grep @default for the plan's key invariant' hook needs the plan to name a greppable guard symbol (weak for diffuse fixes). Revisit if experiment-cell runs become frequent again.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-19: WONTFIX. Real anti-waste but too narrow to carry — only bites the experiment-cell DNF-retry path (maintainer-only 2x2 evolve construct, infrequent), and the 'grep @default for the plan's key invariant' hook needs the plan to name a greppable guard symbol (weak for diffuse fixes). Re-file if experiment-cell runs become frequent and the wasted-pipeline cost recurs.

title: Escalation ladder: also escalate defer-WITHOUT-substantive-question (not just open-bead DNFs)
description: Follow-up to flow-recv (PR #272, the tier:light + opus-escalation ladder). flow-recv's ladder (verb-evolve.md drain step C) only escalates OPEN-bead DNFs: a sonnet-tiered run that DNFs on context/rate-limit/infra leaves its bead open, re-appears in bd ready, and the launcher bumps it sonnet->opus->park. The original ticket ALSO listed 'defers WITHOUT a substantive open question' as an escalation trigger, but that case was scoped OUT (CQ1): a deferred bead sets status=deferred and leaves bd ready, so the prose-only launch-time ladder structurally cannot see it. Catching it needs (a) verb-spec.md to stamp a STRUCTURED defer-reason at defer-and-exit time distinguishing 'gave up, no real question' from 'has a substantive open question', and (b) a new drain sub-step that scans status=deferred beads and reopens+escalates the no-question ones to opus. Both are outside flow-recv's stated blast radius (evolve_select.py, queue_select.py, verb-evolve.md). BLAST RADIUS: verb-spec.md (defer-and-exit recipe + a defer-reason marker), verb-evolve.md drain (new deferred-scan sub-step). Non-hot prose-adjacent (verb-spec.md is NOT a guard file). EVIDENCE: flow-recv DECISION:(advisor) comment + advisor adjudication noting CQ1 is a requirement-drop not a ticket-answer.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: (advisor) proceed — auto-ship. Non-hot (verb-spec.md + verb-evolve.md are not guard files; decided-probe is_hot:false), maintainer-only evolve loop, reverts cheap, prose-only. Independent assessor scored 84%/proceed; all cited weaknesses closeable in-run, converted to mandatory implementer commitments below. Design: A3 is a pure status-flip (bd update --status open) that re-injects no-question deferred sonnet beads into the EXISTING open-bead ladder; A3 writes NO marker so the shared SONNET-LADDER attempt counter stays single-owner (step C owns the opus escalation write).

MANDATORY IMPLEMENTER COMMITMENTS:
1. advisor-defer split (verb-spec.md line-209 note): stamp [defer-reason: open-question] when the advisor defer cites maintainer-only information; [defer-reason: no-question] when it cites the advisor's OWN lack of confidence. The reason is in the one-paragraph ruling already in context.
2. empty/zero-intent BAIL is NOT escalatable: a BAIL whose reason is empty/zero-usable-intent ticket context stamps [defer-reason: open-question] (a stronger model cannot invent ticket content); only a planning give-up a stronger model could plausibly overcome stamps no-question.
3. A3 gate precision: A3 runs BEFORE step B, so model_per_key is not yet computed — A3 determines sonnet-tier-ness DIRECTLY from the bead labels (tier:trivial OR tier:light) with HOT-FIRST precedence (a hot bead with a tier label is NOT sonnet-tiered, never escalated by A3).
4. Run seam_check.py after the edits (both files are prose-CLI seam docs); confirm no new flag/subcommand and the doc tests stay green.

title: tier:light + opus-escalation ladder: sonnet whenever the context fits, opus as fallback
description:
Expand worker-model tiering from binary (tier:trivial -> sonnet, else strong) to context-footprint-gated, per the 2x2 experiment findings (flow-xqt verdict doc + flow-xqt.2 tallies): sonnet matched opus on every small-footprint task (W1/W2 parity; W3 sv WON; W4 both sonnet cells delivered, sf built the experiment's deepest artifact) and died ONLY on big-read tasks (3 context-overflow DNFs: metric.py ~1000 lines, 698-line seam_check, lease+dispatch guard files) — the predictor is read size, not task difficulty.

BUILD (three pieces, one PR):
1. NEW LABEL tier:light — stamped at filing by the audit/propose producers (verb-evolve.md section 2 criteria addition): non-hot AND <=2 planned files AND no touched file over ~500 lines AND no guard/cross-file seam work. Mutually exclusive with hot and tier:trivial (trivial subsumes light; keep both labels — trivial stays the stronger claim). Producers re-check file sizes at the freshness gate (sizes drift).
2. SELECTOR MAPPING — evolve_select.py + queue_select.py model_per_key: tier:light -> sonnet (same pass-through as tier:trivial today). Non-hot unlabeled stays [evolve] worker_model; hot unchanged (launcher default — experiment validated hot=opus unconditionally: sonnet overflowed on every guard-file task).
3. ESCALATION LADDER (drain prose, verb-evolve.md drain step C + reap/cleanup awareness): a sonnet-tiered run (trivial OR light) that DNFs (tempo blocked: rate-limit/context/infra) or defers WITHOUT a substantive open question gets ONE relaunch at opus on a later drain turn — strip no labels, the ladder is launch-time logic keyed on a prior-attempt marker (e.g. a bead comment 'sonnet-attempt-1 DNF <reason>' the selector prose checks). Second failure parks for the human as today. A defer WITH a real open question is NOT escalated (it needs an answer, not a bigger model).

EVIDENCE: flow-xqt.2 wave tallies (W3 sf 'Prompt is too long' vs W4 sf success = footprint, not tier); registered scorecards on flow-6wyb/flow-cfj8/flow-g0yf/flow-p4pp/flow-vhuk. Worker-model seam shipped in flow-ly9r/PR#245; tier:trivial semantics in verb-evolve.md section 2 (hot/trivial mutual exclusion).
BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.py, queue_select.py, verb-evolve.md (guard-adjacent prose: drain step C; verb-evolve is not a guard file), tests for both selectors. Non-hot. The label is judgment-stamped like tier:trivial today — no new script gate.
MEASURABLE: tier stamped into ship events since flow-a1ti.1/PR#250, so light-tier revert/follow-up correlation is trackable from day one.
close_reason: Shipped PR #272 (merged 2026-06-12, v0.39.0): tier:light label + selector mapping (evolve_select/queue_select -> sonnet) + one-shot opus escalation ladder in drain prose

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
DECISION: (advisor) proceed — auto-ship. Non-hot (no guard file in planned set), reverts cheap, the only code change (selector model_per_key) is unit-tested, pieces 1+3 are prose that cannot break runtime. Advisor ruled the design sound (mark-at-launch + infer-DNF-from-reappearance-in-bd-ready; deferred-with-questions structurally excluded since it leaves bd ready). Verdict is on the REVISED scope below.

MANDATORY IMPLEMENTER COMMITMENTS (folded from the advisor's closeable holes):
1. The tier:light->sonnet clause must hit EVERY resolution-prose locus, not just verb-evolve §2: verb-evolve.md §2 (label criteria) + §3 line 81 (--labels pass-through note) + drain step C lines 226 & 235 (model-resolution restatements); AND verb-queue.md lines 43/58/99 (day-job resolution restatements). Doc-drift-spans-references — keep all loci consistent.
2. planned_files INCLUDES verb-queue.md (its model_per_key resolution prose is a sibling of the queue_select.py code change; omitting it ships doc drift / trips the post-implement reconcile).
3. Piece 3 escalation ladder is PROSE-ONLY in verb-evolve.md drain step C (selectors stay pure no-side-effect functions). Mechanism: before each launch read bd show <key> --include-comments; no marker -> launch at selector-resolved model + write a marker comment under a DISTINCT stem (e.g. SONNET-LADDER:, NOT a _DECISION_/_DEFER_ stem, to avoid triage-scan + decided-probe collision); sonnet-attempt-1 marker present (key re-appeared in bd ready => DNF) -> override model to opus (omit --model so it inherits launcher opus default) + write attempt-2 marker; attempt-2 present (second failure) -> do NOT relaunch, bd update <key> --status blocked + comment with the 'flow --auto could not self-approve' triage stem (parks for human).
4. CQ2 resolved per ticket: the WHOLE open-bead DNF class escalates (ticket names rate-limit/context/infra as triggers). Keep the documented risk note: a transient global rate-limit can burn an opus attempt and park a good bead — accepted (false negatives accepted in this repo).

SCOPE CUT (CQ1): defer-WITHOUT-a-substantive-open-question escalation is SCOPED OUT of this PR. A deferred bead leaves bd ready, so the prose-only launch-time ladder structurally cannot see it; catching it needs verb-spec.md defer-reason stamping + a deferred-bead drain scan, both OUTSIDE the ticket's stated blast radius (evolve_select.py, queue_select.py, verb-evolve.md, tests). Scoping to open-bead DNFs covers the 3 motivating context-overflow cases exactly. A follow-up bead is filed to preserve the dropped requirement.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CQ1 follow-up filed as flow-4hug (defer-without-substantive-question escalation, scoped out of PR #272). PR #272 is green + version-stamped (0.39.0) + review-clean; self-merge was blocked by the auto-mode classifier (human-merge keystone) — needs manual merge.

title: ops deadman v2: crash-capture via trap-EXIT + hung-run grace detection (harvest closed PR #266)
description: FOLLOW-UP from the 2x2 experiment quality-first re-scoring (flow-xqt.2 sensitivity addendum; wave-4 judge scorecard flagged this as the deepest design of the wave). The merged wave-4 winner (PR #264) records 'end ok' only on clean success — a crashed runner leaves a start-only record detected solely via the 36h staleness bar. Closed PR #266 ([EXP sf]) did it strictly better: _RUN_OUTCOME=fail default + trap-EXIT writing end+outcome on ANY exit (crash captured immediately), plus a hook-side hung-run condition (start with no end past grace: >3h nightly / >6h weekly) — the witnessed zombie class. HARVEST: fold trap-EXIT recording + the hung-run grace condition into the merged ops templates + session-start.py staleness check (#266's diff is the reference; keep #264's jsonl record shape, do NOT switch to #266's plain-text per-schedule files). Reminder: templates are copy-deployed, note manual redeploy. BLAST RADIUS: ops templates + hooks/session-start.py + hook tests. Non-hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) PROCEED, ~88%. Harvest #266's trap-EXIT + outcome=fail-default + hung-run grace onto #264's merged JSONL baseline (keep run-record.jsonl shape, reject #266 plain-text per-schedule files). Non-hot, copy-deployed templates inert until manual redeploy, hook exception-swallowed, clean revert. Mandatory implementer commitments: (1) add a hung-detection test that discriminates the fix from #266 — nightly start T-50h, end T-49h ok, start T-4h must read HUNG (last_start>last_end_ts past 3h grace), NOT stale; #266's last_end-is-None branch would mis-read stale. (2) hand-smoke the template EXIT trap inside the {...}|tee subshell: forced early-exit after record start writes end fail; happy path reaches _RUN_OUTCOME=ok then end ok; set _RUN_OUTCOME=fail before registering trap; delete literal 'record end ok'. (3) README documents the JSONL shape + new outcome/hung/grace semantics, do NOT paste #266's plain-text table. PR writeup: trap-EXIT-fail carries bulk of value (immediate crash signal vs 36h wait); hung-detection complements (catches SIGKILL/reboot, not the wrapper-completes-but-claude-zombie case #264 scoped out). Do not expand to outcome-on-completed-run detection.

title: metric workspace guard: align to .flow/.initialized (harvest closed PR #263's convention)
description: FOLLOW-UP from the 2x2 experiment quality-first re-scoring (flow-xqt.2 sensitivity addendum). The merged wave-3 winner (PR #261) guards metric.py on a bare .flow/ dir; the spec (original flow-h8s7) named the validate_workspace convention, which is .flow/.initialized (validate_workspace.py:277). Closed PR #263 ([EXP of]) implemented exactly that: _require_workspace gating on .flow/.initialized with a not-a-workspace stderr naming the resolved root. HARVEST: align the merged guard to the .initialized marker (one predicate change + fixture updates; #263's diff is the reference). Divergence today: a .flow dir without the marker passes metric's guard but fails validate_workspace. BLAST RADIUS: metric.py + tests. Non-hot, small.

title: is_shipped (beads) conflates closed with shipped; reflect observes a false ship event for closed-unmerged beads
description: tracker_beads.BeadsAdapter.is_shipped (tracker_beads.py:551-611) returns state=not_yet_observed whenever status==closed AND _git_log_first_commit(key) finds any commit referencing the key, with NO gate on whether that commit landed on the default branch or the PR merged. Reflect step 5 (stage-reflect.md) treats not_yet_observed as 'landed-but-not-yet-frozen' and observes an immutable ship event. So a bead closed administratively-unmerged (e.g. an experiment cell closed with 'lost tiebreak, PR closed unmerged', or any manual close of an open-PR bead) whose work commit sits on its feature branch reads as shipped and stamps a FALSE ship event into the flow-vs-control metric corpus. Witnessed on flow-qmtd: PR #259 state=CLOSED merged=False, commit 40c1255 NOT in origin/main (HEAD 6dac335), bead closed 'wave-2: lost tiebreak. PR #259 closed unmerged' -> is-shipped returned not_yet_observed (false positive). This run skipped the observation by hand on merge-evidence override (PR merged=False + commit absent from origin/main). FIX (non-trivial, beads has no native merge/PR state): gate not_yet_observed on the commit being reachable from the default branch (git merge-base --is-ancestor <commit> origin/<default>), or consult the forge for PR merge state, or honor a closed-unmerged closure_reason. Note: is-shipped evidence reported closure_reason: null while bd show displayed a real 'Close reason' string — the JSON closure_reason field may not carry the displayed reason, so closure_reason alone is an unreliable signal. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet. Not a hot/guard file.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: PRE-LAUNCH VALIDATION (2026-06-12, vs main v0.41.0): gap confirmed at tracker_beads.py:551-611 + _git_log_first_commit:713. TWO SPEC CORRECTIONS: (1) do NOT use git merge-base --is-ancestor as the shipped-gate; this repo squash-merges, so evidence.commit_sha (feature-branch tip) is NEVER an ancestor of main and the check false-negatives every legitimately shipped bead. Use the key-in-commit-message grep over git log origin/<default>, mirroring PR#274's revert-watcher join. (2) closure_reason null is a wrong-key bug: bd JSON carries close_reason, adapter reads closure_reason; honoring close_reason is viable as secondary signal. If the fix repoints commit_sha to the on-main sha, also update metric.py's comment describing commit_sha as branch tip.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — non-hot, single adapter method, pure logic, revertible, unit-covered. Gate is_shipped not_yet_observed on a default-branch commit per maintainer corrections. Mandatory implementer commitments (verified pre-bootstrap): (1) KEEP a word-boundary guard on the key match — prefix false-positive is real: the key lands in the squash BODY as a 'ticket: <key>' trailer, and 'git log origin/main --grep=flow-a1ti' matches children flow-a1ti.2/.3; grep %B and re-check with the metric.py word-boundary regex (?<![\w.-])key(?![\w.-]), mirroring PR#274. (2) Read bd's close_reason field, not closure_reason (confirmed: flow-qmtd.close_reason populated, closure_reason null); keep the evidence KEY name closure_reason to avoid schema/doc drift. (3) Best-effort 'git fetch origin <default>' before the grep — only stage-reflect calls is_shipped (no observation sweep), so switching from HEAD to origin/main without a fetch would REGRESS the hot-auto-merge observation that today works via the feature commit on HEAD; suppress fetch failure (offline/sandbox). metric.py 'tip' comment update is moot (no such comment exists). Files: tracker_beads.py, tests/test_beads_adapter.py, inventory.md.

title: [EXP ov] lease identity has no per-session component: second /flow do on the same ticket passes the mutex against a LIVE lease
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:137-141 cmd_init reuses run_id from state.json on resume; lease.py:241-252 acquire checks existing.run_id == run_id BEFORE expiry (owner re-acquire succeeds on a live lease); lease.py:329-337 assert_lease_still_mine checks only run_id/boot/hostname (pid informational, lease.py:62). FAILURE SEQUENCE: bg --auto run A mid-merge-stage CI wait, lease live and heartbeating (#231). Operator misjudges it dead, runs /flow do T in the same worktree: B's init reads the same state.json -> same run_id -> owner-re-acquire branch passes despite the live lease. Every later guard is blind (identical run_id/boot/host); either side's release drops the other's live lease; A and B interleave begin/finish on one state.json -> double-executed non-idempotent stages (duplicate commits/PR/merge). This is the exact 'two concurrent /flow do on one ticket' the lease.py:1 docstring claims to prevent; #231 closed the drain-reap path only. FIX DIRECTION (tighten-only): mint a per-acquire session nonce stored in the lease and carried by the dispatching session (not derivable from state.json); owner re-acquire of a LIVE lease requires the nonce; --force/takeover rotates it. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py + dispatch_stage.py (guard files, hot, every dispatch call). Needs corpus/test coverage for acquire/re-acquire/takeover paths.

EXPERIMENT CELL ov of 2x2 (parent flow-t7wu); do not coordinate with sibling cells. Treat this bead as the complete task specification.
close_reason: wave-6 ov: 8/10, lost to of on score. PR #269 closed unmerged.

TOTAL 8/10. time-to-PR 18m01s. Lost on score — the wave the rubric finally discriminated.

description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:137-141 cmd_init reuses run_id from state.json on resume; lease.py:241-252 acquire checks existing.run_id == run_id BEFORE expiry (owner re-acquire succeeds on a live lease); lease.py:329-337 assert_lease_still_mine checks only run_id/boot/hostname (pid informational, lease.py:62). FAILURE SEQUENCE: bg --auto run A mid-merge-stage CI wait, lease live and heartbeating (#231). Operator misjudges it dead, runs /flow do T in the same worktree: B's init reads the same state.json -> same run_id -> owner-re-acquire branch passes despite the live lease. Every later guard is blind (identical run_id/boot/host); either side's release drops the other's live lease; A and B interleave begin/finish on one state.json -> double-executed non-idempotent stages (duplicate commits/PR/merge). This is the exact 'two concurrent /flow do on one ticket' the lease.py:1 docstring claims to prevent; #231 closed the drain-reap path only. FIX DIRECTION (tighten-only): mint a per-acquire session nonce stored in the lease and carried by the dispatching session (not derivable from state.json); owner re-acquire of a LIVE lease requires the nonce; --force/takeover rotates it. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py + dispatch_stage.py (guard files, hot, every dispatch call). Needs corpus/test coverage for acquire/re-acquire/takeover paths.

title: [EXP of] seam_check v2: gate the descriptor stdout-JSON keys and role-string literals the do-loop prose parses
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): seam_check validates only argparse flags/subcommands (seam_check.py:36-38, validate() :283) + MODULE.md/registry gates. But SKILL.md:111-114 tells the agent to parse ~16 stdout-JSON keys (done, blocked_by, reason, stage, handler_type, head_sha, ticket_dir, output_path, roles, reference_doc, subagent_type, skill_name, skill_args, reconciled_drift, finished, holder, run_id) -- all currently present in dispatch_stage.py (:59-70,:159-217,:346-379,:507), zero gate. A key rename passes seam_check + unit tests and breaks every run at SKILL.md step 4b. Sibling ungated literal: stage-registry.toml:75 roles=[records_diff_baseline] whose ONLY reader is SKILL.md:116 prose; renaming it silently disables the implement diff-baseline hook that the commit stage's git apply depends on. FIX: extend seam_check with (1) a descriptor-key gate -- extract the emitted-key set from dispatch_stage (static dict literals or a declared constant) and check prose-cited keys against it; (2) a role-literal gate -- prose role strings must exist in stage-registry roles arrays. Same one-directional missing-only philosophy as the surface-cell gate (flow-yyn). Out of scope (note for later, do not build now): exit-code cites, embedded .flow paths, PR_URL= token. BLAST RADIUS: seam_check.py + tests; possibly a small declared-constant refactor in dispatch_stage.py (then hot). High value: this is the same drift class seam_check was built for, one layer deeper.

title: [EXP of] ops deadman: scheduled evolve loop writes a durable run-record; SessionStart hook surfaces staleness
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10): ops/nightly-evolve.sh.template + ops/weekly-epic.sh.template only tee to ~/.flow-evolve/logs/<ts>.log -- no run-record, no heartbeat, no staleness surface (grep heartbeat/deadman/stale in ops/ empty). The failure mode is witnessed, not hypothesized: the nightly template's own PATH comment (lines 26-29) is the fossil of a real silent death (launchd bare-PATH -> claude command not found, masked by by-hand testing); zombie --bg --auto runs were a second witnessed class. A dead nightly loop is currently discovered only by noticing PRs stopped appearing. Skeptic-adjudicated task-sized (not epic). FIX (one PR): (1) ~5 lines per template appending start/end/outcome to a durable run-record file; (2) a staleness function + render block in plugins/flow/hooks/session-start.py (fires every SessionStart, walks to any .flow root; absence of the record file naturally gates the warning to machines where the schedule is armed) warning 'nightly loop stale >36h / weekly >8d'; (3) note the manual redeploy step in the PR (templates are copy-deployed to ~/.flow-evolve/, the fix is inert on the live machine until redeployed). BLAST RADIUS: ops templates + hooks/session-start.py + hook tests. Non-hot, additive observability. Does not resurrect flow-qp7's per-run heartbeat (different object: per-schedule-fire).

EXPERIMENT CELL of of 2x2 (parent flow-zajc); do not coordinate with sibling cells. Treat this bead as the complete task specification.

comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Clean non-hot additive observability; design faithful to the per-schedule-fire bead intent (age-only epoch gate, not per-run health). Safe to auto-ship: low blast (maintainer-local templates + file-presence-gated additive hook path), revertible, CI runs hook tests. MANDATORY implementer commitment (closeable in-run): inject the flow-evolve base dir into session-start.py cli_main via an optional kwarg defaulting to Path.home()/'.flow-evolve' (NOT a hardcoded Path.home() globally monkeypatched in tests) so test_cli_main_silent_outside_workspace stays environment-independent on the armed maintainer machine and the new independence tests share the same DI seam. Matches flow-cfj8 pattern.

title: [EXP of] metric.py silently returns all-zeros when invoked from the wrong cwd (workspace-root resolution finds empty workspace, exit 0)
description:
EVIDENCE (reproduced live 2026-06-10): running recall --metric / metric.py from inside plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts resolves an empty workspace and returns all-zero metrics with exit 0; the same invocation from the repo root returns real data (tickets-per-week shipped=18, time-to-pr n=4, revert 0/17, friction 1.43/46 runs). This nearly produced a false 'no data accumulated' conclusion during an epic-lens analysis -- an unguarded consumer (the future trend roll-up, a scheduled producer, or the flow-oe9 revisit check) would mis-read loop health the same way. FIX: when workspace resolution finds no .flow (or an empty events store) fail loud (non-zero exit + stderr naming the resolved root) or at minimum stamp the resolved workspace root into the JSON output so consumers can sanity-check. Match whatever convention validate_workspace uses for not-a-workspace. BLAST RADIUS: metric.py + tests; possibly recall.py forwarding (getattr-gate any new arg per the flow-nn4 memory). Non-hot.

EXPERIMENT CELL of of 2x2 (parent flow-h8s7); do not coordinate with sibling cells. Treat this bead as the complete task specification.

Both are in-run verifiable and red-CI-caught if missed.

title: [EXP of] pending_mutations.compute_key: production-dead export with its logic duplicated inline in _do_append
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): compute_key def at pending_mutations.py:83-85; git grep -wn compute_key over the whole repo returns only the def, its __all__ entry (:330), and tests/test_pending_mutations.py. cli_main (:309) wires only append/list/compact; no prose names compute-key. Smoking gun: _do_append at pending_mutations.py:122 reimplements the identical sha256(ticket+op+canonical)[:16] hash inline instead of calling compute_key, so the idempotency-key recipe is defined twice and tested only via the copy production never runs (silent-divergence hazard). Contrast the live sibling pattern: recall_pending.compute_pending_id IS called by its production path (recall_pending.py:88,151). FIX (either): (a) make _do_append:122 call compute_key (makes export+tests honest), or (b) delete compute_key + __all__ entry + retarget its tests at append_mutation output. BLAST RADIUS: pending_mutations.py + tests/test_pending_mutations.py. No seam impact (no prose/CLI refs).

EXPERIMENT CELL of of 2x2 (parent flow-mfjn); do not coordinate with sibling cells. Treat this bead as the complete task specification.

title: [EXP of] self-evolution.md still claims hot/conflicted PRs always wait for a human (auto_merge_hot + version_recoverable drift)
description:
EVIDENCE (loci, verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): self-evolution.md:41 reap described as 'hot / not-green / conflicted -> left for the human'; :47 'conflicts always wait for a human'; :62 'Hot / non-green / conflicted always wait for a human.' title: [EXP sv] lease identity has no per-session component: second /flow do on the same ticket passes the mutex against a LIVE lease
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:137-141 cmd_init reuses run_id from state.json on resume; lease.py:241-252 acquire checks existing.run_id == run_id BEFORE expiry (owner re-acquire succeeds on a live lease); lease.py:329-337 assert_lease_still_mine checks only run_id/boot/hostname (pid informational, lease.py:62). FAILURE SEQUENCE: bg --auto run A mid-merge-stage CI wait, lease live and heartbeating (#231). Operator misjudges it dead, runs /flow do T in the same worktree: B's init reads the same state.json -> same run_id -> owner-re-acquire branch passes despite the live lease. Every later guard is blind (identical run_id/boot/host); either side's release drops the other's live lease; A and B interleave begin/finish on one state.json -> double-executed non-idempotent stages (duplicate commits/PR/merge). This is the exact 'two concurrent /flow do on one ticket' the lease.py:1 docstring claims to prevent; #231 closed the drain-reap path only. FIX DIRECTION (tighten-only): mint a per-acquire session nonce stored in the lease and carried by the dispatching session (not derivable from state.json); owner re-acquire of a LIVE lease requires the nonce; --force/takeover rotates it. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py + dispatch_stage.py (guard files, hot, every dispatch call). Needs corpus/test coverage for acquire/re-acquire/takeover paths.

title: [EXP sv] seam_check v2: gate the descriptor stdout-JSON keys and role-string literals the do-loop prose parses
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): seam_check validates only argparse flags/subcommands (seam_check.py:36-38, validate() :283) + MODULE.md/registry gates. But SKILL.md:111-114 tells the agent to parse ~16 stdout-JSON keys (done, blocked_by, reason, stage, handler_type, head_sha, ticket_dir, output_path, roles, reference_doc, subagent_type, skill_name, skill_args, reconciled_drift, finished, holder, run_id) -- all currently present in dispatch_stage.py (:59-70,:159-217,:346-379,:507), zero gate. A key rename passes seam_check + unit tests and breaks every run at SKILL.md step 4b. Sibling ungated literal: stage-registry.toml:75 roles=[records_diff_baseline] whose ONLY reader is SKILL.md:116 prose; renaming it silently disables the implement diff-baseline hook that the commit stage's git apply depends on. FIX: extend seam_check with (1) a descriptor-key gate -- extract the emitted-key set from dispatch_stage (static dict literals or a declared constant) and check prose-cited keys against it; (2) a role-literal gate -- prose role strings must exist in stage-registry roles arrays. Same one-directional missing-only philosophy as the surface-cell gate (flow-yyn). Out of scope (note for later, do not build now): exit-code cites, embedded .flow paths, PR_URL= token. BLAST RADIUS: seam_check.py + tests; possibly a small declared-constant refactor in dispatch_stage.py (then hot). High value: this is the same drift class seam_check was built for, one layer deeper.

title: [EXP sv] ops deadman: scheduled evolve loop writes a durable run-record; SessionStart hook surfaces staleness
description:
EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10): ops/nightly-evolve.sh.template + ops/weekly-epic.sh.template only tee to ~/.flow-evolve/logs/<ts>.log -- no run-record, no heartbeat, no staleness surface (grep heartbeat/deadman/stale in ops/ empty). The failure mode is witnessed, not hypothesized: the nightly template's own PATH comment (lines 26-29) is the fossil of a real silent death (launchd bare-PATH -> claude command not found, masked by by-hand testing); zombie --bg --auto runs were a second witnessed class. A dead nightly loop is currently discovered only by noticing PRs stopped appearing. Skeptic-adjudicated task-sized (not epic). FIX (one PR): (1) ~5 lines per template appending start/end/outcome to a durable run-record file; (2) a staleness function + render block in plugins/flow/hooks/session-start.py (fires every SessionStart, walks to any .flow root; absence of the record file naturally gates the warning to machines where the schedule is armed) warning 'nightly loop stale >36h / weekly >8d'; (3) note the manual redeploy step in the PR (templates are copy-deployed to ~/.flow-evolve/, the fix is inert on the live machine until redeployed). BLAST RADIUS: ops templates + hooks/session-start.py + hook tests. Non-hot, additive observability. Does not resurrect flow-qp7's per-run heartbeat (different object: per-schedule-fire).

title: a1ti child-3 (HOT): per-drain-turn main-CI health gate -- red main pauses auto-merge + files a P0 bead
description:
Child 3 of accepted epic flow-a1ti. HOT -- guards the live self-merge surface.

GAP: auto-merge proves PR CI green pre-merge, but nothing checks MAIN's health; two concurrently-green PRs that semantically conflict land on main untested, and the red is discovered only when a later run inherits it. No daemon exists to host a standing watcher, so this is a PER-DRAIN-TURN GATE (skeptic correction), not a watcher.

BUILD: at each drain turn (and before any evolve_self_merge/reap promotion), probe the default branch's CI rollup (forge_cli ci-rollup on the latest main sha -- absorb the known transient-401 flake per the hardened CI-wait probe pattern, PR#238); if red: pause hot+non-hot auto-merge for the turn, file a deduped P0 bead naming the failing sha/check, surface in the drain report. Green or transient-noise resumes normally. CANCELLED/SKIPPED/NEUTRAL are not failures (PR#238 memory).

BLAST RADIUS: evolve_self_merge.py / evolve_reap.py decision path + verb-evolve.md drain prose + harness corpus cases for the new decider branch. HOT (merge-path machinery); adjudicate via the normal hot merge-guard review.

Per-drain-turn main-CI health gate before any evolve_self_merge/reap promotion; red main pauses auto-merge for the turn + files deduped P0; CANCELLED/SKIPPED/NEUTRAL not failures; absorb transient-401 per PR#238 probe pattern. Hot: ship via adjudicate_hot merge-time guard review.

title: a1ti child-2: revert watcher -- join ship events to tracker reopens AND git log reverts on main; durable revert events + readout
description:
Child 2 of accepted epic flow-a1ti. DEPENDS ON child-1 (tier stamp makes revert-by-tier correlation possible).

BUILD: extend the revert detection beyond tracker-only. Today metric.py _classify_revert joins ship events to bd reopen->reclose timelines; a git revert on main with no bead reopen is INVISIBLE. Add: scan git log on the default branch for revert commits (git log --grep=revert + Revert-of subject parse), join to ship events by PR/commit sha, emit durable revert events through the ship-event seam (append, never mutate). Output: a readout line in the metric (reverts by source: tracker vs git) -- NOT auto-filed feedstock beads; the skeptic dropped revert->feedstock until n_reverts >= 1 (zero reverts ever as of 2026-06-10; revisit when the first one lands).

BLAST RADIUS: metric.py + observe_ship_event.py seam + tests. Non-hot. Guard against scripts/metric.py::cwd-silent-empty (flow-h8s7).
close_reason: Shipped PR #274 (merged 2026-06-12, v0.40.0): git-revert watcher on main, durable revert events via ship-event seam, reverts-by-source readout (tracker vs git)

title: a1ti child-1: stamp tier + plugin version into ship events and friction entries (forward-only, immutable)

Child 1 of accepted epic flow-a1ti (merged-outcome feedback). FORWARD-ONLY: every merge before this lands is permanently uncorrelatable -- highest urgency in the epic.

BUILD: observe_ship_event.py stamps two new script-owned keys at observe time (ml7.1/PR#175 pattern): tier (from the bead's tier:trivial label at ship time -- the label is MUTABLE and un-stamping destroys history, the stamp is the immutable record) and plugin_version (the flow plugin version that produced the run, read from plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json at observe time -- this is the prose-attribution salvage: lets recall --metric correlate a prose/plugin release with subsequent friction-per-run and revert rate). flow_friction.py entries gain the same plugin_version stamp. Extra-key rejection contract: both scripts own their stamped keys; update the allowed/owned key lists + stage-reflect.md:192 owned-keys parenthetical (coordinate with flow-26zg which fixes that same line).

COORDINATION: flow-xqt.1 (arm field) edits the same observe_ship_event.py seam -- SERIALIZE after it (xqt.1 launched 2026-06-10). Child 3 of accepted epic flow-xqt. DEPENDS ON child-2 (needs >=N completed pairs).

BUILD: the honest in-repo writeup of the pre-registered comparison: the numbers per arm, the verdict against the PRE-REGISTERED criterion (no moving goalposts), and the live possibility stated up front in the epic -- vanilla may win on small leaves with flow paying off only at concurrency/fleet scale; if so, that BOUNDS where the machinery belongs and is itself the finding (feeds VISION's 'better must be measurable'). Include: per-pair table, aggregate stats, confound notes (pairs that broke the size-class match, interventions that contaminated an arm), and the decision recommendation (keep/expand/bound the state-machine layer). Location: docs/ or repo root next to VISION.md -- spec decides. BLAST RADIUS: one doc. Non-hot.
