Also witnessed: evolve_select anchor partition cannot see this collision pre-bootstrap (anchor derives from planned_files, stamped at bootstrap; a launch-ledger marker carries no file info) — same-file beads slip the partition during the launch→init window. Launch after pu38 lands.

title: forge seam: GH_TOKEN fallback when gh keyring auth fails 401 in headless runs
description: MACHINERY (surfaced flow-8l6q run 5fe2f2a5b052f855, friction BLOCKER major). create_pr.py + forge_cli.py rely on gh's ambient (keyring) auth. In a headless/detached --auto session, gh keyring token retrieval is unreliable: gh api (REST 'gh api user' AND GraphQL 'gh pr list') returned HTTP 401 8/8 times, sandboxed and unsandboxed, even though 'gh auth status' showed logged-in (vdsmon, repo scope) and 'gh auth token' reliably returned a valid gho_ token. Exporting GH_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) made every gh call succeed immediately. This blocked create_pr for a full turn and required manual GH_TOKEN injection to recover. FIX (proposed): in the github forge adapter (forge_cli.py / create_pr.py gh invocation path), when a gh call fails with HTTP 401, retry once after seeding GH_TOKEN from 'gh auth token' (or seed it proactively when present). Scope to the gh seam; keep it a single retry to avoid masking a genuine auth-expired state. Evidence: create_pr.py forge detect step calls gh pr list; forge_cli.py wraps gh. corpus delta: not scored - no concrete edit yet. Not a 9-file guard file; not hot.
close_reason: Root cause was macOS keychain contention under parallel gh processes, not a missing engine fallback; fixed at config layer (token re-stored via gh auth login --with-token --insecure-storage → hosts.yml, keychain out of hot path; verified 8 parallel calls green). Engine-side GH_TOKEN fallback would be speculative plumbing for a solved environment problem.

title: drain step C: advance checkout + marketplace update at clean batch boundaries (mid-drain)
description: Today the post-drain-only advance meant every batch all day ran pre-drain code: the #230 orphan recovery hit the bare-git-push bug whose fix (flow-wjfs/PR#225) was already merged, ztfv's lease-heartbeat and 7eqw's self-tombstone stayed inert for hours, and the defer-bar policy (z8wd/PR#221) only governed runs launched after a manual advance. Fix: verb-evolve.md drain step C gains a pre-launch advance -- when live_runs is empty AND launched_pending is empty (no run mid-pipeline whose stage prose could be swapped underfoot), run the existing post-drain advance recipe (git fetch + checkout -- .beads/ + merge --ff-only origin/main + claude plugin marketplace update vdsmon-flow, try-and-skip never force) BEFORE fanning out the batch. Mid-run code swaps are the hazard this guards: a running session reads stage reference docs on demand, so the advance must only happen when nothing is mid-pipeline. Same-drain compounding instead of next-drain. Prose-only change to verb-evolve.md step C + the post-drain section gets a cross-ref. Maintainer requested 2026-06-10.

title: review_loop headless CI poll dumps a traceback on empty ci-rollup
description: references/stage-review_loop.md section 1 headless-fallback poll: `forge_cli ci-rollup 2>/dev/null | python -c 'import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin).get("status","pending"))'`. Before checks register, ci-rollup returns empty/non-JSON stdout, so json.load raises JSONDecodeError and the inline python prints a full multi-line traceback into the run output on each early poll. Cosmetic only (the loop continues, reached green at flow-s85/PR#235), but noisy in --auto run logs. Fix: guard the inline parse (read stdin first, treat empty/JSONDecodeError as 'pending') in the documented recipe, or have forge_cli ci-rollup always emit {status:pending} when no checks exist yet. Owning file: references/stage-review_loop.md section 1. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only edit, no scripts/*.py change.

title: Port hardened PR-watch probe to stage-review_loop.md §1 + stage-merge.md CI re-waits
description: flow-vmzu/PR#234 added a hardened PR-watch Monitor probe (forge_cli ci-rollup exit-code check + detect-pr null short-circuit + consecutive-error budget + guaranteed terminal break on green/failed/merged-closed/error-budget/cap) to references/verb-evolve.md version_recoverable CI-rewait. The SAME latent spin bug remains unfixed in two siblings: references/stage-review_loop.md:25 (§1 CI-wait Monitor) and references/stage-merge.md:103,123 (version-stamp re-wait + version_remerge re-wait). All use 'ci-rollup ... 2>/dev/null | python -c "...get(\"status\",\"pending\")"' which swallows the process exit code, so an errored gh reads as pending forever (no error budget -> silent hang). EMPIRICAL: the review_loop §1 poll for PR#234 itself took 2 transient gh HTTP 401s mid-poll during flow-vmzu's own run; only a hand-hardened poll survived to green. Fix: port the verb-evolve.md hardened probe to both docs (style-match each site; stage-merge re-waits know $PR_ID; stage-review_loop knows $PR_ID). Prose-only. CI_STATUS enum is green/pending/failed (forge.py:47), not success/failure/red. Confidence: high (the fix pattern is already shipped + dogfooded in verb-evolve.md). corpus delta: not scored — prose-only, no scripts/*.py edit. Blast radius: review_loop + merge stage prose.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Port the canonical hardened PR-watch probe (verbatim from PR#234 branch) into stage-review_loop.md §1 and stage-merge.md §3, inlining self-contained (no cross-ref to verb-evolve.md, since #234 is still open and edits a different file → no conflict). For stage-merge's two re-wait sites, define the probe ONCE at the end of §3 and reference from both placeholders (mirrors verb-evolve house pattern). $PR→$PR_ID; add BRANCH binding in review_loop §1. Safe to auto-ship: prose-only, non-hot, one-commit-revertible; worst case of a mis-port is a bounded recoverable Monitor hang (cap=25/timeout), not corruption; seam_check gates the load-bearing prose↔CLI seam; the probe is already dogfooded green on #234.

title: merge-stage §3 push-state guard @{u} false-negatives in bg --auto worktree
description: stage-merge.md §3's push-state guard uses 'git rev-parse @{u}' to prove all local commits were pushed/CI'd. In a bg 'claude --bg /flow <key> --auto' run the feature branch has NO upstream tracking ref (create_pr.py pushes the branch but without -u, and the sandboxed shared main .git/config write is blocked — same root as the worktree-push-no-upstream-tracking memory / flow-wjfs). So 'git rev-parse @{u}' returns empty, HEAD \!= '' is true, and the guard reports 'uncommitted/unpushed' -> every self-merge on a bg --auto run would be skipped and left for the human/drain, defeating Layer-2 self-merge for the autonomous path. Observed on flow-495l/PR#233 (this run): worked around by directly verifying origin/<branch> ref == HEAD == gh PR headRefOid == CI-green SHA, then proceeding. The stamp-commit push also needed an explicit 'git push origin HEAD:<branch>' (bare 'git push' exits 128 with no tracking). Fix candidate: make the §3 push-state guard compare HEAD against the resolved remote ref (origin/<branch>, or gh headRefOid) instead of @{u}, and document the explicit-refspec push for the stamp commit — so bg --auto self-merge is not blocked by the missing tracking ref. stage-merge.md is a reference doc (non-hot). Related: friction id dd7ea786, sibling bead flow-06jg (orchestrator .out capture), and the parent flow-495l/PR#233 which fixed EnterWorktree + subagent edits. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only, no scripts/*.py edit.
close_reason: superseded by merged work on origin/main: §3 push-state guard @{u}->origin/$BRANCH (PR#230) + explicit 'git push origin <branch>' for stamp commit (PR#225). No-op against @default (flow-749 drift). No PR opened.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto: already resolved on origin/main — no-op fix (flow-749 drift-vs-base). Both fix points the ticket asks for are present at origin/main: (1) §3 push-state guard compares HEAD against $(git rev-parse origin/$BRANCH) after 'git fetch origin $BRANCH', NOT @{u} — landed by PR#230 (508df30, 'merge §3 push-state guard reads origin/$BRANCH not @{u} (empty in flow worktrees)'); (2) stamp + restamp commits push via explicit 'git push origin $BRANCH' — landed by PR#225 (a2b250c, 'version_remerge recover + stamp push use explicit git push origin <branch>'). Verified: no residual 'git rev-parse @{u}' comparison and no bare 'git push' remain in stage-merge.md at origin/main; prose §3 (line ~182) rewritten to explain the no-upstream-tracking root cause (flow-wjfs). The working checkout lagged origin/main by 26 commits, surfacing the bug as still-open. Bootstrapping would have produced an empty no-op PR. Closing as superseded-by-main.

title: bg --auto: do-loop .out capture via Write tool blocked in worktree (SKILL.md step d)
description: In a bg 'claude --bg /flow <key> --auto' run the session/orchestrator cwd is pinned at the repo root and the harness bg-isolation guard rejects the Write tool inside the .flow/worktrees worktree. SKILL.md:151 (subagent capture branch) and SKILL.md:161 (skill capture branch) both instruct 'Capture the Agent's response with the Write tool' to $TICKET_DIR/stages/<STAGE>.out — that path is inside the worktree, so Write is blocked. Observed on flow-495l's own run (PR#233): wrote all four stage .out files (implement, commit, create_pr, review_loop) via python3 heredoc instead. flow-495l/PR#233 documents the EnterWorktree(path) cwd-pin refusal and the SUBAGENT edit fallback in verb-spec.md step 7 + verb-do.md, but NOT the orchestrator's own .out capture. Same root cause, distinct surface. Fix: add a bg --auto fallback note to SKILL.md do-loop step d (the subagent + skill capture sub-branches) — write .out via Bash/Python (printf/heredoc) when the Write tool is blocked. SKILL.md is a guard/hot file (∈ triage._GUARD_FILES). corpus delta: not scored — prose-only, no scripts/*.py edit. knowledge.jsonl id cd5a61103c751218.

title: session_cleanup skips tempo=blocked sessions whose bead is terminal (zombie blind spot)
description: evolve_session_cleanup requires tempo==idle, so a run that DIES blocked (rate limit, permission ask, auth outage) is skipped forever even when every doneness signal holds: bead closed, lease non-live, PR merged, transcript idle past the stale threshold. Witness 2026-06-10: flow-mozu's session (a687c41b) sat state=blocked/tempo=blocked after its PR #230 was orphan-reaped and bead closed; maintainer had to ask why it was still in the panel; manual stop+rm. Fix: treat tempo=blocked like the non-clean-terminal state case -- eligible when bead terminal AND lease non-live AND transcript idle >= stale-idle threshold (the three independent signals already gate it). Keep skipping blocked sessions whose bead is NOT terminal (a genuine needs-input run). Files: scripts/evolve_session_cleanup.py + its tests. Blast radius: session GC only; fail-safe direction preserved by the bead-terminal requirement.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Admit tempo=blocked sessions to cleanup eligibility, gated by the existing terminal-bead + lease-non-live + transcript-idle-past-stale-threshold signals. Change the tempo veto from '\!= idle' to 'not in (idle, blocked)' and force the stale threshold for blocked. Also update the verb-evolve.md:159 prose + module docstring (doc-drift discipline; drift isolated to one ref). Safe to auto-ship: session-GC-only leaf, no importers, non-destructive resumable side effects, trivially revertible, TDD + CI cover the blocked path.

title: Canonical PR-watch recipe for drain orchestrator: forge_cli ci-rollup + error budget
description: The drain D-wait/CI-rewait prose tells the orchestrator to poll with Monitor but gives no canonical probe; an ad-hoc 'gh pr view --json statusCheckRollup' jq filter spun silently forever when gh errored intermittently (err fallback string matched no exit branch) and mis-detected pending on StatusContext-shaped rollup entries -- the maintainer had to notice the hang by eye (2026-06-10, PR #230 wait). Fix: verb-evolve.md drain D-wait + version_recoverable CI-rewait sections should prescribe a concrete probe loop built on forge_cli ci-rollup (already classifies CANCELLED/STALE/NEUTRAL correctly) with (a) a consecutive-error budget that emits and exits instead of spinning, (b) MERGED/CLOSED state short-circuit, (c) guaranteed exit on every terminal state. Evidence: this session's pr_watch.sh; status key is 'green'/'red' not 'success'/'failure'. Prose-only; possibly a small reference snippet. Blast radius: drain orchestration prose.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Replace the placeholder CI-rewait comment in verb-evolve.md version_recoverable with a concrete hardened PR-watch Monitor recipe (forge_cli ci-rollup + detect-pr): consecutive-error budget that emits+breaks on repeated gh-error exit codes (not the latent 2>/dev/null|.get('status','pending') spin), MERGED/CLOSED short-circuit via detect-pr returning null, guaranteed terminal exit on green/failed/merged-closed/error-budget/overall-cap, using the real status enum green/pending/failed (ticket's 'green/red' is wrong). D-wait gets only an anti-spin discipline cross-ref, NOT a ci-rollup poll (it is an orchestration-state wait; the in-flight run drives its own CI). Safe to auto-ship: prose-only in one reference doc, blast radius = drain orchestration prose, trivially git-revertible, seam_check (CI-gated) catches any phantom subcommand/flag. is_hot=false (verb-evolve.md not a guard file). Independent opus assessor scored 88% (>=70 floor), ruled proceed on all three axes. Follow-up: stage-merge.md:103,123 share the same latent placeholder shape (out of scope here).

title: PR #227 committed maintainer-local .claude/settings.json as a tracked repo file
description: MACHINERY (surfaced during flow-h4hs reflect, friction 97059041): PR #227 ('fix: survive harness bash sandbox in bg runs') committed .claude/settings.json into the repo as a TRACKED file (present in origin/main 18cd9b7, absent at 1486067). .claude/settings.json is maintainer-local Claude Code settings (permissions/hooks/env), not project source; tracking it publishes one machine's local config and makes any session's local edits to it show as repo changes. Side effect: it masks flow-h4hs's cited symptom (an untracked copy of settings.json no longer surfaces in git status, so check-ownership stops flagging it) — but that is incidental, not intentional. Fix candidate: git rm --cached .claude/settings.json and add .claude/settings.json to root .gitignore (mirror the existing .claude/settings.local.json line). This is now SAFE because flow-h4hs/PR#232 made diff_extract.check_ownership exclude the whole bootstrap-copied .claude/ tree, so the re-untracked-and-copied settings.json will no longer trip the commit stage. Not hot (.gitignore + settings.json are not guard files). corpus delta: not scored — fix touches .gitignore + git index, not a scripts/*.py decider.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: BLOCKED mid-run (run 99e27e442f7b66ce): the change is correct and lint-green in the worktree (git rm --cached .claude/settings.json + .gitignore line), but the commit stage's capture-implement-diff aborts on it. A cached-removal staged deletion of a now-gitignored planned file is misclassified as untracked-new, so the _IgnoredPlannedFile guard fires (exit 1: 'planned file(s) gitignored, cannot be committed: .claude/settings.json'). This is a harness bug, not a problem with the change. Filed flow-8l6q (HOT machinery: exempt staged deletions from the untracked/intent-to-add set in diff_extract.capture_implement_diff). flow-kwai blocked-by flow-8l6q; re-run after that lands + the marketplace checkout advances.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: the implementation wall is gone — flow-8l6q/PR#239 fixed diff_extract's misclassification of staged deletions of now-gitignored planned files. Relaunch and proceed with the original change (git rm --cached .claude/settings.json + .gitignore line); the commit gate now passes it. NOTE: launch only after the maintainer checkout advances past PR#239, or the run will still execute the old diff_extract.

title: bg --auto run cannot EnterWorktree(path) or Edit/Write in its .flow worktree (session cwd pinned at repo root)
description: A bg-launched 'claude --bg /flow <key> --auto' run has its session cwd pinned at the repo root. verb-spec.md step 7's EnterWorktree(path=<worktree>) then refuses ('current working directory is the repository root, not an isolated worktree — switching is only available to sessions whose working directory is inside a worktree'), AND the harness bg-isolation guard rejects Edit/Write inside the .flow/worktrees worktree (a real linked git worktree) for the orchestrator AND for spawned subagents (subagent cwd pinned too). Workaround proven on flow-ztfv/PR#231: cd the persistent Bash cwd into the worktree FIRST — that moves the session cwd, after which EnterWorktree(path) reports 'is the current working directory' (already there) and the whole tail drives via Bash with --workspace-root . (subagents fall back to Bash/Python string-replace for edits). Fix candidate: document the cd-first fallback in references/verb-spec.md step 7 (+ verb-do.md), so a bg --auto run does not stall on EnterWorktree. NOT a flow script bug — a Claude Code bg-harness cwd-pin interaction flow must work around in prose. references/verb-spec.md is not a guard file (non-hot). corpus delta: not scored — prose-only, no scripts/*.py edit.

title: check-ownership flags bootstrap-copied .claude/settings.json as unowned at commit
description: MACHINERY (flow-mozu commit stage, friction afb1da85b5974aaf8fc1199bdc6a4188): flow_worktree.py bootstrap copies the whole .claude/ dir into the run worktree (copied:['.claude']) so the worktree session inherits hooks/permissions. But .claude/settings.json is neither gitignored (root .gitignore ignores only specific .claude/* paths: settings.local.json, scheduled_tasks.*, shell-snapshots/, todos/, history/, statsig/, projects/, worktrees/ — NOT settings.json) nor tracked in origin/main, so it lands as an untracked, committable file in the worktree. diff_extract.py check-ownership lists it under unowned_changes and exits 3, blocking the commit stage, even though the path-scoped implement.diff + 'git commit -F' would never include it. Notably check-ownership DOES exclude .flow/ run scratch (.flow/tickets/<KEY>.md untracked was not flagged) but has no equivalent exclusion for the bootstrap-copied .claude/ scaffolding. Hit live flow-mozu/PR#230; worked around by rm-ing .flow/worktrees/<wt>/.claude/settings.json before re-running check-ownership (the rm itself needed sandbox bypass — settings.json is a sandbox-protected write path). Fix candidate: diff_extract check-ownership should exclude the bootstrap-copied .claude/ scaffolding the same way it excludes .flow/ scratch (mirror that exclusion), OR flow_worktree.py should not copy settings.json (copy only the dirs the worktree session needs). corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet (candidate touches diff_extract.py, a guard file; would score before applying).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (auto-adjudicator; advisor absent, independent opus assessor) proceed. Mirror the .flow/ exclusion in diff_extract.check_ownership to also skip bootstrap-copied .claude/ scaffolding (the untracked, non-gitignored .claude/settings.json that the worktree bootstrap copies in). Right fix and safe to auto-ship: blast radius is the single-purpose commit-ownership gate, the real commit guard is path-scoped patch staging from implement.diff, clean one-line revert, strong CI coverage. Broad .claude/ chosen over narrow settings.json to mirror the .flow/ precedent and cover any bootstrap-copied non-gitignored .claude file (nothing under .claude/ is tracked product code). Hot guard-file change; merge stays gated by guard-property review + CI + agent diff review.

title: drain reap merges a LIVE run's green PR as orphan during its merge-stage CI re-waits (stale lease window)
description: Hit live on flow-72d9/PR#226: the run's own merge stage was mid-§3 (stamp pushed 523b212, DIRTY, version_remerge to a3931e3, CI re-wait green) when a parallel drain pass classified the PR as an orphan ('run died with green PR'), merged it at 14:01:05Z, closed the bead, deleted the remote branch, and reaped the worktree out from under the live session (cwd deleted mid-command; run state.json + lease destroyed, merge stage could never advance/release). Root cause: the run lease refreshes only on dispatch_stage next/advance, but stage-merge §3's bounded CI re-waits keep the session between dispatch calls for 20-40+ min, so the lease reads stale/non-live and the reap's lease-gate passes. Outcome converged here (the drain merged the exact CI-validated SHA a3931e3; version walked 0.30.0->0.30.1 correctly) but the race is structural: two agents driving the same PR's merge concurrently, and the live session loses its worktree mid-operation. Candidate fixes: (a) refresh the lease from the merge stage's wait loop (heartbeat alongside the CI Monitor), (b) reap honors an open-PR-with-recent-branch-push signal as liveness, (c) launch_ledger-style marker for in-merge-stage runs. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Right fix = unify liveness on the run lease. (1) Heartbeat-refresh the lease inside each stage-merge §3 CI re-wait Monitor poll loop (TTL 1800s) so a 20-40+min §3 keeps the lease live; (2) add a CI-tested lease-liveness gate to evolve_reap.classify (green PR whose run lease reads live/corrupt -> new skipped_live bucket, not merge/skipped_hot/version_recoverable), making the drain reap a true orphan-only safety-net in code. Safe to auto-ship: is_hot=false (no guard files touched), narrow blast-radius (maintainer-only drain/self-merge; classify gains keyword-default-None param, byte-identical until reap() opts in), clean revert, monotonic/fail-safe (a bad heartbeat degrades to today's behavior: §1 already-merged short-circuit + already-lease-gated reap_worktree are the backstops; no path regresses below status quo), gated by in-run code_review + seam_check + CI. Score 93.

title: merge §3 push-state guard uses git rev-parse @{u}, false-trips 'unpushed' in flow worktrees
description: MACHINERY (flow-wjfs merge stage, friction id 9fc7724cf5c643f4a315ffc1dea2e972): stage-merge.md §3 push-state guard at line 80 reads 'git rev-parse @{u}' to prove HEAD was pushed (and therefore CI'd). In a flow worktree, @{u} is EMPTY because upstream tracking was never recorded — the same sandbox-blocked shared .git/config write that flow-wjfs fixed for the push COMMANDS. So the guard false-trips 'uncommitted or unpushed' and an unattended self-merge would skip, parking a green PR for the human. Hit live on flow-wjfs/PR#225 (HEAD==origin/<branch>==a207878, genuinely pushed + CI green, yet @{u} empty). Worked around by hand by comparing HEAD to the remote-tracking ref 'git rev-parse origin/<branch>' after an explicit 'git fetch origin <branch>'. Fix: stage-merge.md §3 guard should resolve the pushed SHA via 'git rev-parse origin/' (fetch first), not @{u}; update the @{u} reasoning at line 145 to match. flow-wjfs scoped only the push commands, so this distinct @{u}-read site was out of its scope. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only reference edit, no decider script change.

title: stage-commit.md /tmp redirect fails under sandbox; use TMPDIR
description: references/stage-commit.md step 3 instructs 'compose_commit.py ... > /tmp/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt'. Sandboxed sessions (default Bash sandbox) deny writes to bare /tmp ('operation not permitted: /tmp/flow-commit-flow-7eqw.txt', observed flow-7eqw run 2026-06-10); only $TMPDIR and job tmp dirs are allowed. Every sandboxed --auto run hits this at commit and must improvise. Fix: replace the path with ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt across steps 3/4/6 (same file; step 4 Read-before-Write note unaffected). Prose-only, no engine scripts. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only edit cannot move the deciders.
close_reason: Superseded by PR #227 (merged 2026-06-10): same stage-commit.md TMPDIR fix landed there; PR #228 closed unmerged.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Apply the prose fix as planned — replace the 5 bare /tmp/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt occurrences in references/stage-commit.md (step 3 redirect + note, step 4 prose x2, step 6 commit) with ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt. Safe to auto-ship: single reference doc, no engine scripts, not hot, no seam_check seam touched, cleanly revertible, human PR review still backstops prose quality. ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} is already the established convention in verb-init.md/verb-spec.md.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Superseded by external PR #227 (fix/sandbox-bash-snags) which lands the same stage-commit.md TMPDIR fix plus broader sandbox hardening. Close once #227 merges.

title: version_remerge recover: bare 'git push' fails in flow worktrees (no upstream tracking)
description: MACHINERY (flow-z8wd merge stage, friction id 90380211525b4008aa995d241380acfd): version_remerge.py recover's push step runs bare 'git push', which exits 128 ('no upstream branch') in a flow worktree — create_pr.py pushes the branch without setting tracking, and setting it later needs a main-repo .git/config write that the sandbox blocks. recover then exits 2 (tool error) with the merge commit stranded locally; stage-merge prose routes exit \!=0/3 to 'leave PR for the human', so an unattended run parks a PR whose recovery is one explicit push away. Hit live on flow-z8wd/PR#221: remerge commit b93c8c9 landed locally, push failed, orchestrator recovered by hand with 'git push origin <branch>'. Fix: version_remerge.py push step should run 'git push origin <branch>' explicitly (it already knows --branch) instead of relying on upstream tracking; same check for the stamp-commit push in stage-merge.md §3 prose (it also uses bare 'git push' — worked this run only by luck of ordering? no: it failed too, recovered with explicit push). corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — replace bare 'git push' with explicit 'git push origin <branch>' at version_remerge.recover's two push sites + the stamp-commit push in stage-merge.md §3. Correct (upstream-independent, matches the by-hand recovery on flow-z8wd/PR#221) and safe to auto-ship: tiny blast radius (2 engine lines + 1 prose line + test assertions), reversible, CI-covered (version_remerge suite + seam_check), non-hot (stage-merge.md not in triage._GUARD_FILES).

title: evolve_reap: CLEAN PR with duplicate version stamp merges with no version walk
description: Sibling gap to flow-5fp: evolve_reap's drain loop merges green CLEAN PRs directly without any version-equality check, so a duplicate-stamp PR (branch version == main version, CLEAN merge state) lands with no version walk on the reap side too. Mirror of the stage-merge §3 guard / version_remerge restamp shipped in flow-5fp.
close_reason: merged via PR #226 (orphan reap: run died with green PR)

title: stage-implement.md pre-flight: warn that git rm stages deletions and breaks git apply --cached --check
description: The implement-stage commit-gate pre-flight (references/stage-implement.md, 'Pre-flight the commit gate' paragraph) recommends capture-implement-diff + 'git apply --cached --check --binary implement.diff'. A deletion ticket naturally starts with git rm, which stages the deletion; the apply-check then fails with 'does not exist in index' because it assumes index == HEAD. Evidence: flow-qp7 implement subagent hit exactly this, self-recovered via git reset + re-capture (implement.out 'Commit-gate pre-flight' section). An unattended lower-context agent could misread it as a real packaging failure. Fix: add one sentence to the pre-flight paragraph telling the implementer to keep the index clean before the check (git reset if anything was staged; capture-implement-diff diffs the working tree against the baseline, staging is unnecessary). Prose-only edit, no scripts touched. corpus delta: not scored — prose-only edit cannot move the deciders.

title: --auto runs self-tombstone their session at run completion (panel GC)
description: A finished claude --bg /flow <key> --auto session lingers in the claude agents panel until a drain turn's A2 cleanup collects it -- often a LONG time (event-driven turns + 300s transcript-idle bar). Maintainer wants finished sessions stopped+removed as soon as effectively complete. Proposal: as the LAST act of an --auto run (after release + reflect + ship-event, end of the do-loop), the run schedules its own teardown: detached 'nohup sh -c "sleep 10; claude stop <own-job-id>; rm -rf <own-job-dir>"' (job id from $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR basename, validated 8-hex; rm path validated under ~/.claude/jobs/). --auto mode ONLY -- an attended run must never kill its session. Transcript is untouched so claude attach still works. Drain's A2 cleanup stays as the safety net for runs that die before reaching the tail. Files: references/verb-do.md (completion step) or references/stage-reflect.md tail; prose-only. Evidence: this drain (2026-06-10) left 11 stale sessions from a prior run and the maintainer watched live ones linger all day. Blast radius: session lifecycle only; no engine scripts.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed. Approach right: teardown anchored at SKILL.md do-verb step 5 after release (common to all four loop-exit paths; structurally cannot self-kill mid-merge since merge precedes the loop exit), recipe mirrors shipped A2 conventions (8-hex job-id validation, ~/.claude/jobs/<8hex>$ path regex, stop-before-rm, timeout 90 claude stop </dev/null), --auto-only gate via CLAUDE_JOB_DIR presence (the only signal; --auto stamped nowhere), drain A2 stays safety net, sleep 30 over ticket's 10 so the final summary streams. Safe to auto-ship: blast radius two scoped tokens in SKILL.md (pointer paragraph fenced to --auto-after-release + one Bash(nohup:*) frontmatter entry) with do-loop semantics/gates untouched and rm -rf triple-guarded + regex-confined; reversibility clean single git revert, no state migration; CI = seam_check (0 stage-doc cites, no new CLI seam) + lint + full pytest, same prose-not-executed exposure as already-shipped A2. Known honest gap: self-stop extends A2's other-stop precedent (A2 skips --self-job); environment-level, bounded, non-destructive worst case (leaked job dir, A2 sweeps it).

title: Raise --auto self-approve bar: exhaust self-confidence-raising before defer/block
description: Maintainer directive (2026-06-10, recorded on flow-5fp's TRIAGE-DECISION): in this low-stakes self-target repo, an --auto run that hits sub-90% confidence or an advisor block citing CLOSEABLE holes (missing tests, unmapped errors, unacknowledged-but-correct behavior changes) must first attempt to close those holes itself — write the failing tests, fix the error mapping, run a bounded spike — and re-assess, deferring/blocking ONLY when confidence genuinely cannot be raised without user-only information or true irreversibility. False negatives are explicitly accepted by the maintainer. Witness: flow-5fp advisor block named three concrete closeable holes and deferred instead of closing them. Files: references/verb-spec.md (--auto path + advisor framing), possibly references/verb-triage.md advisor-adjudication section. Blast radius: gate prose only, no scripts; changes when --auto parks work, not what ships (merge-time gates unchanged).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — plan correctly encodes the flow-5fp maintainer directive: close-the-holes revision round placed in verb-spec.md --auto step 4 after the independent rating (one-round hard bound; skipped on BAIL / user-only gaps / decided beads), closeability classification added to the adjudication step-2 framing (closeable holes convert to mandatory implementer commitments on proceed, never block-grounds), block reserved for surviving walls. Safe to auto-ship: prose-only, changes WHEN --auto parks work not WHAT ships; hot hard-floor, 70% floor, defer-stem coupling, and PR-review keystone untouched; trivial revert. Mandatory implementer commitments: (1) defer/block stem 'flow --auto could not self-approve' stays byte-identical (verify vs triage.py _DEFER_STEM); (2) post-revision parentheticals match existing step-5 trigger phrasing; (3) decided-skip wording keeps the decided sub-branch ruling on hotness, not plan quality; (4) seam_check.py + test_live_docs_are_green green, no new stage-doc citations; (5) verb-spec.md (router line ~9 + section) and verb-triage.md advisor-minted bullets updated in the same change.

title: lease.boot_id() returns '' under sandboxed Bash (sysctl blocked) -> false lost-lease exit 7 at advance
description: During flow-2ge's final merge-stage advance, the harness sandbox blocked 'sysctl -n kern.bootsessionuuid' (Operation not permitted); lease.boot_id() (lease.py:112) swallows the SubprocessError and returns '', and the ownership guard then reads boot mismatch (on-disk uuid != '') -> exit 7 lost lease, on a lease the run still owned. All earlier advances in the same session passed, so sandbox policy can flip mid-session. Workaround: re-run advance unsandboxed. Possible fixes: treat empty CURRENT boot as inconclusive in the ownership compare (match on run_id+hostname when current boot is ''), or fall back to a cached boot id from acquire time. lease.py is a guard file; weakening the compare needs the guard-property review. Evidence: flow-2ge run 26cfa8bf487de3d1, friction id 6ca260efc9ec4ebfb550f0f6dacec867. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Apply the boot-compare relaxation in assert_lease_still_mine — change line 331 from 'current_boot is not None and lease.boot_id \!= current_boot' to 'lease.boot_id and current_boot and lease.boot_id \!= current_boot', harmonizing it with the already-symmetric both-non-empty rule in classify (lease.py:372-376) and acquire's reboot_clearable (lease.py:258-261). Independent adjudicator scored 92/100, verdict proceed. Safe to auto-ship despite hot/guard-file status (adjudicate_hot on): the boot check is downstream of the unchanged run_id identity gate (line 329), run_id is 64-bit per-run CSPRNG (secrets.token_hex(8)) never reused across reboots, so the relaxation can only RAISE LESS in the empty-id case and can never grant passage to a mismatched run_id — no steal possible. Single production caller (_guard_lease_ownership, dispatch_stage.py:280), CI keeps the both-non-empty boot-mismatch test green, one-line revert. Merge-time guard-property review remains the gate.

title: worker_model config: drained runs default to opus (trivial→sonnet, hot→inherit launcher default)
description:
Today evolve_select.py / queue_select.py emit model_per_key only for tier:trivial beads (→ sonnet); every other launched key omits --model, so the bg run inherits the user's saved default model. With the maintainer's default now fable, every non-trivial drained run silently runs at fable (2x opus cost). Intended tiering: fable for the interactive orchestrator/evolve loop only; opus for spawned worker runs; sonnet for trivial.

Change:
1. workspace.toml: new key [evolve] worker_model (string, optional; this repo sets 'opus'). Absent → current behavior (omit flag) so other projects are unaffected.
2. evolve_select.py + queue_select.py: model_per_key resolution per launched key becomes: tier:trivial (non-hot in evolve) → 'sonnet' (unchanged); hot label → OMIT (hot/guard-file runs inherit the launcher default, i.e. fable — highest-stakes changes get the strongest model); else → worker_model when configured.
3. Prose: verb-evolve.md §C + §3-dry-run and verb-queue.md §4/§C already append --model <m> generically from model_per_key, but the surrounding wording ('absent → inherits the strong default model'; 'every other bead omits the flag') must be updated to describe the three-tier mapping. Grep the whole references/ set for drift (sonnet/trivial mapping is described in at least verb-evolve.md:194).
4. Tests: extend the model_per_key tests in tests for evolve_select/queue_select with worker_model set/unset, hot, trivial, trivial+hot cases.
5. seam_check after prose edits.

Note: subagents inside a bg run inherit the run's session model, so worker_model=opus also makes the run's plan/implement subagents opus — that is the desired 'background tasks it spawns are opus by default' behavior.

title: verb-spec --auto has no disposition for infra failure preventing agent spawn; codify leave-open branch
description: Evidence (flow-aod first launch): the --auto path hit a monthly-spend-limit error on the Plan subagent spawn; every step from there (Plan, independent assessor, adjudication) needs Agent calls, so the run could neither plan nor self-approve. verb-spec.md --auto step 5 enumerates dispositions only for planner OUTPUTS (clarifying questions / sub-90 / BAIL) — an infra wall that prevents spawning agents AT ALL has no recipe. Chosen disposition that worked: leave the bead open and untouched (the wall is environmental, not ticket-intrinsic; defer/block would hide the bead from drain even after the limit is raised, forcing manual reopen), emit a terse failed line, no comment/no state writes; relaunch after the limit reset succeeded cleanly from scratch. Risk left open: a drain loop relaunches into the same wall repeatedly. Fix direction: verb-spec.md --auto should codify this infra-failure branch (leave-open + terse exit; optionally a launch_ledger-style backoff). Blast radius: one reference doc, prose-only. corpus delta: not scored - no concrete edit yet.

title: stage-review_loop Monitor recipe dies in headless turn-bounded sessions; document foreground poll fallback
description: Evidence (flow-aod run): stage-review_loop.md §1 prescribes a Monitor for the CI wait ('Foreground sleep is blocked, so launch a Monitor'), but in a headless session that ends at each turn boundary (this run: rate-limit interrupt + per-turn relaunch) a Monitor/background task dies at turn end and its notification never arrives — the earlier background assessor in the same run died exactly this way. A bounded foreground poll loop (while+sleep inside ONE Bash call, explicit iteration cap, 600s timeout) worked and reached CI green in ~30s. Fix direction: stage-review_loop.md should name the headless fallback (bounded foreground poll) next to the Monitor recipe and say when to choose it. Blast radius: one reference doc, prose-only. corpus delta: not scored - no concrete edit yet.

title: dispatch owned-drift reconcile: extend to engine component for planned engine-file edits
description: MACHINERY (flow-o6c reflect): advance after the commit stage exited 1 'drift: engine' on a run whose ONLY deliverable was a planned edit to plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-evolve.md. In the self-target spec->EnterWorktree->do path the session invokes the engine via the worktree's own scripts, so snapshot skill_root==worktree (flow-alz/PR#111) and the engine component hashes the worktree's scripts/*.py + references/*.md — committing the planned edit re-hashes it. The owned-drift auto-reconcile in dispatch covers only the workspace_toml/stage_registry components mapped to planned_files (verb-do.md exit-1 matrix); the engine component is never owned-reconcilable, so EVERY self-target run that edits an engine file via worktree-invoked scripts halts at the first post-commit advance and needs a manual recover.py reload-snapshot + next round-trip (this run's exact workaround; friction id 7a97df5c401f45739d9a4925477a3e35). Candidate fix: in dispatch's drift handler, when ALL paths whose hashes changed within the engine component are in this run's planned_files, treat as owned drift -> reload baseline + reconciled_drift marker, same as the existing two components. Blast radius: dispatch_stage.py (guard file -> hot); behavior change only on the drift-halt path; headless --auto runs launched from the main checkout (skill_root==main) are unaffected. corpus delta: not scored -- no concrete edit yet.
close_reason: wontfix: stale post flow-2pp/PR#200 (engine component anchors main checkout; planned worktree edits can't trip engine drift) and proposed fix would re-open the 2pp TOCTOU hole. Adversarially verified by the --auto run (byte-identical engine hash before/after committed planned edit). Residual lagging-main trip (flow-mozu) is a separate mechanism, refile if it recurs.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: bead is STALE — the described bug no longer reproduces on origin/main, and the proposed fix would weaken a safety guard. Recommend CLOSE (wontfix), not implement.

Why stale: the bead rests on the pre-flow-2pp premise that the 'engine' snapshot component hashes the WORKTREE's scripts/*.py + references/*.md (skill_root==worktree, flow-alz). flow-2pp (PR #200, merged) re-anchored _engine_component to hash the MAIN checkout's tree (main_root = first 'git worktree list --porcelain' stanza, independent of skill_root). So a worktree-based run's committed planned edit never changes the hashed engine tree → no engine drift from the run's OWN edits. Verified empirically on flow-dpz0 (planned references/verb-evolve.md edit via worktree scripts → post-commit advance did NOT trip 'drift: engine'), and re-confirmed this run by an independent adversarial check (byte-identical engine hash before/after a committed planned edit; tests test_engine_anchors_on_main_root_from_worktree_copy + test_component_files_maps_engine_to_none on origin/main).

No surviving owned-drift config: the only other candidate — a standalone /flow do directly in a single checkout — is dead because the engine component is active ONLY on a protected branch while a run commits to a feature branch (mutually exclusive by design; machinery_edit also refuses protected-branch self-edits).

Why the fix is harmful: the engine component stores only {branch, tree_hash} (no per-file entries), and component_files deliberately maps engine→None. The proposed 'all changed engine files in planned_files → owned' fix would require adding per-file storage AND making engine owned-reconcilable, which re-opens the exact TOCTOU hole flow-2pp closed: a genuine mid-run marketplace/main-checkout swap touching a path that coincides with planned_files would be falsely reconciled instead of aborting.

Residual (separate, NOT this bead's scope, not refiled): flow-mozu observed a 'drift: engine' trip at the FIRST next caused by local main LAGGING origin/main (snapshot basis instability), a different mechanism than this bead's planned-edit drift; if it recurs it warrants its own bead. To unstick: confirm staleness and close, reopen (status->open) and re-run WITHOUT --auto if you disagree.

title: stage-merge §3: duplicate version stamp is invisible to DIRTY-only recovery
description: MACHINERY (flow-5ba merge stage, friction id a20a762a4103430bbb974e971580639a): when a sibling drain run merges main to version X while this branch is also stamped X, git reports mergeStateStatus CLEAN (identical content on both sides of the version line) so stage-merge §3's DIRTY branch never fires, and merging as-is lands a PR with NO version walk — two PRs share one version number and the marketplace update sees no new version. Hit live on flow-5ba/PR#213: branch stamped 0.28.3, flow-cam/PR#212 merged main to 0.28.3, state CLEAN + CI green; only a manual equality check caught it. Worked around by re-running version.py stamp against fresh origin/main (0.28.4) + push + CI re-wait. Fix: stage-merge.md §3 should compare the branch's stamped version against fresh origin/main's BEFORE reading MERGE_STATE (equal => re-stamp + re-wait, exactly like the DIRTY path); alternatively version_remerge recover could detect already-at-same-version (not already-at-next) and re-stamp. Prose-only fix in references/stage-merge.md, possibly plus a version_remerge guard. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete scripts edit yet.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: advisor ruled for the dual fix (stage-merge §3 pre-MERGE_STATE version-equality guard + version_remerge recover clean-path same-version restamp, additive 'restamped' status, no new CLI surface; evolve_reap gap deferred to a follow-up bead) but blocked auto-ship — behavior change on the self-merge success path (clean-merge path goes from no-op to write+commit+push) governing every evolve run in this self-target repo, with three unclosed holes: (a) guard also fires on a never-stamped branch whose tree sits at main's version (unacknowledged behavior change, though restamp is idempotent-correct); (b) clean-path working-tree read must map FileNotFoundError/OSError to ToolError or a raw OSError escapes cli_main as exit-1 traceback — and test_remerged_clean_when_no_conflict (+ test_remerged_clean_shape_unchanged_under_commit_type) must create on-disk version files or they crash; (c) §3 prose must re-fetch origin immediately before the comparison (stamp's earlier fetch is stale post-CI-re-wait). Judgment settled, this is a safety hold. Implementer conditions on unstick: honor (a)-(c), keep exit codes 0/2/3 + JSON-additive status + seam green, file the evolve_reap CLEAN-duplicate follow-up bead. To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open) and re-run WITHOUT --auto, or merge by hand.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: proceed with the dual fix (stage-merge §3 version-equality guard + version_remerge recover same-version restamp, additive 'restamped' status). Maintainer directive: do not defer on closeable holes — close them yourself as part of the run. Specifically: (a) the restamp firing on a never-stamped branch sitting at main's version is ACCEPTED as intended behavior (it is idempotent-correct; document it in the stage-merge prose and cover it with a test); (b) map FileNotFoundError/OSError on the clean-path working-tree read to ToolError so no raw traceback escapes cli_main — add the test the advisor named; (c) close every remaining hole the advisor enumerated with explicit tests rather than deferring on it. The evolve_reap gap stays a follow-up bead as proposed. General policy going forward: this repo is low-stakes and merges revert cheaply — exhaust self-confidence-raising (write the missing tests, handle the named edge cases, bounded spike if needed) and raise to the human only when confidence genuinely cannot be raised without user-only information.

title: evolve audit: re-validate evidence freshness against origin/main before filing
description: MACHINERY (flow-5ba reflect): the audit filed flow-5ba at 2026-06-10T03:25 asking for a multiprocessing contention test for the bootstrap-claim flock, but PR#189 (63833d8) had already merged that exact test (test_concurrent_bootstraps_exactly_one_wins) at 2026-06-09T18:47 — the evidence snapshot (labels evid:dd9478756de6 / evidfile:e7b09644eb40) predated the merge and the filer did not re-check origin/main at bd-create time. The plan-stage drift-vs-@default discipline caught it and shrank scope to the residual release check (PR#213 shipped that), but a freshness re-check in the audit filing path (confirm the asked-for artifact is still absent on freshly-fetched origin/main before bd create) would avoid spending plan cycles on already-shipped work. Related prior art: flow-cam (coverage claims false via grep miss) — this is the staleness-race flavor of the same failure class. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

title: evolve audit coverage-gap evidence can claim already-tested paths; verify against the live suite before minting
description: Evidence (flow-cam): audit minted a bead claiming cmd_init expired-foreign->5 and cmd_next corrupt-lock->7 were untested at the dispatch layer, but test_init_stale_foreign_lease_returns_5 (test_dispatch_stage.py:801) and test_next_corrupt_lock_returns_lease_lost_no_advance (:1181) both existed and passed at origin/main, with the same 62-test count the evidence itself cited. Detection miss (grep pattern), not repo drift; line cites were accurate. Cost: a full plan + independent assessment + adjudication call to discover the premise was false; an --auto run without the plan-stage re-verification would have shipped a no-op or duplicate tests. Fix direction: the audit's coverage-gap detector should confirm a claimed-missing test by grepping/collecting the actual suite (e.g. pytest --collect-only -q | grep) before filing, or stamp the bead with the exact grep used so plan can falsify it cheaply. corpus delta: not scored - no concrete edit yet.

title: dispatch_stage advance --output-path accepts nonexistent file silently
description: During flow-ocp the orchestrator passed --output-path for implement.out on advance BEFORE writing the file (ordering slip); advance recorded the path with no existence check and returned success, leaving a dangling stage-output reference until the orchestrator backfilled it. Later consumers (review_loop reads create_pr.out PR_URL=, reflect bundles stages/*.out) assume the file exists. Cheap guard: dispatch_stage advance warns (or fails) when --output-path names a missing file. dispatch_stage.py is a guard file -> hot. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

title: sibling classify-then-mutate lease TOCTOU: flow_worktree reap + evolve_session_cleanup
description: flow-ocp fixed the recover.takeover classify-outside-flock TOCTOU via lease.takeover_clear (single flock across classify+remediate, PR#211). The independent assessor found the same shape elsewhere: flow_worktree.py:348 (reap) and evolve_session_cleanup.py:216 classify a lease then mutate (git worktree remove --force of the containing worktree) with no flock bridging decision and action. Same TOCTOU family: a lease can go live between classify and the destructive mutation. Different blast radius (worktree removal, not lease yanking). Candidate fix: route both through lease.takeover_clear or re-classify under the lease flock immediately before the mutation. corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Fix ONLY locus 1 (flow_worktree.reap classify-then-worktree-remove TOCTOU). Hold the lease flock ACROSS classify + git-worktree-remove via a new lease.py helper classify_then(ticket_dir, now, teardown, ...) that classifies under the flock and runs the caller teardown thunk while still holding it (mirrors the existing takeover_clear single-flock shape; routing through takeover_clear alone is INSUFFICIENT because it releases its flock before the external remove). Locus 2 (evolve_session_cleanup.py:216) is a MISCITE — pure classifier, destructive ops are non-destructive/resumable claude-stop + rm -rf of a ~/.claude/jobs tombstone (verb-evolve §A2), no worktree removal — NO code change. recover.takeover already routes through takeover_clear (recover.py:76), not a sibling gap. Independent adjudicator: proceed on both axes (design-correct + safe-to-auto-ship: additive to a wide-blast lease.py, behavior-preserving reap receipt, cleanly reversible, CI+reap-suite-gated). adjudicate_hot=true so the hot proceed is not downgraded; merge-time guard review + CI remain the keystone. Merge reviewer MUST: (1) read the race regression test for genuine flock serialization not just its name; (2) confirm classify_then docstring states the doomed-inode invariant (flock fd survives unlink of the removed worktree); (3) note flock non-reentrancy — teardown must not re-enter any lease fn.

title: Delete orphaned heartbeat.py read-only remnant: zero callers, zero writers after flow-dwd/PR#179
description:
EVIDENCE: after flow-dwd/PR#179 removed the heartbeat producer and the recover.py consumer, heartbeat.py (195 LOC) is fully orphaned: read_progress/detect_hung/the `read` CLI have ZERO callers — no script imports the module, no prose invokes it, and nothing anywhere writes a `<ticket_dir>/<stage>.progress` file (the module docstring itself says "none does today"). The classify logic (hung/wedged/no_progress) guards a scenario that cannot occur. Maintainer verdict 2026-06-10: "having this only adds noise" — full sweep including the guard-file mentions.

SCOPE (full heartbeat eradication; touches guard files, is_hot expected, maintainer TRIAGE-DECISION on record):
- git rm scripts/heartbeat.py + scripts/tests/test_heartbeat.py.
- _registry.py: drop the `default_heartbeat_required` parse (dataclass field + entry.get line) and any tests asserting it.
- stage-registry.toml (GUARD FILE): remove every `default_heartbeat_required = ...` line and the header-comment block describing the field as informational. Verify validate_workspace passes after removal.
- state.py (GUARD FILE): fix the stale module-docstring line "spec are deferred to phase 7-full (when heartbeat + lease lifecycle land)" — lease lifecycle landed, heartbeat is deleted; rewrite to describe current reality, comment-only change, no code.
- MODULE.md: hand-remove the heartbeat.py row (known gap: no seam_check gate catches a phantom row after script deletion — memory `deleting-script-leaves-phantom-module-md-row`).
- inventory.md: remove/adjust heartbeat rows or mentions.
- _timeutil.py docstring: update the module list mentioning heartbeat.py.
- Final check: `grep -ri heartbeat` over scripts/ + references/ + SKILL.md + stage-registry.toml returns nothing (tests/fixtures of OTHER modules excepted only if load-bearing).

ORDERING / SAFETY NOTES for the run:
- stage-registry.toml is snapshot-pinned: this MUST go through the normal pipeline as a planned edit (owned drift auto-reconciles per SKILL.md do-loop note) — never via machinery_edit mid-run from another run.
- state.py + stage-registry.toml in planned_files → is_hot:true → merge-stage guard review (§6A) before self-merge. Expected, not a blocker: maintainer decision recorded on this bead.

BLAST RADIUS: deletion of an orphan module + its tests + doc rows + one dead config parse + inert registry config lines + two stale comments. No reachable behavior changes. Verification risks: validate_workspace schema after field removal; stage-registry edit must be in planned_files so the drift guard owns it.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: full heartbeat eradication approved, including the guard-file touches (stage-registry.toml default_heartbeat_required lines + header block; state.py stale phase-7 docstring line). Maintainer 2026-06-10: 'we should get rid of stuff in state.py as well... having this only adds noise.' Comment-and-config-only changes to the guard files, no code-path edits; ship via the hot lane (merge-stage guard review).

title: recover takeover TOCTOU: classify outside flock can quarantine/unlink a freshly-valid lease
description: Evidence: recover.takeover() classifies the lease (recover.py:76 area) OUTSIDE the lease flock, then calls lease.quarantine_corrupt_lock (recover.py:81) which takes the flock and renames whatever run.lock NOW holds, then unlinks. Failure sequence: (1) human runs recover --takeover on a corrupt run.lock; (2) classify reads corrupt; (3) a concurrent process acquires the lease, writing a VALID run.lock; (4) quarantine renames that valid lease and takeover unlinks — yanking a live run lease (lease-exclusivity violation) and losing the corrupt forensic evidence. Likelihood low (human-driven path, narrow window) but the fix is small: re-verify unparseability under the flock inside quarantine_corrupt_lock (rename only when still corrupt), or hold one flock across classify+quarantine+unlink in takeover. Tightens the quarantine + exclusivity properties; deletes nothing. Blast radius: lease.py + recover.py; lease.py is a guard file -> hot, property review required.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Single-flock takeover_clear primitive in lease.py + defense-in-depth re-verify inside quarantine_corrupt_lock is the right fix shape: the TOCTOU is real on both takeover branches (classify at recover.py:76 unflocked; corrupt branch re-locks and renames whatever run.lock holds now; stale branch bare-unlinks), and lock-around-existing-calls is infeasible because flock_blocking is non-reentrant (fresh fd + LOCK_EX per call), making the _quarantine_locked extraction a correctness requirement. Safe to auto-ship: blast radius tightly bounded (hot loop acquire/refresh/release untouched; new primitive has one caller, the human-driven recover path), reversibility clean (one revertable commit, lease format unchanged), CI coverage strong (full pytest incl. multiprocess contention tests + ruff + ty; additive-only contract keeps existing takeover/payload tests as regression net). Draft PR still faces merge-time hot-guard diff review. Implementer should also fold in the stale classify docstring fix at lease.py:361.

title: Enumerate MODULE.md prose-gloss importer rows so the drift gate covers them
description: Evidence: MODULE.md:31 says tracker.py "imported by the adapters + tracker_cli" — omits sync.py; true set (grep/AST): tracker_jira, tracker_beads, tracker_cli, sync. MODULE.md:43 says forge.py "the adapters + forge_cli + create_pr" — glosses forge_github, forge_bitbucket. Prose-gloss rows are invisible to seam_check.module_md_importer_drift (it gates enumerable rows only), so drift in them is uncatchable; rewriting both rows as full enumerations fixes the sync.py omission AND puts the rows under the existing gate. Ground truth: the AST importer set. Blast radius: MODULE.md doc-only, mechanical, non-hot.

title: Remove phantom "per plan line N" citations (plan document no longer exists)
description: Evidence: 5 loci cite a deleted plan document — branch_ticket.py:10 ("per plan line 1012-1013"), diff_extract.py:5 ("per plan line 955"), diff_extract.py:25 ("per plan line 1014-1016"), ticket_frontmatter.py:39 ("per plan line 1017-1020"), inventory.md:630-631 ("per plan line 1010-1020"). No plan* file exists anywhere in the repo; the citations point future maintainers at a consultable document that cannot be found. The implemented semantics are correct; only the anachronistic citations go. Fix: delete/reword the citation fragments in all 5 loci together (one bead, multi-locus). Blast radius: comment/doc-only; touches diff_extract.py (guard file) so the diff rides the hot path — comment-only change, property review trivially passes.

title: Test flow_worktree bootstrap-claim flock under contention
description: Evidence: the per-ticket bootstrap claim flock (_check_sibling_lease, flow_worktree.py:249-295) is held across worktree-add -> state-seed -> frontmatter stamp; tests/test_flow_worktree.py (19 tests) covers duplicate-claim refusal (test_bootstrap_refuses_live_sibling_lease) but has NO concurrent-contention test (state.py has a multiprocessing analogue to copy) and no test that the flock releases on success (post-bootstrap re-acquire). Two simultaneous spec approvals racing is the exact case the claim exists for; serialization is currently unverified. Fix: test-only — multiprocessing contention test + release check in tests/test_flow_worktree.py. Blast radius: tests only, non-hot.

title: Test lease.boot_id Linux branch and failure fallback
description: Evidence: boot_id() has darwin/linux branches (lease.py:112-127); tests cover only darwin (test_boot_id_darwin_uses_sysctl). The Linux /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id read (lease.py:121-124) and the exception->"" fallback (lease.py:125-127) are never exercised with injected OSError/SubprocessError. boot_id gates reboot-clearance in acquire(); a broken Linux path silently returns "" -> wrong reboot_clearable classification (stale lease wrongly clearable or wrongly held). Fix: test-only — mock sys.platform + the /proc read + subprocess failure in tests/test_lease.py. Blast radius: tests only, non-hot.

title: Test dispatch_stage lease-error exit codes 5 and 7 (recovery signal paths untested)
description: Evidence: cmd_init catches lease.LeaseExpiredForeign -> exit 5 (dispatch_stage.py:162-167), the documented stale-lease recovery signal, but tests/test_dispatch_stage.py (62 tests) never triggers it (no expired-foreign fixture). cmd_next corrupt run.lock -> bare LeaseError -> EXIT_LEASE_LOST 7 (dispatch_stage.py:289-294) also unexercised at the dispatch layer (covered only inside lease.py tests). Exit 5/7 are what SKILL.md + verb-do.md recovery routing branch on; a regression silently degrades both to generic exit 1 and recover prose misroutes. Fix: test-only — add tests forging an expired foreign lease and a corrupt run.lock, asserting exit codes. Blast radius: tests/test_dispatch_stage.py only, non-hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed with residual-scope tests, not close-as-done. Ticket's literal gaps are stale (cmd_init expired-foreign -> 5 tested at test_dispatch_stage.py:801; cmd_next corrupt run.lock -> 7 tested at :1181), but the recovery prose consumes PROCESS exit codes and no cli_main test asserts 5 or 7; the refresh-time LeaseLost -> 7 branch (dispatch_stage.py:396-401) is shadowed by _guard_lease_ownership and untested. Safe to auto-ship: tests/test_dispatch_stage.py only, no production code, clean one-file revert, CI (pytest+ruff+ty) gates a broken test.

title: Delete inert mypy-style type-ignore comments (ty ignores them)
description: Evidence: ty honors only `# ty: ignore[rule]`; mypy-style `# type: ignore[...]` comments are inert (mise run lint fully green with them present, so ty never flagged those lines). Loci: forge_github.py:258 (`# type: ignore[typeddict-item]` on the status_lit TypedDict return — prefer annotating status_lit with the Literal type, then drop), tests/test_state.py:137, tests/test_jira_adapter.py:65, tests/test_jira_adapter.py:83, tests/test_tracker.py:153. Keep tests/test_state.py:155 (`# ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]` — correct syntax, load-bearing). Value: dead-weight comments imply suppressions that do not exist; mixed syntax invites copying the inert form. Blast radius: comment-only + one annotation, verified by mise run lint staying green. Non-hot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — correct cleanup, real type-safety improvement on forge_github.py annotation (status_lit: str -> CI_STATUS), zero runtime blast radius, fully CI-gated, trivially reversible.

title: Counterfactual baseline: measure flow against vanilla Claude Code on matched tickets
description:
## The gap (research-witnessed)

flow has never been measured against the obvious null hypothesis: plain Claude Code + plan mode + a worktree per ticket, no dispatcher, no leases, no snapshots. The 2026-06 deep-research pass (103-agent verified survey) confirmed the architecture is novel — and confirmed nobody, including flow, has evidence the state-machine layer beats the vanilla loop on outcomes. flow-ml7 closes the *internal* measurement loop (attribution, time-to-PR, revert-rate) but has no control arm: every number it produces is flow-vs-flow over time, never flow-vs-baseline. "Better must be measurable, or it is vibes" (VISION.md) currently stops one rung short — flow can prove it is improving, not that it is *better than not existing*.

## Grounding (web cites)

- arXiv 2604.03515 (13-scaffold source taxonomy): catalogs control loops and sandboxing but NO state-integrity machinery — flow's lease/snapshot/drift-guard layer has no measured baseline anywhere in the literature. A matched-pair comparison would be the first datapoint, period.
- arXiv 2511.13646 (Live-SWE-agent): the only published runtime self-evolver justifies itself via benchmark deltas vs its own base scaffold (65.0 vs 53.3 on SWE-bench Verified-60). flow's equivalent claim (dispatcher + evolution loop earn their complexity) is currently unfalsifiable.
- METR reward-hacking writeups: self-validated improvement claims without an external control are exactly the failure mode the field is converging on guarding against.

## VISION anchor

The keystone risk named at the top of this epic's own evidence: "self-evolving" degrades into "self-occupying" if the loop's value is only ever scored by the loop. A control arm is the cheapest external referee available — same model, same repo, same tickets, machinery on vs off.

## Shape (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via expand)

1. KEYSTONE — control-arm run recipe. A documented, repeatable way to run one leaf bead via bare `claude --bg` (plan mode + worktree + manual PR) with NO flow dispatcher, capturing the same evidence flow-ml7.1 stamps (start ts, PR ts, outcome, intervention count) into a ship-event marked `arm: control`. Reuse the existing ship-event seam; do NOT build a second pipeline. dedup `epic:counterfactual-baseline::child-1-control-arm`.
2. Matched-pair protocol + metric join. Pair selection rule (leaf, non-hot, similar size class — e.g. alternate assignment over the chore/bug queue), N target (>=8 pairs), and a `recall --metric` comparison report (time-to-PR, revert-rate, interventions, completion rate per arm). Pre-register the success criterion BEFORE running pairs. dedup `epic:counterfactual-baseline::child-2-matched-pairs`.
3. Verdict doc. Honest writeup of the result in-repo, including the live possibility that vanilla wins on small leaves and flow only pays off at concurrency/fleet scale — if so, that bounds where the machinery belongs and is itself the finding. dedup `epic:counterfactual-baseline::child-3-verdict`.

## Confounds to state up front (settle at spec)

Bead difficulty heterogeneity (mitigate via size-class pairing + alternation, not cherry-picking); operator effect (both arms run `--auto`-equivalent, unattended); cost (control arm burns the same tokens — cap at the N target). Maintainer-lane: epic-typed parent never auto-drains; children run via `/flow <key>` at the accept gate.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE: all 3 children shipped (xqt.1 #248 control-arm instrument, xqt.2 #252 protocol+metric, xqt.3 #271 verdict doc). The counterfactual baseline exists: docs/research/xqt-counterfactual-verdict-2026-06.md. Headline synthesis: flow quality equal-or-better everywhere measured, decisively better at the complexity ceiling; cost is time not quality. Protocol v2 recorded on this epic for replication.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
PROTOCOL v2 (maintainer-approved 2026-06-11; governs any replication and the future-work section of the flow-xqt.3 verdict doc). Supersedes the v3 pre-reg's scoring design after the run-1 lesson: the /10 rubric saturated on 4 of 6 waves and the time-to-PR tiebreak smuggled speed into a quality verdict (see flow-xqt.2 sensitivity addendum — quality-first re-scoring inverted the registered 5-1 to flow 3, vanilla 1, ties 2).

PRINCIPLE: flow's intent is maximum output quality; speed matters ONLY when quality is exactly equal, and quality deltas compound over time (a closed concurrency seam pays on every future takeover) while speed cost is paid once. Scoring is therefore lexicographic: quality first, speed reporting-only.

1. PRIMARY — pairwise forced-choice -> Bradley-Terry/Elo. Per wave, walk every cell pair's diffs side by side (4 cells = 6 comparisons) and force 'A better / B better / indistinguishable', each verdict citing at least one concrete artifact difference (file:line). Aggregate across waves into a per-cell strength score. No absolute cap, so no saturation; byte-identical artifacts come out true ties.

2. SECONDARY — severity-weighted defect ledger. Adversarially enumerate defects per artifact (missed locus, absent test, convention miss, latent seam, scope creep, style violation); each defect is a falsifiable file:line claim, bucketed blocker/major/minor/nit. Quality order = lexicographic on counts (fewest blockers, then majors, then minors, then nits). Used to break pairwise ties and to ground the pairwise verdicts in citable evidence.

3. SPEED — time-to-PR recorded and reported per cell, NEVER decisive. A residual exact tie after 1+2 is declared a tie (split or alternate the merge), not speed-broken.

4. GROUND TRUTH (slow, objective) — per merged artifact, track: (a) follow-up-fix rate (beads later filed against it — run-1 precedent: flow-g8l7 + flow-grp4 are post-merge defects against wave-3/4 registered winners that the quality-first losers did not have); (b) revert rate (flow-a1ti.2 watcher); (c) for test-heavy tasks, mutation kill rate as an objective test-quality number.

5. JUDGING HYGIENE — judge still unblinded by branch names (acknowledged); mitigations: per-verdict citations mandatory, and optionally a second independent judge on disagreement-prone waves (composes with the flow-0nhg/flow-rjmo review-metric/panel proposals).

6. DNF semantics unchanged from v3 (outage DNF = judged among survivors, no nudges, contamination noted).

title: version.py stamp: semantic bump — minor on feat, patch otherwise
description: Maintainer wants the version number to communicate content (2026-06-09 drain retro): queue_select, the /flow queue verb, and the engine snapshot guard shipped as 0.27.x patch bumps despite being feat: commits. The stamp is mechanical (next-patch from origin/main, content-blind) by design — keep the merge-time one-liner shape, add type awareness: version.py stamp reads the commit type (from the branch HEAD subject's conventional-commit prefix, or an explicit --commit-type flag the merge prose passes from the bead frontmatter commit_type) and bumps MINOR on feat, PATCH otherwise. Concurrency design unchanged: still next-from-fresh-origin/main per merge; only which field increments varies. MUST stay type-aware in version_remerge recovery (it re-stamps on DIRTY; a feat branch re-stamped as patch would regress the intent) — thread the same type source through version_remerge.py recover. Prose: stage-merge.md §3 stamp line + any version_remerge mention. Tests beside existing version tests incl. the remerge path. Not hot (version.py/version_remerge.py not in guard set).
close_reason: merged via PR #206 (drain, maintainer-reviewed) — semantic version bump live, minted 0.28.0 for itself

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Plan is the correct ticket reading — type-aware stamp (minor on feat via --commit-type flag > HEAD subject > patch) threaded through version_remerge.recover with a branch-only commit-scan fallback (remerge HEAD is the stamp chore commit). Safe to auto-ship: blast radius tight (both scripts consumed only at merge time by stage-merge §3 + verb-evolve drain reap), wrong bump is monotonic metadata and reverts cleanly, CI (new pytest cases + seam_check live-docs gate) catches realistic failure modes. Independent rating 88%, verdict proceed.

title: spec/adjudication prose: skip advisor probe when session model is Fable (advisor absent by design, Agent fallback is primary)
description: Maintainer info (2026-06-09): when the session model is Fable (claude-fable-5), the advisor tool is NOT available in the harness — and is not needed; the documented fallbacks (general-purpose Agent for the confidence rating, fresh-context strong-tier Agent for ship adjudication) are the primary path. Patch the prose so a Fable session skips the advisor probe (ToolSearch for advisor) instead of attempting it every run: SKILL.md spec-gate line, references/verb-spec.md step 4 (confidence) + step 5 adjudication branch + auto step (line ~139), references/stage-plan.md:69.
