title: Autonomous day-job queue drain: extend the proven evolve-drain fleet to the maintainer's non-evolve ready queue
description:
## The gap (vision-anchored asymmetry)

The maintainer has a full autonomous multi-ticket consumption loop for SELF-IMPROVEMENT beads (evolve-labelled): `/flow evolve drain` runs select -> launch -> wait -> reap, self-merges green leaves, serializes hot, cleans up orphans, all unattended. The maintainer's DAY-JOB ready queue — normal beads tickets outside evolve — has NO equivalent. SKILL.md marks `/flow spec` as one-ticket-per-run; multiple keys prompt the user. So a maintainer can drain 5+ evolve improvements overnight, but must invoke `/flow <ticket>` individually for 5+ day-job tickets.

## VISION anchor (and the honest tension)

VISION.md stakes the thesis on "automate myself away as far as it will go" and names flow as "my primary driver for getting things done" — not self-improvement only. The asymmetry proves the pipeline is not yet the maintainer's primary driver for ALL work, only evolve work.

THE AUTONOMY-LINE TENSION (must be triaged, not glossed): this GENERALIZES the path the skill itself labels DANGEROUS. `/flow evolve drain --include-proposals` already auto-drains judgment work PAST the human spec-plan gate, and the skill flags it dangerous and off-by-default. A day-job drain does the same for ALL day-job tickets: each launched `--auto` run self-plans and self-merges at >=90% confidence, BYPASSING the per-ticket spec gate that VISION reserves for the maintainer ("Judgment -> propose, and I triage"). The mitigating valve is the SAME one --include-proposals relies on: a run that cannot self-approve DEFERS in place and exits, so genuinely ambiguous tickets bounce back to the human rather than shipping wrong. But that valve is exactly what is already deemed dangerous when applied to judgment work. Expanding autonomous drain to day-job tickets changes what the maintainer EXPERIENCES running unattended — that is "what flow is for", the maintainer's call, which is precisely why this is filed as an epic to triage and never auto-built.

## Why it is plausible (the infrastructure already exists)

select / launch / wait / reap / self-merge / lease are proven on evolve and transfer cleanly; the tracker protocol already lists by status (bd ready). Day-job tickets are NOT hot-gated (hotness is evolve-machinery-only), so the hot-serialization layer is absent, simplifying the logic. The open risk the maintainer must weigh: the day-job queue may be small/infrequent enough that batch processing yields no real savings (a quiet live run would surface this); and the spec-gate bypass is the crux of the triage decision, not a detail.

## Blast radius

A new `/flow queue` verb + queue-select logic (sibling to evolve_select.py), a queue-drain consumer loop (sibling to evolve_drain.py), triage.py listing integration, and a memory-dedup check across queues. Touches the dispatcher entry surface and the worktree lifecycle; reuses all existing stages, self-merge, and review_loop infrastructure. No safety guard at risk. Medium-high but narrow — it is mostly the evolve fleet machinery re-pointed at a second candidate set.

## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via §E expand)

1. (NET-NEW) `/flow queue` verb: read-only queue status + a --dry-run mode listing day-job ready beads (non-evolve, non-parked), lease liveness, backpressure — mirrors `evolve drain --dry-run`. dedup epic:autonomous-day-job-drain::child-1-queue-verb.
2. (NET-NEW) queue-select core: selection + backpressure adapted from evolve_select.py for day-job beads; drops in-flight, enforces a separate backpressure cap, same anchor-dedup partitioning; emits model tier per ticket. dedup epic:autonomous-day-job-drain::child-2-select-core.
3. (NET-NEW) queue-drain consumer: launch + wait + reap loop mirroring evolve_drain.py; launches ready tickets via `claude --bg "/flow <key> --auto"`, waits on lease liveness, reaps merged + exited worktrees, loops until done or parked. dedup epic:autonomous-day-job-drain::child-3-drain-consumer.
4. (NET-NEW) triage integration: extend triage.py list to show day-job ready/deferred/blocked alongside the evolve equivalents; reopen flow unchanged. dedup epic:autonomous-day-job-drain::child-4-triage-integration.
5. (NET-NEW) cross-queue memory dedup verification: confirm reflect/memory does not collide for a ticket run individually vs as part of a batch; document the namespace seam (no code change if it already holds). dedup epic:autonomous-day-job-drain::child-5-memory-dedup.

## Grounding cited

Vision cite ("automate myself away as far as it will go"; "primary driver for getting things done") + reproduced witness (SKILL.md one-ticket-per-run gate; `/flow evolve drain --dry-run` proves the fleet pattern works; --include-proposals is the existing DANGEROUS analog whose defer-and-exit valve this reuses).

## Recommended default: needs-discussion (it expands unattended autonomy past the per-ticket spec gate — the maintainer must accept that line, not the agent). Confidence 0.72.
close_reason: epic complete: all 5 children shipped (hw1.1 #199, hw1.2 #194, hw1.3 #201, hw1.4 #192, hw1.5 #190) — /flow queue verb + queue_select/queue_status/queue_drain cluster live

title: Post-hoc transcript trace-mining producer: surface failure modes the in-flight friction log never recorded
description:
## The gap (web-cited + a structural blind spot in the producer set)

flow's friction feedstock is flow_friction.py — an append-only log HAND-WRITTEN by the do-loop prose, IN-FLIGHT, only when a step notices it worked around a snag (SKILL.md "Friction logging (in-flight)"). Producer A (the reflect sling) and Producer B (evolve audit, a cold scan over SOURCE CODE) both consume that log or read code. NEITHER mines completed run TRANSCRIPTS. A failure mode the prose never thought to log — a silent retry, a stage that took three attempts, a recurring drift the agent ate without writing an entry, a stall gap — is invisible to the loop. The feedstock is self-REPORTED, never self-EVIDENT.

## The field moved here (the grounding)

Mining raw execution traces post-hoc is now a recognized discovery surface:
- Self-Harness (arXiv 2606.09498) "Weakness Mining": reads execution traces, clusters failed runs by verifier-grounded failure signatures (terminal cause + agent-behavior contribution + reusable mechanism), feeding the harness-proposal stage.
- Addy Osmani, Agent Harness Engineering: names the frontier directly — "agents that analyze their own traces to identify and fix harness-level failure modes."
- LangChain, The Anatomy of an Agent Harness: agentic judges inspect production traces, identify emerging failure modes, mint reusable labels.

## VISION anchor

"The worker at the station sees what I can't" — the transcript IS the worker's record, and flow currently only sees the friction the worker remembered to write down. A trace miner makes lived friction self-evident rather than self-reported, deepening Producer A's feedstock. Maintainer-gated, self-target only (mines flow's OWN dogfood run transcripts under ~/.claude, never user telemetry — not a platform). Produces deduped PROPOSALS through the existing seam; never auto-implements. A net-new producer at a DISCOVERY altitude flow lacks — distinct from the measurement epics, which grade edits flow already knows it made.

## Distinct from existing producers

- reflect (Producer A) = in-flight, self-reported friction during a run.
- evolve audit (Producer B) = cold static scan of source code.
- evolve propose / epic = generative judgment + theme-scale.
- THIS = post-hoc mining of completed-run TRANSCRIPTS for failure modes none of the above recorded. Net-new feedstock, not a re-run of an existing scan.

## Blast radius

New read-only producer script + a reflect/evolve entry point. Reads transcript JSONL (filesystem-scan only — NEVER `claude agents --json`, which blocks on a TTY per the known gotcha) + the existing friction log, files deduped evolve beads through the existing flow_beads_create.py + evid:<fingerprint> seam, so the existing drain consumer picks them up with zero new consumer code. Additive; no existing-contract change; no safety guard touched.

## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via §E expand)

1. (NET-NEW) trace_mine.py extract: read a finished run's transcript (~/.claude job/session JSONL, filesystem-scan only), emit structured per-stage events (retries, tool-errors, eaten drifts, stall gaps). dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-1-extract.
2. (NET-NEW) Verifier-grounded failure-signature clustering: group events into Self-Harness-style signatures (terminal cause + mechanism), dedup against already-logged flow_friction.py entries so the miner only surfaces MISSED friction. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-2-cluster.
3. (NET-NEW) File mined signatures as deduped evolve beads through flow_beads_create.py + the evid: fingerprint, so the existing drain consumer picks them up. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-3-file.
4. (NET-NEW) Wire as a scheduled producer: add a trace-mining pass alongside evolve audit on the nightly cadence (loop-engineering.md), so transcript mining runs unattended. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-4-schedule.

## Grounding cited

Web cites (Self-Harness Weakness Mining arXiv 2606.09498; Addy Osmani trace-analysis frontier; LangChain agentic trace judges) + reproduced witness (flow_friction.py is hand-logged in-flight; no producer reads run transcripts; friction.jsonl shows recurring RECONCILE classes the in-flight logger captured only partially).

## Recommended default: needs-discussion (a net-new producer adds a standing surface; weigh against keeping the producer set lean). Confidence 0.66.
close_reason: Epic complete — the post-hoc trace-mining producer ships end to end: PR#406 extract (run-window-scoped transcript event miner, 3 adversarial review rounds), PR#416 cluster (failure signatures + missed-friction complement vs friction.jsonl), PR#422 file (deduped proposal beads with no-re-mining evidence), PR#425 nightly wiring (bounded selection, failure-isolated, propose-only). The in-flight friction log's blind spots — tool errors, silent retries, eaten drifts, stall gaps that no stage ever logged — now surface nightly as evidence-carrying beads for the maintainer's triage.

To process: run /flow evolve epic flow-44p to decompose into the preview children, then drain them. Accepting this net-new producer surface is a maintainer needs-discussion call (conf 0.66). comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-19: ACCEPT. Constraints binding: read-only, self-target ONLY (mines flow's own dogfood transcripts under ~/.claude, NEVER user telemetry), filesystem JSONL scan ONLY (never 'claude agents --json' — TTY-blocks, known gotcha), files deduped PROPOSALS through the existing flow_beads_create.py + evid: seam (zero new consumer code), never auto-implements, no existing-contract or safety-guard change. Decomposing into the 4 preview children; reopening epic.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: ALTITUDE PASS 2026-07-02: acceptance stands but SEQUENCE AFTER flow-oe9's recurrence detector. Evidence: the drain keeps up with discovery (bd ready -l evolve ≈ 1 bead), so the loop is not discovery-starved; meanwhile RECONCILE (47 of 87 friction events) recurs ungraded because the verify rung is absent. Adding a fourth discovery producer before fixes can be falsified grows backlog, not thesis. Bonus once sequenced right: child-2's dedup-vs-friction.jsonl gains a consumer in oe9's grader.

title: Pre-merge non-regression eval: gate harness self-edits on a frozen-fixture corpus before merge
description:
## The gap (web-cited + a named VISION floor that is unenforced)

flow accepts a harness self-edit on CI-green (ruff + ty + pytest + seam_check) plus one advisor confidence call and the in-run merge reviewer (evolve_self_merge.py). CI proves the edit did not break the unit suite IN ISOLATION; it never proves the edit IMPROVED the loop versus a baseline. metric.py (MODULE.md) measures shipped-tickets/week and time-to-pr POST-HOC over a window; baseline_collect.py is a one-time migration baseline. Neither scores a PROPOSED self-edit against frozen behavioral fixtures BEFORE it merges. A `grep -iE 'eval|replay|regression|fixture'` over scripts/ + references/ returns no eval/replay/regression harness. So VISION.md's "better must be measurable, or it is vibes" is asserted but UNENFORCED at the acceptance gate.

## The field moved here (the grounding)

A self-improving harness is gated by a fixture corpus with verifier-grounded scoring, not by "the change looks right + CI is green":
- Self-Harness (arXiv 2606.09498): a harness self-edit is accepted only if it improves at least one split without degrading the other, across a held-in + held-out fixture set; rejected proposals are logged, never merged.
- Meta-Harness (yoonholee.com/meta-harness): every proposed harness change is tested on held-out tasks and scored before deployment; the eval corpus IS the acceptance gate.
- Addy Osmani, Agent Harness Engineering: the "ratchet principle" — every harness line traces to a specific failure scored by an eval harness, not vibes.

## Distinct from the two existing measurement epics (the dedup)

- flow-ml7 = AGGREGATE window throughput metrics (tickets/week, time-to-pr, revert-rate, attribution); answers "is flow's OUTPUT improving." POST-SHIP, windowed.
- flow-oe9 = POST-HOC grading of an individual MACHINERY fix (did its friction CLASS stop recurring); substrate = friction.jsonl joined to MACHINERY entries; needs weeks of accumulated data; an advisory READOUT, never a gate.
- THIS epic = PRE-MERGE prevention: a deterministic frozen-fixture corpus over pure decider scripts, scored on a CANDIDATE self-edit, as a non-regression GATE at merge time. Different control point (before-merge gating vs after-ship monitoring), different substrate (frozen decider fixtures vs friction-recurrence join), and crucially NO historical-data dependency — it is buildable now while flow-oe9 must wait for data to mature.

The discriminator a maintainer can accept in one line: pre-merge prevention (this) vs post-hoc detection (flow-oe9) vs throughput measurement (flow-ml7) are three different control points on the same "is a self-edit honest" question.

## VISION anchor

Serves the thesis ("software can improve and heal itself when a capable agent is given a real loop") — the loop must KNOW an edit helped, not assume it. This is the missing measurement floor VISION names by hand. Single-user, engine-internal (a measurement gate on "how flow runs", the agent's domain). Squarely build-side instrumentation; the gate routes a regressing edit to the human rather than auto-shipping it, so it tightens the floor rather than eroding it.

## Blast radius

New engine surface: a frozen-fixture corpus dir + a scoring CLI extending the metric seam (stdlib-only), wired as an advisory signal at reflect and a non-regression check at merge. Touches evolve_self_merge.py (a guard-adjacent merge-gate change — hot path, guard-property review applies on its child), metric.py, references/stage-reflect.md, references/stage-merge.md. Engine-internal; no user-facing contract change.

## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via §E expand)

1. (NET-NEW) Deterministic decider-fixture corpus: capture N frozen (input -> expected-output) cases for the pure deciders already amenable to it (evolve_select.decide, evolve_drain.decide, evolve_self_merge.decide, triage.is_hot_change), held-in + held-out split. dedup epic:self-edit-regression-eval::child-1-fixture-corpus.
2. (NET-NEW) harness_eval.py score CLI: run a candidate skill-checkout against the corpus, emit a verifier-grounded pass/regress delta per split (extend the metric seam, stdlib-only). dedup epic:self-edit-regression-eval::child-2-score-cli.
3. (NET-NEW) Wire the score as an advisory signal into reflect lens-B: a self-edit proposal surfaces its corpus delta alongside the advisor confidence (stage-reflect.md), so a regressing edit is visible before it is slung/applied. dedup epic:self-edit-regression-eval::child-3-reflect-advisory.
4. (NET-NEW) Non-regression gate at the merge stage: evolve_self_merge.decide consumes the delta; a self-edit that regresses a split blocks auto-merge (Self-Harness's no-degradation rule), routing to the human. Guard-property review applies (hot path). dedup epic:self-edit-regression-eval::child-4-merge-gate.
5. (NET-NEW) Backfill the corpus from the friction log + closed evolve beads: seed held-in cases from real past failures so the ratchet is grounded in lived friction, not synthetic. dedup epic:self-edit-regression-eval::child-5-backfill.

## Grounding cited

Web cites (Self-Harness arXiv 2606.09498 held-in/held-out non-regression acceptance; Meta-Harness eval-corpus gate; Addy Osmani ratchet principle) + reproduced witness (no eval/replay/regression harness exists in scripts/; metric.py is post-hoc, baseline_collect.py is one-time).

## Recommended default: build-now (no data-maturity dependency, unlike flow-oe9; it is the buildable-today half of the self-edit-honesty question). Confidence 0.72.
close_reason: epic complete: all 5 children shipped (63q.1 #191, 63q.2 #198+#202, 63q.3 #203, 63q.4 #204, 63q.5 #195) — frozen decider corpus + harness_eval scorer + reflect advisory + fail-closed merge gate live

description: Parent epic flow-6gx. Depends on child-1. A merge-time (or post-merge CI) step writes the derived version to the two files on main. FLOOR OBLIGATION (load-bearing): the two version files are inside snapshot.py _tree_hash, so the canonical-snapshot drift guard currently protects them; this child MUST preserve that drift property over engine content (snapshot still hashes engine content; only the per-PR version-line edit leaves the path). Replace the property with a provably-equivalent one, never drop it. Blast radius: a merge/post-merge step, snapshot.py (HOT, guard file), CI. Likely HOT. Run via /flow <key>.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CORRECTION (found at flow-6gx.1 spec): the two version files are NOT in the snapshot hash. snapshot.py:45 globs only *.py/*.sh/*.md/*.toml (no .json), and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json sits at repo root OUTSIDE plugin_root. So this child's stated 'preserve the snapshot drift property over the version files' obligation is MOOT - they were never protected by the snapshot. The real drift property to keep is minimal: version files stay .json + marketplace.json stays at repo root. The MACHINERY: provenance coupling (flow-6gx.5) is the separate live concern, not the snapshot.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SCOPE EXPANDED (maintainer decision): this child now ABSORBS flow-6gx.2 (closed, folded). One atomic PR: (a) remove flow_worktree.py _with_version_bump_files + the implement-stage mandatory per-PR bump (was .2), AND (b) add the merge-time stamp that writes version.py's computed value to plugin.json+marketplace.json on main (was .4). Atomic because the two cannot split without a broken intermediate (double-bump or frozen version). version.py (flow-6gx.1, merged PR#185) is the compute seam this consumes.

title: Retire version_remerge.py + the serial-drain merge constraint once PRs carry no version line
description: Parent epic flow-6gx. Depends on child-2. Once PRs no longer carry the version line, the DIRTY-on-version-only conflict class vanishes, so version_remerge.py (Option B, shipped at flow-hso/PR#156) and the SERIAL version_recoverable merge constraint in verb-evolve.md drain become dead weight. Delete the helper, remove the serial-merge constraint, unblock parallel drain merges. Prove zero remaining callers before deletion. Blast radius: delete version_remerge.py, verb-evolve.md drain prose, evolve_reap.classify version_recoverable bucket. Run via /flow <key>.
close_reason: merged via PR #187 (finalization: simplify version_remerge + reflect-provenance + prose collapse; absorbs .5/.6)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: REFRAMED (flow-6gx.4 decision): the merge-time stamp keeps a version line on the branch at merge, so the DIRTY-on-version conflict class did NOT vanish and version_remerge is RETAINED. This child is now 'SIMPLIFY version_remerge', not 'retire' it. Re-scope at spec: e.g. share the write path with version.py.write_version (introduced in flow-6gx.4), trim now-dead prose, narrow the recovery. Do NOT delete version_remerge.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: SCOPE EXPANDED to the epic's FINALIZATION (absorbs flow-6gx.5 + flow-6gx.6, both closed-folded). One PR: (a) SIMPLIFY version_remerge.py - reuse version.py.write_version for its two-file writes (dedup; RETAINED, not retired - branches still carry a version line at merge per the .4 decision); (b) reflect-provenance (.5): stage-reflect.md:87 + self-evolution.md:30 stop telling the self-heal to bump version on its branch (it commits touched skill files; version stamps at merge; the MACHINERY commit sha is the durable provenance); (c) prose collapse (.6): remove the now-stale per-PR-bump / byte-identical / advisory-recompute framing across the doc set, KEEPING the still-valid drain version_recoverable DIRTY-recovery prose. Decision: combine for one review/merge per the 'ship the epic' goal.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
AUTHORITATIVE SCOPE (surface mapped by spec Explore; follow exactly):

PART A - simplify version_remerge.py (dedup, NOT retire):
- recover() writes the bumped version via its own _set_version_in_file() (~lines 106-112) inside the per-file loop (~150-155). REPLACE that write with a single call to version.write_version(cwd=cwd, version=next_ver) (shared helper from flow-6gx.1), and DELETE _set_version_in_file. KEEP: is_version_only_conflict/_conflict_set (strict detector), _strip_version (content-identity), git merge/abort/add/commit/push. version_remerge is RETAINED (the merge-stamp puts a version line on the branch -> DIRTY-on-version recovery still happens). Update tests/test_version_remerge.py if the internal write path shape changed.

PART B - reflect-provenance prose (2 loci; self-heal must NOT bump version on its branch - it stamps at merge; commit touched skill files + record the commit sha as MACHINERY provenance):
- references/stage-reflect.md ~L87: 'bump version in plugin.json (always patch...flow-8we) and commit the touched skill files + the bump on that feature branch' -> 'commit the touched skill files (no version bump - it stamps at merge, stage-merge.md S3) and record the commit sha in the MACHINERY entry as provenance'.
- references/self-evolution.md ~L30: 'bump the plugin version, commit the touched skill files on that branch, record the commit sha' -> 'commit the touched skill files on that branch (no version bump - stamps at merge), record the commit sha as provenance'.

PART C - prose collapse (fix NOW-WRONG, LEAVE still-valid):
- FIX references/verb-evolve.md ~L124: 'every PR bumps the two version files...' -> merge-stamp reality (siblings' merge-time stamps walk main's version forward; a later PR can go DIRTY on the version line if main merges during its post-stamp CI re-wait).
- FIX CLAUDE.md ~L51 'Version bumps' invariant: rule (both files in sync) stays TRUE; clarify WHEN = at merge time via version.py stamp, not per-PR.
- DO NOT TOUCH (still valid): stage-merge.md S3 stamp + DIRTY/version_remerge recovery; stage-implement.md 'do NOT bump here'; verb-evolve.md:151 liveness caveat; the drain version_recoverable recovery.

This PR does NOT bump its own version in implement (new scheme). It is human-merged (proposal gate skips); the maintainer stamps at merge. Gates: mise run test + mise run lint + seam_check.

title: Drop plugin.json/marketplace.json from the PR content path; remove _with_version_bump_files
description: Parent epic flow-6gx. Depends on child-1 (derivation seam). Remove flow_worktree.py _with_version_bump_files so PRs stop auto-injecting the two version files into planned_files and stop editing them inline. Update the planned_files auto-injection + the content-ownership commit gate (diff_extract check-ownership) so a PR with no version-line edit is clean. THIS is the child that kills the per-PR planned_files reconcile tax (flow-014, flow-4lb, the 22/30 RECONCILE friction). Blast radius: flow_worktree.py, diff_extract.py (hot-adjacent; settle hotness at spec), planned_files prose. Run via /flow <key>.
close_reason: folded into flow-6gx.4: dropping the per-PR bump and adding the merge-time stamp cannot land separately without a broken version window (double-bump if .4 first, frozen version if .2 first). Combined into one atomic swap under .4.

title: KEYSTONE: version-derivation seam computing the next patch at release/merge time
description: Parent epic flow-6gx (version-derivation-off-content-path). KEYSTONE child. Add a single source (e.g. version.py) that computes the next plugin patch version from merge-sequence or commit-count at release/merge time, replacing the per-PR hand-bump. This is the seam every other child builds on; build first. DESIGN FORK to settle at spec (carried from the epic): merge-time (committed by a post-merge step) vs release-time derivation, constrained by whether Claude-Code marketplace consumption requires a committed version on main at all times. Blast radius: new version.py + whoever reads the plugin version. Non-hot. Run via /flow <key>.

title: Retire the per-PR inline version bump: derive the plugin version at merge/release time
description:
## The premise that aged (architecture-era shift, witnessed)

The invariant "on any change to the plugin, bump `plugin.json` + `marketplace.json` in sync" (CLAUDE.md) forces EVERY PR to edit two tracked content files inline. Sound when flow shipped one PR at a time; the N-concurrent-drain reality outgrew it. The evidence is the apparatus built to survive the collision the premise manufactures:

- `flow_worktree.py` `_with_version_bump_files` auto-injects the two version files into `planned_files` for any `plugins/flow/` change, putting the version edit on the content/ownership path of every maintainer PR.
- `version_remerge.py` (whole file, ~8K) exists ONLY to auto-resolve the version-line conflict when sibling drain PRs all bump the same base: strict `VERSION_FILES` detector, serial re-merge, re-push, mandatory CI re-wait.
- `verb-evolve.md` §drain forces the `version_recoverable` set to merge SERIALLY: a hard throughput ceiling on the drain loop imposed purely by the version line.
- The "version is advisory; recompute against the worktree base; always patch never minor so concurrent bumps stay byte-identical (flow-8we)" reasoning is re-explained across ~10 prose docs (stage-implement.md, verb-spec.md, stage-reflect.md, stage-plan.md, stage-merge.md, ...). A premise so load-bearing it had to be restated at every seam.
- Current version is 0.27.56: 56 patch bumps, each a manufactured content-path edit. No release/tag workflow exists (`.github/workflows/` holds only `test.yml`); nothing derives the version anywhere except the per-PR hand-edit.

## Why an epic, not the already-filed pile (the decisive test)

Every filed bead + helper PRESERVES the premise and only makes the collision cheaper: flow-8we (deterministic byte-identical bump), `version_remerge` (Option B serial recovery), `_with_version_bump_files` (auto-add to planned_files). This epic REMOVES the premise: the version is derived at merge/release time (commit-count or merge-sequence to patch), PRs never touch the two version files. That deletes `version_remerge` entirely, deletes `_with_version_bump_files`, deletes the serial-merge constraint in drain, and collapses the advisory-recompute prose across ~10 docs. No single PR holds that: it spans worktree bootstrap, the drain merge loop, the merge stage, the reflect provenance path, and the whole prose layer. The pile is structurally downstream of the premise and can never reach it. Several filed beads about the current seam (flow-lia, flow-4lb, flow-56s) are likely MOOTED by this epic; the spec should check whether they fold in or close.

## VISION anchor

"Better must be measurable, or it is vibes": removes a per-PR collision tax and a serial-merge throughput ceiling on the very drain loop that ships every self-improvement (fewer friction events per drain pass, faster batch completion; `recall --metric` is the seam). Serves "the system is where the magic is" by deleting accidental machinery instead of adding surface. RECONCILE is 22 of 30 friction events over the window; this premise is the largest single source.

## Robustness floor (load-bearing, must hold)

The two version files are inside `snapshot.py` `_tree_hash`, so the canonical-snapshot drift guard currently protects them. Relocating version off the content path MUST PRESERVE the drift property: the snapshot still hashes engine content; only the version-line edit leaves the per-PR path. The reflect self-heal fast-path stamps the version on its feature branch as `MACHINERY:` provenance; the new design must keep a durable version-stamped record of a machinery self-edit (the property), even if the literal bump moves to merge time. Replace the property, never drop it. State both in the spec.

## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via §E expand)

1. (NET-NEW) Version-derivation seam: a `version.py` computing the next patch from merge-sequence / commit-count at release time; single source replacing the hand-bump. dedup `epic:version-derivation-off-content-path::child-1-derivation-seam`.
2. (NET-NEW) Drop version files from the PR content path: remove `_with_version_bump_files`; PRs stop editing `plugin.json`/`marketplace.json`; update planned_files auto-injection + the content-ownership gate. dedup `epic:version-derivation-off-content-path::child-2-off-content-path`.
3. (NET-NEW) Retire `version_remerge` + the serial-drain constraint: once PRs do not carry the version line, the DIRTY-on-version-only class vanishes; delete the helper and unblock parallel drain merges. dedup `epic:version-derivation-off-content-path::child-3-retire-remerge`.
4. (NET-NEW) Stamp the marketplace/plugin version at merge: a merge-time (or post-merge CI) step writes the derived version to the two files on main; preserve the snapshot drift property over engine content. dedup `epic:version-derivation-off-content-path::child-4-merge-stamp`.
5. (NET-NEW) Preserve machinery provenance: reflect self-heal records its version stamp from the derivation seam, keeping the `MACHINERY:` durable-record guarantee. dedup `epic:version-derivation-off-content-path::child-5-provenance`.
6. (NET-NEW) Prose collapse: delete the advisory-recompute / byte-identical-bump explanations across the ~10 reference docs; one pointer to the derivation seam. dedup `epic:version-derivation-off-content-path::child-6-prose-collapse`.

## Blast radius

Wide but bounded; the version seam everywhere, no user-facing behavioral surface. `flow_worktree.py`, `version_remerge.py` (delete), `snapshot.py` (preserve the drift property), the merge stage, reflect provenance, ~10 reference docs. The flat-`scripts/` invariant is untouched (no file moves). Guard-floor survival is the spec's first obligation.

## Open design question (why needs-discussion before build-now)

Whether Claude-Code's marketplace consumption requires the version to be a COMMITTED value on main at all times (which constrains "derive at release time" toward "derive at merge time, committed by a post-merge step"). A decomposition detail, not a viability blocker, but it is why this is propose-and-triage, not a slam dunk.

## Grounding cited

file:line evidence the premise aged at system scale (the apparatus built to survive it) + the loudest witnessed friction class (RECONCILE 22/30). Not asserted.

## Recommended default: needs-discussion (merge-time-vs-release-time derivation), then build-now. Confidence 0.66.
close_reason: epic shipped: per-PR version bump retired, version stamps at merge. .1 (#185 version.py seam) + .4 (#186 atomic swap, absorbs .2) + .3 (#187 finalize, absorbs .5/.6). main at 0.27.59.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: LINEAGE (found at expand time): flow-hso (CLOSED, shipped PR #156 version_remerge.py) named TWO fix-shapes for the parallel-drain version conflict: (A) compute the bump at MERGE time not plan time, (B) merge-stage auto-rebase+re-bump on version-only conflict. The loop shipped B (version_remerge, Option B) and DEFERRED A. flow-8we MACHINERY note also named 'removing the version line from feature PRs entirely' as 'the only thing that would also kill cross-batch collisions' and chose the lighter deterministic-bump instead. flow-6gx IS option A scoped up to an epic. Value OVER the shipped Option B: B only recovers the drain-time merge conflict; it leaves the per-PR planned_files reconcile tax (flow-014, flow-4lb -> the 22/30 RECONCILE friction) untouched. Only premise-removal kills BOTH that conflict class AND the reconcile round-trip, and deletes version_remerge entirely. Not a dup of a shipped fix; the deferred structural superset.

title: evolve_session_cleanup state-floor guard leaks finished --auto runs (zombie sessions)
description: EVIDENCE: drain of 11 proposals left 5 bg --auto sessions un-reaped by evolve_session_cleanup despite their beads being terminal (flow-dwd/5mu/8q6/837 CLOSED+merged, flow-1y5 DEFERRED). The A2 cleanup classify skipped all 5 with reason 'state floor not met (state=working/blocked, tempo=idle)'. ROOT: a bg '/flow <key> --auto' run finishes its pipeline (PR merged, bead closed) but its job state.json never flips state working->done, so the state-floor guard (requires state in {done,stopped}) PERMANENTLY skips it. The transcripts were idle 190-2039s and leases non-live; only the stale state field blocked reap. This is the zombie-session accumulation the maintainer flagged. SECONDARY: state='blocked' (flow-837, a CLOSED bead) is also stale/wrong. FIX DIRECTION (judgment): add a secondary reap path — when the bead is terminal (closed/blocked/deferred) AND lease non-live AND transcript idle beyond a LONGER threshold (e.g. 2x idle, or 900s), reap regardless of the stale state field, since bead-terminal+lease-dead+long-idle is independent proof the run finished. Keep the existing fast path. BLAST RADIUS: evolve_session_cleanup.py classify predicate + its test; no other caller. Not a guard/safety file (absent from triage._GUARD_FILES). CONFIDENCE: 0.8. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build — concrete repro, bounded, maintainer-witnessed.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CORRECTION (witnessed cleaning up this very drain): the proposed fix 'rm regardless of stale state field' is WRONG — rm does not work. The daemon (supervisor, long-lived) OWNS the job registry; rm-ing ~/.claude/jobs/<id> is immediately RE-MATERIALIZED by the daemon as a stub (verified: intent field went empty, state stayed 'working', dir reappeared). evolve_session_cleanup's rm-based A2 path is fundamentally defeated for any session the daemon still has registered. Also: claude stop needs a TTY (timeout-killed in a headless drain shell; zero stop events in daemon.log). REAL root cause: a bg --auto run finishes but the daemon never transitions its registry entry to done/stopped, and there is no daemon-sanctioned dismiss CLI (the §drain A2 doc admits 'none in 2.1.168'). REAL fix direction: either (a) the run must signal the daemon to mark itself done at pipeline end (so claude agents drops it), or (b) cleanup needs a real daemon dismiss API when one ships — NOT more rm. Until then these linger until the panel is closed/Ctrl-X'd or they auto-expire. Keep this bead OPEN as a known harness limitation.

title: Fix metric.py attribution capture: stamp run_id + timestamps at ship time (forward-only)
description: Child 1 / KEYSTONE of epic flow-ml7. metric.py:_state_path (metric.py:130-131) hardcodes workspace_root/.flow/runs/<ticket>/state.json and bypasses the memory redirect that ship-events use, so classify_attribution (metric.py:148-150) always hits not-a-file -> ATTR_NOT_ATTRIBUTED for every shipped ticket. time-to-pr gates on ATTR_VIA_FLOW so its zero is downstream of the same break. FIX (settled at spec): stamp run_id + plan/create_pr timestamps + completion status into the REDIRECTED store at ship time (ship-observe / reflect path), NOT a lookup-redirect — historical worktrees are reaped, so only stamp-at-ship-time recovers data, forward-only (state that as a known limit). One fix lights up BOTH shipped_via_flow and time-to-pr from the same stamped data. Ground-truth defect: metric.py:17 / metric.py:137-140 docstrings promise a join the code cannot deliver. Ships on its own merits even if the epic is shelved. Blast radius: metric.py, the ship-observe/reflect stamp site (_memory_paths.py or stage-reflect.md), tests/test_metric.py. Non-hot.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #175 (squash-merged); measurement loop attribution stamp lands forward-only

title: evolve epic §E expand mints duplicate children for pre-existing re-parent targets
description: Found by the first live /flow evolve epic run (flow-ml7). §epic.E files children with dedup-key 'epic:<track>::child-N-<symptom>', a different namespace from pre-existing beads' '<relfile>::<symptom>' keys. So when an epic's decomposition preview names an EXISTING bead as a child (flow-ml7 lists flow-148 + flow-1y5 to re-parent), a naive expand does NOT collide and WOULD mint duplicates. Fix: §E must distinguish 'materialize net-new child' (file fresh) from 're-parent existing' (bd update <key> --parent <epic>, never re-file). Grounding: flow-ml7 EXPAND CAUTION block.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Fix §epic.E expand by branching on the decomposition preview's existing-vs-net-new marker: net-new children file fresh via flow_beads_create.py (unchanged); children the preview tags 'PRE-EXISTING CHILD to RE-PARENT (do NOT re-file): <key>' are reparented with bare 'bd update <key> --parent <epic>' (no relabel). §C promotes that marker convention (already emitted organically by flow-ml7's preview) to a contract so §E's branch is reliable; §E-only would leave the bug latent on an unmarked future preview. Safe to auto-ship: tiny blast radius (one prose section, maintainer-run path, NOT the hot do-loop), trivial prose-revert reversibility, CI unchanged (seam_check stays green on the kept flow_beads_create.py net-new invocation). Preventive, not a live fix — flow-ml7's first expand already reparented flow-148/flow-1y5 correctly by hand.

title: Close the measurement loop so flow can prove its own improvement
description:
## The gap (witnessed, reproduced ground truth)

flow's measurement layer — the seam VISION.md:54 makes load-bearing ("better must be measurable, or it is vibes") — is structurally blind on two of the three vision-named measures, and absent on both proposed ones. A self-evolving loop that cannot attribute its own ships or time them cannot score its own improvement; the thesis is unfalsifiable from the inside.

Reproduced from `plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/`:
- `metric.py tickets-per-week --namespace flow` -> `shipped_via_flow: 0, shipped_backend_not_attributed: 12` (flow ships work but attributes none of it to flow).
- `metric.py time-to-pr --namespace flow` -> `n_measured: 0` (no time-to-PR data captured over 12 shipped tickets).
- `metric.py friction-per-run --namespace flow` -> `events_per_run: 1.21` (the WORKING contrast).

## Root cause (single asymmetry, verified)

Ship events are written through the memory redirect (`_memory_paths.resolve_memory_base`), so all 12 land correctly in the shared main `.flow/flow/ship-events/`. But the attribution join `metric.py:_state_path` (metric.py:130-131) is hardcoded `workspace_root/.flow/runs/<ticket>/state.json` and does NOT go through the redirect. A run's `state.json` lives only inside its worktree (`.flow/runs/<key>/`, see memory `flow-run-state-lives-in-worktree`), which `reap` tears down after merge. So the join in `classify_attribution` (metric.py:148-150) hits the not-a-file branch for every event -> always `ATTR_NOT_ATTRIBUTED`. `time-to-pr` gates on `ATTR_VIA_FLOW`, so its zero is downstream of the same broken join. `friction-per-run` works precisely because its feedstock flows through the redirect. The metric that uses the redirect works; the two that bypass it are blind. Pattern across history: flow shipped the MEASURE half of each metric (flow-p81 time-to-pr, flow-eia friction-per-run, both closed) while the OBSERVE/attribution half stayed broken or absent.

## Why an epic (vision-leverage)

Highest-leverage epic class per loop-engineering.md: the meta-loop improves the engine that produces every other improvement. The loop's control structure is healthy (maker/checker, compounding memory, separate-verifier stop all hold); the gap is in measurement instrumentation, the one thing the vision makes load-bearing. Lowest ouroboros risk: this closes an already-implied track, it does not build a net-new empire.

## Decomposition preview (do-loop-sized children; materialize on accept via expand)

1. KEYSTONE (NET-NEW, not yet filed) -- fix the attribution capture seam. dedup `epic:measurement-loop-closure::child-1-attribution-capture`.
   DESIGN NOTE for spec: the lens's two options are NOT equivalent. "Redirect the `_state_path` lookup to the memory base" recovers NOTHING historical -- the 12 already-shipped tickets' worktrees (and their state.json) are already reaped and gone (verified: only 2 unreaped worktrees survive on disk). Only STAMP-AT-SHIP-TIME works: have the ship-observe / reflect path stamp run_id + plan/create_pr timestamps + completion status into the redirected store at ship time. Attribution then lights up for tickets shipped AFTER the seam lands, forward only -- that is acceptable and should be stated as a known limit, not chased backward. This single fix lights up BOTH `shipped_via_flow` and `time-to-pr` from the same stamped data.
   GROUND-TRUTH NOTE: this child is closer to a ground-truth defect than judgment -- metric.py:17 / metric.py:137-140 docstrings promise an attribution join the code cannot deliver (a code/contract mismatch). It should be drained on its own merits EVEN IF this epic is shelved; do not let the theme wrapper gate the one fix that delivers most of the value. The remaining judgment is only the stamp-site design choice, settled at spec.

2. PRE-EXISTING CHILD to RE-PARENT (do NOT re-file): flow-148 -- revert-rate (the 3rd vision-named measure) has neither a metric nor a capture seam. Maintainer DECISION on record (option A: reopened-bead join via the classify_attribution pattern). On expand, `bd update flow-148 --parent <this-epic>`; do not mint a new child for it.

3. PRE-EXISTING CHILD to RE-PARENT (do NOT re-file): flow-1y5 -- tokens-per-run metric (measurable hook for worker-model tiering). Maintainer leans to the lighter outcome-metric alternative; settle depth at spec. On expand, `bd update flow-1y5 --parent <this-epic>`.

4. TREND ROLL-UP (NET-NEW, optional, last) -- a single `recall --metric trend` (or one roll-up call) that prints all four measures over the window so the loop's health reads in one call. Only after children 1-3 capture real data. dedup `epic:measurement-loop-closure::child-4-trend-rollup`.

EXPAND CAUTION: §E files children with dedup-key `epic:<track>::child-N-<symptom>`, a different namespace from flow-148 / flow-1y5's existing `<relfile>::<symptom>` keys -- so expand would NOT dedup-collide and WOULD mint duplicates of those two. Re-parent them instead of re-filing; only children 1 and 4 are net-new and materialize fresh.

## Blast radius

`metric.py`, the ship-observe / reflect stamp site (`_memory_paths.py` or `stage-reflect.md` path), `state.py` (token field for child 3), `references/verb-recall.md` + `stage-reflect.md` prose, `tests/test_metric.py`. No safety-guard files; non-hot. The keystone touches read + a new stamp write, low risk, high leverage.

## Recommended default: build-now

Grounding cited: reproduced metric output (shipped_via_flow:0 / n_measured:0) + verified file:line root cause (metric.py:130-131 bypasses the redirect ship events use) + verified worktree-reap lifecycle (historical state gone). Witnessed, not asserted. Confidence 0.85.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE: measurement loop closed — attribution stamps, time-to-PR/revert-rate measures, trend roll-up (flow-ml7.2/PR#278) shipped; with flow-a1ti, flow measures its own improvement end to end. Force-close over flow-1y5: deferred-optional, survives as standalone bead.

title: evolve drain termination gate ignores launched_pending: returns action=done while a just-launched run is still in its pre-lease plan phase (hot bead held behind it)
description: Observed live during /flow evolve drain: flow-7f1 was launched (claude --bg /flow flow-7f1 --auto), its bg session read state=working/tempo=active, bead OPEN, and it was in launch_ledger + launched_pending — but it had not yet created its worktree/lease/branch (hot bead, plan stage runs the advisor confidence gate, slow). evolve_drain returned {action:done, parked:[flow-7f1], held_hot:[flow-bgh], launched_pending:[flow-7f1,...], live_runs:[]}. Root cause: the termination gate (verb-evolve.md:179 / evolve_select action logic) treats action=done when launch is empty AND no in-flight lease reads live|corrupt. A launched-but-pre-lease run has liveness 'absent' (no lease file yet) so it is non-blocking — but it is demonstrably in-flight (launched_pending says so, and the bg session is working). The 'absent==non-blocking' rule is deliberate for WITHHELD/parked finished runs (verb-evolve.md:181), but it conflates two cases: (a) a run that finished+parked (correctly non-blocking) vs (b) a run still bootstrapping pre-lease (incorrectly non-blocking). launched_pending distinguishes them: a key in launched_pending has NOT finished. Consequence: the loop can falsely terminate during the launch->init window, abandoning the held hot bead (flow-bgh here) until a human re-runs drain. FIX: action should be 'wait' (not 'done') when launched_pending is non-empty (a launched run has not yet registered/finished), OR the gate should treat launched_pending keys as blocking the same way live leases do. Add a test: launched_pending non-empty + no live lease + held_hot non-empty -> action=wait, not done. Blast radius: drain loop correctness / premature termination; non-guard files (evolve_select.py/evolve_drain.py).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed on Approach B (non-hot). Fix the termination gate in the evolve subsystem, NOT at dispatch_stage init (a guard file -> hot). evolve_drain.cli_main removes a launch marker once its run registers (live lease OR open PR), so the key drops out of launched_pending at registration (persisting past later merge/teardown); decide() then treats a non-empty launched_pending as blocking -> wait (excluded from parked). Adds launch_ledger.remove(). Safe to auto-ship: blast radius = evolve_drain.cli_main + one new ledger fn, no guard files; reversible (purely additive); CI-covered (decide/remove/cli_main unit-tested). Bounded by the 1800s launch TTL, design-consistent with the selector. D-wait already cap-and-reloops, so a lease-less launched_pending wait polls safely; prose clarified.

title: merge-stage hot gate is label-only; misses guard-file hotness (skips §2 review)
description: evolve_self_merge.decide() (plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/evolve_self_merge.py:50) computes is_hot = 'hot' in labels — LABEL-ONLY. The merge stage (references/stage-merge.md §1) ties the §2 independent guard-property review to that is_hot. So an evolve bead that touches a safety-machinery guard file (snapshot.py/lease.py/dispatch_stage.py/diff_extract.py/state.py/_atomicio.py/_locking.py/machinery_edit.py/flow_friction.py) but was never 'hot'-LABELLED returns is_hot=false and AUTO-MERGES to main WITHOUT the §2 review — violating the CLAUDE.md hot-auto-merge invariant ('A HOT leaf PR may auto-merge ... gated by ... agent diff review'). Observed on flow-1fy/PR#170: bead labelled evolve (not hot), planned_files contained snapshot.py; gate returned {action:merge, is_hot:false} while triage.py decided --files returned is_hot:true. The merge ran the §2 review only because the operator overrode the gate by hand. Same CLASS as the is-hot-guard-omits-flow_worktree memory, but here the gate ignores file-path hotness ENTIRELY, not just one omitted file. FIX: decide() (or its CLI wiring in cli_main, evolve_self_merge.py:118 _bead_labels) must OR in file-path hotness — flag is_hot when planned_files (from .flow/tickets/<key>.md frontmatter, or the run baseline) intersects the safety-machinery guard-file set, reusing the same guard set triage.py uses (and fixing that set's flow_worktree omission too, per flow's is-hot-guard memory). Add a unit test: an evolve, non-hot-labelled bead whose planned_files include snapshot.py -> decide()/gate returns is_hot=true.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) FIX as specced — OR guard-file hotness into evolve_self_merge.decide()'s is_hot (mirror evolve_reap._effective_hot / triage.is_hot_change), reading planned_files from .flow/tickets/<key>.md frontmatter; also add flow_worktree.py to triage._GUARD_FILES. Safe to auto-ship: the change is purely ADDITIVE over the existing label check (is_hot = label OR guard-file hit), so it can only make more things hot (more reviews/blocks), never fewer — no regression vs today. Blast radius is broad (all is_hot_change callers) but is_hot_change's list->bool contract is unchanged; only the guard set grew conservatively. Clean revert, CI + the hot PR's own §2 guard-property review at merge gate it.

title: evolve_reap.classify mis-buckets a substantively-hot bead (hot-in-prose, no hot label) into version_recoverable, bypassing the hot-isolation + guard gate
description: Observed live during /flow evolve drain: flow-1fy (PR #170) edits snapshot.py (a safety-machinery guard file) and the bead self-declares HOT ('must ride the hot path for the guard-property review'), but the bead carries NO 'hot' label (labels: evid:..., evidfile:..., evolve). evolve_reap.classify (evolve_reap.py:114-189) decides is_hot from the labels_index only, so it read flow-1fy as non-hot and routed its green-DIRTY PR into version_recoverable -- the explicitly NON-hot auto-recover bucket (verb-evolve.md:122-149). Per design 'A HOT DIRTY PR never lands in version_recoverable (classify keeps it in blocked); hot conflicts are not auto-recovered' (verb-evolve.md:149). Consequence: the version_remerge auto-recover path (pushes a NEW SHA) would have fired on a hot guard-file PR, bypassing both the hot one-at-a-time isolation and the merge-time guard-property review -- AND racing the still-live run's own in-run hot merge reviewer. Caught only by a manual guard review + advisor here. Root cause: hot-ness is determined two ways that can disagree -- triage.py _GUARD_FILES / is_hot at file-time vs the runtime 'hot' LABEL evolve_reap reads. classify should treat a bead as hot when EITHER it carries the hot label OR its PR diff touches a _GUARD_FILES guard file (snapshot.py, lease.py, state.py, dispatch_stage.py, machinery_edit.py, _atomicio.py, _locking.py, diff_extract.py, flow_friction.py, ...). Same class as [[is-hot-guard-omits-flow-worktree]] (hot-in-substance, not-hot-mechanically). Blast radius: any guard-file bead filed without an explicit hot label can be auto-recovered/auto-merged without the guard gate -- a safety-machinery bypass. Flag HOT (touches the reap classifier + guard semantics).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Fix: compute effective hotness in evolve_reap.classify as ('hot' in labels) OR triage.is_hot_change(pr changed-file paths), applied at BOTH the loop branch and the _hot_eligible/promotion predicate; reap() adds 'files' to the gh pr list --json fields; extract paths with f.get('path','') (classify stays total). Reuse triage.is_hot_change, do not duplicate _GUARD_FILES (that would recreate the two-sources-disagree class this bead kills). Safe to auto-ship unattended because the fix is safety-monotonic: every reclassification it can cause moves toward hold-for-human (guard DIRTY: version_recoverable->blocked; guard clean+flag-off: merge->skipped_hot; guard clean+isolated: is_hot:False->True so the guard property-check fires). Broadening hotness can only grow len(hot_eligible), which only suppresses a promotion, never creates an unwanted one; a bug degrades to status-quo-or-more-conservative, cannot open a new bypass. Blast radius maintainer-only (classify runs only in the drain reap step), clean revert, new unit tests + seam_check gate it. Pre-flight verified live: gh pr list --json files DOES populate per-PR paths.

title: seam_check: add MODULE.md surface-cell completeness check against argparse
description: seam_check validates prose->CLI call sites against argparse (scripts_missing_from_module_md checks row presence), but does NOT validate that MODULE.md surface cells enumerate all argparse subcommands. This let triage.py's 'adjudicate-enabled' and 'adjudicate-hot-enabled' subcommands drift undetected for multiple PRs (flow-hjd). Proposed fix: add a seam_check helper that parses each MODULE.md surface cell via argparse introspection and ERRORs when a script's add_parser subcommands are not fully enumerated in the cell. File: scripts/seam_check.py.

title: Phase 2: /flow evolve epic high-altitude producer
description: New evolve sub-verb: web-reaching lenses, conviction-gate brake (engage-if-grounded, not default-refute), bounded spike, decomposition gearing (epic parent + proposal children, reuse evolve_select :140 epic filter), epic:<slug> dedup, weekly ops runner. Depends on Phase 1 doc landing.

title: Phase 1: loop-engineering.md nested-loop map + vendored ops/ templates
description: New references/loop-engineering.md (frame + 3 nested loops + scheduled outer loop + producer-altitude axis + canon map), self-evolution.md cross-link, ops/ sanitized templates (nightly-evolve.sh + plist + README), version bump. Watch seam_check 3+ stage-doc limit. Ships alone.

title: loop-engineering: nested-loop map + high-altitude epic producer
description: Map flow's real nested-loop architecture (scheduler->evolve->do-loop) and engineer a new high-altitude producer /flow evolve epic (web-grounded, conviction-gated, spike-capable, weekly). Plan: ~/.claude/plans/quirky-honking-balloon.md
close_reason: Both phases shipped: map #162 + epic producer #169

title: launch_ledger.py prune crashes: AttributeError 'Namespace' has no attribute 'json'
description: Repro: `python3 scripts/launch_ledger.py prune --workspace-root .` -> AttributeError at launch_ledger.py:142. The prune subparser (launch_ledger.py:121-122) never defines --json, but cli_main:142 reads args.json for both prune and list. The 'list' subparser defines --json (line 126); prune does not. Fix: add `p_prune.add_argument('--json', action='store_true')` to mirror list (line 126), OR drop the args.json branch on the prune path. Blast radius: tiny, single file, behavior-preserving (prune logic untouched). Impact: the evolve drain step-A hygiene line (verb-evolve.md:97 'launch_ledger.py prune') always crashes; harmless because live_keys() self-expires markers by TTL on read, but the hygiene prune never runs so stale marker files accumulate.

title: add test for evolve_self_merge._bead_labels error branches (auto-merge keystone)
description: evolve_self_merge._bead_labels() (evolve_self_merge.py:86-102) has three error branches: returncode != 0 (:93), json.JSONDecodeError (:97), empty-list 'data[0] if data else {}' (:100). grep _bead_labels over scripts/tests/ returns zero hits; the CLI tests (test_evolve_self_merge.py:69-97) drive only the happy returncode-0 bd-show path. decide() itself is fully covered. This is the hot-auto-merge keystone reading bead labels; an error returns [] which fails SAFE ([] -> decide() sees no 'evolve' label -> action:skip -> PR left for human, never an erroneous auto-merge). Low severity (fail-safe) but the keystone's error paths deserve a 3-line test. Fix: add a test exercising the 3 error branches return []. Blast radius: test-only, bounded.

title: metric.py friction-per-run subcommand is undocumented in verb-recall.md (and MODULE.md)
description: metric.py defines THREE subcommands: tickets-per-week (:508), time-to-pr (:517), friction-per-run (:523). references/verb-recall.md:21-40 documents only tickets-per-week and time-to-pr; friction-per-run is absent. MODULE.md:82 also describes metric.py narrowly ('Throughput calculator (shipped tickets/week...)'). Reachable today via recall.py --metric generic forwarder (recall.py:232-235 forwards any subcommand to metric.cli_main), so this is missing-documentation, not a contradiction. Fix: document friction-per-run in verb-recall.md (primary locus) and widen the MODULE.md:82 description. Blast radius: 2 doc loci, primary references/verb-recall.md.

title: MODULE.md triage.py surface row under-enumerates its argparse subcommands
description: scripts/MODULE.md:93 lists triage.py's subcommands as only 'list' / 'decided', but triage.py has FOUR: add_parser('list') (:362), 'decided' (:366), 'adjudicate-enabled' (:371), 'adjudicate-hot-enabled' (:377). The two missing ones are live (called by references/verb-spec.md:142,185 and references/verb-triage.md:86), so the surface table just lags. seam_check passes because it validates prose->CLI invocations against argparse, not the MODULE.md surface table against argparse. Fix: add the two missing subcommands to the MODULE.md:93 surface cell. Blast radius: one MODULE.md row.

title: background-pipeline.md describes the stale workspace.toml memory-redirect mechanism
description: references/background-pipeline.md:42 says 'The bootstrap writes [memory].root into the worktree workspace.toml' and :44 says 'The modified workspace.toml stays unstaged'. That mechanism changed (flow-sai/PR#65): the redirect now lives in the gitignored .flow/memory-root sibling and the tracked workspace.toml stays byte-identical. Contradicting code: flow_worktree.py:143-145,164 (writes wt_flow/'memory-root', comment 'NOT in workspace.toml: the tracked workspace.toml stays byte-identical') and _memory_paths.py:42-44 (resolve_memory_base reads .flow/memory-root sibling first). MODULE.md:23,61 already describe the current sibling mechanism correctly; background-pipeline.md is the only doc still on the old story. Fix: rewrite the 2 stale lines to the sibling-file mechanism. Blast radius: 2 lines, one doc.

title: remove dead _now_iso() + its sole-use datetime import in sync.py
description: sync.py:28-29 defines _now_iso(); it has zero callers. grep across scripts/ shows the only other _now_iso matches are SEPARATE per-file definitions (tracker_cli.py:48, dispatch_stage.py:53, status.py:21, recover.py:25), each used locally; no 'from sync import _now_iso' anywhere, no test/hook ref. The 'from datetime import UTC, datetime' at sync.py:20 is used ONLY by _now_iso (sync.py:29 is the file's only other datetime/UTC use), so it dies with the function. Provably-safe deletion: remove sync.py:28-29 and sync.py:20. Blast radius: zero, purely local.

title: snapshot verify path crashes (instead of fail-closed drift abort) on the reinstall race it guards
description: snapshot.py compute_snapshot/classify_drift (the drift-verify path) do not catch OSError/FileNotFoundError. _tree_hash rglob-enumerates plugin files then reads each (snapshot.py:98 read_bytes); a plugin reinstall (rm+rewrite, the exact threat named in the module docstring) deleting a tracked *.py/*.md/*.toml between enumerate and read raises FileNotFoundError. Same for transiently-absent workspace.toml/stage-registry.toml at snapshot.py:173-174 via _read_text (:67). Only narrow excepts exist: tomllib.TOMLDecodeError (:112) and json.JSONDecodeError (:290). Propagation is unguarded: compute_snapshot -> classify_drift (:282) -> dispatch_stage._gate_drift (:264) -> cmd_next (:326) -> cli_main (:588). Asymmetry is the tell: the INIT write path IS wrapped (dispatch_stage.py:194-210 try/except Exception) but the VERIFY path is bare. Scope: this is a DIAGNOSABILITY fix, NOT a safety bypass. The drift gate runs before any state mutation, so the fail-closed exit-code property already holds (stage stays pending on crash); the defect is that it dies with an opaque traceback instead of returning the structured drift-abort payload that routes the agent to /flow recover. TIGHTENING: catch OSError in compute_snapshot/classify_drift, treat a vanished tracked file as drift -> clean fail-closed abort. Strictly adds protection; never weakens a guard. HOT: touches snapshot.py (safety-machinery guard file), must ride the hot path for the guard-property review. Blast radius: every cmd_next (every stage advance, incl. backgrounded --auto runs).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Right fix: wrap the compute_snapshot recompute in classify_drift with except OSError, returning (False, detail, []) — empty comps routes through _gate_drift to the existing _DRIFT_ABORT exit-1, so a vanished tracked file fails closed cleanly instead of crashing. Scope fork resolved OUT: do NOT guard the snapshot.sha read (:281); the sha lives in the run dir, not plugin_root, so the reinstall rm+rewrite threat does not touch it, and its presence/absence is the load-bearing fail-open/closed switch (separate ticket if ever wanted). Safe to auto-ship: happy path byte-identical, only the crash case changes (crash->structured abort), gate runs before any state mutation, trivially reversible, except OSError only (not Exception). HOT but adjudicate_hot=true: the merge-time guard-property review stays the real gate.

title: evolve drain: auto-stop idle background sessions whose bead has closed (agents-panel hygiene)
description:
FRICTION (manual cleanup). A 'claude --bg /flow <key> --auto' run does not exit when its work finishes — after PR-merge + the reflect stage the session goes IDLE but lingers in 'claude agents' / the agents panel, so a multi-bead drain leaves a pile of stray idle sessions the maintainer kills by hand (claude stop <id>).

Ask: the drain loop should stop them itself. Each turn (e.g. after step A reap), enumerate sessions via 'claude agents --json' (fields: sessionId, cwd, status) and 'claude stop <sessionId>' any session that is BOTH (a) status idle AND (b) maps to a bead now in a terminal state (closed/blocked/deferred).

Guards (load-bearing, do NOT erode):
- NEVER stop a 'busy' session — busy == mid-reflect (reflect runs AFTER PR-merge: memory extraction + machinery self-repair). Killing it loses the self-evolution learning. This is the same reason reap is lease-gated.
- Only stop sessions whose cwd is under THIS repo's worktrees (<repo>.worktrees/feature-*) or the maintainer checkout — never another project's sessions.
- NEVER stop the orchestrator's own session (the one running the drain).
- Map session->bead by cwd basename (feature-<key>-*) or branch.

Likely files: references/verb-evolve.md (reap/loop prose adds the cleanup pass) + maybe a small scripts/ helper to list+filter stoppable sessions. Non-hot (no guard file). Verified live 2026-06-08: stale idle sessions for flow-bud/jud/bzz/hso sat in the panel after their PRs merged until stopped by hand.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
MECHANISM (verified live 2026-06-08). A 'claude --bg' session is a 'job' under ~/.claude/jobs/<sessionId>/ (state.json: state, tempo, intent, name, cwd, sessionId). The agents panel lists these dirs; a stopped session lingers as a dot tombstone. 'claude stop <id>' kills the PROCESS + drops it from 'claude agents --json' (the live list), but LEAVES the job dir -> dot persists. The panel's Ctrl+X removes the job dir (confirmed: a Ctrl+X'd session's ~/.claude/jobs/<id>/ was gone). The transcript lives separately at ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<id>.jsonl and is NOT touched, so the session stays resumable (claude attach / new session) after either stop or dir-removal.

Full vanish (what the automation should do per done bead):
1. claude stop <id>   # kill process if still live
2. rm -rf ~/.claude/jobs/<id>/   # Ctrl+X-equivalent: drop the panel tombstone

Guards (load-bearing):
- Only when state.json state in {done, stopped} AND tempo == idle (NEVER a busy/working job: tempo/status can LAG -- saw 'idle' in claude agents --json while claude logs showed the session actively 'Wibbling', i.e. mid-reflect). Prefer the most live signal.
- Verify <id> is NOT in 'claude agents --json' live list before rm.
- Only jobs whose cwd is under this repo / its worktrees. NEVER another project's job, NEVER the orchestrator's own job ($CLAUDE_JOB_DIR basename).
- Map job->bead via state.json intent ('/flow <key> --auto') or cwd basename (feature-<key>-*); only remove when that bead is terminal (closed/blocked/deferred).
- Transcript is preserved by design (resumable); document that so a user isn't surprised the dir-removal is non-destructive.

Note: rm bypasses the daemon (Ctrl+X likely routes through it); safe for done/stopped jobs (proven), but the cleaner long-term path is a daemon-sanctioned dismiss if one exists in a future claude CLI (re-check 'claude agents'/'claude' subcommands for a prune/forget verb; none in 2.1.168).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Build a filesystem-only cleanup pass for the evolve drain loop. Enumerate ~/.claude/jobs/*/state.json directly (NEVER claude agents --json — it blocks on a TTY headless, and the drain itself can run headless). New pure read-only classify script (evolve_session_cleanup.py) mirrors evolve_reap's pure-core + prose-side-effects split; the destructive claude stop + rm -rf job-dir live in reviewable verb-evolve.md prose. Liveness gate is layered + conservative (ANY signal busy -> skip): the LEASE is the primary guard (skip on live/corrupt — same mechanism reap already uses to skip mid-reflect sessions, so the catastrophic kill-mid-reflect failure is inherited-guarded, not a new tunable), with transcript-mtime + state floor as belt-and-suspenders. Self-job + cross-project + terminal-bead gates fail safe toward NOT stopping. Safe to auto-ship: narrow blast radius (one call site, not a widely-imported helper), single-PR reversible, rm -rf touches only the job dir (transcript preserved, session resumable). claude stop TTY-unknown is structurally bounded by timeout 10 claude stop <id> </dev/null. mtime threshold default 5min (lease floor bounds it). is_hot=false (no guard file). Live-verify claude stop non-blocking + mtime cadence at implement.

title: add [evolve] adjudicate_hot flag: maintainer-only knob to lift the hot hard-floor (advisor proceed + merge-review gate hot changes instead)
description:
MAINTAINER AUTONOMY (self-target only; default OFF so user projects keep the floor).

Motivation: in the maintainer self-target repo the human reviews every PR and always agrees with the advisor's rulings, so the pre-bootstrap hot hard-floor (flow-aen) only re-asks a question always answered yes -> pure friction. Stakes are low (no data/prod; worst case a workflow skips a phase = recoverable inconvenience), so this repo wants the floor down.

Design (floor-only): a new bool flag [evolve] adjudicate_hot, default False (mirrors advisor_adjudicates reader but inverted default; user projects unaffected). When True:
- flow_worktree._enforce_hot_floor returns early (skips the bootstrap refusal).
- verb-spec.md step 5.3 hot hard-floor: a hot change's advisor 'proceed' is NOT downgraded to block.
Unchanged (retained safety nets): advisor adjudication still rules (proceed/block/defer); the <70% confidence floor + residual-wall blocks stay; the merge-time guard-property review (evolve_self_merge.decide, keyed on the hot label) + CI still gate every hot auto-merge. So adjudicate_hot removes ONLY the blanket 'hot can never self-proceed' rule, not the system's specific judgments (a narrow advisor block like flow-hso still parks).

Files: scripts/triage.py (adjudicate_hot reader + adjudicate-hot-enabled CLI subcommand + __all__ + _default_to_list token), scripts/flow_worktree.py (_enforce_hot_floor gate), references/verb-spec.md (step 5.3 + step 6 floor note), references/verb-triage.md (doc), .flow/workspace.toml (adjudicate_hot = true here), scripts/tests/test_triage.py + test_flow_worktree.py, plugin.json + marketplace.json version bump.

title: evolve_select misses the launch->init window: a just-launched --auto run (no lease/branch yet) is re-emitted in launch[] and can break one-hot-per-pass isolation
description:
ROBUSTNESS / HOT (one-hot isolation safety invariant; CLAUDE.md calls it load-bearing for maintainer auto-merge).

Evidence (live, 2026-06-08 drain): launched flow-hso (cfaae0ae), flow-bud (d79b8a3f), flow-jud (95078c44, hot) via 'claude --bg /flow <k> --auto' at ~17:47. ~5min later evolve_drain.py returned launch=['flow-4lb','flow-bzz','flow-hso'], held_hot=['flow-jud','flow-yga'], live_runs=['flow-bud']. Two faults in that window: (1) flow-hso re-emitted in launch[] though already running; (2) flow-4lb offered as the single hot slot while flow-jud (hot) was already launched -> two concurrent hot beads launchable, violating one-hot-per-pass.

Root cause: evolve_select in-flight detection has two channels, BOTH post-init. _gather_refs (evolve_select.py:189) joins on gh-PR + git branch refs (exist only after create_pr / first push). _live_run_keys (evolve_select.py:212-227) seeds extra_keys from a LIVE pre-PR lease, but that lease is written by dispatch_stage.py init, which runs only after plan+bootstrap+EnterWorktree. A run still in plan/bootstrap (no worktree, no .flow/runs/<key>/, no lease) is invisible to both -> the launch->init window (minutes for a complex plan) is uncovered. _hot_inflight (evolve_select.py:230-262) inherits the same blind spot, so the hot slot is mis-free during it.

Mitigation today: verb-evolve.md step C prose ('briefly wait until keys register a branch/PR'). Fragile: undefined duration, depends on operator/agent discipline, and a slow plan can exceed any short cap. Lease backstops double-EXECUTION (second init refuses), but NOT the two-hot-launch isolation break (lease is per-ticket).

Fix candidate: a launch ledger. Drain step C writes a per-key pending-launch marker (with a TTL to self-expire a dead launch) at 'claude --bg' time; select() seeds inflight_keys AND _hot_inflight extra_keys from it; the marker is superseded once the real lease/branch registers. Closes the pre-init window for both re-launch suppression and hot serialization without relying on a manual wait.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: hot change (one-hot isolation safety invariant) with no recorded DECISION -> code-enforced hot floor would refuse at bootstrap. Parked at file time; this is future-work, not part of the current drain. To unstick: record a TRIAGE-DECISION here, reopen (status->open), and run WITHOUT --auto.

title: parallel evolve drain: every PR bumps plugin.json/marketplace.json at plan time, so only the first merges clean and all siblings conflict on the version line
description: Evidence: 2026-06-08 drain launched 8 --auto runs; bootstrap stamps the version bump into plugin.json + marketplace.json at PLAN time (base+1). As siblings merged (#140,#142,#144,#146), main's version advanced, and the remaining PRs (#143 flow-doo, #145 flow-nr4, #141 flow-sxi) flipped CLEAN->DIRTY - merge-tree confirms the ONLY conflict is the version-bump lines in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json + plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json; all code changes merge clean. Their runs hit the conflict at the merge stage, could not auto-merge, and exited leaving conflicted PRs + in_progress beads. This is structural: any parallel drain of >1 non-hot bead self-serializes on the version line, so N-1 of N PRs always conflict and need manual rebase+re-bump. FIX-SHAPE: compute the version bump at MERGE time (not plan time), or have the merge stage auto-rebase + re-bump the version files when the only conflict is the version lines (deterministic: take base version, bump +1). Blast radius: every multi-bead drain; today cost 3 manual conflict resolutions.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: advisor adjudication ruled DIRECTION SETTLED but BLOCKED auto-ship (held for human merge/review of unattended auto-merge surgery).

DIRECTION (settled, Option B — additive merge-time recovery, NOT A1): keep the implement-time version bump as-is (interactive + user-project runs rely on it being IN the PR for the human to merge; they never hit the self-merge stage). Add a merge-time recovery path at BOTH merge sites (in-run references/stage-merge.md §3, and the reap loop in references/verb-evolve.md + scripts/evolve_reap.py). When a green PR is blocked DIRTY and the ONLY conflicting files are the two version files (plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json): git fetch; merge origin/<default> into the feature branch; auto-resolve the version files to (main's current version + 1, deterministic); ABORT and leave-for-human on ANY non-version-file conflict; commit; push; squash-merge. The reap loop merges multiple PRs per pass serially, so each PR must re-fetch main AFTER the prior merge (the bump walks forward X+1->X+2->...).

ROOT CAUSE (verified): the version-line conflict is irreducible whenever a branch sets an absolute version value AND main independently advanced that line past the branch's merge-base. flow-8we's byte-identical-bump only holds when all siblings share one base AND none merges before the others; under staggered drain timing main's version walks forward and later-merging PRs go DIRTY on the version line. evolve_reap.classify buckets them blocked (mergeStateStatus not in {CLEAN,DRAFT}) and stage-merge §3 has no recovery -> stranded conflicted PRs + in_progress beads.

WHY BLOCKED (not safe to auto-ship, confidence ~78%): blast radius = the unattended auto-merge path on every multi-bead drain; the dangerous failure is a buggy 'only version files conflict' detector auto-resolving a REAL code conflict and landing wrong code to main unattended (not cleanly reversible); CI can unit-test the bump math but cannot cover the live merge-main-in -> push -> squash behavior (needs a real concurrent multi-PR drain to verify); and this is flow rewriting its own auto-merge safety machinery THROUGH that same machinery. CLAUDE.md: 'simplify presentation, never the safety machinery.'

OPEN DESIGN QUESTIONS for the human: (1) shared helper script (e.g. version_remerge.py) invoked by both merge sites vs duplicated prose; (2) the stage-merge §3 'merge ONLY the CI-validated SHA' invariant — the re-bump pushes a commit CI never saw; carve-out for the provably-non-behavioral version-number-only delta, or re-wait for CI?; (3) serial re-fetch ordering across the reap pass merge loop.

To unstick: answer the open questions here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan + implement interactively, then merge by hand (do not auto-merge this one).

title: Measurable hook for worker-model tiering: per-run token metric (recall --metric tokens-per-run)
description:
FOLLOW-UP to flow-ch6 (worker-model tiering, merged PR #146). The VISION 'Move fast where breakage is cheap' posture names TWO honesty gates: visible (shipped in flow-ch6 via the persistent tier:trivial label) and MEASURABLE. The measurable half was deliberately deferred at spec time (maintainer call: the saving is self-evidently substantial, and there is zero per-run token data captured anywhere today).

GOAL: prove the tiering saving under recall --metric, so the win is measured not modeled.

WHY SUBSTANTIAL (the reason it was deferred): nothing captures per-run tokens today. Confirmed during flow-ch6 audit: no tokens/usage field in state.py StageRecord, ship-event evidence, or anywhere in scripts/. So this is NEW plumbing, not a metric.py-only add:
1. CAPTURE: instrument the run to record per-run token usage (input+output). Source TBD — session/API usage, or a dispatcher-level capture. This is the hard part and the design fork (how to get token counts at all).
2. STORE: a tokens field on state.py StageRecord (or a top-level state.json field / sidecar), written as the run progresses.
3. AGGREGATE: a metric.py 'tokens-per-run' subcommand (reads ship-events + state), exposed via the existing recall.py --metric passthrough (no recall.py edit needed — it is a generic forwarder; just add the subparser + an if-branch in metric.cli_main, getattr-gating any new arg).

ALTERNATIVE (lighter, worth weighing at spec): an OUTCOME metric instead of true tokens — count tier:trivial runs (bd list --status closed --label tier:trivial) and their clean-ship-vs-revert rate, with a MODELED token estimate (run count x opus/sonnet delta). Measures whether the bet is safe + a back-of-envelope saving, with zero capture plumbing. Decide depth at spec.

LIKELY FILES: scripts/state.py (token field), scripts/metric.py (subcommand + compute fn), the capture site (dispatcher or session instrumentation, TBD), tests/test_metric.py.
BLAST RADIUS: state.json schema + metric. No guard/safety file. Non-hot.
RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (the capture-source fork) — lean to the lighter outcome-metric alternative unless true token capture is cheap to source.
close_reason: wontfix: tiering win self-evident, true token capture not cheap, Option-B outcome metric underdetermined (no revert denominator + modeled constant); low value

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: deferring — the metric's output shape is underdetermined and its headline number is not advisor-supplyable. Details:

(1) SPEC-HOLE DISCOVERY (the important one): the named metric 'clean-ship-vs-revert rate' is NOT computable from 'bd list --status closed --label tier:trivial'. On revert the tier:trivial label is REMOVED (verb-evolve.md:195 un-stamps the pattern), so that query returns SURVIVORS ONLY — the reverted side is gone and no total-downshifted count is stored anywhere. There is no denominator for a safety RATE. Options: (a) report only surviving clean trivial-run count x modeled delta and scope reverts explicitly OUT, or (b) source revert count from git revert commits against feature/<key>-* (new plumbing) to recover the safety-rate half.

(2) The saving headline is 'surviving_count x per_run_token_delta', but the per-run opus/sonnet token delta is a MODELED constant the ticket itself says is captured nowhere (zero token data today). Auto-shipping would mean fabricating that number. What delta should the metric use (a maintainer-supplied constant / --per-run-token-delta default, or a cited source)?

(3) Naming: the title says subcommand 'tokens-per-run', but Option B reports an outcome count x a modeled estimate, not measured tokens. Keep literal 'tokens-per-run' (documenting the divergence) or use an honest name like 'tier-savings'?

CAPTURE-FORK NOTE: true per-run token capture (the title's premise) is confirmed NOT cheap to source on origin/main — no usage/tokens field in scripts/, dispatch_stage.py has no sessionId access, state.json carries no transcript linkage.
