So the maintainer's 'unless true token capture is cheap' escape clause fails; the lighter outcome metric (Option B) is the right path, pending the shape decisions above.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-19: WONTFIX. Measuring the worker-model tiering saving isn't worth the plumbing — the win is self-evidently large (the original spec deferred the measurable half for exactly this reason). True per-run token capture is confirmed NOT cheap on origin/main (no usage field in scripts/, dispatch has no sessionId), and the lighter Option-B outcome metric has an unresolved spec-hole (no revert denominator — tier:trivial is un-stamped on revert per verb-evolve.md:195) plus a modeled-constant headline captured nowhere. Closing rather than carrying a low-value underdetermined metric. Reopen if per-run token data ever lands for another reason (then it's a cheap metric.py add).

title: is_hot_change hot floor absent from the clean >=90% self-approve path (exists only in adjudication/decided sub-branches)
description: Evidence: in the first default-on adjudication drain (2026-06-08), flow-6mx (hot; planned_files include scripts/machinery_edit.py; triage.is_hot_change([...machinery_edit.py])==True) PROCEEDED through the --auto adjudication branch and built PR #144 (CLEAN) instead of blocking. verb-spec.md:184 mandates 'A hot change can NEVER proceed - downgrade any proceed on a hot change to block', but the floor is enforced only by the headless --auto agent reading the prose, so a non-compliant run leaks it. Same exposure for the broad-blast block (verb-spec.md:193) and the <70% confidence floor - all prose-gated. The hot floor is the load-bearing safety net of flow-rbr; prose-only enforcement is unreliable. FIX-SHAPE: code-enforce the floor - e.g. bootstrap/dispatch (flow_worktree.py create or dispatch_stage.py init) refuses to advance an --auto run whose planned_files trip is_hot_change unless a recorded DECISION:/TRIAGE-DECISION: is on file, so a hot bead cannot reach implement without a human decision regardless of agent compliance. Blast radius: --auto adjudication path only; PR #144 was caught and held as draft for human merge (diff was a benign guard strengthening this time, but next time could weaken). Value: closes the gap between the designed hard-floor and its actual enforcement.
close_reason: merged via PR #147 (code-enforced hot floor at bootstrap)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Update: PR #144 auto-merged to main at 2026-06-08T16:32:09Z despite the drain operator converting it to draft as a hold. The owning run's in-run merge stage re-readied the draft and squash-merged it (layer-2 guard-property review passed it as a benign guard strengthening, which was correct). Two distinct gaps: (1) spec hot hard-floor leaked (this bead's root finding); (2) operator draft-as-veto does NOT stop a live run's own merge stage - it only skips the drain reap step - so a hot PR re-readied and merged through an explicit human hold. Net landing was safe (CI-green guard strengthening), but the veto override is its own concern.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CORRECTION (root cause was misattributed above). flow-6mx has NO DECISION:(advisor) comment, which by design means it did NOT take the adjudication branch (a proceed there always writes one). It took the **clean >=90% self-approve path**: yesterday's plan was 86% (deferred); re-planned today it cleared 90% with a clean (NONE) plan, hit 'NONE AND assessor >=90% -> auto-approve ... No adjudication needed', and went straight to bootstrap. That path has NO is_hot_change check. The hot hard-floor (verb-spec.md:184) lives ONLY in the adjudication-fork and decided sub-branches, NOT the clean >=90% path. So this is NOT 'agent non-compliance at line 184' (that branch was never on the run's path) - it is STRUCTURAL: the clean self-approve path is missing the hot gate, and it PRE-DATES flow-rbr (flow-rbr added the floor only to its new branch). verb-spec.md:143 falsely claims 'the hot hard-floor ... [is] in both branches' - true for ADJ-on vs opt-out, but a clean >=90% plan routes to the same hot-check-free path either way. The code-enforcement fix-shape (bootstrap/dispatch refuses to advance an --auto run whose planned_files trip is_hot_change without a recorded decision) is the RIGHT fix precisely because it guards ALL self-approve paths uniformly, not just the prose branch. Primary file should be verb-spec.md (clean self-approve branch) + a code gate in flow_worktree.py create / dispatch_stage.py init.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Deferred by drain operator to freeze HOT autonomous draining until flow-aen (hot is_hot_change floor absent from the clean >=90% self-approve path) is fixed by a human. With the hot floor structurally broken, a hot bead that re-plans clean at >=90% self-ships unreviewed (proven by flow-6mx/#144 this drain). Reopen (bd update <key> --status open) once the floor is code-enforced. Non-hot evolve beads remain drainable.

title: inventory.md required-fields table omits the e2e stage required_fields = [e2e_recipe]
description: DOC OMISSION (found during flow-7m8). inventory.md's 'Required fields per stage' list (around lines 566-568) documents only universal, implement, and commit, but omits the e2e stage's required_fields = ["e2e_recipe"], which IS present in stage-registry.toml:105. Distinct from flow-7m8's wrong-name drift (commit_message): this is a missing-entry gap, not a wrong value. Behavior-preserving doc fix: add a bullet '- e2e.required_fields = ["e2e_recipe"]' to the list. Deliberately left out of flow-7m8 scope to avoid over-reach.

title: implement stage: make plugin version-bump mandatory when planned_files holds plugin.json
description: MACHINERY (minor, flow-sxi). stage-implement.md ~L45 gates the plugin version-bump on 'if the plan calls for a version bump', but the CLAUDE.md invariant makes the plugin.json+marketplace.json bump MANDATORY for ANY plugins/flow/ change. flow-sxi's --auto plan listed both version files in planned_files (bootstrap auto-added them) yet did not explicitly instruct a bump, so the implement subagent skipped it; the orchestrator caught it manually at commit (0.27.31->0.27.32). Gap: nothing at implement time keys the bump off plugin.json being in planned_files. Fix candidate: stage-implement.md step 2 should make the bump unconditional when planned_files contains plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (always patch, sync marketplace.json). Evidence: references/stage-implement.md:45.

title: record-baseline drops bootstrap-auto-added planned_files; diff capture should source from frontmatter
description: MACHINERY (major): diff_extract.record_baseline (scripts/diff_extract.py:247-270) stores planned_files purely from the orchestrator-passed --files and never reads .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md frontmatter. Both capture-implement-diff (diff_extract.py:292) and check-ownership (diff_extract.py:349) then key off baseline.json's planned_files. The spec bootstrap auto-adds the version-bump files (plugin.json + marketplace.json) to FRONTMATTER planned_files only (memory bootstrap-auto-includes-version-bump-files). So when the do-loop pre-handler (SKILL.md step 4c) sources PLANNED_FILES from the plan's real-files list instead of frontmatter, the auto-added version bumps are silently EXCLUDED from the captured implement.diff and the commit drops them. check-ownership cannot catch it (it is also baseline-keyed, not frontmatter-keyed), so a broken plugin publish (version files not bumped in the commit) slips through unless the orchestrator manually notices. Observed on flow-7m8: had to re-record the baseline with all 3 frontmatter planned_files before commit. Fix options: (a) record_baseline reads frontmatter planned_files (it has ticket-dir, can resolve the ticket key) and unions/replaces the passed --files; or (b) add a consistency guard that warns/fails when baseline.planned_files != frontmatter planned_files at capture/commit time. diff_extract.py is a safety-machinery file (hot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Deferred by drain operator to freeze HOT autonomous draining until flow-aen (hot is_hot_change floor absent from the clean >=90% self-approve path) is fixed by a human. With the hot floor structurally broken, a hot bead that re-plans clean at >=90% self-ships unreviewed (proven by flow-6mx/#144 this drain). Reopen (bd update <key> --status open) once the floor is code-enforced. Non-hot evolve beads remain drainable.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Reopened: the hot is_hot_change floor is now code-enforced at bootstrap (flow-aen / PR #147, live on main @ a2373f6, plugin 0.27.37). This hot bead can re-enter the queue safely now — an --auto drain will hit the floor and BLOCK it (defer-stem + status blocked, surfacing in /flow triage) instead of self-shipping, so it waits for a human decision rather than auto-merging unreviewed.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: hot change (hot label) with no recorded DECISION — the code-enforced hot floor (flow_worktree.bootstrap) would refuse this at bootstrap. Parked for maintainer triage instead of burning a drain launch to re-derive the same block. To unstick: record a decision here (TRIAGE-DECISION: ...), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: Approved (maintainer, explicit unblock). Ship the fix as described in this bead: record-baseline must source the diff-capture file set from ticket frontmatter planned_files (including bootstrap-auto-added version-bump entries), not a stale/narrower set. Hot guard file (diff_extract.py) -> the in-run guard-property review at merge + CI still gate the landing.

title: Worker-model tiering: run producer-stamped-trivial beads at a cheaper model
description:
FEATURE (judgment-side proposal). Authorized by the new VISION.md section 'Move fast where breakage is cheap' (merged PR #139): the loop may run a cheaper worker tier on producer-vetted-trivial, non-hot work without re-asking per instance, gated on the run being VISIBLE + the win MEASURABLE.

GOAL: stop spending the strong model (opus) on trivial runs (e.g. one-line doc-drift fixes). Whole-run downshift moves the DOMINANT cost (main orchestrating session + all inline stages), not just the implement subagent.

DESIGN (converged + adversarially vetted this session):
1. STAMP (producer side, ~free): /flow evolve audit|propose already classifies each finding (an opus agent that read the code). When it judges a finding mechanical + bounded + non-hot, it adds a label 'tier:trivial' on the flow_beads_create.py call. Absent => default (strong model). No separate judge call — the producer IS the judge, inline with work it already does.
2. THREAD (select -> drain): evolve_select.py pulls 'bd ready -l evolve --json' which already carries labels; thread the tier into the launch set. evolve_drain.py JSON: each launch entry gains a 'model' field (today launch is bare keys -> becomes objects; the one schema ripple).
3. LAUNCH (verb-evolve.md drain step C, ~line 136): conditional one-token change — 'claude --bg [--model sonnet] "/flow <key> --auto"' when tier:trivial.
4. VISIBLE hook (honesty condition #1 from the posture): record which runs were downshifted (a field in the ship-event via observe_ship_event.py and/or reflect_inputs bundle), so a bad PR is instantly correlated to its tier, reverted, and the pattern un-stamped. Move-fast works only when breakage is seen.
5. MEASURABLE hook (honesty condition #2): surface tokens-per-run (extend metric.py, expose via recall --metric) so the saving is provable, not vibes.

LIKELY FILES TO CHANGE: flow_beads_create.py (or the producer prose in references/verb-evolve.md §audit/§propose) for the stamp; evolve_select.py + evolve_drain.py for the thread; references/verb-evolve.md for the launch flag; observe_ship_event.py + reflect_inputs.py for the visible hook; metric.py (+ recall.py passthrough) for the measurable hook. Spec stage should confirm the exact set.

DO NOT RE-EXPLORE (already refuted this session):
- Launch-time heuristic on planned_files: FATAL — planned_files is stamped post-launch (evolve_select.py:8-12), unavailable when the model is chosen. The stamp/label approach above dodges this (label is on the bead pre-launch).
- Per-STAGE downshift of only the implement subagent: value-inversion — fires only on trivial runs where implement is the cheapest component, every gate stays opus, saving ~rounding error. Whole-run downshift is the point.
- Safety concern (non-hot leaf self-merges on worker judgment, no independent reviewer per stage-merge.md §2 hot-only, no human keystone on drain auto-merge): a real property change, now ACCEPTED under the merged move-fast posture for the reversible non-hot product surface. The trivial set is non-hot by construction so it never touches the safety machinery (guards are hot). No precondition-B opus-reviewer-restore needed (it would claw back the savings).

BLAST RADIUS: drain launch path + select + producer stamp + two observability hooks. Touches no guard/safety file. Non-hot.
CONFIDENCE: ~0.8 the design ships clean; the open risk is purely the false-downshift rate (a terse bead understating a subtle change), accepted as cheap+reversible per the posture.
RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (maintainer decided in the conversation that produced PR #139).

title: create_pr.py has no internal retry on a transient forge list error (gh GraphQL 5xx)
description: create_pr.py's gh pr list (forge detect-pr) failed once with a transient GitHub GraphQL 5xx ('Something went wrong while executing your query'), aborting the stage; a manual re-run succeeded immediately. A bounded retry (2-3x with backoff) on transient forge list/detect errors would let an unattended --auto run survive a GitHub hiccup instead of failing create_pr. Owning file: scripts/create_pr.py (or forge_cli.py detect-pr).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Add bounded retry to forge_github.GitHubAdapter via a new _ok_read helper used ONLY by the idempotent detect_pr read; retry on ANY non-zero gh return (no brittle transient-string allowlist — a permanent error fails identically on retry at bounded backoff cost, and this survives novel 5xx phrasings), 3 attempts / 2 retries, injected sleep. Mutating paths (open_pr/merge/mark_ready/delete_branch) keep _ok unchanged — never blind-retry a create/merge. Safe to auto-ship: is_hot=false, blast radius contained (detect_pr has 2 read call sites), clean revert, new unit tests + CI cover it; authorizes draft PR only, human merge keystone holds.

title: seam_check truncates invocation at ; / | / && BEFORE stripping quoted spans -> false unknown-flag on a quoted value containing a sequencing char
description: find_invocations (seam_check.py ~L131-140) truncates args at the first of ('&&','||','|',';') and only THEN runs _VALUE_SPAN_RE.sub to strip quoted values. A `--text "...; ..."` value (a ; inside the quoted string) is cut mid-span, unbalancing the quote, so _VALUE_SPAN_RE can no longer match it and inner --flags leak as false ERRORs. Hit live on verb-spec.md: a block-comment --text containing 'Judgment settled; this is a safety hold' reported 'tracker_cli.py comment: unknown flag --auto'. Fix: strip value-spans before truncating at sequencing operators, or only split on operators outside quoted spans. Owning file: scripts/seam_check.py.

title: init's best-effort snapshot write can silently disable the TOCTOU drift guard for a whole run
description: ROBUSTNESS / HOT (touches scripts/dispatch_stage.py, a safety-machinery guard file -> must ride the hot path for guard-property review). dispatch_stage.py:190-192 writes the canonical snapshot under contextlib.suppress(Exception) and init still returns exit 0 + reports success. If that write throws (disk full, perms, partial FS), no snapshot.sha is left. Every later cmd_next calls classify_drift, which fails OPEN on a missing sha (snapshot.py:277-279: 'if not sha_path.exists(): return True, "no snapshot to verify", []'). So the workspace.toml/stage_registry/handler-tree drift guard the snapshot.py docstring promises is silently absent for the run's entire life, and a failed-write-during-init is indistinguishable from never-emitted. The defect is the SILENCE, not the best-effort policy (the suppress is deliberate: 'a snapshot write failure must not block the run'). Fix direction: surface the failed write (friction entry / stderr warn / a marker the run can see) so observability is tightened WITHOUT eroding the intentional availability choice; do not turn the suppress into a hard block. Value: restores the load-bearing TOCTOU guarantee. Reviewability: scoped to init error handling + the absent-vs-failed distinction.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: advisor ruled the stderr + JSON-marker approach correct (surface the failed best-effort write in cmd_init, framed by snapshot_sha_path().exists() to distinguish fail-OPEN absent-sha from fail-CLOSED partial-write, init still returns exit 0) but BLOCKED auto-ship — this is a HOT guard change to dispatch_stage.py TOCTOU/snapshot machinery, and the ticket itself flags it HOT and demands it 'ride the hot path for guard-property review'. Judgment is settled, this is a safety hold honoring the ticket's own review request. To unstick: reopen (status->open) and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively, or merge the change by hand.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: Approved (maintainer). Implement the advisor-settled design: in dispatch_stage.cmd_init, surface a failed best-effort canonical-snapshot write via a stderr warning + a JSON marker (e.g. snapshot_write_failed:true), using snapshot_sha_path().exists() to distinguish fail-OPEN (absent sha — the deliberate availability choice, keep it) from fail-CLOSED (partial write). init still returns exit 0; do NOT turn the contextlib.suppress into a hard block. Restores observability of the TOCTOU/drift guard without eroding the best-effort policy. HOT guard change to dispatch_stage.py: run it so the in-run merge stage's guard-property review fires (that review is the keystone the ticket asked for).

title: inventory.md commit stage required_fields stale (commit_message vs commit_type/commit_summary)
description: DOC DRIFT. inventory.md:568 documents `commit.required_fields = ["commit_message"]`, but the live stage-registry.toml:121 declares required_fields = ["commit_type", "commit_summary"]. references/stage-commit.md (lines 18,24,33,61) and tests/test_lint_ticket.py:241-244 both confirm the registry is truth; the `commit_message` field is unused/nonexistent. Drift is isolated: grep of references/, SKILL.md, MODULE.md, inventory.md finds `commit_message` ONLY at inventory.md:568. Value: a wrong required-field name in the API contract table misleads anyone reading the stage spec. Blast radius: one line in one doc, behavior-preserving. Reviewability: trivial.

title: flow_beads_create.py aborts when summary value begins with two dashes
description: bd create receives the summary as a leading positional/token, so a value starting with '--' (seen at flow-58y reflect when a MACHINERY summary led with '--auto') is parsed as an unknown flag and create exits 2. Fix: forward the title via --title=<value> (=-joined, dash-immune) or place a '--' end-of-flags separator before positionals in flow_beads_create.py. Low blast radius, doc/script-local.

title: auto-mode spec self-derive loses drift-vs-@default verification when Plan subagent is skipped
description: verb-spec.md:95 says the drift-vs-@default rule (re-read a cited content/drift finding at the freshly-fetched @default base before stamping planned_files) is enforced inside the Plan subagent (stage-plan.md:42). The --auto path mandates that subagent, but when the orchestrator self-derives planned_files from a content/drift finding via a direct advisor() shortcut (no Plan subagent), the rule has no carrier and a stale-launcher-checkout drift claim can enter the plan. flow-58y: plan stamped a 5th MODULE.md row (tracker_beads) as drifted off a grep of the lagging local main checkout; origin/main (86f34a9) already had it fixed. Caught + dropped at implement (verifies against worktree base); cost one minor RECONCILE. Same family as flow-749. Proposed fix: in the auto-mode self-approve branch of verb-spec.md, state that any orchestrator-derived planned_files entry stamped because of a file's current content must be re-verified at @default base, mirroring stage-plan.md:42, so the rule holds even when the Plan subagent is shortcut.

title: merge stage push-state guard: git rev-parse @{u} fatals when the PR auto-merged and origin deleted the branch, yielding a misleading SKIP
description:
references/stage-merge.md:52 push-state guard runs: [ "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" != "$(git rev-parse @{u} 2>/dev/null)" ]. When the PR has already auto-merged and origin deleted the branch BEFORE the merge stage runs, @{u} fatals (no upstream), the command-substitution yields empty, and 'HEAD != ' trips the SKIP path with the misleading reason 'uncommitted or unpushed changes CI never validated' — masking the real already-merged state.

EVIDENCE: MACHINERY knowledge entry a6fcb333c0257e54 (flow-7nl, 2026-06-07). Confirmed LIVE on origin/main: git show origin/main:plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-merge.md line 52 still reads the bare rev-parse @{u} with no pre-check for an already-MERGED PR. No commit (#66-#133) addresses it; no open/closed bead tracks it (flow-7nl was a DIFFERENT defect: stage-registry commit-stage description naming).

VALUE: outcome is correct (PR already merged, nothing to do) but the SKIP reason is wrong/misleading, masking already-merged state in unattended evolve-drain runs — friction when reading drain logs. FIX: pre-check 'gh pr view --json state' for MERGED to short-circuit cleanly before the rev-parse. BLAST RADIUS: single hot-path reference doc (stage-merge.md prose), low risk, no code change.
close_reason: STALE (workflow-verified 2026-06-12): exact proposed fix shipped as PR#135 (2ca708a, stage-merge.md MERGED short-circuit before rev-parse) and the fataling @{u} removed repo-wide by PR#230 (508df30, guard reads git rev-parse origin/$BRANCH). Adversarially verified vs main@1cd1f68.

title: MODULE.md importer rows misattribute the adapter factory seam (phantom + wrong-importer rows survive PR #128)
description:
MODULE.md 'imported by' / 'imports' rows still drift on origin/main AFTER flow-fx7 (PR #128), which only fixed the OMITTED-importer category. Four rows remain wrong, verified against origin/main:

MISATTRIBUTED (row hides the make_*/factory indirection that is the architectural point of the pluggable seam):
- MODULE.md:33 tracker_jira.py says 'imported by tracker_cli' -> actual: tracker.py:359 (lazy in make_tracker factory); tracker_cli imports it 0 times.
- MODULE.md:45 forge_github.py says 'imported by forge_cli' -> actual: forge.py:175 (lazy in make_forge); forge_cli 0 times.
- MODULE.md:46 forge_bitbucket.py says 'imported by forge_cli' -> actual: forge.py:179; forge_cli 0 times.

PHANTOM (row names imports that do not exist):
- MODULE.md:81 validate_postmortem.py says 'imports snapshot, state' -> actual imports (validate_postmortem.py:42-44): ticket_frontmatter, _memory_paths(ship_events_dir), _timeutil(parse_iso). Imports NEITHER snapshot NOR state.

VALUE: these rows are the live dependency map read before a refactor; the misattributed ones actively misrepresent the factory->adapter indirection (the whole point of both pluggable seams) and send a refactorer chasing imports that don't exist. seam_check covers prose->CLI invocations, not these tables (ungated, per flow-fx7/flow-nr4). BLAST RADIUS: doc-only, single file MODULE.md, no code change.

title: spec-stage drift check reads lagging local checkout, not @default base
description: MACHINERY: spec/plan reads the launcher's local checkout (which lags origin/main per CLAUDE.md), but the --auto tail bases the worktree off @default (origin/main, fetched fresh). So a content-drift finding cited at plan time can already be fixed upstream. flow-749: the spec completeness probe saw stage-registry.toml:113 compose-commit.py as drift and stamped stage-registry.toml into planned_files; the @default worktree already read compose_commit.py (fixed by PR #130), so the planned fix was a no-op (the guard still shipped, planned_files is a ceiling so no harm). FIX: spec-stage content/drift verification should git-fetch and assess against origin/<default> (or @default), not the working checkout, so plan-time drift findings match the base the tail will branch from. Evidence: references/verb-spec.md (steps 3-5 read-only exploration on launcher checkout); CLAUDE.md 'branch off origin/main, never local main (lags)'.

title: seam_check misses script-name drift in stage-registry.toml descriptions
description: MACHINERY: seam_check.py docs_to_check (~353-358) scans only SKILL.md + references/*.md, never stage-registry.toml description fields nor scripts/*.md tables. So a script basename drifting in those (e.g. compose-commit.py vs real compose_commit.py, flow-7nl) is invisible to the seam gate; only a hand-filed ticket catches it. PROPOSAL: extend the seam check (or a sibling checker) to scan stage-registry.toml [[stage]].description prose for *.py basenames and assert each resolves to a real scripts/ file. CONSTRAINT: must NOT scan scripts/inventory.md — its archived build-log table writes every real underscore script with a hyphenated display name uniformly (lint-ticket.py, branch-ticket.py, diff-extract.py, compose-commit.py, memory-append.py, pending-mutations.py); that is a uniform display convention, not drift, and a naive hyphen check false-positives on it. Evidence: stage-registry.toml:113, seam_check.py:353-358, scripts/inventory.md:505-512.

title: MODULE.md 'imported by' tables are ungated and drift
description: MODULE.md library rows enumerate each module's importers, but nothing verifies them: seam_check.py guards CLI invocations in prose, not these narrative importer lists. So every new import edge silently rots a row (flow-fx7 fixed six). Proposal: a cheap check (test or seam_check extension) that, for each 'imported by X, Y, Z' row, greps 'import <mod>' across scripts/*.py and asserts the row matches the true static importer set. Would auto-detect this drift class instead of minting manual beads. Evidence: flow-fx7 found six stale rows AND a seventh (tracker_jira row 33) still stale after; one row (tracker_beads) was wrong, not just incomplete.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: the design is sound (gate MODULE.md 'imported by' rows against an AST-derived true importer set in seam_check.py, ERROR-level, repair all currently-drifted rows) but one decision needs a human ruling. The '_memory_paths.py' row is deliberately 'imported widely' (~7 importers) to avoid high-churn enumeration. The plan would reverse that, enumerating it so the checker can gate it (and would add the matching opt-out mechanism question for prose cells). Enumerate-and-gate (forcing-function, but churns the row on any of the 7 importers' edits) vs keep 'imported widely' as an explicit opt-out the checker skips (leaving that one row ungated)? This changes both MODULE.md and the checker's row-matching design. Independent confidence rated 88% (<90 gate). To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Ship Option A (tolerant parsing), not Option B (canonical reformat). Add two standalone pure helpers to seam_check.py — true_importers() (AST-walk scripts/*.py for the static importer map) and module_md_importer_drift() (parse only rows whose 'imported by ...' tokens all resolve to real local stems, hold those exact, skip prose rows like 'the adapters'/'imported widely'), wired into main() as ERROR Problems, tests in the existing tests/test_seam_check.py. Mirrors the two precedent tolerant/asymmetric helpers (scripts_missing_from_module_md, scripts_missing_from_registry_descriptions); Option B is gold-plating. Safe to auto-ship: dev-tooling CI gate (is_hot=false, no safety machinery), single-PR reversible, and self-gated — a buggy parser turns test_live_docs_are_green/the live-doc test red so the leaf can't go green. Verified independently: 11 enumerable rows all match AST truth (live-doc green, zero MODULE.md edits), flow-fx7's phantom (tracker_cli !import tracker_beads) is the exact class caught. Accepted residual (note in PR, non-blocking): prose-gloss rows stay unchecked, e.g. sync's real import of tracker is hidden by the 'the adapters' row.

title: machinery_edit.py CLI main() and _load_payload() have no test (self-edit payload parsing uncovered)
description: TEST GAP (self-edit guard input gate). EVIDENCE: machinery_edit.py:182-206 main() and machinery_edit.py:177-179 _load_payload() are untested; test_machinery_edit.py covers only apply_edit() (grep for main/_load_payload -> zero). _load_payload parses the JSON payload + file I/O that drives a self-edit; its OSError/JSONDecodeError branches and main()'s exit codes are the CLI gate to the self-edit machinery. BLAST RADIUS: an unvalidated/malformed payload could reach apply_edit. VISION: floor-adjacent (input gate to the self-edit guard); auto-drainable (test-only addition).

title: state.py force_stage_status() recovery API has no direct test (4 error branches uncovered)
description: TEST GAP. EVIDENCE: state.py:279 defines the public force_stage_status() with 4 error branches; test_state.py has zero references to it (grep). It is the state mutation behind /flow recover's retry/skip/reset of a stage. flow-ixl (closed) covered recover.py 'detect' + force INTEGRATION paths (a different file/basename); the state.py unit-level branches of force_stage_status remain directly untested. BLAST RADIUS: operator recovery correctness — an untested branch can leave a run in an inconsistent stage state. VISION: tighten reliability of the recovery path; auto-drainable (test-only addition).

title: MODULE.md 'imported by' lists are stale: six library rows omit real importers
description: DOC DRIFT (provably-safe; verify each line before editing). MODULE.md library rows enumerate where each module is imported; six rows are incomplete/wrong vs current code. LOCI: (1) lease.py row (MODULE.md:14) omits flow_worktree.py:44. (2) state.py row (MODULE.md:12) omits recover.py:20, status.py:17, reflect_inputs.py:34. (3) heartbeat.py row (MODULE.md:15) claims 'recover / state / _registry' but only recover.py:18 imports it (state.py and _registry.py do NOT). (4) pending_mutations row omits tracker_cli.py:44. (5) bundle_discover row omits resolve_handler.py:33. (6) tracker_beads.py row omits triage.py:26. None affects runtime (seam_check guards CLI invocations, not these prose tables). FIX: update each row's importer list. BLAST RADIUS: doc-only. VISION: provably-safe, auto-drainable.

title: stage-registry.toml commit-stage description names compose-commit.py (hyphen); the actual script is compose_commit.py
description: DOC DRIFT (provably-safe). EVIDENCE: stage-registry.toml:113 (commit stage description) names 'compose-commit.py' with a hyphen, but the actual script is compose_commit.py (underscore). It lives in a description field the dispatcher never executes (the registry is read by stage name/handler, not by parsing description prose), so nothing breaks at runtime. seam_check does not catch it: docs_to_check (seam_check.py:353-358) scans only SKILL.md + references/*.md, never the registry. FIX: hyphen -> underscore at stage-registry.toml:113. BLAST RADIUS: 1 char, no runtime effect. VISION: provably-safe, auto-drainable. HOT: touches stage-registry.toml.

title: evolve_select.py defines an unused _EVOLVE_STATUSES constant (dead duplicate of the one in evolve_reap.py)
description: DEAD CODE (provably-safe deletion). EVIDENCE: evolve_select.py:49 defines _EVOLVE_STATUSES = 'open,in_progress,blocked,deferred,closed'. Repo-wide grep finds 3 occurrences total: the def in evolve_select.py:49 (0 other refs in that file), plus an independent def+use in evolve_reap.py:53 and evolve_reap.py:189. evolve_select.py never reads its own copy. No dynamic/registry/getattr reference (it is a plain module constant). FIX: delete evolve_select.py:49. BLAST RADIUS: 1 line, zero behavior change. VISION: provably-safe, auto-drainable.

title: machinery_edit.py self-edit guard fails open on any git-exec error (allows the edit when branch-detection errors out)
description: PROPERTY: human-merge keystone — a machinery self-edit must never commit to main/master/dev/develop. EVIDENCE: _current_branch in machinery_edit.py:83-87 returns None both on OSError and on a non-zero git return code; machinery_edit.py:127-141 then treats None as neither in _PROTECTED nor in {HEAD, ''}, so BOTH guards fall through and the edit is ALLOWED. The docstring (machinery_edit.py:32) intends None->allowed ONLY for the non-repo unit-test fixture, but the same None is returned for ANY git failure — a transient git exec error, a corrupt .git, an index lock — even when skill-root is genuinely checked out on main. SCENARIO: git transiently errors while skill-root is on main -> _current_branch returns None -> guard passes -> apply_edit writes the file -> the subsequent commit lands directly on main, bypassing PR review. PRECONDITION: git transiently unavailable/erroring while on a protected branch (narrow). FIX-SHAPE (do not implement): distinguish 'not a git repo' (allow) from 'git failed but a repo exists' (refuse, fail-closed) — probe git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree first; only a clean false/no-repo result maps to allow, any error while a .git is present refuses. VISION: the floor — the keystone guard must fail closed; a guard property is never dropped, even on green. HOT: machinery_edit.py is a guard file.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: headless plan rated 86% (sub-90) on a HOT guard file and raised one open design point — when 'git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree' returns a clean 'false' (skill-root inside a bare repo or a .git dir, not a work tree), should _current_branch map that to ALLOW or REFUSE? The plan mapped 'false' -> refuse, but this ticket's own FIX-SHAPE says 'only a clean false/no-repo result maps to allow', i.e. 'false' -> ALLOW. The planner inverted it. The rest of the plan is sound and matches the FIX-SHAPE: rewrite _current_branch to probe --is-inside-work-tree first, distinguish 'not a git repo' (None -> allow, preserves the unit-test fixture) from 'git failed/errored while a repo is present' (new sentinel -> refuse, exit 2, fail-closed); add an apply_edit guard that refuses on the sentinel BEFORE the _PROTECTED / {HEAD,''} checks; extend tests/test_machinery_edit.py with the sentinel-refuse case plus real-git integration coverage of _current_branch (currently untested). To unstick: confirm the 'false' mapping (per FIX-SHAPE it is ALLOW), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: lease.py reboot-clear ignores hostname: a live foreign host's lease can be stolen on shared .flow storage
description: PROPERTY: run lease exclusivity (one run per ticket). EVIDENCE: lease.py:255-257 decides reboot_clearable from boot_id ONLY (bool(existing.boot_id) and bool(current_boot) and existing.boot_id != current_boot), never comparing hostname. A real reboot keeps the same hostname + changes boot_id; a DIFFERENT live host changes both — the two are conflated, so a healthy foreign host's lease is treated as 'rebooted, holder cannot exist'. ASYMMETRY proves it wrong: the Lease dataclass records hostname (lease.py:55) and assert_lease_still_mine DOES compare it (lease.py:325-327); only the acquire-side reboot-clear path drops the hostname check. classify mirrors the same gap at lease.py:365. SCENARIO: host A holds the lease for ticket T; its lease lapses (TTL expiry mid-stage with a long gap between dispatch calls). Host B sharing .flow runs dispatch init T -> read_lease returns A's lease -> is_expired true -> existing.boot_id(A) != current_boot(B) -> reboot_clearable=True -> B overwrites A's LIVE lease and proceeds. Both runs now mutate T's state.json + worktree until A self-detects at its next _guard_lease_ownership (dispatch_stage.py:312) and raises LeaseLost. PRECONDITION: multi-host shared .flow storage (NFS/shared mount); single-host is unaffected. BLAST RADIUS: two concurrent runs mutating one ticket. FIX-SHAPE (do not implement): require same hostname for reboot-clear (existing.hostname == hostname AND existing.boot_id != current_boot); mirror in classify (lease.py:365). VISION: the floor — run lease exclusivity is a named safety property; tighten, never erode. HOT: lease.py is a guard file.

title: concurrent /flow <key> --auto launches both bootstrap a worktree for the same bead; worktree-local lease cannot serialize them
description: EVIDENCE (flow-wsv, 2026-06-06): two /flow flow-wsv --auto runs launched ~8s apart both ran to a PR. PR #123 (branch feature/flow-wsv-audit-drift-all-loci, created 08:48:45) and PR #124 (branch feature/flow-wsv-evolve-audit-enumerate-all-loci, created 08:55:22) carried the SAME three-file fix for the SAME bead; #123 self-merged at 08:53:58 and closed the bead, #124 was superseded (CLOSED, never merged) and its worktree reaped out from under the running session (cwd-deleted mid-stage). ROOT CAUSE: the run lease is <ticket_dir>/run.lock with ticket_dir=.flow/runs/<key>/ living INSIDE each run's own worktree (lease.py:135 run_lock_path, :13 docstring; flow_worktree.py create seeds a fresh worktree+.flow per run). Two bootstraps of the same bead create two independent ticket_dirs -> two independent run.lock files; the flock at lease.py:223 only serializes acquire/refresh/release WITHIN one worktree, never across worktrees, so dispatch_stage.py init returns resumed:true for each in isolation and neither sees the sibling. The bead-status signal that COULD catch it is discarded: #123's run had already moved the bead open->in_progress, so #124's ticket-stage claim hit exit 3 'no transition to in_progress available', but stage-ticket.md step 3b reads exit 3 as the benign self-resume case and continues silently, so a sibling's claim is indistinguishable from one's own resume. The drain loop's evolve_select drops in-flight beads, but a direct /flow <key> --auto launch bypasses select entirely, so the lease is the only intended guard and it does not span worktrees. VALUE: wasted a full unattended run (worktree, commit, PR, CI) and risks a confusing mid-stage cwd-deletion when the loser is reaped; the duplicate version-bump only auto-resolved because the deterministic patch bump made both PRs byte-identical (flow-8we), masking a DIRTY race in the general case. FIX DIRECTION: add a pre-bootstrap duplicate-claim guard at the spec/flow_worktree.py-create choke point (or a canonical shared per-ticket lease under the main .flow, acquired BEFORE worktree creation, keyed by ticket only) that refuses to bootstrap a second concurrent run when the bead is already in_progress with a live sibling run/branch/worktree, distinguishing a true self-resume (same run_id) from a sibling claim. BLAST RADIUS: touches the bootstrap/lease seam (flow_worktree.py, lease.py, possibly verb-spec.md) - guard/safety machinery, so HOT.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: HOT safety-machinery change (bootstrap/lease seam) with sub-90% plan confidence (78%) and two genuine design forks a human must settle before code is written:

1. Bootstrap-claim handoff / liveness predicate. The chosen design is a flock-backed canonical per-ticket CLAIM acquired in flow_worktree.bootstrap() before 'git worktree add' (rejecting Fork A=relocate-the-run-lease because reap/evolve_select/evolve_drain all classify the PER-WORKTREE lease to build their live-set, so one canonical lease collapses their liveness map; rejecting Fork C=pure-inspection because the check->worktree-add window is not atomic). The claim is released at bootstrap exit, but the worktree-local run.lock is first written later by do's cmd_init (after EnterWorktree). So there is a window where the new worktree has a seeded state.json (run_id) but no run.lock. Decision needed: should the duplicate-liveness predicate treat 'branch+worktree exists with a non-terminal state.json but no run.lock' as a live sibling (recommended; closes the gap, no contract change), OR should bootstrap seed the worktree-local run.lock itself (changes the deliberate bootstrap-holds-no-lease contract in flow_worktree.py docstring + verb-spec step 7)?

2. --auto duplicate refusal observability. On the new exit-4 duplicate-claim refusal, should the superseded --auto run exit silently-clean with NO tracker mutation / NO friction entry (recommended; the sibling owns the bead and its status), OR leave a RECONCILE/observability breadcrumb so the double-launch surfaces in /flow triage?

Note: there is NO legitimate self-resume at the bootstrap choke point (every bootstrap mints a fresh worktree + run_id; real resumes re-enter via do/cmd_init), so the guard need only detect 'is a live sibling present?', not compare run_ids. Full plan (Goal/Files/Approach/Tests/Risks) is ready to action once these two are settled.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: advisor ruled the canonical-per-ticket-claim design (flock-backed claim under main .flow, acquired in bootstrap() before 'git worktree add', run.lock untouched) is the RIGHT approach, but BLOCKED auto-ship of this HOT run-mutex change. (1) The two design forks are maintainer-flagged-OPEN in a defer-stem comment, not a recorded DECISION, and that comment explicitly asked to re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively. (2) Auto-ship fails the CI-coverage axis: the plan's own test strategy puts 'real two-process races out of scope', so CI-green proves the liveness predicate in isolation but NOT that the claim actually serializes the concurrent-bootstrap race this bug is about; a concurrency-mutex fix whose core behavior CI cannot exercise must not blind-ship. Note: mechanical is_hot_change is false (flow_worktree.py is not in the guard-file set), but the change is broad-blast on the bootstrap/lease seam, which is the non-hot-but-unsafe case the verdict catches. Judgment settled (design is rulable, plan is solid, independent confidence ~80% and ready to action interactively); this is a safety hold. To unstick: answer the two forks here, reopen (status->open) and re-run WITHOUT --auto, or merge by hand.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
TRIAGE-DECISION: Both forks settled (maintainer). Design per advisor: flock-backed canonical per-ticket CLAIM under main .flow, acquired in flow_worktree.bootstrap() BEFORE 'git worktree add', worktree-local run.lock untouched.
Fork 1 (liveness predicate): treat 'branch+worktree exists with a non-terminal state.json but no run.lock' AS a live sibling (the recommended option). Closes the bootstrap-claim -> cmd_init window WITHOUT changing the deliberate bootstrap-holds-no-lease contract that reap/evolve_select/evolve_drain depend on for their per-worktree liveness map; and since no legitimate self-resume exists at the bootstrap choke point, detecting 'is a live sibling present?' is sufficient (no run_id compare needed).
Fork 2 (duplicate refusal observability): silent-clean exit on the new exit-4 duplicate-claim refusal — NO tracker mutation, NO friction entry. The sibling owns the bead + its status; the superseded run touches nothing.
RUN MODE: interactive only. CI cannot exercise the real two-process race (the plan scopes it out), so this concurrency-mutex fix must NOT blind-ship via --auto. Kept status=blocked deliberately to hold it out of the nightly drain; run '/flow flow-594' WITHOUT --auto when ready — this recorded decision settles both forks, ExitPlanMode is the human gate.

title: evolve audit doc-drift miner should enumerate ALL loci of a PR-introduced drift, not just one file
description: MACHINERY (minor): the §audit doc-drift miner (verb-evolve.md:47, a read-only agent fan-out) filed flow-t97 scoped to references/verb-evolve.md only, but PR #119's drain-termination vocabulary drift (liveness-gated/non-live-lease/'no run is live') also lived in references/self-evolution.md lines 37+39 (the drain-loop overview). The flow-t97 --auto run discovered the second locus only at implement-time via a repo-wide grep, forcing a mid-run planned_files reconcile (RECONCILE friction) to fold it in; had the grep not run, an identical drift would have shipped unfixed for a follow-up that re-triggers a full CI loop. Fix: instruct the doc-drift miner (verb-evolve.md §audit, line ~47) that PR-introduced vocabulary drift typically spans every reference file describing the same subsystem, so a drift finding must grep the whole references/ + SKILL.md doc set for the stale phrasing and enumerate ALL loci (every file path) in one bead's planned-files, rather than scoping to the single file the diff happened to surface.

title: ci-rollup classifies transient CANCELLED (superseded duplicate run) as CI failure
description: forge_github._classify_rollup (plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/forge_github.py:184-227) maps any terminal non-SUCCESS verdict (CANCELLED/NEUTRAL/SKIPPED/STALE) to status='failed', and any_failed wins over any_pending. When a PR has duplicate concurrent workflow runs (push + pull_request on the same SHA), GitHub's concurrency group cancels the superseded run; the transient CANCELLED conclusion makes ci-rollup return 'failed' for a moment though every real check ends SUCCESS. Surfaced in flow-483 PR #120: review_loop CI Monitor broke on a spurious 'failed' (gh pr checks all-pass, re-poll green). Unattended this trips a phantom fix cycle / STATUS=failed on a green PR. Fix: treat CANCELLED as pending (re-run/superseded), not failed; consider NEUTRAL/SKIPPED too. Do NOT fold CANCELLED into green — the green gate must stay 'every check completed-SUCCESS' to match evolve_reap.rollup_is_green (hot auto-merge gate). Knowledge entry id 87ff08752652e2c1.

title: verb-evolve.md drain-termination prose omits corrupt-blocking (PR #119 drift)
description: MACHINERY (minor): flow-wo5/PR #119 made evolve_drain.decide treat a 'corrupt' lease as BLOCKING (wait on live OR corrupt; done requires neither) and updated evolve_drain.py's module docstring, but references/verb-evolve.md §drain 'The loop' (lines ~129-133) still describes wait as 'a still-LIVE in-flight run', done as 'no in-flight run is live', and 'termination is liveness-gated', omitting that a corrupt lease now also blocks until a human runs recover takeover. Doc drift introduced by the PR; verb-evolve.md was not in planned_files so it could not ride along without tripping content-ownership. Fix: update those three loop bullets + the 'liveness-gated on purpose' sentence to say a run blocks when its lease is live OR corrupt.

title: state.read creates state.json.lock before checking state.json existence
description: MACHINERY (minor, hot): state.read (plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/state.py:195) enters 'with flock_blocking(_lock_path(td))' BEFORE checking state.json existence. flock_blocking creates state.json.lock and its parent dirs, so a read against a missing/wrong ticket-dir materializes <td>/.flow/runs/<key>/state.json.lock instead of cleanly reporting absence. Surfaced in flow-wo5 when a dispatch advance ran with --workspace-root resolving to the scripts dir (shell cwd drift across Bash calls); the stray scripts/.flow/runs/flow-wo5/state.json.lock then tripped commit check-ownership exit 3, needing a manual rm + re-check. Fix candidate: stat the state.json path before acquiring the flock in state.read (state.py:189-200) and return the not-found tuple early, so a read never leaves lock/dir residue for a non-existent run. state.py is named safety machinery -> property-check review.

title: nested .flow/ from a wrong-cwd dispatch leaks run-state into the commit ownership gate
description: MACHINERY (minor): the do-loop dispatch calls use --workspace-root . and assume cwd == worktree root, but nothing enforces it. If a prior bash step leaves cwd in a subdir (e.g. plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts after a 'cd scripts && pytest'), a dispatch_stage advance run there either errors ('no state.json at .../scripts/.flow/runs/<KEY>') or, on init, creates a phantom scripts/.flow/runs/<KEY>/ tree. Root .gitignore:28-38 anchors its .flow patterns to repo root ('.flow/runs/' contains a mid-pattern slash, so git anchors it), so a nested .flow/ is NOT ignored. The 0-byte scripts/.flow/runs/flow-ghd/state.json.lock then surfaced as an unowned change and tripped diff_extract.py check-ownership (exit 3) at the commit stage. The gate did its job (refused, never silently committed), so severity is minor. Fix candidates: (a) add an unanchored '**/.flow/' rule (or per-pattern '**/.flow/runs/' etc.) to .gitignore so a nested .flow can't leak; (b) harden the do-loop prose so dispatch bash steps cd to the worktree root first, or have dispatch_stage refuse to init/advance when cwd is not the resolved workspace root. EVIDENCE: .gitignore:28 ('.flow/runs/' root-anchored), diff_extract.py check-ownership exit 3 path. NOTE: .gitignore is a repo/PR artifact so this was NOT applied at reflect-time (post-PR-churn boundary).

title: planned_files omits version-bump files -> reconcile on every plugin-code evolve bead
description: Every evolve bead that edits flow PLUGIN code pays a post-implement reconcile: planned_files is stamped at bootstrap from the plan's 'Files to change' and never includes the two version-bump files (plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), yet the implement stage ALWAYS bumps them per the version-bump invariant (stage-implement.md:45). The implement subagent flags them and the documented reconcile (widen planned_files -> re-record baseline -> re-capture diff) handles it cleanly, but it costs a round-trip on every plugin-code ticket. Fix candidate: have spec/bootstrap auto-include the two version-bump files in planned_files whenever the change touches the plugin (flow self-target), OR have stage-plan.md instruct planners to list them under 'Files to change'. Evidence: flow-5dh run hit RECONCILE friction; stage-implement.md:45 (version bump), verb-spec.md step 6 (planned_files stamping). Linked knowledge entry id d0c98340c59bdc1d.

title: BeadsAdapter.get() returns zero comments live: omits --include-comments and reads body not text
description: EVIDENCE: tracker_beads.py get() calls _run_json(['show', key]) WITHOUT --include-comments, and _comment_from_json reads raw.get('body') while bd keys comment bodies under 'text'. Against the live DB this makes adapter.get(key)['comments'] ALWAYS empty (verified: adapter.get('flow-2pp')->0 comments, raw _run_json(['show','flow-2pp','--include-comments'])->2). IMPACT: /flow triage list shows '(no open-question comment)' for every real deferred bead (the _open_question scan sees no comments); any get()-based comment consumer is silently blind. flow-6rl's decided() probe and its triage-list blocked-surfacing both sidestep this by doing their own raw 'show --include-comments' fetch reading 'text', so decided-mode is unaffected; this bead is the underlying adapter fix. FIX: add --include-comments to get()'s show call and make _comment_from_json read raw.get('text') (fall back to 'body'). BLAST RADIUS: adapter.get() is called broadly; adding --include-comments means every get() now fetches comments (perf + a behavior change for callers that did not expect them). Judgment call -> propose, not auto-apply. Owning: scripts/tracker_beads.py get + _comment_from_json. NOT hot (core adapter, not a guard file).
close_reason: merged via PR #143 (drain closeout, version-conflict resolved)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: ticket flags this 'propose, not auto-apply' (blast radius: get() is called broadly, e.g. triage.py, tracker_cli.py, so every caller would start fetching comments). OPEN DECISION: make get() fetch comments unconditionally (matches the FIX text; simplest) vs gate behind an opt-in param (get(key, *, include_comments=False)) so only triage opts in and existing callers' perf/behavior stays untouched. Mechanical fix is otherwise fully specified and verified: (1) get() show call -> add --include-comments; (2) _comment_from_json read raw.get('text') then fall back to 'body'. TDD tests: assert --include-comments in show args; assert a text-keyed comment marshals into body; assert body-keyed fallback still works (keeps existing fixture green). To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Make beads get() fetch comments unconditionally (add --include-comments to the show call; _comment_from_json reads text then falls back to body). The 'propose, not auto-apply' hold rested on blast radius ('get() is called broadly'), which is empirically false: exactly two protocol get() call sites exist, tracker_cli.py:104 (JSON debug dump, comments fine) and triage.py:193 (which wants comments, the bug being fixed). Both benefit, neither regresses. Monotonic improvement (comments go from wrongly-empty to correctly-populated), single-file + test, trivially reversible, CI-covered, not hot. Restores Protocol/Jira parity (Jira get() already populates comments unconditionally). Rejected the opt-in param: it only protects callers that expect empty comments, none exist, and it would diverge from Jira's signature. Safe to auto-ship: blast radius 2 sites, cleanly revertible, TDD-covered.

title: evolve_select blind to pre-PR live runs: premature drain 'done' + unenforced hot serialization
description:
EVIDENCE: evolve_select.py:188 _gather_refs joins in-flight via 'gh pr list' + remote 'git for-each-ref' (branch-name join, _is_inflight line 102). A launched run in early stages (plan/implement/code_review, pre-push) has only a LOCAL worktree branch -> no remote ref, no PR -> _is_inflight=False.
CONSEQUENCE 1: evolve_drain returns action=done with liveness {} while a launched run is genuinely live. Observed 2026-06-06: flow-56s live in implement (lease pid 85395 exp 05:52Z), flow-6rl live in code_review (lease pid 85602 exp 05:37Z); select returned done, held_hot=[flow-eel,flow-ghd,flow-wo5,flow-yga]. Obeying 'done' strands the held hot beads until a human re-runs drain.
CONSEQUENCE 2: _hot_inflight (line 211) can't see a pre-PR hot run, so one-hot-at-a-time serialization is unenforced. During this drain flow-56s was launched while flow-6rl was still live (both hot) -> two parallel hot runs, risking a self-merge race under auto_merge_hot.
FIX: detect in-flight from the per-ticket lease/run-state (.flow/runs/<key>/run.lock, live unexpired lease), not only remote refs/PRs. evolve_drain already reads leases for liveness; feed that into select inflight_keys + _hot_inflight so a pre-PR live run counts -> action=wait (not done) while live, and a live hot blocks launching another hot.
VALUE: closes a drain correctness gap (premature termination + double-launch of hot beads). BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.py partition/select + evolve_drain liveness wiring + tests for pre-PR inflight detection.

title: do-loop self-drift on stage-registry.toml/handlers halts: owned-reconcile carve-out misses stage_registry when skill_root==worktree
description:
ROOT CAUSE: in the spec->EnterWorktree->do flow the do-loop executes scripts from the run's worktree, so _skill_root_from_script() (dispatch_stage.py:129,305; snapshot.py:50,174) resolves skill_root to the WORKTREE, not a separate main checkout. compute_snapshot reads the stage_registry component from stage_registry_path(skill_root) = worktree/stage-registry.toml (snapshot.py:174).

CONSEQUENCE A (this run, flow-alz): a ticket whose deliverable IS editing stage-registry.toml drifts the snapshot's stage_registry component (classify_drift snapshot.py:208). The owned-drift auto-reconcile only covers workspace_toml-sole drift (flow-u3s / PR#66), so the next dispatch_stage.py advance returns exit 1 'config/version drift mid-run' and forces a manual recover.py reload-snapshot detour. An unattended --auto run following SKILL.md's literal drift handling ('surface + /flow recover, break the loop') would HALT and defer instead of completing. The same applies to any wired skill: handler edited in-worktree.

CONSEQUENCE B: self-evolution.md step 2b claims machinery APPLY-NOW edits carry zero re-review churn because the skill checkout is a separate tree from the run's worktree. In this self-target dogfood flow skill_root == worktree, so a machinery edit to the worktree skill tree lands in the OPEN PR and re-triggers CI + review. The churn-free premise is false here.

FIX PROPOSAL (maintainer judgment, not auto-applied): extend the owned-drift carve-out so a drift whose changed component files all map to the run's planned_files (a deliberate, self-inflicted edit) auto-reloads the snapshot and continues, while still halting on foreign/concurrent drift; and correct/condition the self-evolution.md churn-free claim for the skill_root==worktree case. Touches snapshot.py (classify_drift), dispatch_stage.py (advance drift path), SKILL.md do-loop drift handling, references/verb-do.md, references/self-evolution.md.

EVIDENCE: snapshot.py:13-14,174,208; dispatch_stage.py:129,305,233; remediated this run via recover.py reload-snapshot (recover verb). Friction id 7ccf715c (DRIFT/major).

title: seam_check.py does not guard SKILL.md/prose <-> stage-registry.toml mapping drift
description: flow-0n8 removed a '## Stage handler routing' section in SKILL.md that statically re-enumerated the stage->reference_doc mapping already canonical in stage-registry.toml (reference_doc fields). Root cause it was able to rot: seam_check.py (scripts/seam_check.py) only validates prose script-invocation seams (${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/*.py calls), it has NO check that prose listings of stage->reference_doc stay in sync with stage-registry.toml. The registry header (stage-registry.toml:2-3) says adding a stage = 'append entry + drop a reference doc' with no SKILL.md update step, so any re-introduced enumeration silently drifts. FIX: add a seam_check pass (mirroring the existing scripts_missing_from_module_md helper, flow-aut.9) that flags any SKILL.md/reference prose that re-lists stage->reference_doc mappings, or asserts none exist outside the registry. Hot file (SKILL.md + stage-registry.toml + seam_check.py).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: the fix design is sound (a count-based seam_check guard, mirroring scripts_missing_from_module_md, flagging any doc that cites > N distinct registry stage->reference_doc mappings — robust where structural/pairing heuristics are not), but one MAINTAINER-WEIGHABLE design knob is unresolved: the threshold N. Live corpus max distinct stage-doc cites per doc = 2 (SKILL.md and verb-evolve.md). N=3 (max+1) is a tight tripwire but margin-of-1: any future doc legitimately citing a 3rd stage-doc trips it. N=5 (majority-of-registry) gives headroom but misses a partial 3-4 re-list. The ticket's stated alternative ('asserts none exist outside the registry') is infeasible as-worded since legit single/double cites exist (max 2). Pick the policy: tight tripwire (N=3) vs headroom (N=5). Two concrete corrections found during planning, fold them into the implementation when reopened: (1) the citation/registry regex MUST use char class [a-z0-9_]+ (NOT [a-z_]+) or it silently misses stage-e2e.md (the digit) — registry parse would return 9 docs not 10. (2) DROP the planned stage-registry.toml header-comment edit: it violates the 'never self-edit stage-registry.toml mid-run' invariant (and the snapshot-drift it causes in spec->EnterWorktree->do, flow-alz/PR#111); the fix is seam_check.py + tests only, no registry/SKILL.md edit needed. To unstick: answer the threshold here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Deferred by drain operator to freeze HOT autonomous draining until flow-aen (hot is_hot_change floor absent from the clean >=90% self-approve path) is fixed by a human. With the hot floor structurally broken, a hot bead that re-plans clean at >=90% self-ships unreviewed (proven by flow-6mx/#144 this drain). Reopen (bd update <key> --status open) once the floor is code-enforced. Non-hot evolve beads remain drainable.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Reopened: the hot is_hot_change floor is now code-enforced at bootstrap (flow-aen / PR #147, live on main @ a2373f6, plugin 0.27.37). This hot bead can re-enter the queue safely now — an --auto drain will hit the floor and BLOCK it (defer-stem + status blocked, surfacing in /flow triage) instead of self-shipping, so it waits for a human decision rather than auto-merging unreviewed.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: hot change (hot label) with no recorded DECISION — the code-enforced hot floor (flow_worktree.bootstrap) would refuse this at bootstrap. Parked for maintainer triage instead of burning a drain launch to re-derive the same block. To unstick: record a decision here (TRIAGE-DECISION: ...), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRIAGE-DECISION: Approved (maintainer, explicit unblock). Ship the fix as described in this bead: add a seam_check.py guard for the SKILL.md/prose <-> stage-registry.toml stage-mapping drift, mirroring the existing seam guards. Hot guard file (seam_check.py) -> the in-run guard-property review at merge + CI still gate the landing.

title: dispatch_stage.py module docstring omits the advance and release subcommands
description: EVIDENCE: dispatch_stage.py:5 docstring states 'Subcommands: init, next, finish, status' but the actual CLI (argparse subparsers, dispatch_stage.py:483-504) registers SIX: init, next, advance, finish, release, status. advance and release are missing from the docstring. MODULE.md:11 already lists all six correctly, so the script's own header is the stale surface. VALUE: corrects the module's self-documentation (advance = finish+next round-trip and release are both on the do-loop hot path, SKILL.md steps 4e/5). BLAST RADIUS: docstring-only edit to dispatch_stage.py; no code/behavior/guard-property change (provably-safe). FIX: update line 5 to list init, next, advance, finish, release, status.

title: seam_check.py does not lint /flow <verb> --flag prose against each verb's argparse surface
description: EVIDENCE: seam_check.py:34 _SCRIPT_RE = r'\$\{CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR\}/scripts/([a-z_]+\.py)' only matches python3 scripts/<x>.py invocations in prose; it never cross-checks slash-prose like '/flow <verb> --flag' in references/*.md against the argparse surface of scripts/<verb>.py. So a reference doc that names a phantom or renamed verb flag passes the seam gate. CORROBORATION (git history of reactive doc-fix PRs that a prose-flag lint would have gated at merge time): #103 'redirect phantom flags', #95 'reconcile prose shows...', plus #90/#92/#77/#82 doc reconciles. The reflect/knowledge store carries this as MACHINERY-major (flow-bbb): proposed as a prose-lint pass, never landed; still present in code. VALUE: converts a recurring reactive doc-drift class into a pre-merge gate failure; strengthens the prose<->CLI seam invariant that is core to the thesis. BLAST RADIUS: extends seam_check.py (not a guard file, not hot); additive lint, the risk is false positives, mitigated by scoping to documented verbs. FIX: add a pass that, for each '/flow <verb> --flag' (and bare-token verbs) in SKILL.md + references/*.md, resolves the verb to its scripts/<verb>.py (or the verb's documented flag set) and flags any --flag the argparse surface lacks; gate it under tests/test_seam_check.py like the existing live-docs check.
close_reason: duplicate of flow-eel (same seam_check verb-flag prose-lint gap)

title: stage-registry.toml header doc drift: required_fields undocumented; roles overclaims recognised reflect_anchor/ship_observer
description: EVIDENCE: (1) The registry field-semantics header (stage-registry.toml:9-24) documents name/default_handler/roles/etc. but OMITS required_fields entirely, yet required_fields is live and load-bearing: used at lines 69 (implement: planned_files), 98 (e2e: e2e_recipe), 113 (commit: commit_type, commit_summary) and consumed by _registry.py:30,69 + lint_ticket.py:47-55,74 to drive ticket-frontmatter linting. A load-bearing field is invisible in the file's own contract block. (2) The header (lines 23-24) advertises roles as 'open-ended strings the dispatcher recognises (records_diff_baseline | reflect_anchor | ship_observer | ...)', but only records_diff_baseline has a consumer (SKILL.md:115 pre-handler hook). reflect_anchor/ship_observer (declared at line 159) are emitted into the descriptor at dispatch_stage.py:316 but grep finds NO reader in any script or reference doc -> the dispatcher does not 'recognise' them, it only passes them through. A convention the code does not enforce by construction. VALUE: corrects the registry's self-documentation so the contract block matches reality. BLAST RADIUS: edits stage-registry.toml (hot file) header comments (+ optionally wiring the roles); doc-only fix is provably-safe -> HOT but low-risk. FIX: document required_fields in the header; either wire reflect_anchor/ship_observer or drop them from the 'recognises' claim (correct the overclaim).

title: SKILL.md 'Stage handler routing' section duplicates the registry (thinness + 3rd drift surface)
description: EVIDENCE: SKILL.md:194-210 ('## Stage handler routing') statically re-enumerates each stage->reference_doc mapping (stage-ticket.md, stage-code_review.md, stage-plan.md, ...). This is neither router, spec gate, nor do-loop skeleton, so it violates the SKILL.md thinness invariant (CLAUDE.md: 'SKILL.md stays thin'). The mapping is already canonical in stage-registry.toml reference_doc fields (lines 43,57,72,86,101,116,130,144,158,172) and the execution path reads descriptor.reference_doc (SKILL.md:127,129), never this list -> dead duplication. Worse, it is a hand-maintained THIRD drift surface: the registry header (stage-registry.toml:2-3) says adding a stage = 'append entry + drop a reference doc' with no mention of updating SKILL.md:194-210, and seam_check.py does not cover prose<->registry mapping, so this list silently rots. VALUE: restores SKILL.md thinness, removes a drift surface. BLAST RADIUS: edits SKILL.md (hot path file) -> HOT. FIX: move the content to references/verb-do.md or drop it (the data is the registry); the do-loop already reads descriptor.reference_doc.

title: Snapshot write failure silently disables drift detection for the whole run (fail-open)
description: EVIDENCE: dispatch_stage.py:183 wraps the canonical-snapshot write in `with contextlib.suppress(Exception): write_snapshot(...)` and cmd_init still returns exit 0. snapshot.py:244-245 classify_drift returns `(True, 'no snapshot to verify', [])` when snapshot.sha is absent. So if the init write raises (ENOSPC/EACCES at snapshot.py:192-195 atomic_write_text, or an OSError from _tree_hash read_bytes during a handler-plugin reinstall/file delete), no snapshot.sha is ever written, init succeeds, and EVERY subsequent next/advance reports drift_ok=True. The TOCTOU drift guard is silently off for the run's entire life and the original error is swallowed (nothing logs it). SCENARIO: a workspace.toml edit, stage-registry swap, or wired-handler code change mid-run goes undetected, defeating exactly the threat snapshot.py exists to stop. VALUE: closes a fail-open hole in load-bearing safety machinery (TIGHTEN-not-erode). BLAST RADIUS: touches snapshot.py + dispatch_stage.py (both guard files) -> HOT, must ride the in-run guard-property review. FIX OPTIONS: either do not suppress the init write (fail init if the canonical snapshot cannot be established), or have classify_drift distinguish 'intentionally absent' from 'expected-but-missing' via a sentinel written on successful init so a missing-but-expected snapshot aborts rather than passes.
close_reason: duplicate of flow-ghd (same snapshot fail-open; flow-ghd carries the fail-closed fix)

title: Triaged+reopened bead re-defers under auto-mode on the already-answered question (triage loop has no exit)
description:
PROBLEM: spec auto-mode (--auto) has one disposition when it cannot self-approve — defer-and-exit (verb-spec.md defer recipe). A bead the maintainer just triaged and reopened (status open, DECISION comment posted by the triage reopen recipe, verb-triage.md) can hit auto-mode again (e.g. via evolve drain) and RE-DEFER on the exact question already answered. The triage->reopen->re-defer cycle never converges. Repro: evolve drain --include-proposals deferred 11 judgment beads; maintainer answered all via triage (comment + transition --to-state open); re-running auto-mode would re-defer them.

ROOT CAUSE: the 90pct confidence gate conflates two uncertainties — (1) JUDGMENT (what to build), resolved by the triage answer; (2) IMPLEMENTATION (can I build it correctly), NOT resolved by triage. A defer on (1) must never recur post-triage; a defer on (2) is legitimate but silently dropping out of bd ready is wrong.

DESIGN — decided mode (maintainer-approved 2026-06-05):
1. MARKER: the triage reopen path (verb-triage.md / triage.py) must stamp a durable, detectable marker on the bead when it reopens-with-answer — a triaged label and/or a stable comment stem (the answers posted use a DECISION: prefix; do not rely on free text — add a structured marker). This is the signal that judgment is settled.
2. DETECTION: spec/auto-mode, at gate time, detects the marker. When present it runs in decided mode automatically (no extra flag needed for the drain path, which launches /flow KEY --auto unchanged). An explicit --decided flag exists as the override / manual form and for clarity.
3. DECIDED BEHAVIOR: (a) ingest the DECISION comment(s) into the planner context as authoritative on the open question, so judgment-uncertainty is gone; (b) FORBID defer. If the planner still cannot reach 90pct on a NEW implementation-level wall: if the bead is HOT (touches a hot/guard file per the verb-evolve step-2 hot-file set) -> BLOCK and surface (failed/recover, parked draft PR, stays visible), NEVER blind-ship; if the bead is CLEAN -> PROCEED best-effort with the strongest plan. NEVER silently re-defer in decided mode.
4. GUARDRAIL: no-defer must never mean blind-ship on hot/guard work. The hot->block floor is load-bearing (flow-2pp drift guard, flow-wo5 lease.py are live examples in the current queue).

FILES (hot — rides the hot path, guard-review gates the merge): references/verb-spec.md (defer recipe + the decided branch), SKILL.md (spec gate prose — keep thin), references/verb-triage.md + scripts/triage.py (stamp the marker on reopen), references/verb-evolve.md (drain auto-picks decided via marker; document), plus tests for the marker stamp + the decided disposition matrix (hot->block, clean->proceed, judgment-answered->no-redefer). Run seam_check.py after prose edits.

VALUE: closes the triage cycle so a human answer actually sticks; unblocks autonomous drain of triaged judgment work without an infinite defer loop. BLAST RADIUS: changes the auto-mode disposition surface; gated behind the triaged marker so untriaged auto-mode behavior is unchanged (still defers). HOT.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1 (critical, anti-self-defeat): detection MUST trigger on the DECISION: comment stem ALREADY present on the 9 reopened beads (flow-0mi flow-dwd flow-2pp flow-837 flow-wo5 flow-g3e flow-148 flow-8q6 flow-vuc), verified against the actual triage.py reopen code — OR the plan includes a post-merge re-stamp of those 9. They must NOT re-defer after this ships (that is the exact bug). triage.py must stamp the same marker reliably going forward.
AC2 (classifier conservatism): the clean-vs-hot split reuses the EXACT verb-evolve step-2 hot/guard file set (do not reinvent it) and DEFAULTS TO HOT/BLOCK when the file set is ambiguous. Rationale: in this maintainer self-target repo, clean->proceed-best-effort = self-merge of a green PR at <90% gated ONLY by CI; CI catches broken, not wrong. The classifier is load-bearing for autonomous merges.
AC3 (YAGNI): drop the explicit --decided flag unless a concrete manual-override caller is named in the plan; marker auto-detection is the sole trigger (covers the drain path + the motivating case). Extra flag = surface on hot files verb-spec.md + SKILL.md gate.
AC4 (expectation): document that HOT triaged beads (flow-2pp, flow-wo5) still BLOCK on any residual wall; only CLEAN triaged beads self-drain autonomously. This feature drains the clean subset, not all 9.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: the plan is solid (88%) but rests on one unsettled JUDGMENT fork the maintainer must decide. At the spec --auto gate (pre-bootstrap, no PR exists yet), how should a HOT + decided + sub-90%-implementation bead behave?
(A) Still bootstrap and proceed; let the EXISTING evolve merge-gate park its draft PR for guard-review (held_guard/skipped_hot, verb-evolve.md §drain) plus a surfacing comment. Matches 'parked draft PR, stays visible'; avoids re-introducing a relaunch loop.
(B) Have the spec gate itself REFUSE to bootstrap and keep the bead visible another way. Matches the word 'BLOCK', but needs an extra no-relaunch marker or it recreates the very loop this ticket closes.
The plan assumes (A). This is a hot disposition-surface change to the never-blind-ship floor, so the fork should be settled by a human, not blind-picked. To unstick: answer here (A or B), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: seam_check can't lint /flow <verb> --flag slash-prose (let phantom recover flags ship)
description: seam_check.py lints only ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/*.py invocations (its _SCRIPT_RE); /flow <verb> --x slash-prose in references/*.md is uncatchable. This let flow-bbb's 5 phantom /flow recover --reset-*/--reapply-implement flags ship in references/stage-ticket.md + references/stage-commit.md against a recover.py surface of only {detect,takeover,abort,reload-snapshot,retry,skip}. Proposed fix: a prose-lint pass that greps /flow <verb> --x across references and cross-checks each verb's flags against the matching scripts/<verb>.py argparse surface. Owner: scripts/seam_check.py. Source: knowledge.jsonl DEVIATION f534bcbd66901022.

title: implement stage verifies tests but not the project lint/format gate, so formatter-dirty code fails CI
description: stage-implement.md step 5 (references/stage-implement.md:55-58) instructs only 'Run the project's full relevant test suite ... iterate until green'. It never tells the implement subagent to run the project's lint/format gate that CI enforces. On flow-2ec the subagent ran 'ruff check' (lint) ad hoc but not 'ruff format --check'; the new test file was lint-clean but formatter-dirty, passed implement, then failed the CI 'lint' job ('1 file would be reformatted'), costing a review_loop fix cycle (reformat + commit 9835f58 + push). Fix: stage-implement.md step 5 should require running the project's full CI-equivalent gate before declaring green, explicitly including any formatter --check step (a file can be lint-clean yet format-dirty), not just the linter and tests. For this repo that gate is 'mise run lint' (ruff check + ruff format --check + ty) AND 'mise run test'. Severity: minor (one self-healed cycle, no manual intervention). Owning file: references/stage-implement.md.

title: [test] bitbucket adapter's mark_ready/merge/delete_branch untested, unlike github twin
description:
grep mark_ready|merge|delete_branch over tests/test_forge_bitbucket.py returns zero hits, while tests/test_forge_github.py:146 (test_mark_ready_merge_delete_argv) pins all three. forge_bitbucket.py:159 mark_ready PUTs {draft: False}, :167 merge builds the squash payload (merge_strategy: squash), :176 delete_branch shells git push origin --delete. All three are LIVE: wired through forge_cli.py (lines 81-92, 129-151) and invoked by the self-merge keystone in references/stage-merge.md:56-59. The bitbucket payloads diverge materially from the gh argv, so mirrored assertions pin backend-specific behavior, not boilerplate.

EVIDENCE: tests/test_forge_bitbucket.py (zero hits for the three ops, full 204-line file read); test_forge_github.py:146 (twin pins all three); forge_bitbucket.py:159,167,176; forge_cli.py:81-92,129-151; references/stage-merge.md:56-59,63 (self-merge/teardown path + PR<->bead consistency guard).

BLAST RADIUS: Adds tests only (tests/test_forge_bitbucket.py); touches no engine code. Risk that mirroring assertions surfaces an actual behavior mismatch, which is a finding not a mechanical change.

CONFIDENCE: 0.55

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build -- robustness floor: a now-proven (bkt parity) wired backend's mutating PR ops must carry the same test guard as their github twin, or a silent regression in the self-merge/teardown path goes uncaught.

title: Tracker Capability.payload_schema + advertised capabilities list is scaffolding for a never-built capability cross-check (strip or build?)
description:
tracker.py:155 declares payload_schema: dict[str, Any] | None on the Capability TypedDict; it is populated as None across all 28 advertisement sites (tracker_jira.py:67-80 and tracker_beads.py:86-99, 14 caps each) and read by ZERO consumers (grep for payload_schema finds only the literal-None entries + the field decl + its comment). The comment at tracker.py:151-154 says a validator rejects extra/edit payloads that exercise a capability where supported=false using this schema, but validate_workspace.py:8-9 explicitly defers the capability cross-check to phase 7-full and no such validator exists. The advertised capabilities list itself (tracker.py:273, forge.py:137) is read only by round-trip tests (tests/test_tracker.py:42-43, test_beads_adapter.py:118); the real per-method gate is the NotSupported raise in each typed op, not a list read. forge.py's own Capability omits payload_schema entirely, confirming the field never matched a real need.

EVIDENCE: tracker.py:155 (field decl), :151-154 (comment promising validator), :273 (capabilities list); tracker_jira.py:67-80, tracker_beads.py:86-99 (28 None sites); validate_workspace.py:8-9 (cross-check deferred to 7-full); forge.py:99,137 (Capability omits payload_schema). Roadmap mention is inventory.md:513 inside the archived build-log table; no open bead, no in-flight branch.

BLAST RADIUS: tracker.py Capability TypedDict + 28 None-valued sites in the two tracker adapters; any future capability cross-check would need either the field rebuilt or the deferred feature shipped. forge Capability unaffected (already omits it).
