Strip is mechanical but the strip-vs-build choice is judgment.

CONFIDENCE: 0.5

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion -- this is the same declared-but-unconsumed forward-contract template as flow-vuc; the strip-vs-build call is genuinely judgment, and two beads from one pattern suggests a maintainer curation question on whether to mass-file one bead per unconsumed field.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: strip-vs-build judgment call, not mechanical. The strip is trivial (payload_schema: 28 None sites, zero consumers, forge omits the field, validator deferred to 7-full and never built), but the DIRECTION — strip the forward-contract field vs build the deferred capability cross-check (inventory.md:513) — is a maintainer call, and pairs with flow-vuc as a 'one bead per unconsumed field?' curation question. To unstick: decide strip vs build here, reopen (status->open), re-run WITHOUT --auto.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: STRIP the field. Remove Capability.payload_schema (28 None sites, zero consumers, forge omits it, validator never built). Behavior-preserving.

title: time-to-PR is the unfiled third leg of the vision's measurement trio (metric.py exposes only tickets-per-week)
description:
VISION names 'faster time-to-PR' FIRST in the measurement trio ('faster time-to-PR, fewer friction events per run, a lower revert rate -- recall --metric is the seam', VISION.md:54) yet metric.py:357 registers exactly ONE subparser, tickets-per-week. The other two legs are filed (flow-148 revert-rate, flow-eia friction-per-run); time-to-PR has NO bead. Unlike flow-148, the feedstock EXISTS today: state.py:50,52,66 records run started_at + per-stage started_at_iso/finished_at_iso, and observe_ship_event.py:83-85 validates shipped_at in an immutable ticket-keyed ship-event, so both plan-start->create_pr-finish and ticket-fetch->shipped_at durations are computable from already-captured immutable evidence (no new capture seam).

EVIDENCE: metric.py:357 (single tickets-per-week subparser); VISION.md:54 (time-to-PR first in trio); state.py:50,52,66; observe_ship_event.py:83-85; recall.py:232-235 (--metric passthrough already exists).

BLAST RADIUS: New time-to-PR subparser in metric.py reading state.json timestamps + create_pr/reflect stage finish times; a --metric forwarding line in recall.py (passthrough exists). Read-only over existing JSONL/state, no writer, no schema change. Design choice: which stage pair anchors the duration (plan-start->create_pr-finish vs ticket-fetch->shipped_at).

CONFIDENCE: 0.74

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build -- a VISION-named, half-built measurement system (one of three legs implemented) with feedstock already captured; the only open question is the anchor-pair design choice.

title: _runner.py hosts two runner contracts but a third (cwd-bound closure) is reinvented locally in 7 modules
description:
EVIDENCE: _runner.py exports only Contract A (positional-cwd, default_runner() @ _runner.py:11/14) and Contract B (keyword-only, kw_default_runner() @ _runner.py:22/25); MODULE.md:96 documents only these two. A third shape — Runner = Callable[[list[str]], CompletedProcess[str]] with cwd closed into the closure — has no home, so it is re-declared locally in 7 modules. Five are bit-for-bit identical closures binding cwd=str(repo): evolve_reap.py:49/64, create_pr.py:41/67, forge_bitbucket.py:38/43, evolve_select.py:44/64, forge_github.py:32/35. Two differ under the SAME repeated Runner type name: evolve_self_merge.py:79-83 binds NO cwd (runs ambient); flow_beads_create.py:56/127 takes cwd per-call and is in fact a verbatim duplicate of the EXISTING Contract A default_runner body. The smell is one repeated type alias bound to three different runtime cwd behaviors across sibling evolve-cluster modules — a correctness-adjacent footgun, not LOC cosmetics.

SCOPE (from skeptic): the genuinely-uncovered shape is the 5 cwd-bound modules — add a third factory to _runner.py for them. flow_beads_create.py should simply import the existing Contract A default_runner rather than get the new factory (its body is identical). evolve_self_merge.py stays distinct (ambient, no cwd bound) and must NOT be folded into a cwd-binding factory.

BLAST RADIUS: Add a third factory to _runner.py and rewire 5 importers across forge adapters (forge_github, forge_bitbucket) + evolve cluster (evolve_reap, evolve_select) + create_pr (a WIRED handler); separately repoint flow_beads_create at Contract A. NOT mechanically behavior-preserving: a naive single-factory replacement would silently bind a cwd into evolve_self_merge it currently does not have, so the no-cwd and per-call variants must be preserved distinctly. Touches a wired handler (create_pr.py) and forge adapters.

CONFIDENCE: 0.6

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build

title: Canonical snapshot drift guard omits flow's own engine code; protects only external skill: handler bundles
description:
EVIDENCE: snapshot.py:8 docstring asserts the snapshot defends against 'a plugin reinstall could swap a handler's code', but _handlers_component (snapshot.py:134-135) iterates ONLY handlers whose value startswith 'skill:'. The flow self-target's .flow/workspace.toml [pipeline.handlers] has zero skill: entries (all inline / subagent:Plan / subagent:general-purpose / none), so the 'handlers' component is an empty dict. compute_snapshot (snapshot.py:173-178) therefore hashes only workspace_toml text + stage_registry text. known_marketplaces.json shows installLocation = the live checkout /Users/victordsm/repos/personal/flow (source=directory), and CLAUDE.md documents advancing that checkout + 'claude plugin marketplace update' mid-stream -- so advancing the checkout swaps dispatch_stage.py / state.py / diff_extract.py / inline reference docs under a running pipeline with no drift detection. dispatch_stage resolves skill_root via _skill_root_from_script() (from __file__) = the installed plugin = main checkout, same place stage_registry already reads from.

VISION ANCHOR: robustness floor names 'the canonical-snapshot drift guard (the harness config can't change mid-run undetected)'. The guard's coverage is asymmetric against its OWN stated threat model (docstring names handler-code-swap): it protects external bundles but not flow's own engine -- the code that actually runs in the maintainer self-target where mid-run checkout advances are a documented workflow.

BLAST RADIUS: snapshot.py compute path + dispatch_stage gate. Adding a tree_hash over skill_root would read from MAIN (same as stage_registry today), while reflect self-heals commit to the WORKTREE and validate next-run -- so legitimate in-run self-edits would NOT trip it, making the fix cleaner than first feared. Counter-rationale for triage: external skill bundles are independently updatable mid-run via their own marketplace, whereas flow's own engine can be defensibly treated as the trusted base for a session -- a reviewer could call current scope intended. Not a dup of flow-ghd (interrupted-sha-write fail-open, a different mechanism).

CONFIDENCE: 0.4

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (real asymmetry in named safety machinery, but engine-as-trusted-base may be intended scope; surface for maintainer triage)
close_reason: merged via PR #200 (drain, maintainer-delegated; design revised per skill-root bistability trace; adversarial guard review: sound, property_removed false)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: genuine maintainer-triage decision remains open (planner self-rated 78%, raised a clarifying question).

OPEN DECISION: is the engine's exclusion from the canonical-snapshot drift guard a BUG to fix, or INTENDED scope (engine-as-trusted-base for a session)? If guarded, accept the recommended BRANCH-GATED design — hash skill_root's engine tree only when skill_root is on a protected branch (main/master/dev/develop), so the guard is active exactly in the marketplace-tracks-main setup where mid-run 'git pull' + 'claude plugin marketplace update' is the real threat AND machinery_edit already REFUSES self-edits — over (a) wontfix or (b) unconditional hashing (which also needs a reload-snapshot after every machinery self-edit, expanding blast radius beyond this ticket)?

CORRECTION TO TICKET RATIONALE (verified against code): the ticket claims the fix is safe because 'reflect self-heals commit to the WORKTREE.' That is FALSE. machinery_edit.apply_edit edits skill_root's OWN working tree (not the run's worktree), only when skill_root is on a feature branch. The real reason a skill_root hash is safe in the documented (main-tracking) setup is the protected-branch REFUSAL in machinery_edit — the same _PROTECTED gate. Ticket conclusion (safe) holds; its mechanism is wrong. Branch-gating makes the guard's active window the exact complement of the self-edit-allowed window.

SHAPE IF APPROVED: add an 'engine' component (_tree_hash over skill_root, branch-gated, excluding stage-registry.toml to avoid double-coverage) to compute_snapshot/_payload/drifted_components in snapshot.py; flows through dispatch's existing classify_drift->verify->drifted_components seam with no dispatch_stage change. Confirm you want mid-run engine advances to ABORT in-flight runs (exit 1) rather than silently swap engine code.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: FIX, branch-gated. Hash skill_root engine tree into the canonical snapshot ONLY when skill_root is on a protected branch (main, master, dev, develop), active exactly in the marketplace-tracks-main threat window. Do NOT hash unconditionally (avoids a reload-snapshot after every machinery self-edit).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: post-decision implementation block on a hot guard change. The judgment (FIX, branch-gated) is settled; this is a safety/correctness concern surfaced at plan time that needs a maintainer interactive pass.

UNRECONCILED PREMISE — what skill_root resolves to DURING a run:
- The DECISION gates the new engine hash on skill_root being a protected branch (main/master/dev/develop). But _skill_root_from_script() resolves from the do-loop scripts' __file__, and proven memory (flow-alz/PR#111, snapshot-only-workspace-toml-drifts-from-worktree) records that in the primary spec->EnterWorktree->do flow skill_root == the run's WORKTREE (a feature branch), NOT main. The do-loop invokes ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/dispatch_stage.py and that dir is the worktree's copy after EnterWorktree.
- If skill_root==worktree during runs, the branch-gated engine component is ABSENT for the dominant fire-and-forget flow (feature branch is not protected). The guard would activate ONLY for standalone /flow do launched from a main checkout. That is materially narrower than the ticket's stated threat ('advancing the main-tracking checkout mid-run swaps dispatch_stage.py / state.py under a running pipeline'), which the DECISION was recorded to address. The fix as specced may be largely inert against its own motivating threat.
- The auto-generated plan is internally inconsistent on this exact point: it asserts skill_root==main (to justify the guard being active) AND that reflect self-edits 'commit to a feature-branch skill_root' so they don't trip the hash. Both cannot hold for the same run — the tell that the premise was inherited from the ticket verbatim, unverified.

SECONDARY (design, maintainer's call): the plan extends scope to a SECOND guard file by extracting _branch.py and rewriting machinery_edit.py to import it (DRY over a 4-element {main,master,dev,develop} set that won't drift). Duplicating ~10 lines in snapshot.py keeps the blast radius to snapshot.py + its test. The DECISION didn't ask for the machinery_edit edit.

To unstick: confirm what _skill_root_from_script() actually resolves to during spec->EnterWorktree->do (read-only trace), reconcile the gate's active window with that, then reopen (status->open) and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively. If the intended scope really is main-checkout-only coverage, state that explicitly so the design matches the threat model.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: TRACE RESULT (read-only, 2026-06-09, requested by the 11:57 blocker): swept 12 run transcripts incl. today's live --auto runs. The loaded SKILL.md embeds the MAIN-checkout absolute path (${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} substituted at skill load), so do-loop engine invocations default to /Users/victordsm/repos/personal/flow/plugins/.../scripts/*.py — main checkout, even after EnterWorktree. BUT 4 of 12 transcripts ALSO contain repo-relative invocations (python3 plugins/flow/.../dispatch_stage.py with cwd=the worktree), which resolve to the WORKTREE copy. So _skill_root_from_script() is BISTABLE mid-run — it flips between main and worktree per invocation depending on how the agent typed the path. This reconciles the contradiction: flow-alz observed worktree resolution (relative-path invocations), the ticket assumed main (absolute-path invocations); both happen, in the same run. CONSEQUENCE for the DECISION: a branch-gated engine hash keyed on _skill_root_from_script() does not just go inert on feature branches — it FLAPS (component present when computed via main-path invocation, absent via worktree-path invocation) and would abort healthy runs with spurious drift. REVISED SHAPE: anchor the engine component on a STABLE root — resolve the main checkout via git worktree list (the flow_worktree.py idiom), hash THAT engine tree, branch-gate on THAT checkout's branch (protected => active). This matches the threat exactly (mid-run advance of the marketplace-tracked main checkout), is invocation-path-independent, and machinery_edit's protected-branch refusal keeps the active window the complement of the self-edit window, as the DECISION intended. Worktree engine copies stay uncovered (run-private; only the run itself mutates them).

title: heartbeat producer side is unwired -- hung detection is structurally blind
description:
EVIDENCE: heartbeat.py:102 write_progress + the 'write' CLI subcommand (heartbeat.py:267,297) have zero callers outside heartbeat.py's own cli_main and tests -- verified via grep over scripts/*.py (non-test) and SKILL.md+references/*.md. quarantine_stale (167) + identity_ok (144) are reachable only from write_progress, so transitively dead in production too. The consumer recover.py:69-71 calls only read_progress + detect_hung, which therefore always read None / never classify hung. inventory.md:833 records enforcement was deferred to 'phase 7-full ships heartbeat', which never wired the producer. (MODULE.md:15 'imported by recover/state/_registry' is misleading: state.py only name-drops in comments; _registry.py only parses default_heartbeat_required, a field stage-registry.toml:13 itself flags INFORMATIONAL ONLY.)

VISION ANCHOR: 'A mismatch is ground truth; the repair is not' (VISION:44) -- consumer wired, producer not, is a half-built feature: recover detect emits a 'progress' hung-classification map that is structurally always empty.

BLAST RADIUS: heartbeat.py producer functions (write_progress/quarantine_stale/identity_ok) + the 'write' CLI subcommand + their tests. recover.py keeps read_progress/detect_hung (consumer path untouched). Either deletion (remove producer + downgrade recover detect hung-classification to the no-op it already effectively is) or wiring (call 'heartbeat write' from the do-loop per-op so detection fires) -- both change recover's effective behavior, so NOT behavior-preserving.

CONFIDENCE: 0.82

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (delete the dead producer vs wire it into the do-loop is a real direction call; both shift recover's effective behavior)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: this is a genuine direction call a human must settle. DELETE the dead heartbeat producer vs WIRE it into the pipeline.

Planner findings (both directions change recover detect's effective behavior, so neither is behavior-preserving):
- Producer (write_progress / 'heartbeat write' CLI / quarantine_stale / identity_ok) has ZERO non-test callers; consumer recover.py:69-71 therefore always reads None and emits an always-empty 'progress' hung-map.
- WIRE is NOT the 'call heartbeat write from the do-loop per-op' one-liner the ticket implies: the do-loop is stage-granular (no per-op seam), so wiring means editing every long-running references/stage-*.md to emit per-op writes, adding a quarantine_stale stage-start call site, AND new consumer prose in verb-recover.md to actually act on the hung classification (it ignores the map today). skill: handlers are opaque and cannot cooperate.
- DELETE is minimal and in-scope: feature was deferred to 'phase 7-full ships heartbeat' (inventory.md:833) and never wired; VISION:44 ('a mismatch is ground truth; the repair is not') argues against a detection that can never fire. Planner recommends DELETE.

OPEN QUESTION: DELETE (recommended) or WIRE? To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: DELETE the dead heartbeat producer. Remove write_progress, the heartbeat write CLI, quarantine_stale and identity_ok (zero non-test callers) plus the always-empty recover.py:69-71 consumer that reads them. Do NOT wire it into the pipeline.

title: Wired stage error-prose points to 5 /flow recover remediation flags that don't exist
description:
EVIDENCE: references/stage-ticket.md:100 (/flow recover --reset-ticket <KEY>), :102 (--reset-frontmatter); references/stage-commit.md:45 & :57 (/flow recover --reset-baseline), :88 (--reapply-implement '(phase 8c)'). recover.py --help exposes ONLY {detect,takeover,abort,reload-snapshot,retry,skip} -- none of these 5 flags exist (verified via python3 recover.py --help). verb-recover.md (the canonical recover ref) and recover.py agree on the real surface; only these two wired ## Errors sections name the phantom flags. seam_check.py cannot catch it: it lints ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/*.py invocations, not /flow recover --x prose.

VISION ANCHOR: 'the code is what's true now' (VISION:44) + robustness floor. recover verb has SHIPPED (recover.py + verb-recover.md, 6 subcommands) with a DIFFERENT surface than the '(phase 8c)' breadcrumbs promised, so these are stale-and-wrong, not honest placeholders: the doc advertises --reset-*/--reapply-implement flags the shipped impl never delivered. When ticket-fetch or commit-apply fails mid-run, the wired error prose hands the agent a recovery command that errors out.

BLAST RADIUS: references/stage-ticket.md + references/stage-commit.md, both wired inline-stage reference_docs the dispatcher feeds to the ticket/commit agents. Doc-only edit, but the resolution is a judgment call (build the flags, redirect to the real retry/skip/abort surface, or both) -- not mechanical. Risk: leaving a phantom flag misroutes an in-flight recovery.

CONFIDENCE: 0.82

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (redirect the 5 phantom-flag mentions to the real recover surface {retry,skip,abort,takeover,reload-snapshot}; low-risk doc edit on a live recovery path)

title: Bundle-manifest contract fields (required_capabilities, required_outputs, side_effects, stage_compatibility) are schema-validated but never consumed
description:
EVIDENCE: bundle_discover.py:72-76 declares required_capabilities/required_outputs/side_effects/stage_compatibility on ManifestSkill and :168-176 validates each as list[str] (via _str_list_field). grep for attribute reads (.required_capabilities|.required_outputs|.side_effects|.stage_compatibility) across plugins/ returns ZERO hits; nothing reads the values. Real consumers confirm: resolve_handler.py / init.py._provides_skill read only skill.handler_string (init.py:60-61); init.py._compose_handlers reads only manifest.bundle_name + skill.handler_string (lines 406-410). Fields are parsed and rejected-if-malformed but no stage acts on them. (_registry.py is a SEPARATE registry, not a consumer of this ManifestSkill.)

VISION ANCHOR: 'generality is added only when I personally hit the wall' (VISION:27) + 'adds surface -> drop or escalate' (VISION:78). A forward-contract pre-declaring capabilities/outputs/side-effects the engine never enforces, on an external-facing .flow-bundle.toml seam, is speculative generality.

BLAST RADIUS: bundle_discover.py parser + ManifestSkill dataclass; any installed .flow-bundle.toml setting these fields would need them kept (or validation removed). Schema validation of external bundle input has standalone reject-malformed value, so this is a judgment about whether the forward-contract is intended, not provable dead code.

CONFIDENCE: 0.5

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (was the forward-contract intended? external-schema reject-malformed has standalone value; this is exactly what the proposal channel exists to resolve)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve (judgment call on an external-facing contract seam; headless planner returned clarifying questions, confidence 72%). Two open decisions a human must settle before code:

1. KEEP / REMOVE / HARDEN? The four fields (required_capabilities, required_outputs, side_effects, stage_compatibility) are read by no stage (removal candidate as speculative generality per VISION:27,78), BUT: (a) they are a documented phase-7-deferred forward-contract (stage-registry.toml:17-18, inventory.md:238-242); (b) the installed ship-it/0.3.4 bundle ACTIVELY populates required_outputs/side_effects/stage_compatibility; (c) their reject-malformed validation has standalone value but is itself untested -> a third option is KEEP + ADD the missing validation test. Which path?

2. args_schema scope: identical status (zero non-test reads, same 'opaque, validated by skill' framing) but outside the named four fields. If the principle is 'unread forward-contract => remove', include args_schema in the same change, or treat as its own decision?

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: KEEP + add validation test (harden). Do NOT remove required_capabilities, required_outputs, side_effects, stage_compatibility: ship-it/0.3.4 actively populates three of them and they are a documented phase-7-deferred contract. Add the missing reject-malformed validation test. Apply the same disposition to args_schema.

title: evolve_drain cli_main not-maintainer (exit 4) untested, unlike all 3 evolve-cluster siblings
description:
evolve_drain.py cli_main has an untested not-maintainer (exit 4) boundary. The maintainer gate keeps the self-evolution drain dormant on user projects; an untested exit-4 boundary is a silent-failure surface the sibling deciders already guard.

EVIDENCE:
- evolve_drain.py:23 documents 'Exit codes: 0 ok; 2 = bd/git/gh failed; 4 = not a maintainer setup'.
- cli_main returns 4 at lines 122 (resolve_maintainer_repo is None / dormant) and 134 (NotMaintainer from select), 2 at 137 (ToolError), 0 at 143.
- tests/test_evolve_drain.py (tests at lines 17-97) covers ONLY decide() + helpers (_run_dir_for, liveness_map); never calls cli_main. All four exit codes uncovered at the CLI boundary.
- Sibling not-maintainer coverage in the same cluster: tests/test_flow_beads_create.py:88 test_cli_not_maintainer_exit_4 (tests cli_main exit-4 directly); tests/test_evolve_select.py:237 + tests/test_evolve_reap.py:329 test the underlying NotMaintainer raise.
- resolve_maintainer_repo (maintainer.py:38,50) reads _global_config_path(); the test_flow_beads_create.py:88 pattern (monkeypatch maintainer._global_config_path to absent toml, plain workspace, assert cli_main(...)==4) transplants directly.

BLAST RADIUS: Test-only, additive (tests/test_evolve_drain.py). No production change. evolve_drain is consumed by the /flow evolve drain loop but a test does not touch the wired handler.

title: Commit content-ownership gate is filename-level only; unplanned hunks in an allowed file slip through
description:
Content-ownership commit gate verifies touched FILES against planned_files, not hunks. A planned file edited beyond the planned change passes the gate, so out-of-plan hunks ride into the PR.

EVIDENCE:
- diff_extract.py:270-313 (check_ownership) compares touched FILES against baseline planned_files only; a file in planned_files passes regardless of which/how many hunks changed.
- diff_extract.py:277-279 docstring: 'Filename-level gate ... Hunk-level ownership against implement.diff is a deeper check deferred to a later phase.'
- diff_extract.py:255 capture_implement_diff runs 'git diff --binary --raw <head_sha> -- <planned_files>' (pathspec-scoped), capturing EVERY hunk in a planned file including out-of-plan ones; stage-commit.md:84 'git apply --cached --binary implement.diff' then stages all of them.
- stage-commit.md:9 NOT 'git add .' only excludes files OUTSIDE planned_files, not stray hunks inside a planned file.
- dev-history.md:22 + inventory.md:613-619 independently flag the same deferral.
- VISION.md:70 names 'the content-ownership commit gate (a PR holds only what was planned)' as a floor property.

PROPOSED: tighten diff_extract.py from file- to hunk-granularity ownership.

BLAST RADIUS: Tightens diff_extract.py (a named safety-guard file) from file- to hunk-granularity; affects every commit's ownership check. Risk: hunk-attribution false-positives blocking legit edits. Robustness-adjacent. Does NOT pre-authorize widening any gate (VISION:74) -- maintainer decision.

CONFIDENCE: 0.4

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion
close_reason: Not implementable as specified: a hunk-level ownership gate would compare implement.diff to itself (tautology, always passes). No intended-hunks ground-truth artifact exists. Closing; revisit only as a from-scratch intended-hunks artifact design.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve (planner returned clarifying questions; matches the ticket own confidence 0.4 / needs-discussion).

FINDING: the proposed hunk-level ownership gate is NOT implementable as stated. A hunk gate needs a ground-truth record of WHICH hunks were authorized. None exists: the plan stage emits planned_files (file paths only); baseline.json stores planned_files + pre-implement blob shas (the BEFORE state); implement.diff is by construction the implement stage entire delta over the planned files. So the only thing finer than file-level the gate could compare against is implement.diff itself, and a hunk gate comparing implement.diff to implement.diff is a tautology that always passes. The set it would enforce is exactly the set that already leaks. Tightening therefore requires a NEW input (an intended-hunks artifact), which is a new plan-stage output + baseline-schema change + gate change = a stage-contract change on the what-flow-is-for side of the ownership line (VISION:74), a maintainer decision.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
1. Is there an intended-hunks / line-range ground-truth source anywhere in flow that the planner missed? If so, point to it and the real gate can be planned against it.
2. If none, which landing: (A) close as not-implementable-as-stated and correct the misleading deferred/later-phase docs (diff_extract.py:277-279, inventory.md:615-620; VISION:70 flagged for maintainer edit) so the codebase stops implying it is merely queued work; (B) open a new DESIGN ticket to introduce an intended-hunks artifact; or (C) leave as-is, needs-discussion.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: [proposal] make the auto-vs-propose line a backlog split, not a label filter
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (structural change to the evolve subsystem — judgment; runs via /flow <key> through the plan gate, NOT drained). HOT-ADJACENT: touches verb-evolve.md + self-evolution.md + evolve_select.py (none are SKILL.md/stage-registry/CLAUDE.md/wired-handler/guard-file, so not hot by the file rule, but it is a behavior change to the self-evolution loop — review accordingly).

WHY: today the auto-vs-propose line is a LABEL FILTER inside evolve_select (proposal-labelled evolve beads go to held_proposal, never launched). That requires the held_proposal machinery AND tempts the hacky 'relabel proposal->audit to ship it' path. Cleaner: make the line a BACKLOG SPLIT. drain only ever queries 'bd ready -l evolve' (evolve_select.py:17,223) and merge='inline' is repo-level (workspace.toml:53, self-merges any run), so a PLAIN (non-evolve) bead is simply invisible to drain and runs through the normal /flow <key> pipeline — where the spec front-half IS the triage and ExitPlanMode IS the accept. Engaging a proposal is already the flow; it needs zero proposal-specific machinery.

CHANGES:
1. evolve propose (verb-evolve.md §propose step C): file JUDGMENT findings as PLAIN 'proposal' beads (label 'proposal' only, NOT 'evolve'). Provably-safe findings stay in the autonomous lane (labelled 'evolve').
2. DELETE held_proposal from evolve_select.py (lines ~129-143,155,190): once propose stops dual-labelling, no evolve bead ever carries 'proposal', so the filter is dead. Drop it + its tests.
3. DROP the 'audit' label as a required tag (it is decorative — nothing in evolve_select branches on it; the lane is defined by 'evolve', identity by the auto-added evid:/evidfile: fingerprints). evolve propose/audit provably-safe findings get just 'evolve' (+ 'hot' when guard-touching). Update §audit step 3 + §propose step C label guidance.
4. drain Report (verb-evolve.md §drain): remove the held_proposal section (nothing is held in the evolve lane anymore).
5. self-evolution.md: reframe the auto-vs-propose line as the backlog split — 'evolve'-labelled = the loop's autonomous lane (drain); plain 'proposal' = the maintainer's lane (/flow <key>, plan gate). Update the diagram/Consumer text.
6. MIGRATION: the existing proposal beads (flow-eia, flow-g3e, flow-837, flow-0mi, flow-148, flow-3ji) get 'evolve' stripped (keep 'proposal') — done out-of-band when this was agreed.

LANE MODEL (the new mental model):
  evolve-labelled  -> autonomous lane  -> drain --auto, unattended   (audit findings + reflect-sling friction)
  plain 'proposal' -> human-driven lane -> you: /flow <key>, plan gate (generative judgment work)

ACCEPTANCE: evolve_select has no held_proposal; an evolve propose run files a judgment finding as a non-evolve 'proposal' bead; bd ready -l evolve never shows a proposal; seam_check + lint + test green; verb-evolve/self-evolution describe the backlog split.

RELATED: companion to flow-5a6 (/flow triage for the DEFERRED queue) — together they define the two held/parked queues: deferred (run started, parked on the plan gate -> /flow triage) and proposal (never started, awaits the plan gate -> /flow <key>). Neither needs a relabel hack.

title: [proposal] machinery_edit protected-branch refusal is bypassed in detached-HEAD state
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (defensive hardening of a keystone guard — judgment on the refusal predicate's default). CONFIDENCE 0.4 (LOW — mechanically real but skeptic showed it is not reachable today). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (one-line + one test, cheap insurance). HOT — touches machinery_edit.py, a named guard file.

MECHANICAL FACT (verified empirically): _current_branch (machinery_edit.py:66-85) runs 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', which returns the literal 'HEAD' in detached-HEAD state; _PROTECTED={'main','master','dev','develop'} (line 63) does not contain 'HEAD', so 'branch in _PROTECTED' (line 126) is False and the apply PROCEEDS. The protected-branch refusal is silently bypassed when detached.

WHY LOW CONFIDENCE (honest, from an adversarial skeptic that REFUTED the original 'hot reachable hole' framing): the guard resolves SKILL-ROOT's own working tree (the marketplace plugin checkout), which no flow path detaches — it sits on main (marketplace-tracks-main) or a feature branch (maintainer dogfooding). The CLAUDE.md:43 detached-HEAD note is about a throwaway gh-pr-merge spot, a DIFFERENT working tree. And a detached commit does not advance main's ref, so it is outside the guard's stated main-ref-advance threat model. So this is a silent-failure HOLE on a keystone guard, but not currently reachable.

WHY FILE ANYWAY (not dropped): it is a one-line predicate fix (treat 'HEAD'/empty/detached as refuse-by-default) + one test, on a safety guard, where green will never catch the gap. Cheap insurance against a future path that does detach skill-root. Maintainer's call whether the defense is worth the line.

Blast radius: machinery_edit.py only; predicate change + one test. No caller changes. Rides the hot path (guard file).

VERIFIED: architecture-coherence lens + independent skeptic (skeptic refuted reachability, confirmed the mechanical fact — filed as low-value defensive proposal, NOT audit, NOT a current hot hole).

title: [proposal] self-heal/machinery-edit protocol triplicated across 3 reference docs (drift risk)
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (reorg of the thesis docs — judgment). CONFIDENCE 0.7. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (consolidate to one canonical home + pointers). HOT — touches references/stage-reflect.md, a wired inline-stage reference_doc the dispatcher embeds into the reflect agent's prompt.

Evidence: the APPLY-NOW vs PROPOSE+RECORD machinery-edit protocol is stated in full THREE times. stage-reflect.md:68-104 (step 2b, ~36 lines: machinery_edit.py invocation, exit codes, snapshot caveat, protected-branch refusal, the sling). self-evolution.md:27-29 re-narrates the same in-place-self-heal / sling split with the same machinery_edit / protected-branch / snapshot caveats; self-evolution.md:55 re-documents the snapshot caveat already in stage-reflect.md:88. verb-evolve.md:9 re-states the producer-A-vs-B + auto-vs-propose framing a third time. The hot-file guard LIST is hand-duplicated at stage-reflect.md:99 and verb-evolve.md:52 — two copies of the same lease.py/snapshot.py/... set that can drift independently.

Rationale: VISION 'self-evolution is the thesis' — the one mechanism the project bets on is the hardest to hold because its protocol is smeared across three files kept in sync by hand. One canonical home (self-evolution.md narrative; stage-reflect.md / verb-evolve.md point to it) makes the thesis legible.

Blast radius (HOT): doc-only but stage-reflect.md is a wired reference_doc — the reflect subagent executes its step-2b prose, so the protocol must stay REACHABLE in stage-reflect.md, not just relocated. A guard-property check applies (does trimming step 2b to a pointer change what the reflect agent actually does?). Maintainer reorg call. The duplicated guard list is the concrete drift risk if left as-is.

VERIFIED: reorg lens. Lens-verified, not independently skeptic'd.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve (planner confidence 0.75, one scope-flipping open decision). OPEN QUESTION: the hand-duplicated 9-file hot-file guard list (stage-reflect.md:99 and verb-evolve.md:54) is this ticket's named 'concrete drift risk', but both copies are OPERATIONAL (the agent picks the hot label while filing a bead in each flow) and no code constant owns the set. A strictly doc-only reorg cannot eliminate the duplication without breaking one operational doc's self-sufficiency. Two paths: (a) DOC-ONLY (the RECOMMENDED DEFAULT) — consolidate the narrative into self-evolution.md + pointers, keep step-2b operationally self-sufficient, and leave the guard list duplicated-but-marked (sync-comment on each copy); the named drift risk is flagged, not eliminated. (b) WIDER — additionally extract the 9-file set to a single code-owned source (e.g. a constant in flow_beads_create.py both docs + the filing path reference), eliminating the drift but touching scripts/. The rest of the reorg (narrative -> self-evolution.md canonical, stage-reflect.md step 2b stays a self-sufficient executable checklist with only the pure-WHY framing trimmed to a pointer, verb-evolve.md:9 collapsed to a pointer) is settled and doc-only either way. To unstick: pick (a) or (b) here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: DOC-ONLY consolidate. Move the self-heal/machinery-edit narrative into self-evolution.md plus pointers; keep stage-reflect.md:99 and verb-evolve.md:54 operationally self-sufficient. Leave the 9-file hot-list duplicated-but-marked with a sync-comment on each copy. Do NOT extract to a code constant in this ticket.

title: [proposal] split inventory.md — live contract reference vs archived phase build-log in one file
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (reorg — judgment on where the live/archived line falls). CONFIDENCE 0.78. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (a dev-history.md destination already exists). NOT hot (doc-only).

Evidence: scripts/inventory.md:1 titles itself 'API/contract reference + build log'; :3 admits in a Navigation note that 'The Phase X / Known holes sections below are archived history, not current status.' Live contract tables (state.json schema :404, Forge operation surface :183, .flow-bundle.toml schema :220) are interleaved with archived build narrative — Out-of-scope for phase 3 :516, Known phase 8-mvp holes :611, Dispatcher state machine (phase 7-mvp) :389, Phase 8b-mvp memory cohort :633. Roughly half the ~837 lines are phase-dated history. A dev-history.md already exists (29 lines) as the declared home for build status, so the archived sections have a destination.

Rationale: VISION 'the system is where the magic lives' + 'the code is what's true now' — a maintainer asking 'what is the state.json contract today' must scroll past dead 'deferred to phase 8' narrative; two altitudes in one file makes the factory harder to hold.

Blast radius: doc-only. inventory.md is referenced by MODULE.md:3 and CLAUDE.md; moving the archived phase sections to dev-history.md means updating those two pointers. No code, no seam (seam_check only parses script invocations, none here). The per-section live/archived call is the judgment, hence proposal not mechanical.

VERIFIED: reorg lens. Lens-verified, not independently skeptic'd.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: the central live/archived judgment is open. Do the per-script CLI-surface tables in the "Phase 8-mvp helpers" (:528), "Phase 8b-mvp memory cohort" (:633), and "Phase 5-mvp integration layer" (:755) sections STAY in inventory.md as live contract, or MOVE to dev-history.md? The ticket lists ":633 Phase 8b-mvp memory cohort" under archived narrative, but those subsections contain live CLI flag/exit-code tables, and MODULE.md:3 delegates detailed tables to inventory.md. This governs ~half the file and a maintainer should settle it. To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: KEEP as live contract. The Phase-8/8b/5 CLI flag and exit-code tables stay in inventory.md (MODULE.md:3 delegates detail there); only relabel the headings to drop the Phase-N build-history framing. Do NOT move them to dev-history.md.

title: [proposal] validate_postmortem.py is a fully orphaned 254-LOC module (zero importers, never wired)
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (judgment — keep-for-future vs delete is the maintainer's call). CONFIDENCE 0.62. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (likely delete). NOT hot.

Evidence: validate_postmortem.py (254 LOC) has ZERO programmatic importers (grep 'import validate_postmortem' across the engine -> none), zero prose/SKILL.md/reference wiring, zero stage-registry.toml / workspace.toml references. The only inbound mentions are a comment in _timeutil.py:4,18 ('validate_postmortem.py grew the strict Z-required variant') and its own test. git log: it entered at the initial claude-skills import (0313c3e) and was only ever touched by one unrelated sweep (408c22c), never wired into a stage. Its one live contribution (the strict-Z time contract) was already absorbed by _timeutil.

HONEST FRAMING (corrected): it stays a proposal NOT because it has a __main__ CLI (that cli_main/__main__ trailer is a uniform repo-wide convention on every lib, not evidence of intent — established by a separate skeptic). It stays a proposal because it is a fully orphaned MODULE the maintainer may still intend for future use; deleting an orphan is a judgment call, not provably-dead ground truth.

Rationale: VISION 'adds surface or can't be anchored here -> drop or escalate' — a self-standing validator no stage invokes is surface with no thesis anchor.

Blast radius: deleting touches validate_postmortem.py + tests/test_validate_postmortem.py; update the stale _timeutil.py:4,18 comment that name-drops it. No engine import, no seam.

VERIFIED: simplification lens (with git-log falsification). Lens-verified, not independently skeptic'd.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: disposition is a maintainer judgment call. The ticket records this as proposal / needs-discussion (likely delete) at CONFIDENCE 0.62, 'the maintainer's call' — delete-vs-keep-for-future flips the entire plan (full delete vs no-op), so an autonomous run must not settle it.

The mechanical delete plan is unambiguous and high-confidence if the answer is DELETE: git rm scripts/validate_postmortem.py + scripts/tests/test_validate_postmortem.py; reword the stale _timeutil.py:4,18 name-drop (keep the require_z strict-Z contract, drop only the dead reference); and ALSO remove the MODULE.md:81 live-map row — the ticket's blast radius UNDERCOUNTED this (it listed only the .py + test + _timeutil comment). Note seam_check's scripts_missing_from_module_md gate is one-directional (script->row), so a leftover row would pass CI silently but is still wrong. Leave dev-history.md untouched (archived build log).

To unstick: answer here (delete, or keep-for-a-future-postmortem-stage), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: DELETE. git rm scripts/validate_postmortem.py + scripts/tests/test_validate_postmortem.py; remove the MODULE.md:81 live-map row (blast radius undercounted this); reword the _timeutil.py:4,18 name-drop, keeping the require_z strict-Z contract and dropping only the dead reference. seam_check scripts_missing_from_module_md is one-directional, so a leftover row would NOT be caught: remove it by hand.

title: [proposal] revert-rate (3rd vision-named measure) has neither a metric nor a capture seam
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (feature + a design question — judgment). CONFIDENCE 0.5. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (scope the capture mechanism before building). NOT hot.

Evidence: VISION.md:54 names 'a lower revert rate' alongside time-to-PR and friction-per-run as what recall --metric is the seam for. No revert/regression signal is captured anywhere: grep revert|rollback|reopen across scripts hits only compose_commit.py:30 (commit-type enum), tracker_beads.py:500 (bd reopen transition primitive, not joined to ship-events), and beads status strings. observe_ship_event.py writes ship-events that are IMMUTABLE once created and record only forward shipping (shipped_at + evidence) — by construction they cannot carry a post-ship revert. So unlike friction-per-run, this has NO feedstock to read yet.

SCOPING QUESTION (for triage): what counts as a revert — a git revert commit, a reopened bead, a reverted/closed PR? The answer decides the capture mechanism (a post-merge revert observer vs a tracker-reopen-to-ship-event join). 2-part: observe, THEN measure.

Rationale: without a revert measure, 'auto-ship only on ground truth' has no way to detect that an auto-shipped change later proved wrong — the exact failure the ground-truth gate bounds.

VERIFIED: feature lens + independent skeptic SURVIVES (gap is real; docked to 0.5 — naming-in-vision is not felt friction, and the capture design is unscoped).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: this is a proposal with an unresolved scoping question that must be settled before any code is written. BLOCKING: what counts as a revert, and therefore which capture mechanism to build — (A) a reopened/re-closed bead joined to its ship-event at metric-compute time [recommended: only option with an existing trigger point, reuses the classify_attribution join pattern, but reopen is a noisy proxy]; (B) a git revert commit observer [highest-fidelity, but no trigger point in a pipeline that ends at draft-PR]; or (C) a reverted/closed-PR observer [forge-coupled, no post-merge trigger point]? This answer fixes the revert-count numerator schema and the entire observe-half file list. The measure half (a revert-rate subcommand in metric.py reusing load_ship_events as the denominator + verb-recall.md docs) is specifiable now; the observe half is blocked on this. Note the hard invariant: ship-events are immutable by O_EXCL construction, so revert capture must be additive (new parallel record or pure compute-time join), never a new field on the ship-event. To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: Reopened-bead join (option A). Define a revert as a shipped bead that is reopened and re-closed, joined to its ship-event at metric-compute time (reuse the classify_attribution join pattern). Accept reopen as a noisy proxy. Measure half: a revert-rate subcommand in metric.py reusing load_ship_events as the denominator, plus verb-recall.md docs.

title: [proposal] recall --metric can't report friction-events-per-run (feedstock captured but unread)
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (new measurement capability — judgment about what to count / how to window). CONFIDENCE 0.72. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (low-risk, read-only, closes a vision-named loop). NOT hot.

Evidence: VISION.md:54 names 'fewer friction events per run' as a recall --metric measure, but metric.py exposes exactly ONE metric (tickets-per-week, the only subparser at metric.py:357). friction.jsonl is append-only: flow_friction.py:84-93 writes structured entries (run_id, ticket, stage, type, severity, ts) and nothing reads them across runs (grep: only the appender + _memory_paths touch the path; reflect consumes only the current run's slice via reflect_inputs). The data to compute the metric exists per-run and is discarded in aggregate.

Rationale: directly closes VISION's ''better' must be measurable, or it is vibes' — the self-evolution thesis needs a friction-trend number to show the harness is getting leaner, not churning.

Blast radius: a new friction-per-run subparser in metric.py + a forwarding line in recall.py (--metric passthrough already exists). Adds a reader, no writer, no schema change. Read-only over existing JSONL.

VERIFIED: feature lens with its own falsification pass (confirmed no reader exists). Lens-verified, not independently skeptic'd.

title: [test] forge_cli cli_main exit-1 (ForgeError) untested, unlike tracker_cli twin
description:
TEST-ONLY, NO PRODUCTION EDIT (not hot — touches tests/test_forge_cli.py, not a wired handler).

SCOPE: exit-1 (ForgeError) ONLY. The originally-bundled exit-3 claim was DROPPED — an adversarial skeptic proved it empirically false (forge_cli uses add_subparsers(required=True) with fixed choices, so an unknown subcommand exits 2 via argparse before cli_main dispatches; the thin pass-through handlers have no input validation, so the documented 'exit 3 = invalid args' is contradicted by the code and is not a twin of tracker's handler-level exit-3). Do NOT add an exit-3 test.

Contract (established): forge_cli.py:183-185 returns 1 on ForgeError; documented at forge_cli.py:32 ('1 = transient/unknown forge error'). Twin: tests/test_tracker_cli.py:455-463 test_tracker_error_returns_1 asserts rc==1 via a _FailingTracker. forge is the declared structural twin of tracker (MODULE.md:39).

Gap: tests/test_forge_cli.py asserts only rc==0 (x7) and rc==2 (line 134); no rc==1.

Fix: add ONE assertion exercising a _FailingForge (mirror of _FailingTracker) so cli_main(...) returns 1 on ForgeError. No production change.

Verified: symmetry lens; skeptic REFUTED the bundled framing but confirmed this narrowed exit-1 kernel is audit-grade (doc-established + twin-backed).

title: [test] create_pr cli_main exit-3 (RefusedBranch) untested at CLI boundary
description:
TEST-ONLY, NO PRODUCTION EDIT (not hot — touches tests/test_create_pr.py, not the wired create_pr handler).

Contract (established): create_pr.py:147-149 maps RefusedBranch -> return 3 inside cli_main; documented at create_pr.py:26 ('3 = refused') AND references/stage-create_pr.md:14 ('Exit 3 -> refused').

Gap: tests/test_create_pr.py asserts cli_main()==0 (line 138) and ==2 (line 147), plus RefusedBranch as a bare exception on the underlying open_or_get_pr (~line 100) — but NEVER cli_main()==3. Siblings 0/2 are covered at the boundary; 3 is not.

Fix: add ONE assertion that cli_main(...) returns 3 on a protected branch, reusing the existing monkeypatch fixtures (_git_runner(branch='main') as in the line-99 test). create_pr.py already returns 3; no production edit.

Verified: symmetry lens + independent adversarial skeptic SURVIVES as audit (doc-established contract, reachable, purely additive test).

title: Add /flow triage: surface + reopen deferred tickets (no mechanism today)
description:
GAP: there is NO mechanism to tackle deferred tickets. An --auto run that cannot self-approve (clarifying question, or sub-90% with a reachable gap) sets status->deferred + comments its open questions, then exits (verb-spec.md auto path). Deferred is UNIVERSAL, not maintainer-only (verb-spec.md: 'behavior is universal'); any user firing /flow <ticket> --auto in parallel (the primary fire-and-forget path) accumulates deferred tickets. But deferred beads go INVISIBLE: they drop out of bd ready (memory bd-ready-excludes-deferred); evolve_select.py:49 treats a CLOSED-or-DEFERRED bead as never-in-flight so drain never launches/parks them; drain's 'parked' only catches in-flight-with-dead-lease (evolve_drain.py:63), NOT historically-deferred. So drain's Report says 'review your deferred beads' (verb-evolve.md:150) but never lists WHICH or WHAT they asked. Recovery today is a fully manual recipe: read the comment, bd update --status open, re-run interactively.

GOAL: a top-level work-state verb /flow triage that surfaces the deferred queue with each bead's open-question comment inline, and reopens one with an answer in a single step. The DECISION stays human (re-run is manual + interactive); the verb automates SURFACING + the reopen mechanics only. Do NOT auto-retry --auto (it would re-defer on the same question).

PLACEMENT: top-level verb, same family as status/recover/sync/baseline (all top-level, none maintainer-gated). NOT the evolve namespace (its Gate refuses non-maintainer workspaces -> would lock users out of unsticking their own runs).

SHAPE:
  /flow triage                    -> list deferred beads + each one's last 'could not self-approve' comment inline.
  /flow triage <key> "answer..."  -> post the answer as a comment + transition deferred->open; print the re-run-without---auto hint.

TRACKER-AGNOSTIC (go through the tracker_cli seam, not raw bd):
  - read open-question: tracker_cli get already returns 'comments' (confirmed).
  - reopen deferred->open: tracker_beads.py:78 allows 'deferred': ['open','closed'] -> reachable via tracker_cli transition. (Only transition TO deferred is blocked per memory tracker-cli-cannot-transition-beads-to-deferred; reopen FROM deferred is fine.)
  - list deferred: SEAM GAP. tracker_cli has get/list-assigned/state/transition/comment/create/is-shipped/download-attachments but NO status-filter list. Add 'tracker_cli list --status <status>' delegating to the tracker adapter (beads: bd list --status deferred --json, title key per memory bd-list-json-uses-title-key). Wire it into the adapter protocol + a beads impl; jira adapter can raise NotImplemented for now if needed.

FILES (anticipated): NEW scripts/triage.py (+ tests/test_triage.py); tracker_cli.py + the tracker adapter protocol + tracker_beads.py (new list-by-status seam, + tests); references/verb-triage.md (new); SKILL.md router table line + verb listing; MODULE.md + inventory.md entries; verb-evolve.md drain Report pointer to /flow triage; version bump plugin.json + marketplace.json in sync; run seam_check.py (new prose->CLI invocations).

ACCEPTANCE: /flow triage lists flow-wo5 (currently deferred) with its commented open question; /flow triage flow-wo5 "<answer>" comments + flips it to open; seam_check green; lint+test green.

HOT: touches SKILL.md (router) + tracker seam -> rides hot review.

title: reconcile prose: widening planned_files via --set stores a string, not a TOML array
description: references/verb-do.md:53 (post-implement reconcile) says to edit planned_files frontmatter 'with the full comma-separated --files list'. A naive ticket_frontmatter.py --set planned_files=a,b,c stores a bare comma STRING (scripts/ticket_frontmatter.py:291 _coerce_value only treats ^[.*]$ as a TOML array), drifting the field off the array shape the bootstrap writes and SKILL.md documents. Functionally harmless (commit reads baseline.json) but leaves malformed frontmatter. Fix: show the array form in the reconcile prose, e.g. --set planned_files='["a","b"]'. Hit on flow-xdj.

title: MODULE.md create_pr row describes gh-direct, stale after forge seam (#88)
description: MODULE.md line 72 (create_pr.py row) reads '(GitHub): push branch + gh pr create' — stale. After the forge seam (#88, line 39: create_pr reaches the host ONLY through forge_cli.py), create_pr.py calls fg.detect_pr/fg.open_pr via the injected Forge adapter; the gh-direct + (GitHub)-only framing now contradicts the forge section in the same file. Fix: reword the row to describe the host-call going through the forge seam (host-agnostic), keep the git push + protected-branch refusal + title-from-HEAD + PR_URL facts. Non-hot doc-only fix.

title: merge stage push-state guard trips on untracked .flow/tickets/, defeating Layer-2 self-merge
description: The merge stage (references/stage-merge.md:52) gates self-merge on '[ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]' to bind the merge to the CI'd SHA. But .gitignore:28 ignores only .flow/runs/, NOT .flow/tickets/. flow_worktree.py create writes .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md every run, so git status --porcelain is ALWAYS non-empty ('?? .flow/tickets/') after bootstrap. The guard therefore trips on flow's own untracked scratch and defers EVERY non-hot eligible self-merge to the janitor, even when HEAD==@{u} and the tracked tree is clean (the actual PR diff is fully committed+pushed+CI'd). Observed on flow-2fe (PR #90): gate returned action:merge, CI green, but §3 porcelain check forced a skip. Fix options: (a) add '.flow/tickets/' to .gitignore alongside '.flow/runs/' (root cause — tickets/ is per-run transient state like runs/); and/or (b) tighten the stage-merge.md guard to ignore untracked flow scaffolding (e.g. check 'git status --porcelain --untracked-files=no' plus an unpushed-commit check, since the real concern is tracked/committed-but-un-CI'd code, not untracked flow scratch). Evidence: .gitignore:27-38, references/stage-merge.md:46-63, git ls-files .flow (only .initialized + workspace.toml tracked). Friction id 423810c5696f47d180e972f11683adda.
close_reason: Moot: superseded by #91 — the merge push-state guard now uses 'git status --porcelain --untracked-files=no', so untracked .flow/tickets/ scratch no longer trips it. Validated live (flow-xdj self-merged #92).

title: Retire external ship-it bundle after forge bkt parity proven on a real work PR
description: forge_bitbucket.py absorbed ship-it's CodeRabbit fetch + verified-resolve logic (PR #88). Once a real Bitbucket work PR validates the bkt adapter (merge/mark_ready endpoints - risk #4), reduce ship-it (~/repos/personal/claude-skills/plugins/ship-it) to a deprecation pointer. Cross-repo, not in the flow diff.
close_reason: Gate satisfied, retirement complete. The forge bkt parity proof landed: /flow FT-1328 --auto ran the full 10-stage pipeline in brinta-data-platform through the bitbucket forge seam (plan -> implement -> commit -> create_pr [Bitbucket PR #2920 via forge_bitbucket open_pr] -> review_loop CI-green -> reflect -> merge stage correctly stopping at the user-project human keystone), and the maintainer reviewed + merged PR #2920 into dev 2026-07-02 (origin/dev contains the fix tip, verified). The run also exercised the jira tracker seam end-to-end and filed real machinery friction (flow-2aj8 transition-pick fix, since shipped PR#408; flow-88b0 subagent stall fix, since shipped PR#413) — parity proven not just by success but by the loop self-correcting on the seam's real defects. The ship-it bundle retirement (all 3 brinta repos on the forge seam, leftovers removed via dp PR#2859 + ai PR#56, global skill dirs deleted) is now fully evidenced with zero regression path.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve (headless plan confidence 35%, 2 open questions). (1) The deliverable (reduce ship-it to a deprecation pointer) lives in the claude-skills repo, but a /flow worktree edits only the flow repo, and the ticket itself says 'Cross-repo, not in the flow diff' — should this run do ONLY flow-repo-local doc reconciliation (Scope B), or is this not-a-flow-change and better executed as a claude-skills PR? (2) Has risk #4 been satisfied — has a real Bitbucket work PR validated forge_bitbucket.py's merge + mark_ready endpoints? Retirement is gated on it and there is no evidence in this repo it has occurred; retiring the proven fallback prematurely is risky. To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: ANSWER (maintainer, 2026-06-09): (2) Risk #4 is satisfied — maintainer confirms real Bitbucket work PRs have shipped via flow multiple times, including today (2026-06-09); the bkt adapter is validated in production use. (1) Scope: the deliverable (reduce ship-it to a deprecation pointer) lives in the claude-skills repo, so execute it as a claude-skills PR, not a /flow run in this repo; flow-repo side is at most doc reconciliation if anything still references ship-it. Residual to note in the retirement PR: confirm whether the merge/mark_ready endpoints specifically were exercised (flow's deliverable is a draft PR; if merges happened in the Bitbucket UI, note that in the PR rather than blocking on it).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CORRECTION (same session, 2026-06-09): the prior ANSWER overstated. Verified all 3 Bitbucket work workspaces (~/bitbucket/brinta-ai, brinta-data-api, brinta-data-platform) still wire create_pr=skill:ship-it:create + review_loop=skill:ship-it:feedback and have NO [forge] block — so today's real Bitbucket runs exercised the ship-it SKILL path, not forge_bitbucket.py. Risk #4 remains unsatisfied, and retiring ship-it now would break all 3 work projects. Real unblock sequence: (a) migrate the work workspaces to the forge seam ([forge] backend=bitbucket + workspace/repo_slug, rewire create_pr/review_loop to the inline forge handlers), (b) one real work PR through that wiring validates merge/mark_ready, (c) then retire ship-it to a deprecation pointer. Re-deferring until (a)+(b) happen.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: PROGRESS 2026-06-09: step (a) DONE for brinta-data-platform — workspace.toml migrated to the forge seam (create_pr/review_loop = inline, [forge] backend=bitbucket workspace=brinta-uy repo_slug=brinta-data-platform, [create_pr] draft=true to preserve ship-it's draft default). validate_workspace green; forge_cli detect-pr smoke-tested against the real Bitbucket API (clean null). Found blocker for step (b): stage-create_pr.md hardcodes --base main but the repo's default branch is dev — filed flow-4k0 (P1) + launched --auto. Do NOT run /flow to create_pr on brinta-data-platform until flow-4k0 merges + marketplace updates. Residual parity gap: forge path does not auto-attach default reviewers (ship-it attached user_account_id); minor, the only reviewer was the maintainer themselves. brinta-ai + brinta-data-api not migrated yet.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: STALENESS PASS 2026-06-19 (maintainer): path is fully DECIDED (see 2026-06-09 answer+correction) — retire ship-it as a claude-skills PR, gated on (a) migrate brinta-ai + brinta-data-api to the forge seam, (b) one real work PR validates forge_bitbucket merge/mark_ready. NOT a human-decision bead — it is blocked-on-mechanical-work tracking. Update: sub-blocker flow-4k0 (create_pr base config) is now CLOSED/merged, so brinta-data-platform step (b) is unblocked; brinta-ai + brinta-data-api still on skill:ship-it. Stays DEFERRED until the two remaining workspaces migrate + one validating PR lands.

comments.author: Victor De Simone

title: Layer 3: evolve namespace + audit/propose/launch sub-verbs (direction B)
description: Once L2 lands, rename evolve's flag-verbs to a namespace grammar: /flow evolve audit|propose|launch, --dry-run a true modifier. Frees ship (=merged elsewhere), disambiguates reap (worktree teardown). Falls out of L2. Spans prose+code+bd-labels+seam; do not edit verb-evolve.md mid evolve-run (snapshot drift).
close_reason: Shipped: L3 evolve sub-verb namespace (audit/propose/launch/janitor/drain) merged via #93 (main d5233b7), dispatch live-smoked.

title: Layer 2: evolve runs self-merge on green (dissolve drain/ship/reap)
description: Now that review_loop is integral (PR #88), evolve runs can self-merge on green instead of the deferred reap pass. Move §6A guard-property-check to an independent in-run reviewer; keep worktree teardown + orphan-sweep as a small janitor (a run cannot rm its own worktree - structural). drain/ship/reap dissolve into launch + janitor. Judgment call - touches the safety machinery, maintainer triage. See plan purring-discovering-porcupine.md + PR #88.
close_reason: Shipped: L2 evolve self-merge on green merged via #89, validated live (flow-xdj self-merged #92 unattended).

title: flow_worktree.py reap_worktree docstring frames leak via dropped --delete-branch
description: flow_worktree.py:231 (reap_worktree docstring) explains the local-branch leak by reference to 'gh pr merge --delete-branch removes only the remote branch'. But the operative reap path (references/verb-evolve.md:81) deliberately dropped --delete-branch entirely: it runs 'gh pr merge --squash' and deletes the remote branch separately via 'git push origin --delete'. So the docstring's framing is the same stale-doc class as flow-2jv (which fixed the sibling stale claim in evolve_reap.py:19). Fix: reword so the rationale does not hinge on a flag the procedure no longer uses (e.g. note that the squash-merge leaves the local feature/<key>-* branch + its registered worktree behind regardless, since gh's branch-delete is skipped and the remote delete is a separate push). Docstring only, zero behavior change. Evidence: flow_worktree.py:229-238 vs references/verb-evolve.md:79-89. Surfaced by the flow-2jv implement-stage grep.
close_reason: merged via PR #90 (watched L2 first-run; self-merge was guard-skipped, fixed in #91)

title: MODULE.md machinery_edit summary omits the protected-branch refusal case
description: MODULE.md:56 summarises machinery_edit.py as 'Refuses out-of-tree + snapshot-pinned paths' but omits its third refusal: a skill-root on a protected branch (main/master/dev/develop) -> exit 2 (machinery_edit.py:25-28,42-43,63,130-131). That refusal is load-bearing: it enforces the never-commit-machinery-to-main keystone. Fix: add the protected-branch case to the MODULE.md one-liner. Evidence: MODULE.md:56 vs machinery_edit.py:130-131. Blast radius: doc only, zero behavior change.

title: evolve_reap.py docstring claims --delete-branch on merge; operative verb dropped it
description: evolve_reap.py:19 docstring says the reap verb runs 'gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch', but the operative procedure (references/verb-evolve.md:79,89) deliberately dropped --delete-branch: the still-registered worktree holds the local branch, so --delete-branch makes an otherwise-clean merge exit 1; the remote branch is deleted separately via git push origin --delete. evolve_reap.py is pure classification (no merge side effects), so verb-evolve.md is ground truth and the docstring is stale. Fix: update the docstring to match (no --delete-branch; note the separate remote delete). Evidence: evolve_reap.py:19 vs references/verb-evolve.md:79,89. Blast radius: docstring only, zero behavior change.

title: content-ownership gate false-flags planned files whose path contains a space
description: diff_extract.check_ownership parses git status --porcelain paths with line[3:].strip() (diff_extract.py:299). Porcelain v1 double-quotes any path containing a space (e.g. ?? "a b.py") regardless of core.quotePath. The literal quotes survive into changed, so a planned file whose path has a space never matches the owned set (which holds the unquoted a b.py), lands in unowned_changes -> ok=False -> exit 3, and the commit gate blocks a legitimate commit forever. Scope: isolated to check_ownership; capture_implement_diff/_untracked_files use git ls-files which does not quote. Fix: unquote porcelain paths (strip surrounding quotes + decode C-escapes) before matching. Evidence: diff_extract.py:294,299. Blast radius: small, only paths with spaces; pure parse fix in one function. HOT: guard file diff_extract.py.

title: evolve --ship §6A merges before reaping worktree: gh exits 1, leaks remote branch, breaks bd-close chain
description: REPRODUCED this --ship pass. references/verb-evolve.md §6A (lines 76-85) runs 'gh pr merge <pr> --squash --delete-branch && bd close <key>' BEFORE flow_worktree.py reap. Because the worktree still holds the feature branch, gh's local-branch delete fails ('cannot delete branch ... used by worktree'), so gh EXITS 1 -> the && short-circuits and 'bd close' never runs (had to close 6 beads by hand). The remote branch is ALSO left undeleted: after the exit-1 merge, all 6 just-merged branches plus 7 older ones (13 total) were still present on origin via git ls-remote. Stale remote feature/flow-* refs feed evolve_select.py:_hot_inflight (line 215-229), which counted closed hot beads aut.6/bly as in-flight and held ALL 4 ready hot beads (launch=[] until I manually 'git push origin --delete' 13 branches). flow-26g (just merged) patched the closed-bead side in the selector but not the branch leak itself. FIX: in §6A, run flow_worktree.py reap (which frees+deletes the local branch) BEFORE gh pr merge, OR drop --delete-branch and let reap own all branch deletion incl. remote; either way decouple 'bd close' from gh's exit code. VALUE: makes the drainer's reap self-cleaning instead of leaking refs that silently block future hot launches. BLAST RADIUS: prose recipe in references/verb-evolve.md (hot: §6A drainer path).

title: evolve drainer: _hot_inflight counts CLOSED/deferred hot beads as in-flight via leaked branches
description:
GROUND TRUTH: evolve_select._hot_inflight (scripts/evolve_select.py:215-230) spuriously holds the only ready hot bead because it treats CLOSED hot beads as occupying the single hot slot.

ROOT CAUSE: _hot_inflight queries hot beads with _EVOLVE_STATUSES (scripts/evolve_select.py:48 = 'open,in_progress,blocked,deferred,closed'). A CLOSED hot bead whose squash-merged remote branch was never deleted (reap --delete-branch postdates older PRs) still has a 'feature/<key>-*' ref in _gather_refs' all-branches scan. inflight_flow_keys & hot_keys is then non-empty -> hot_inflight=True -> partition holds the next ready hot bead under held_hot forever.

REPRO (observed this pass): open_pr_count=0, nothing hot actually running, yet select() returned launch=[], held_hot=['flow-ghd']. Stale remote branches feature/flow-kos-* and feature/flow-a0t-* map to closed hot beads flow-kos+flow-a0t -> false positive.

FIX (tighten, minimal): _hot_inflight should query only TRULY-active statuses 'open,in_progress,blocked' (a CLOSED or DEFERRED bead is never in flight regardless of a leaked branch). Do NOT reuse _EVOLVE_STATUSES there (that constant is correct for the closed-inclusive dedup path _is_inflight uses). Introduce a separate active-only status set for the hot-slot query. Blast radius is one function: _EVOLVE_STATUSES is referenced ONLY by _hot_inflight (line 221), so narrowing a NEW constant leaves the dedup path untouched. Do NOT rearchitect the all-branches scan (intentional: catches a pre-PR bg run). Add a unit test: a closed hot bead + its leaked ref must NOT set hot_inflight.

EVIDENCE: scripts/evolve_select.py:48 (_EVOLVE_STATUSES), :215-230 (_hot_inflight), :192-211 (_gather_refs all-branches scan).
BLAST RADIUS: scripts/evolve_select.py. ~3-line change in one function + one test. No API change. NON-HOT (evolve_select.py is drainer machinery, not a guard file).

title: SKILL.md frames the deliverable as a draft PR, but create_pr opens ready-for-review by default -- which contract is right?
description:
JUDGMENT / user-facing contract (held for maintainer triage, NOT auto-drainable). A mismatch is ground truth; the repair direction here is NOT -- it changes what the user experiences as flow's deliverable, which the vision reserves for the maintainer.

THE MISMATCH (evidence). SKILL.md repeatedly frames the deliverable as a *draft* PR and asserts draft is the normal end state: SKILL.md:3,4,15,21,22 ('draft PR') and SKILL.md:189-191 ('Draft state is the normal end state, not a caveat: never flag it'). But create_pr.py opens **ready-for-review** unless [create_pr] draft=true: create_pr.py:6-7,44-53 (_draft_config default False),113,170. The sibling doc references/stage-create_pr.md:3,12 and verb-do.md:7 also describe ready-for-review. So SKILL.md contradicts both the code and its own sibling docs.

WHY THIS IS A PROPOSAL, NOT AN AUTO-FIX. The draft-vs-ready PR is the user-facing output contract ('you review the draft PR'). Aligning it either way changes the experience, so it is 'what flow is for' = maintainer's call. The --auto defer-valve would NOT catch this: the evidence makes 'align docs to ready' look unambiguous, so a headless planner clears 90% confidently and proceeds; and a prose-only change does not trip the section-6A property review (which checks lease/snapshot/ownership/flock, not contract wording). So left as audit it would auto-merge a deliverable-contract change with no maintainer eyes.

THE FORK:
  A) SKILL.md is stale -> update its prose to 'ready-for-review' (and stop calling draft the normal end state).
  B) create_pr.py's default is the bug -> the whole product framing is draft-as-deliverable with plan-approval as the one human gate, so the code default should flip to draft=true.
Note: the dogfood .flow/workspace.toml:29 sets draft=false, but that is THIS repo's config, not evidence about the general default for deployed user projects.

CONFIDENCE: 0.55 that A is intended (code + sibling docs already say ready, and this repo runs ready). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build A (align SKILL.md prose to ready-for-review) -- but confirm, because B reframes the headline gate story. BLAST: SKILL.md is hot (loaded every run); whichever way, the fix is small and prose/config-only. HOT: touches SKILL.md.
close_reason: merged via PR #246 (version-remerged 0.31.1, guard review passed: scope exact, no property touched)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: evolve cold-audit (doc-drift miner): the same draft-vs-ready root surfaces in stage-registry.toml:121 too. The create_pr handler description claims 'push + gh pr create --draft --fill, idempotent', but create_pr.py defaults to ready-for-review (draft only under [create_pr] draft=true / --draft) and explicitly avoids --fill (create_pr.py:113,127). When flow-rvc resolves the draft-vs-ready direction, fix stage-registry.toml:121 in the same change (which way it reads depends on the resolution). The --fill half is direction-independent: the description should drop --fill regardless. Not filed as a separate audit bead to avoid prejudging this held decision.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: this is the held A-vs-B deliverable-contract fork, and an independent adjudicator ruled defer (confidence 42%, below the 70% floor; unsafe to auto-ship — SKILL.md is hot/loaded-every-run, the change alters the documented product contract, and a prose-direction change has ZERO CI signal). Routing to maintainer triage as the ticket intends.

THE OPEN QUESTION (unchanged): which contract is canonical — (A) align SKILL.md prose to ready-for-review (SKILL.md-only, matches create_pr.py default + stage-create_pr.md + MODULE.md), or (B) flip create_pr.py default to draft=true and treat draft-as-deliverable as the headline gate story?

NEW FINDING (shifts weight AWAY from the recommended A): the reference corpus does NOT cleanly support A. It encodes a deliberate two-phase contract — create_pr opens a *draft*, then review_loop drives CI + reviewer threads to green, and only THEN fires 'PR ready for review'. verb-do.md:7 and background-pipeline.md:53 both explicitly say the ready notification fires NOT at create_pr (which only opens the draft); background-pipeline.md:15, verb-spec.md:80, stage-review_loop.md:3, verb-evolve.md all reinforce draft-as-deliverable. So 'ready-by-default' lives only in create_pr.py + stage-create_pr.md + MODULE.md (the script's mechanical default), while the dominant product framing across the hot router + most references is draft-as-deliverable. That reframes B as 'fix the code to match the dominant documented contract,' not merely 'flip a default' — directly undercutting the ticket's recommended default A. Genuinely the maintainer's call.

SECONDARY ITEM ALREADY RESOLVED: the audit comment's stage-registry.toml --draft --fill claim is stale — on origin/main (and HEAD) the create_pr handler description reads '...git push + open-or-resolve the PR through the forge seam (gh or bkt, per [forge] backend), idempotent' (no --draft, no --fill). The miner read an older checkout; nothing to edit there in either fork.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-10: draft IS the contract and the default. SKILL.md prose is correct as written; fix the code side: create_pr opens DRAFT by default ([create_pr] draft defaults true), ready-for-review becomes the opt-in via explicit draft=false. Bead is now decided — auto-drainable; hot label stands (user-facing contract), merge-time guard review applies.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor, confirming maintainer ruling 2026-06-10) ship B — flip create_pr.py default so [create_pr] draft defaults TRUE (draft-as-deliverable, matching SKILL.md's headline gate framing); ready-for-review becomes the explicit opt-in via draft=false. Scope: create_pr.py (_draft_config + open_or_get_pr signature default + 3 docstrings), references/stage-create_pr.md (lines 3,13), scripts/MODULE.md (create_pr row), tests/test_create_pr.py (flip default-config test + add explicit draft=false opt-out test). stage-registry.toml:120 already clean (no --fill/draft claim); verb-do.md:7 already says create_pr 'only opens the draft'. Safe to auto-ship: blast = 1 default flip + doc alignment, fully reversible, CI covers it (test_draft_config_* asserts the default directly). hot label stands; adjudicate_hot=true lifts the floor; merge-time guard review holds. NOTE: this bead carried a maintainer decision under the stem 'MAINTAINER DECISION:' which triage.py decided does NOT detect (_DECISION_STEMS = TRIAGE-DECISION:/DECISION:), so the probe read decided:false — friction to log for reflect.

title: recover.py 'detect' hung-progress integration and force error-paths are untested
description: TEST GAP (the documented purpose of 'recover detect' is untested end-to-end). (1) The in_progress -> heartbeat.read_progress -> heartbeat.detect_hung loop at recover.py:64-71 is never driven: tests/test_recover.py has no test seeding an in_progress stage with heartbeat progress. heartbeat.detect_hung is unit-tested in isolation, but its INTEGRATION into 'recover detect' (the signal an operator/reaper uses to decide a backgrounded run died) is unverified. (2) The ship-event-attention counting branch (recover.py:50-53, counts .dupe./.corrupt/.quarantine-intent files) is never exercised; test_recover.py:38-43 only asserts the zero case. (3) recover.py _force exit-2 (state is None, recover.py:109-110) and exit-1 (ValueError from force_stage_status on unknown stage, recover.py:113-114) are uncovered; test_recover.py:80-101 only asserts the success path. VALUE: covers the post-hoc hung-run detection that is the documented purpose of 'recover detect', plus the operator-facing error shapes. BLAST: medium -- recover is the operator's remediation surface. RESOLUTION: seed an in_progress+heartbeat run and assert the 'progress'/hung classification; seed poisoned ship-events and assert the count; assert retry/skip fail cleanly on a missing run / unknown stage.

title: sync.py reconcile exception-resilience branch untested: a throwing tracker call could silently drop a queued mutation
description: TEST GAP (durability path). sync.py:119-120 'except Exception -> failed.append(key)' is never triggered: tests/test_sync.py covers applied / externally-satisfied / superseded / each-op-success / unknown-op-falls-through, but no test makes a tracker method RAISE (e.g. network error mid-drain). This branch is what keeps a failed tracker mutation in pending-mutations.jsonl (not dropped) when a live tracker call throws -- the durability guarantee of the mutation queue. VALUE: pins the resilience path so a regression cannot silently drop a pending tracker mutation on a transient error. BLAST: medium -- untested durability branch in the sync drainer. RESOLUTION: add a test injecting a tracker method that raises and assert the key stays queued. (The companion {success:False} -> else->failed at sync.py:80 is structurally the covered unknown-op line, so not a separate gap.)

title: MODULE.md mis-describes create_pr.py as 'gh pr create --draft --fill' and omits its --draft flag
description: DOC DRIFT (doc->code). MODULE.md:60 documents create_pr.py behavior as 'push branch + gh pr create --draft --fill' with flags '--workspace-root [--base main --ticket KEY]'. Code reality: create_pr.py:127-128 explicitly does NOT use --fill (comment 'Not gh --fill: ...'); create_pr.py:144-145 appends --draft ONLY when draft config is true (default ready-for-review, create_pr.py:6-7,44-53,170); and create_pr.py:163-166 exposes a --draft flag that MODULE.md omits. VALUE: MODULE.md is the live navigation map agents read to find/understand a script; it currently mis-states create_pr's PR-open behavior and flag set. RESOLUTION: update MODULE.md:60 to drop --fill, describe ready-for-review-by-default with conditional --draft, and list the --draft flag. BLAST: doc-only.
